title: How can I see my cashflow analytics?
content_text:
Get better visibility over your finances with our in-app summary of your net inflow and outflow. Here's how you can track them in the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the analytics and budgeting icon in the top-right corner
- Tap the 'Net cashflow' widget to dive into your spending insights
The widget displays your net cashflow value and whether it’s positive or negative.

## Cashflow analytics features
- Visualise your income: your total net cashflow for the current cycle appears at the top, with a comparison to the previous cycle
- Swipe left or right across the chart: view data from earlier periods
- Change the timeframe to view spending over different periods: such as one week, one month, six months, or one year. Tap the '1W', '1M', '6M', or '1Y' tabs to switch between timeframes

title: How can I see my spending and income analytics?
content_text:
Track your finances with our spending analytics features, giving you a clear picture of where your money goes. To access these features in the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu of your Revolut app
- Tap the analytics and budgeting icon in the top-right corner
- Tap the 'Spent' widget to view your spending insights
- Tap the 'Income' widget to view your income insights
The widgets display your spend or income value, as well as comparisons from the previous period. 

## Spending or income analytics
- Visualise your spending: your total spending or income from the current cycle appears at the top, with averages shown below as a bar chart. Comparisons from last period can also be seen as a line graph
- Switch between chart types: based on your preference — bar, pie, or line charts 
- Sort your data: by category, merchant, country, currency, or card (available on iOS)
- Change the timeframe: view spending or income over different periods, such as one week, one month, six months, or one year
- Set a custom time period: view spending or income for a specific window of time

## How can I add or edit spending or income categories?
You can customise your categories easily in-app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Manage' in the analytics and budgeting section. This is only visible if you sort by category
- To create a new category, tap 'Create' and follow the prompts
- To edit an existing category, tap the category and make your changes
The spend section doesn't include internal transfers or top-ups to your own Pockets, or any joint accounts.

title: How can I see my total wealth analytics?
content_text:
With our total wealth feature, you can see a detailed breakdown of your long-term assets and liabilities in one place. To access it in-app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the analytics and budgeting icon in the top-right corner
- Scroll down the dashboard to see an overview of your assets and liabilities, and tap the 'Assets' or 'Liabilities' widget to dive into your net worth insights
The widget displays a breakdown of the wealth you hold in your Revolut account, linked accounts and custom assets (these are added by you and not verified by Revolut).

## What makes up my total wealth?
The sum total of all of your cash, savings, investments, crypto, credit, linked accounts and custom assets (not verified by Revolut).

## What is a custom asset?
A custom asset is anything you claim to own that can’t be connected to Revolut through a linked bank or brokerage account. Examples include property, vehicles, watches and other physical items you want to track in one place.
Revolut's involvement: Revolut offers this feature to show you the information you've input. We don't offer advice on investment, tax, or legal matters. We also don't provide any valuation of these assets.
Everything related to custom assets is input by you. We don't verify, certify, evaluate or guarantee anything in relation to these assets. We can't guarantee its validity. Information on custom assets cannot and must not be used as proof of property or for worthiness assessments.

title: How do I add a custom asset to my total wealth view?
content_text:
## How do I add a custom asset?
- Access your total wealth analytics in-app
- Scroll to the bottom and tap 'Add new' and 'Other assets'
- Fill in asset details (only purchase amount and date of purchase are mandatory fields)
- Tap 'Continue'

## How do I remove a custom asset?
- Access your total wealth analytics in-app
- Select the asset you want to remove
- Tap 'Remove asset'

## How do I edit a custom asset?
- Access your total wealth analytics in-app
- Select the asset you want to edit
- Tap 'Edit asset' and edit the fields
- Tap 'Save'

## Does the value refresh periodically?
The value of your custom asset stays the same as what you entered when you added it. You can manually update it anytime:
Select the asset → 'Adjust current value' → enter the new amount → 'Confirm'

title: How to assign and adjust categories
content_text:
## Changing a transaction's category
You can easily re-categorise any transaction from its details screen. This is also where you can update all payments from a single merchant in bulk.
- Open the transaction you want to change
- Tap on the current category
- Select a new category from the list

## Updating categories for similar merchants in bulk
After selecting the new category, a pop-up will ask you which transactions to move.
- All from this merchant: This option will re-assign the selected category to all past and future transactions from this specific merchant
- Only this transaction: This option re-assigns the category for this single transaction only
- Select your preferred option to confirm the change

## Manually adding a transaction to a category
You can assign an existing, unassigned transaction to a category directly from the Category detail page:
- Go to 'Analytics' and tap the category you want to add a transaction to
- Tap 'Manage' on the category detail page
- Select 'Add transaction'
- Select the unassigned transaction(s) from the list and tap 'Confirm'

## Excluding categories from spending
- Go to 'Analytics' and tap the category you want to exclude
- Tap 'Manage' and select 'Exclude from spending'
- Tap 'Confirm'
- The category will then appear under the 'Excluded' list on your main Analytics screen

title: Create and manage custom categories for transactions
content_text:
## Creating a new custom category
If our default categories aren't right, you can create your own custom categories.
- Open any transaction and tap 'Category'
- Tap 'Create' from the 'Category' list
- Choose a name, select a colour, and choose an icon
- Tap 'Save' or 'Confirm' to create your new custom category

## Editing a custom category
- Go to 'Analytics' and tap 'Manage' in the 'Categories' section
- Under 'Custom' categories, find the category you want to edit and tap the three dots icon
- Select 'Edit category'
- Change the name, colour, or icon, then tap 'Confirm' to save your changes

## Deleting a custom category
When you delete a custom category, all transactions previously assigned to it will be re-assigned to the previous default category.
- Go to 'Analytics' and tap 'Manage'
- Under 'Custom' categories, find the category you want to delete and tap the three dots icon
- Select 'Remove category'
- Read the confirmation message and tap 'Confirm' to permanently remove the category

title: How does sorting to investments work?
content_text:
## What investments can I sort my income into?
You can sort your income into stocks and standard ETFs. Remember that when you invest your capital is at risk and the value of your investment can go up as well as down.

## Funds transfer
By sorting your income to investments, you instruct us to transfer funds from your selected main account into your investment account with Revolut Trading Ltd to fund the transaction.

## Commission fees and currency exchange fees
Commission fees apply to all trades executed above your commission-free trading allowance, if applicable. Learn more on this pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help//wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/trading-fees/what-fees-will-i-be-charged-for-my-trading/?geo-redirect=true.
All orders occur in the currency in which the respective instrument is denominated. The final amount that sorts to a different currency may vary. Exchange rates can change, and fees may apply depending on your plan. Currency fluctuations may affect your returns. For more, read this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help//wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies/?geo-redirect=true.

## Order execution
Orders are placed as soon as funds are sorted. Execution depends on the order type. For exchange-traded instruments, it may be immediate if the relevant market is open. Otherwise, it's carried out at the earliest available opportunity. Read more about trading hours in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help//wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/when-can-i-trade/when-can-i-trade/?geo-redirect=true.

title: How are trips defined for travel analytics?
content_text:
We record a trip when you make card payments using your Revolut account in a country that isn't your registered home country. 

## Understanding trips 
A trip is based on your card spend in a single country. Trips are for one country at a time, but you can visit multiple cities within that country. Trips can occur back-to-back, but can't overlap. They're also only linked to your personal accounts, not joint accounts or linked accounts.
For us to log a past trip, it must be at least two days long, with a minimum of two transactions. A current trip that is less than a day long, or has no transactions, will not be saved once it ends. However, you can add dates manually post-trip too.

## When does a trip start?
A trip's start date is set when we first detect your presence in a new, non-home country. This can be triggered by your device's location changing, or when you make your first card transaction there.
If we're identifying past trips from your transaction history, the start date is typically the date of your first eligible transaction in that country for that specific travel period.

## When does a trip end?
The end date of a trip is usually when you return to your home country. We determine this from your device's location changing back, or by your first card transaction made in your home country. A trip can also end if you travel directly to another foreign country. 
For past trips identified from your transaction history, the end date is usually based on your last transaction in the other country before you returned home or moved to a new country.

title: How can I see my travel analytics?
content_text:
Revolut Trips helps you understand your spending habits abroad and keep a record of your travels to countries that aren't your home country. The spending insights are based on your personal account spending.
You can explore spending for past trips, see transactions per trip, and even visualise the countries you've visited on a 3D map.

## How to interact with the travel map?
Revolut Trips uses a 3D map to visualise the countries where you have used Revolut for spending. This can be accessed by selecting the map widget in the analytics dashboard. Countries that customers have visited will have a white glow to them, while unvisited countries will have a dark blue colour.
- Zooming: you can interact with this map by zooming in (pinching in with 2 fingers) to view country details, or zooming out to view get a global (expanding out with 2 fingers)
- Scrolling: use one finger to rotate and spin the globe and explore different countries and continents
- Country selection: customers can only select visited countries on the map. This can be done by either tapping a visited country, or by selecting the country from the trips list
- Sharing trips: you can share a summary of the countries you've visited on social media using the 'Share' button

## Adding or removing a trip
If you want to manually add a trip, you can also do that. This can be done for trips you took before you started using Revolut, or for any trips during which you used other providers for your spending. To manually add a trip:
- Select the ‘+’ icon in the top-right. The process involves selecting a country and optionally selecting dates and transactions to include with the trip
- Trips can be removed manually by users using the edit functionality

## Editing a trip
When a visited country is selected, customers can edit the dates and transactions associated with any trips to that location. 
This can be accessed by tapping the ‘pencil’ icon in the top-right of the selected country.

title: How does travel mode work?
content_text:
When you travel to a new country, your app will automatically welcome you with a widget tailored to your location. This screen provides you with quick access to relevant Revolut features based on your location (e.g. eSIM, nearby ATMs, and Split Bills).
This feature is based on physical card spend or, if enabled, your device's location. Basically, if you use your card in a new country or your location tells us you're somewhere new, it will activate travel mode. You can manage your location sharing permissions in your device settings.

## Travel benefits and your Revolut plan
Your Revolut plan (e.g., Standard, Premium, Metal, Ultra) determines the travel features you have access to.
While all customers may see the location-based travel screen and receive the widget based on your location, access to certain features — like airport lounge passes or travel insurance — depend on your plan.
You can check your specific plan details in-app to see which benefits are available to you. To do so, go to your profile (tap your profile picture or initials in the top-left corner) and go to 'My Plan'. 

## When does travel mode appear?
The travel mode widget appears automatically when you arrive somewhere new and begin spending in a supported destination. If it doesn't appear, it may be for one of two reasons:
- Unsupported country: the feature is currently only available for a select list of popular travel destinations. We are working to add more countries in the future
- Location services are off or card wasn't used in new location: ensure you have enabled location permissions for the Revolut app in your phone's settings or, if location isn't enabled, use your physical card in the new country for the widget to show

title: Using the Income Sorter feature
content_text:
Income Sorter is a money management tool that automatically organises your income into different accounts and Pockets once it enters your personal account. 
After you've set it up, we’ll automatically sort it into the right places every time you're paid. Income Sorter supports:
- One-time distributions: chosen amounts will be distributed as set by you on your next payday
- Recurring distributions: the same amounts will be applied every payday, unless you change them

## How does Income Sorter work?
Income Sorter first recognises your income and then distributes it into supported accounts as designated by you. See below for more details on each step:
- Recognises the transaction: our Income Sorter tool will recognise your income based on the transaction details. This tool recognises the payer (your employer), the amount (with up to 20% variation), and the description of the transaction you want to sort
- Supported accounts: income can be sorted into Joint accounts, Savings accounts, Pockets, Invest tab accounts (including General Investment and Stocks and Shares ISA account) and P2P transfers

## How to set up Income Sorter
There are a few different ways to set up Income Sorter:
- By tapping the transaction you want to sort
- From within a Savings account or Pocket
- From 'Tools' in the analytics and budgeting section — only recurring distributions can be set up from here
You’ll need to choose how to sort and distribute your income before the sorter is activated. You can only sort one recurring income per personal account. 

## How can I adjust or disable Income Sorter?
- Go to your transaction list and tap your salary payment
- Choose 'Go to Income Sorter'
- Alternatively, go to the home screen of your Savings account or Pocket and tap 'Sort your income'. You can also go to 'Tools' within the analytics and budgeting section
- Tap 'Disable' to disable the sorting
You can also edit the distribution or select a different main income transaction to modify the sorter.

title: What is the analytics dashboard?
content_text:
The analytics dashboard allows you to view all your financial insights in one place with improved breakdowns and an easy-to-navigate interface.

## Where can I find the analytics dashboard?
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu of your Revolut app
- Tap the analytics and budgeting icon in the top-right corner
- You’ll see the analytics dashboard with all your insights in one place

## What information can I track?
- Spent widget: track your expenses easily
- Income widget: keep tabs on your earnings
- Cashflow widget: see how your income and expenses compare
- Total Wealth widget: get a breakdown of your assets and liabilities
- Set up a budget: manage your spending effectively
- Set up Income Sorter: automatically organise your income transactions
The selected time preference setting determines how your analytics refresh and the time period for viewing your financial analytics. This allows you to consistently track your spending and income over specific timeframes.

## How to set up the selected time preference
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu of your Revolut app
- Tap the analytics and budgeting icon in the top-right corner
- Select the date or date range in the top-right corner
- Choose your desired frequency (monthly or weekly)
- Select the start date (for monthly) or start day (for weekly)

title: Why wasn’t my income sorted?
content_text:
- Supported transaction types: Income Sorter only works for bank transfers and personal transfers in your Revolut account
- Same currency required: the accounts and Pockets you’ve chosen your income to be distributed into must be in the same currency as your personal account
- Exclusions: this feature doesn’t support sorting income into <18 accounts or investment products
- Inactive accounts: Income Sorter won’t process transactions if your destination accounts or Pockets are inactive
- Threshold not met: if the received income is less than the amount you set to be distributed threshold, Income Sorter will terminate all distributions for that transaction
- Deposit limits reached: if the deposit limit on a Savings account is reached, Income Sorter might fail to process the transfer

title: How does Bulgaria adopting the Euro affect me?
content_text:
Bulgaria will adopt the Euro as a currency on 1 January 2026. This means that the current local currency, the Bulgarian Lev (BGN), will be discontinued. If you have questions about this process and the dates relevant to these changes, you can find the answers below.

## From 27 November 2025, sending BGN won't be possible
We rely on payment partners to provide local payment capabilities (transfers in BGN). Given this, we need to align dates of the discontinuation of our BGN bank transfer services with our partners. 
Your existing BGN beneficiaries will be converted to EUR, and you won't be able to create new BGN ones. You'll only be able to receive BGN bank transfers until 10 December 2025. You can make BGN card transfers until 17 December 2025. Scheduled payments will be cancelled.
You can still use your card to top up, make purchases, and withdraw money in BGN until 31 December 2025. However, these transactions will be automatically converted to EUR, through your EUR Pocket, before being completed. These conversions will be at the fixed exchange rate, with no additional fees.

## BGN Pockets will be closed on 17 December 2025
Your Lev account will be closed on 17 December 2025, and any remaining Lev balance you hold will be converted to Euro on this date. If you don't already have an EUR Pocket, one will be created automatically.
Your Lev balance will be converted at the fixed exchange rate of 1 EUR = 1.95583 BGN, without additional fees. Your account details won't change.
All regular fees — such as subscription plan fees and transaction services — will be charged in Euro instead of Lev from 17 December 2025. You won't be charged any additional exchange fees as a result of this currency change.

title: What is Open Banking and how does it work?
content_text:
## What is Open Banking?
Open Banking provides secure access to your financial data from other institutions, enabling transparency and personalised services.
Additionally, Open Banking can be used for payments, allowing you to make transactions directly from your bank account through Third-Party service Providers (TPPs).

## Is Open Banking safe?
Open Banking is regulated by financial authorities to ensure providers follow strict guidelines to protect customer data and maintain security. It also uses secure methods to transmit data and store data.
Your explicit approval is required to access your financial data or to pay, and you can revoke this approval at any time.

## Can Revolut both share and use Open Banking data?
Revolut can do both. As a sharer, we can let other trusted Third-Party Providers (TPPs) access your financial data in a safe and controlled way. As a consumer, Revolut can also access your data held by other banks.

## Who are Third-Party Providers (TPPs)?
Third-Party Providers (TPPs) use Open Banking API with your permission to access your financial data from other financial institutions to provide various services, such as budgeting, accounting, or payments.
You may see company names like Plaid, Yapily, or TrueLayer in the Open Banking flow because they streamline Open Banking integrations, providing a single platform and data services for banks and TPPs.

title: How will the account transition affect returns/chargebacks?
content_text: The process for returns and chargebacks will not change. They will be processed exactly the same way as before regardless of whether the original transaction occurred before or after the account transition to Revolut Bank UK Ltd ('Revolut Bank'). You do not need to do anything differently.

title: Are funds in my  independent teen account FSCS protected?
content_text:
## Yes, your funds are FSCS protected
Funds in your current account will be deposited with Revolut Bank and covered by the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (‘FSCS’)

## What does this mean?
If Revolut Bank were to go insolvent, up to £120,000 of eligible deposits in your bank account will be protected by the FSCS. 

## What about my savings?
For information on how funds in your Savings account are protected, please read this FAQ.https://help.revolut.com/help/accounts/what-does-the-uk-bank-licence-mean-for-my-account/question-is-my-savings-account-fscs-protected/

title: Can I keep my e-money account?
content_text:
## All qualifying accounts will be moved to Revolut Bank
All qualifying accounts will be moved to Revolut Bank automatically after the end of the notice period, and will be converted to bank accounts as a result. If you don’t want your account to be moved, you can close your existing account before your move date. To close your account:
- Tap your ‘Profile’ icon in the top-left corner of the app
- Scroll to the bottom of the ‘Profile’ section 
- Select ‘Close account’
- Follow the steps to confirm and close your account
You can close your account at any time before or after the move, but we’d be sad to see you go. For more information on why this move is happening, read this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/accounts/what-does-the-uk-bank-licence-mean-for-my-account/question-why-is-revolut-becoming-a-bank-in-the-uk/.

title: Does the account transfer impact my plan insurance cover?
content_text:
## Your insurance cover remains the same
Your insurance provider(s), coverage limits, and excess liability will all stay the same as before the move.  You’ll still be able to make a claim under these policies, as usual. 

## Updated group policy number for Metal cover
If you're currently on our Metal plan, the group policy number for your travel insurance will change to UK409992764. 

## Updated group policy number for Ultra cover
If you’re on our Ultra plan, the group policy number for your travel insurance will be updated to UK409992765. 
Despite the group policy numbers for these plans changing, there are no changes to your cover or ongoing claims.

title: How will I know when my account has been moved?
content_text:
## We'll let you know via email and push notification
We’ll let you know that your account is moving to Revolut Bank 2 months, 1 month, and 1 week before it happens.
We'll also send you a notification when your account has been successfully moved to Revolut Bank, and has changed from an e-money account to a bank account as a result. 
Alternatively, go to the ‘Details’ section in-app to see which entity your account is registered to. You can find the ‘Details’ section under your balance on the home screen.

title: Is my account eligible to be moved to Revolut Bank?
content_text:
All personal account customers who have passed our account opening checks and have an open, active account will be moved to Revolut Bank. In some cases, we may need some additional information from you before your account is moved. 
This information will only be requested in-app or by email. We will not call you for additional information. If we do request information, provide it as soon as possible to avoid disruptions.

## Eligible countries 
To be migrated, an account must also be registered with an address in the United Kingdom. 
Customers who are located in any of the below territories will be considered for transition at a later date: 
- British Indian Ocean Territory
- Guernsey
- Jersey
- Isle of Man
- Gibraltar

title: Is the money in my Savings account protected by the FSCS?
content_text:
## Savings are FSCS protected
Money in your Savings accounts is deposited with third-party partner banks. These savings are protected under the Financial Service Compensation Scheme (FSCS) up to £120,000 per eligible depositor, per bank. 
This means that your savings balances are in scope of the partner bank's FSCS. If you have other products with the partner bank (such as a current account) this may also contribute to your £120,000 limit.
The FSCS limit applies to your total eligible deposits held with the partner bank. The partner bank information is available in-app and in your Savings T&Cs. Savings services are provided by Revolut Ltd in conjunction with the partner bank, not by Revolut Bank. 

## More information
You can learn more about FSCS protection on the FSCS website.https://www.fscs.org.uk/

title: Now that Revolut is a bank, how is my money protected?
content_text:
## Funds will be FSCS protected
This means your funds will be deposited with Revolut Bank and covered by the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (‘FSCS’). 
If Revolut Bank were to go insolvent, up to £120,000 of eligible deposits in your bank account will be protected by the FSCS. Whether a deposit is eligible for protection depends on the specific product as set out below.

## Balances counting towards your Revolut Bank FSCS protection
- Current account (GBP & non-GBP sub account) balances.
- Kids & Teens accounts, if you're the main parent.
- Pocket balances.
- Group Pockets balance, if you're the person who opened the Group Pocket.
- Revolut Pro account balances (GBP & non-GBP sub-accounts).
- Joint accounts. The balance is split 50-50 between the account holders. This means each holder is eligible for compensation up to the max limit of £120,000. So, if a joint account has a total balance of £150,000, each account holder’s share would be £75,000, and would be covered under the FSCS.
For more information on how your protection works, read our FSCS Information Sheet and Exclusions List.https://www.revolut.com/legal/fscs-information-sheet/

## How was my money protected before?
Before your account was moved, funds in your e-money account were safeguarded. Safeguarding helps protect you if Revolut Ltd were to become insolvent, and — as an electronic money institution — Revolut Ltd is required to safeguard your funds under the UK electronic money regulations. 
Once your account has been moved to Revolut Bank, safeguarding no longer applies.

## What about my other products?
For information on how funds in your Savings accounts are protected, read this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/accounts/what-does-the-uk-bank-licence-mean-for-my-account/question-is-my-savings-account-fscs-protected/. Products that are not listed above, including Crypto, Commodities, or Stocks, are provided by different entities and don't fall under Revolut Bank UK Ltd FSCS protection.

title: What do I need to do to move my account to a bank account?
content_text:
## You don’t need to do anything for your account to be moved
 All eligible customers will have their account moved automatically to Revolut Bank UK Ltd (“Revolut Bank”). As a result of this move, your existing e-money personal account will become a personal current account, or simply — a bank account.
If you don't want your account to be moved, you can opt out at any time by closing your account. We’ll be sorry to see you go, but you can find out how to close your account in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/profile-and-plan/closing-account/.
If you would like more information about closing your account, get in touch via our 24/7 in-app chat.

title: What happens to my data while my account is being moved?
content_text:
## Revolut Bank will become a controller of your personal data
Once your account has been moved, Revolut Bank will become a controller of your personal data. See our Customer Privacy Noticehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/privacy/ for more information on how we process your personal data.

title: What will happen to my account when it’s moved?
content_text:
## FSCS protection
On the day we move your account, it will automatically change from an e-money account to a personal current account. You’ll be able to use our services and features without disruption. Your eligible deposits up to £120,000 will be protected by the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS).
For more information about the scheme, and protection eligibility, refer to this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/accounts/what-does-the-uk-bank-licence-mean-for-my-account/question-now-that-revolut-is-a-bank-in-the-uk-how-is-my-money-protected/ and visit our FSCS Information Sheet and Exclusions List. https://www.revolut.com/legal/fscs-information-sheet/

## Updated terms
The following updated terms will apply to your account after it has been moved to Revolut Bank:
- Revolut Bank UK Ltd Personal Current Account Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/terms/ 
- Personal Fees Pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/fees/
- Revolut Group Framework Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/group-framework-terms/ 
In addition to these account terms, we have also updated other terms and policies that may apply to your account to reflect the new structure. You can view them on our dedicated webpagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/.

title: What will happen to my Joint account?
content_text:
## Your Joint account will automatically be moved
Like your personal account, your Joint account will automatically be moved to Revolut Bank. 
Your Joint account and its details will remain unchanged. Your Joint account and personal account will be moved at the same time. If either you or the other Joint account holder decide to opt out of the migration, your Joint account will be closed as part of the account closure process.

title: Who regulates Revolut Bank and where can I complain?
content_text:
## The PRA and the FCA regulate Revolut Bank UK
Revolut Bank UK Ltd is  is authorised by the Prudential Regulation Authority and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority and the Prudential Regulation Authority under registration number 981170. If you need to make a formal complaint to us,  you can find the steps to do so in this article.https://help.revolut.com/business/help/profile-and-setting/app-settings-and-issues/how-can-i-complain/ 
If you aren't satisfied with our final response — or the complaint has not been resolved within 8 weeks of you raising it — you can also refer a complaint to the Financial Ombudsman Service.

title: Why is Revolut becoming a bank in the UK?
content_text:
## FSCS protection
Gaining a banking licence in the UK allows us to offer our customers current accounts, including protection of eligible deposits — up to the value of £120,000 —by the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS).
Learn more about the FSCS.https://www.fscs.org.uk/

## More products
It also means we can develop new banking products, as requested by our customers. Keep an eye on our website, your emails, and in-app notifications, to stay up to date with our newest offerings.

title: Will my account be at risk while it's being moved over?
content_text:
## The move to Revolut Bank won’t increase fraud risk
Moving your account to Revolut Bank, from an e-money account to a current account, won’t increase the risk of fraud. However, it's important to always be wary of potential fraudsters. We'll only contact you about these changes by email or in-app notifications. 
Links in emails should always be treated with caution and can be high-risk. Always double-check emails are received from our verified Revolut address (no-reply@revolut.com) before considering the email legitimate.
We won’t phone or text you about your account changes, so don’t trust anyone who calls you during this transition period. 

## We will only call you in-app
If you’ve been in contact with our support team and have arranged a phone call from Revolut Support, then that call will happen directly within the Revolut app — not through WhatsApp, a regular call, or any other call platform.

title: Will my account details change after my account is moved?
content_text:
## Your details will remain the same
Your local and international personal account details won’t change after your account has been moved. This means your account number, sort code, IBAN, and BIC will remain the same.
For some transfers, you may need address details for Revolut. Our address will also stay the same. To find our address, go to the home screen and look for ‘Details’ underneath your balance. Tap ‘Details’ and check the ‘Bank and Address’ details section.
You’ll also still have full access to all of your account history. This includes any transactions and statements before your account was moved to Revolut Bank.

title: Will my fees and charges change now that Revolut is a bank?
content_text:
## Our fees and charges will remain the same
There will be no changes to fees and charges as a result of your account being moved to Revolut Bank. For more information, visit our Personal Fees pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/fees/.

title: Will Revolut Bank have physical branches?
content_text: Revolut Bank will continue to be a digital service provider, offering our services entirely online through the Revolut app. This allows us to provide you with secure, convenient, and accessible services — anytime, anywhere.

title: Will the account transition have any tax implications?
content_text:
## Tax implications
There won't be any tax implications related to your account moving to Revolut Bank UK Ltd.
If we are yet to collect confirmation of your tax residency and, where required, your taxpayer identification number, you may receive a prompt to provide this as part of the account migration process.
Revolut doesn't advise on tax matters. If you have any questions or concerns, you should seek independent advice from a professionally qualified tax advisor and/or HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC).

title: Will this affect my other Revolut products?
content_text:
## There will be no changes to how our products work
Other products, like Savings, Trading and Crypto accounts, won’t be affected by your account being moved to Revolut Bank. Your Revolut app will work as usual and you’ll be able to use our services and features without disruption. 
Some products which were previously provided by Revolut Ltd will now be provided by Revolut Bank UK Ltd instead, but there'll be no changes to how our products work

## Products provided by Revolut Bank after the move
- Personal accounts: Personal Current Account Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/terms/
- Paid plans: Paid Plan Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/paid-plans/
- Independent teen accounts: Independent Teen Account Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/terms/
- Kids & Teens accounts: Revolut Kids & Teens Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/revolut-under18/
- Joint accounts: Joint Account Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/joint-accounts/
- Pro accounts: Pro Account Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/pro/

title: Why didn't I get a bank account when I signed up?
content_text:
The bank has begun a phased roll-out. This timeline ensures the best possible customer experience, allowing for a seamless and secure migration of our existing UK customers.
This is a transitional period to ensure everything is working as expected. We'll let you know, via email and in-app, when we're ready to move you to a bank account with FSCS protection on eligible deposits.
Eligible customers will receive 2-month, 1-month, and 1-week notices before their account is transitioned. We appreciate your patience.

title: Will my hardware be affected by the account transition?
content_text: Any hardware you have, such as readers, terminals, and PoS devices, is provided by Revolut Ltd and won't be affected by this transition. There will be no changes to the functionality of your devices or the terms you have for them.

title: Will the transition affect my Pro products and services?
content_text:
Your Revolut app will work as usual and you’ll be able to use our services and features without disruption, including your Pro account. 
The products listed below were previously provided by Revolut Ltd, and are now provided by Revolut Bank UK Ltd:
- Your Revolut Pro current account (bank account), previously your Revolut Pro e-money account
- Acquiring services provided through your app, specifically open banking, payment processing, and Revolut Pay

title: Why is my balance now negative?
content_text:
## It could be a delayed payment
In areas with poor connectivity, the merchant may be unable to process the transaction or receive the payment immediately.
To complete the transaction, they might attempt to charge you again on a different day. This can result in a duplicate charge, where you see both a pending payment and a completed payment for the same merchant.
Both transactions might be reflected in your account balance, causing it to be negative. However, the pending transaction should automatically be cancelled or reversed. If not, you can dispute the transaction following the steps described in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/refunds/dispute-a-card-transaction.

## Other common reasons
- Final costs higher than expected: some merchants can block a certain amount on your account and adjust the final cost of the payment later. They may initially charge more than you expect, bringing your account into a negative balance, but later readjust the charge
- Paid plan fees: if you don't have enough funds when Revolut paid plan fees are charged, it can push your account into a negative balance

## Which transaction caused a negative balance?
To check your recent transactions and see which one may have brought your account into a negative balance, follow these steps in-app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Review all your recent transactions in each currency account
- Based on the negative balance and recent transactions, try to calculate or identify which transaction was the one to make your balance turn negative
If you find a transaction you don’t recognise that caused a negative balance in your account, check out this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/paying-by-card-issues/i-don-t-recognise-a-card-payment/i-was-not-supposed-to-be-charged-by-a-specific-merchant.

## What happens if my balance goes negative?
We'll try to use money from your other currency accounts to bring your account back to a positive balance. If there isn’t enough money in your other currency accounts, we'll ask you to add money to your account the next day. After 7 days, we'll start our collection process as outlined in our T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/terms/?geo-redirect=true.
We may report it to debt registries, fraud prevention agencies, or debt collection agencies, which could impact your financial status and future credit or financial eligibility.
If you have a negative balance in your account, you won't be able to make card payments but your account will stay active. In case you're having financial difficulties, contact us. We’ll always do our best to help you.

title: Add money in one tap
content_text:
With one-tap payments, you can transfer money to your Revolut account even faster by pre-authorising easy bank transfers.
You can learn about easy bank transfers in this FAQ/help/adding-money/add-money-with-a-bank-transfer/add-money-with-an-easy-bank-transfer.

## How to set up one-tap payments
In your Revolut app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup'
- Enter the amount
- Under the amount, choose your payment methodhttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method
- Set the currency to GBP
- Tap the info icon next to a previously used account
- Enable and manage one-tap payments
If you don't have a saved account, you'll be asked about one-tap payments the first time you add money via easy bank transfer from a supported bank. After you enable one-tap payments, you won't need to complete any additional steps outside of Revolut as long as the deposit is within your set limits.

## Manage one-tap payments
In your Revolut app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Accounts' and select your local currency
- Tap 'Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup'
- From the dropdown menu under the deposit amount, choose the payment methodhttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method 
- Tap the info icon next to a previously used account
- Edit the limits or revoke access entirely

## Limits and fees
The limit on one-tap payments is £5,000 per month. If you make a transfer that exceeds this monthly limit, you'll need to authorise the transfer with your external bank.
For information on fees, go to this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/receiving-a-bank-transfer.

## Issues with an easy bank transfer
Tap your transfer in your transaction history to get specialised help.
Visit this FAQ for more help with receiving a bank transfer.

title: Add money with a bank transfer
content_text:
To add money to your Revolut account from your external account with a bank transfer, you can use one of the below methods.

## Regular bank transfer
With a regular bank transfer, you'll need to share your Revolut account details with your external bank and initiate the transfer with them. When you're expecting a bank transfer to your Revolut account, we have no visibility on the transfer's progress until it successfully reaches your account.
The timing is determined by the sending bank, and we can't view or influence a transfer before it arrives. However, we can provide an expected arrival date through our incoming transfer tracker. Learn more about transfer timeframes in this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/timeframes-for-transfers.
Once the transfer appears in your account, we'll notify you in-app right away. You will be able to use the funds immediately. To learn more about receiving regular bank transfers, visit this article/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/receiving-a-bank-transfer. To learn how to locate your pre-written salary email in-app, go to this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/can-i-get-my-salary-paid-into-my-revolut-account/can-i-get-my-salary-paid-into-my-revolut-account.

## Easy bank transfer
To add money to your Revolut account from a local bank, you can initiate an easy bank transfer in-app. You won't need to enter any bank details. You'll just need to approve the transfer in your external bank's app or website.
For instructions on how to add money with an easy bank transfer, visit this article/help/adding-money/add-money-with-a-bank-transfer/add-money-with-an-easy-bank-transfer.

## Difficulty with a bank transfer
For guidance when a bank transfer doesn't arrive, visit this article/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/having-an-issue-with-receiving-money/what-should-i-do-if-a-transfer-doesn-t-arrive-to-my-revolut-account in our Help Centre.

title: Add money with an easy bank transfer
content_text:
## To add money with an easy bank transfer
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup'
- Enter the amount
- Under the amount, choose your payment methodhttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method 
- Set the currency to GBP
- Select 'Easy bank transfer' or choose a bank account
- Follow the on-screen prompts and approve the transfer in your external bank app or website
Only some local banks are supported. Most of the time you won't need to input bank details, instead approve it in your bank app or website. After the first easy bank transfer, your external bank account will be added to the 'Methods' section of the 'Adding moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup' screen.
For limits, fees, and timeframes, read this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/receiving-a-bank-transfer. You can also link an external bank account with Revolut for quick deposits in the future by going to the linked accounts menu. Visit this article/help/app-features/linking-external-bank-accounts/how-do-i-link-an-external-account-to-my-revolut-app to learn more about linking external bank accounts with Revolut.

## Timeframes
An easy bank transfer is still a normal bank transfer. Once the money leaves your external account, it usually takes a few minutes for it to show in Revolut, but may take up to 1 business day. You can check the estimated arrival date of your deposit using our in-app tool.

## Cancellations
It's not possible to cancel an easy bank transfer top-up from your Revolut app after it was authorised. Once the money arrives in your Revolut account, you can send it back to your bank account following the steps described in this article/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/how-to-send-money-to-another-bank/how-do-i-send-money-to-a-bank-account.

## Difficulty with a bank transfer
If you're facing difficulties adding money with an easy bank transfer, see potential reasons in this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/with-linked-accounts/why-am-i-unable-to-add-money-with-a-linked-account.

title: Get paid early with Early Salary
content_text:
## Early Salary
With our in-app Early Salary feature, you can receive your salary one business day earlier than usual. All UK-based customers receiving their salaries into their Revolut GBP currency accounts are automatically enrolled in this feature. To start receiving your salary into Revolut and enrol manually: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Add money' and select GBP as the currency
- Tap 'Changehttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method' and select 'Add salary'
- Send the auto-generated email to your employer or share your GBP account number and sort code directly
If you want to opt out of the feature, reach out to our support team through the in-app chat.

## Timeframes and fees
Early payments usually arrive in the late afternoon of the working day before payday. There are no fees for Early Salary — you keep the whole amount, with no deductions.

## Limits
There are no limits on the salary amount you can receive, or the number of transfers you can receive.

## A payment didn't arrive early
Early Salary depends on your payroll provider’s submission time. As soon as we receive the payment information, we'll always try to credit your payment one working day after it was sent by the payroll provider.

title: Card deposit limits and fees
content_text:
## Limits
Our automated security system sets top-up limits for all customers, which reset on a rolling basis (24 hours or 7 days, depending on the limit). They’re based on the total amount you’ve added by card in the past 24 hours or 7 days. These limits adjust as you build a financial history with us. 

## What are my limits?
There is an annual top-up limit for all methods. This annual limit resets on the day your Revolut account was registered. If you reach this limit, you may need to submit additional documentation. All top-ups have a minimum amount, displayed in-app when adding funds. Other limits are shown below: 
- Up to 10 card top-ups are allowed within a 24-hour period. 
- Digital wallet top-ups (such as Apple Pay or Google Pay) are limited to £12,000 (or currency equivalent) within a 7-day period. 
- New customers (within 30 days of joining) are limited to £3,000 (or currency equivalent) within a 24-hour period.

## Revolut fees
Any fees we apply will always be visible to you before you add money to your account. These same fees apply when adding money with Apple Pay or Google Pay. Fees apply for the following card types:
- Business and commercial cards
- Cards issued outside the European Economic Area (EEA) or UK (e.g. Switzerland, US) 
Your account's first card top-up will be free of charge, no matter the card type. Visit this FAQ/help/adding-money/with-card/supported-card-deposits for a list of cards we support for top-ups.

## Non-Revolut fees
Revolut has no control or visibility over the fees your external bank may charge when you use a card issued by them to top up. Some banks may charge you if your card deposit is recognised as a cash withdrawal or advance, international transfer, or a transfer in another currency.

title: How to make cash deposits
content_text:
## I don't see the option to make a cash deposit
Cash deposits have been discontinued from 13 February 2026 onwards. Check your emails from us for more information.
You can still add money to your account via bank transfers, debit cards, Apple Pay, or Google Pay.
If you'd like a financial provider that offers cash top-ups, you can find impartial guidance on these websites: https://www.moneyfactscompare.co.uk https://www.moneyhelper.org.uk/en

title: Disruptions to adding money with Visa cards
content_text:
## What changes are being made by Visa?
From 1 February 2025, Visa will require that Revolut sends additional information to your card issuer when adding money to your account.
This additional information will be processed by your card issuer to complete verification checks, which assist in protecting your money.
Unfortunately, there are a small number of card issuers that aren't currently able to process this additional information. As a result, we expect that these card issuers may decline attempts to add money with a card issued by them.

## Can I still use a Visa card to add money to my account?
You can still add money using a Visa card. The vast majority of banks already support these new requirements and Revolut already sends this additional information. Attempts to add money with cards from banks that already support this process won't be impacted. 
If you do try to add money using a card that doesn't support the new requirements, you may have difficulty:
- adding money to your account through a card saved in-app
- adding money to your account via Apple Pay or Google Pay
- adding money using a payment link

## Which card issuers could this impact?
We expect to see the following card issuers decline attempts to add money:
- BANCO DE LA NACION, HSBC BANK (VIETNAM) LTD., DEUTSCHE BANK  SOCIEDAD ANÓNIMA ESPAÑOLA, HSBC BANK MIDDLE EAST, PAYPAY CARD CORPORATION, AGOS DUCATO S.P.A., ECOBANK RWANDA S.A., OPEN BANK S.A

## More information
You can continue to add money with Mastercard, and other methods of adding money (such as bank transfers) as usual. Learn more about other deposit methods/help/adding-money.
Revolut is actively monitoring the situation, and working closely with our partners at Visa to resume card deposits as usual, and as soon as possible, across impacted card issuers.
If your attempt to add money with a Visa card is declined, we recommend that you try again at a later date.

title: Receiving money to my Revolut card
content_text:
## Card transfers
Revolut cards support the functionality to receive inbound transfers directly to your card via your card number. Certain merchants commonly use this method to provide immediate disbursements, for example, online gambling, gaming, or betting websites.
Money remittance services, like PayPal and Paysend, also opt for card transfers for quick payments via the recipient's card number.

## Timeframes and tips
Card transfer transactions, if accepted by Revolut, should be credited to your account within 30 minutes. If you're expecting a card transfer and you don't see it in your Revolut account yet, try the following:
- Make sure you entered the correct Revolut card number when choosing the destination
- Confirm that the transaction has been approved and that the funds have left your external account
- Check your different currency accounts, as the transfer could have been performed in a different currency
If the funds have left your external account and haven't been credited to your Revolut account, we recommend getting in touch with the merchant.

title: How to add money with a card
content_text:
## Add money with a card
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup
- Enter the amount
- Under the amount, choose your payment methodhttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method
- Set the currency to your card's local currency to avoid exchange fees 
- Select a saved card. If you want to add another card, tap 'Debit card'
- Follow the prompts and tap 'Continue'
If you need to add money in a different currency, you can exchange it in-app after you've topped up. 
We don't support deposits from all cards, countries, and currencies. Learn more in this supported card deposits help article/help/adding-money/with-card/supported-card-deposits, or our card deposit limits and fees FAQ/help/adding-money/card-deposit-limits-and-fees.

## Common reasons for failed card deposits
- Issuer decline: your bank has declined the transaction based on one of their internal rules, such as exceeding certain limits
- 3-D Secure (3DS) error: you didn't authenticate the deposit with your bank, usually via a notification in their app or with a one-time password via text message. You can try again with an Apple Pay or Google Pay deposit. Read this FAQ/help/adding-money/with-card/add-money-with-apple-pay-or-google-pay for more information
For more help, go to your transactions list and select the deposit you're having difficulties with. To learn more about common reasons for failed card top-ups, read this article/help/adding-money/with-card/more-help-with-a-card-deposit.

title: Add money with Apple Pay or Google Pay
content_text:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup'
- Enter the amount
- If Apple Pay or Google Pay isn't already selected, tap the payment methodhttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method drop down
- Set the currency to your card's local currency to avoid exchange fees from your external bank
- Choose 'Apple Payhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/mobile-topup' or 'Google Payhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/mobile-topup'
- Tap 'Pay' 
If you need the money you've added in a different currency, you can exchange it in-app after you have topped up.
We don't support deposits from all cards, countries, and currencies. Learn more about the cards we support in this FAQ/help/adding-money/with-card/supported-card-deposits. For card deposit limits and fees, read this article/help/adding-money/card-deposit-limits-and-fees we've prepared.

## Difficulty adding money with Apple Pay or Google Pay
If you're having difficulty when trying to add money with Apple Pay or Google Pay, make sure you're not trying to use a Revolut card to add money (it might be set as the default card in your digital wallet). If the problem persists, we recommend contacting your external bank.
If your card isn't available for the Apple Pay or Google Pay top-up, add it to Revolut as a top-up card directly. If this works, it means the card is supported, and you can contact Apple or Google support for help with setting it up in your mobile wallet (Apple Pay or Google Pay). 
To find out more about why a deposit didn't work, tap the failed deposit in your transactions. For more information about direct card deposits, visit this FAQ/help/adding-money/with-card/add-money-with-a-cardd.

## Reverting an Apple Pay or Google Pay deposit
There's no option to revert a completed top-up. The fastest way to get the top-up back to the account it came from is to transfer the money you topped up with back to your external bank directly from your Revolut account. You can read about how to do that in this FAQ/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/how-to-send-money-to-another-bank/how-do-i-send-money-to-a-bank-account.
If your top-up is pending, you won't be able to send it back until it's completed.

title: Auto top-ups
content_text:
You can set up auto top-ups in any available currency with a saved card, once it's been added to your account for more than 30 days. You can't use credit cards for auto top-ups. To set up an auto top-up in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' then tap 'Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup'
- Tap on the pre selected methodhttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method
- Tap the info icon next to a saved card
- Tap 'Add money automatically' (this option won't appear if there's no eligible card available)
- Choose the minimum balance to trigger the deposit, and how much should be added
- Tap 'Save'
If you're already using a card eligible for auto top-ups, just tap the circular button next to 'Add money securely' and skip steps 2, 3, and 4 above.

## How to manage auto top-ups
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup'
- Under the top-up amount, choose your payment methodhttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method
- Tap the info icon next to a saved card
- Tap 'Adjust auto top-up' and 'Edit'
- Change the settings or tap 'Delete' to deactivate auto top-ups

## Help with auto top-ups
- Deposit is pending, failed, or reverted: simply tap the deposit in your in-app transactions list for more details
- Auto top-up didn't trigger: check if it's still active and set up as expected. Keep in mind, if your base currency balance falls below the auto top-up threshold after an internal transfer — such as exchanging currencies or adding money to a Pocket — this won't trigger your auto top-up
- Expired card: if your card has expired, you'll need to delete the card and re-add the card, then wait 30 days before you're able to set up an auto top-up with the new card details

title: Manage my deposit cards
content_text:
## How to add or update a deposit card
If you need to add a new card, you can do this when you're ready to make a deposit. Follow the steps in this FAQ.
If your card information is incorrect or out of date, you'll need to delete the card and follow the steps for adding money with a card again. To delete a card in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'Add money'
- Tap the pre-selected top-up methodhttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method
- Tap the info icon next to a saved card
- Tap 'Delete'

## Verifying a deposit card 
We may ask you to verify your deposit card to confirm it's yours. Check your bank statement for a transaction from Revolut that appears as 'Revolut*1234*'. It can take up to one day to appear. Use the 4-digit code to verify your card in the 'Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup' section of the Revolut app.

title: My card deposit didn't work
content_text:
## Pending card deposit
It may take some extra time for your external bank to process the deposit. Once the deposit is pending in Revolut, the money can take up to 30 minutes to be credited to your account. You can check the deposit status in your in-app transactions list.

## Failed or reverted card deposit
Check the reason and refund timeframes by tapping the deposit in your in-app transactions list. The money can take up to 5 business days to be returned to your external bank account. Common reasons for failed card deposits are:
- Failed 3D secure: you didn't authenticate the deposit. Try again or with Apple/Google Pay
- Declined by your external bank
- Insufficient balance
- Restricted account: this can happen for various reasons, such as an expired document
- Technical issues: you can try this card again later or try another method
Contact your external bank for more help. From the 'Home' screen, tap 'See allhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list' to open your transactions list → select the failed deposit → tap 'Download' next to 'Statement' → share this PDF with your bank to investigate.

## Apple Pay or Google Pay issues
If you experience issues adding money with Apple Pay or Google Pay, read this FAQ/help/adding-money/with-card/add-money-with-apple-pay-or-google-pay for more help. If the issue persists, we recommend contacting your external bank.

## Duplicate, missing, or mismatched deposits
Unfortunately, we can't cancel a card deposit. Once you receive the funds, you can transfer them back to your bank account or a card.
You may see a duplicate deposit on your external bank statement. If you don't see both deposits in Revolut, the duplicate charge should be removed from your external bank statement within 5 business days. For more information, we recommend contacting your external bank.
It can sometimes happen that the transaction date shown on your external bank statement is different from what you see in your Revolut app. If your transaction appears to be missing when comparing statements, check if the deposit was recorded on different days.

title: Restricted card deposits
content_text:
## Why are there new restrictions?
Changes to card scheme rules on 30th September 2024 mean that the movement of money from one account to another through a card (adding money to / topping up your Revolut account by card) is now going to be classified differently.
Ultimately, this is a restriction that's mandated by card schemes and Revolut must adhere to this new rule.

## What will change?
Customers won't be able to:
- Top-up using cards issued in India
- Top-up into US accounts using cards issued outside the US
Additionally, a small number of card issuers on the Visa network don't currently support this new classification.

## What top-up methods does this impact?
The following top-up methods won't work with any card the new rules don't support:
- Adding money to your account through saving the card in the Revolut app
- Adding money to your account via Apple Pay or Google Pay
- Adding money to your account via a payment link

## Does this impact other top-up methods?
This does not impact other methods of adding money to your Revolut account.
You can try a card issued on the Mastercard network or try an alternative method to add money to your account. Fees may apply.
For more information on available deposit methods, see this FAQ.

title: Ukraine card top-up limits
content_text:
## Why are there new restrictions?
Changes to Visa rules on 30th September 2024 mean that the movement of money from one account to another through a card (adding money / topping up your Revolut account by card) is now going to be classified differently. 

## What does this mean?
Previously, card top-up attempts from Ukraine were exempt from the monthly limit in Ukraine of ₴100,000.
The change of this classification means that card top-ups to Revolut accounts now count towards this monthly limit.
This monthly limit is enforced by your card issuer and all money transfers from your bank account count towards the limit. Please check with your card issuer to see what transactions your limit applies to.

## What is Revolut doing to fix this?
Ultimately, this is a restriction that is mandated by Visa and Revolut must adhere to this new rule.
This restriction is currently isolated to Visa, so top-ups using a Mastercard card should still work as before.

## Will there be other impacts?
There are a small number of card issuers that aren't prepared to accept money movement transactions. We are working with ‌Visa to ensure that high standards are maintained across all card issuers.
This does not impact other methods of topping up your Revolut account. You can try topping up using a card issued on the Mastercard network or using an alternative deposit method. To learn more about other deposit methods, visit this FAQ. Fees may apply.

title: Why was my top-up reverted?
content_text:
## Technical issues
Temporary issues with Revolut or the card issuer's system may cause a card deposit to appear as pending for a prolonged time, and then get reverted. If that happens, tap the deposit in your transactions list for more information and help.

## Your bank disputed the charge
When you add money to your Revolut account using an external card, sometimes the bank may revert the transaction. This is known as a top-up chargeback. If your bank issues a top-up chargeback, the funds will be temporarily blocked on your Revolut account while the transaction is disputed.
- If the bank you transferred money from wins the dispute, the deposit amount will be refunded to the account you topped up from
- If they lose the dispute, the deposit in your Revolut account will be unblocked
For more detailed information and updates on your dispute, contact your bank.

title: Cards and currencies supported for adding money
content_text:
## Supported card brands
We currently support deposits from the following card brands:
- Mastercard
- Visa

## Unsupported cards
We currently don't support deposits from the following cards:
- Credit cards
- American Express, Cirrus, Discovery, Diners Club, Union Pay, JCB
- Apple cards and gift cards
- Cards issued in unsupported countries like Belarus and Russia
- Cards issued in India
If you're unable to add a specific card in the Revolut app, it usually means it's not supported.

## Supported currencies
You can add money with a card in any of the following currencies:
- AUD
- CAD
- CHF
- CZK
- DKK
- EUR
- GBP
- HKD
- HUF
- ILS
- JPY
- MXN
- NOK
- NZD
- PLN
- RON
- SEK
- SGD
- THB
- TRY
- USD
- ZAR
The same card brands and currencies are supported for requesting money with Payment Links. You can learn more about Payment Links in this FAQ/help/adding-money/with-money-from-friends-or-relatives/requesting-money.

## Disruptions to adding money with Visa cards
Additionally, a small number of card issuers on the Visa network don't currently support adding money to your Revolut account. Visit this FAQ/help/adding-money/questiondisruptionstoaddingmoneywithvisacards for more details.

title: Why your card top-ups may be blocked
content_text:
Card deposits may be blocked because your issuing bank has declined a previous payment using this card, or because you've made multiple unsuccessful attempts to make the payment. These payment blocks can be applied for up to 30 days, due to Visa and Mastercard payment processing rules.

## Reasons for the temporary block
Some common reasons which lead to these payment blocks are:
- Insufficient funds
- Security concerns, like if your card has been reported lost
- Transactions aren't supported by the issuing bank
If you'd like to understand more about these decline reasons, contact your bank or card issuer.

## Add money through other methods
If this happens, you can still use your card with Apple Pay or Google Pay, or try adding money with a different card. For other ways to add money to your account, take a look at the links below:
- Bank transfer/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/receiving-a-bank-transfer
- Internal transfers/help/transfers/internal-transfers/send-or-request-money-from-other-revolut-customers from other Revolut customers
- Easy bank transfers/help/adding-money/add-money-with-a-bank-transfer/add-money-with-an-easy-bank-transfer

title: Requesting money with a payment link
content_text:
Payment links are a secure way to send or receive money without sharing personal information or account details.
They can also be used to send money to a card, bank account, or Revolut account, and to request money from a card, external bank account, or a Revolut account. To learn about sending money with a payment link, visit this FAQ/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/how-to-send-money-to-another-bank/what-recipient-account-details-do-i-need/what-if-i-don-t-know-the-recipient-s-account-details.

## How to create a payment link request
In the Revolut app: 
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'New' (the + sign) in the top-right corner
- Tap 'Create a payment link'
- Tap 'Request money'
- Set the amount to request, or share a generic Revolut.me link and allow the sender to input the amount
To change the amount on a payment link, you’ll need to cancel it and create a new one. You can cancel a payment link at any time before it expires, as long as it’s not already been used. To do so, tap the payment from the transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list, and hit 'Cancel'.

## Limits and timeframes
You can receive a maximum of £250 per week (or the equivalent in other currencies) via card payments. To request money from non-Revolut customers, Revtag discoverability should be enabled in-app. To enable it in the Revolut app:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Accounts' and 'Privacyhttps://app.revolut.com/settings/security/privacyrevolut://app/privacy'
- Tap 'Make me discoverable' and choose 'Revtag'
When you request money, the sender will have 10 days to complete your request and then the link will expire. If the sender was asked to create a Revolut account, it's probably because you’ve reached the weekly limit for payment requests from non-Revolut customers.

## Supported cards and currencies
If a sender chooses to use a card or Apple Pay to send you money, the same rules applicable to card top-ups apply. To see what cards and currencies are accepted, visit this FAQ/help/adding-money/with-card/supported-card-deposits. Google Pay isn't accepted for payment links at this time.

title: How to split a bill with another Revolut customer
content_text:
- In your transactions list, tap the payment you want to split
- Tap 'Split bill'
- Select the Revolut friend or contact you want to split it with and tap 'Continue'
You can adjust the individual amounts owed or just tap Split bill to split the bill evenly. Your contacts will receive a notification showing the request for the amount they owe, which they can accept or cancel. Once they accept the request, the money will arrive in your account instantly.
We don't apply any charges for splitting bills, and they can be settled in any of our supported currencies.

title: Am I covered by Ultra trip cancellation protection if I need to cancel a flight while abroad?
content_text:
Ultra trip and event cancellation protection covers you for expenses if you need to cancel a trip or event before it starts. Therefore, it doesn't cover flight cancellations mid-trip.
If you're already on a trip and need to cancel a portion of it, you may be eligible to submit a claim for trip interruption under your travel insurance policy. Learn more in this FAQ/help/app-features/travel-insurance/cover-for-cancelled-and-interrupted-trips in our Help Centre.

title: Am I covered for trips or events that I didn't purchase with my Revolut account with Ultra trip and event cancellation protection?
content_text:
Your trip or event must be purchased with your Revolut account on an active Ultra plan to be covered. 
Any expenses paid outside your Revolut account, or before you upgraded to the Ultra plan, won't be covered unless you purchased the trip or event no more than one day before upgrading to Ultra.

title: Ultra trip & event cancellation with companion coverage
content_text:
Ultra trip and event cancellation protection covers expenses paid for yourself and travel companion if you cancel, in accordance with the policy T&Cs. This applies only when you, the policyholder, buy a ticket in your name.
The coverage includes companion expenses, but only if neither of you attend the event or trip (neither of you use the tickets booked in your name).

title: How do I get an update on my existing claim?
content_text:
Qover are responsible for the management of all cancel for any reason protection claims. Revolut doesn't have access to your claim and can't confirm your eligibility for cover. You must contact Qover directly to check on an existing claim. 
Qover's contact information can be found in-app: 
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select 'Cancel for any reason' and tap 'Get help'

title: Cancel for any reason complaints
content_text:
If you want to make a complaint in connection with your insurance policy, you'll need to contact the relevant insurance provider directly. If you send any complaint or claim to us, we'll pass it on to the relevant insurer without dealing with it.
To raise a complaint with Qover, email them at mediation@qover.com.
You can also call the Qover team to raise a complaint. The phone number can be found in the complaints section of the policy wording. 

## How to access policy wording
To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu in-app, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Cancel for any reason' and tap 'Documents'

title: How do I make a cancel for any reason protection claim?
content_text:
Revolut Ultra plan customers must cancel and submit a claim no less than one day before the trip or event is due to start (exceptions apply for cancellations made due to a covered reason). To ensure a smooth claims experience, follow the steps detailed below.

## Notify your supplier
Inform your trip or event suppliers immediately and no later than 2 days after you become aware that you need to cancel. You must cancel your trip or event no less than 1 day before the start date. You'll need to provide proof of your attempt to cancel with the supplier when submitting a claim. 

## Gather documentation
Collect the documents needed to submit your claim. See what you need to provide in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/how-do-i-make-a-trip-or-event-cancellation-protection-claim/?geo-redirect=true.

## Submit your claim
Submit your claim as soon as possible and no later than 1 day before the start date of your trip or event. To do this in-app:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Cancel for any reason' and tap 'Claim'
If you have any difficulty submitting a claim, contact us and we can help. 

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of coverage. Revolut's cancel for any reason insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Cancel for any reason' and tap 'Documents'

title: My supplier won’t let me cancel my trip/event. Am I covered with Ultra trip and event cancellation protection?
content_text:
If your supplier doesn't allow cancellations, and you need to submit an Ultra trip and event cancellation protection claim, here's what you should do:
- Contact the supplier: Try to negotiate with the supplier directly to see if they'll offer a refund, credit, or reschedule option
- Gather documents: Collect any evidence that shows the supplier refused your cancellation request, as well as any other necessary documents, like proof of booking and reason for cancellation
- Submit a claim: File a claim in-app, providing all the required documents, including the documents mentioned above

## Important things to remember:
- Timing: You must cancel your trip or event and file your claim as soon as possible, but no later than 1 day before the trip or event starts. Exceptions apply for serious illness, injury, or medical conditions
- Minimum value: To be eligible for cover, your trip or event must be worth at least £51 for listed reasons (after the deductible)
- Waiting periods: Your trip or event must have been booked no less than 3 days before the start date. Trips or events booked less than 3 days in advance aren't covered
- Ultra plan: Coverage is available only if you bought your trip or event while holding an Ultra plan and have kept the Ultra plan until the cancellation date. If you downgrade or bought the trip or event on a different plan, you won't be covered
Learn more about how to claim in this FAQ/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/how-do-i-make-a-trip-or-event-cancellation-protection-claim in our Help Centre.

title: Does moving home affect Ultra trip and event coverage?
content_text:
Your insurance is based on the address and country of residence linked to your Revolut account. If you move home (or to another country) and don't update your address in your account, you may not be eligible for cover and future insurance claims could be declined.
Trips (for leisure purposes only) must start and end at your primary residence, which is the address linked to your Revolut account. You can read more about how trips are defined in this article/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/what-is-a-trip. 
Make sure to update your address in your Revolut account whenever you move. To find instructions on how to do this, read this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/how-do-i-change-my-country-of-residence.

title: Will I get a full refund for my trip or event cancellation?
content_text:
Ultra cancel for any reason protection offers cover for 100% of non-refundable costs, if you have to cancel a trip purchased with your Revolut account for a reason that is specifically covered in the policy.

## Cancellation reasons eligible for 100% compensation
- Death, serious injury, or sickness of plan holder, trip companion, or a family member 
- Termination of your employment or a trip companion’s employment, subject to certain conditions
- A new permanent paid job is accepted and requires you or your trip companion to attend, preventing you from travelling
