- Serious damage to your primary residence, within 10 days prior to your trip or event
- Theft of you or your travelling companion's documentation, preventing you from travelling 
- You or your travelling companion were in a traffic accident on the departure date

## Key exclusions
To qualify for 100% reimbursement, you must not be claiming for any of the following:
- Any serious illness or injury that you knew about when you booked the trip and that could have reasonably led to cancellation
- Cancellations for trips or events after you have already left your country of residence (i.e. while you're already travelling)
- Injuries or illnesses that did not require treatment from a certified doctor
- Trips or events booked for business purposes
- Any loss or problem that you knew about or expected when booking the trip, or before you purchased your Revolut Ultra plan

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of coverage. Revolut's cancel for any reason insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select 'Cancel for any reason' and tap 'Documents'

title: What are the limits of cover and deductibles?
content_text:
Depending on whether you’re claiming for a cancellation due to an insured reason or for any reason, different limits and deductibles apply. 

## Cancellation for an insured reason
- Coverage: 100% of non-refundable costs for trips cancelled for an insured reason. Learn more about what’s insured in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/what-are-the-covered-reasons-to-get-100-reimbursement-for-trip-event-cancellation/?geo-redirect=true
- Deductible: £50
- Trip value: minimum value of £51 after the deductible
- Coverage limits: maximum of £5,000 per claim and annually

## Cancellation for any reason
- Coverage: 70% of non-refundable costs for trips or events cancelled for any reason
- Deductible: 30%
- Trip/event value: minimum £25 after the deductible has been applied
- Coverage limits: maximum of £2,500 per claim and up to £5,000 annually

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of coverage. Revolut's cancel for any reason insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'Insurance'
- Then, select 'Cancel for any reason' and tap 'Documents'

title: Getting started with cancel for any reason protection
content_text:
Cancel for any reason protection is exclusively for Revolut Ultra plan customers.
This insurance covers non-refundable cancellation costs for trips and events purchased with your Ultra plan. You must book through official merchants and cancel at least one day before the start date.
The amount you're covered for will vary depending on whether your reason for cancellation is specifically covered by the insurance or if you're cancelling for any reason. 

## Cancellation for an insured reason
- 100% reimbursement of non-refundable trip costs if you cancel for a reason covered by the policy. Learn more about the reasons covered in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/what-are-the-covered-reasons-to-get-100-reimbursement-for-trip-event-cancellation/?geo-redirect=true
- All claims are subject to a £50 excess
- The trip must have a value of at least £51 (after the excess is applied) to be eligible for cover
- Maximum claim limit of £5,000 per claim, with a total annual limit of £5,000
This cover applies for trips only. Cancellation of an event for an insured reason is covered by the ticket cancellation protection under Revolut’s Everyday Protection insurance. Learn more about what’s covered in these FAQshttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/everyday-protection-insurance/?geo-redirect=true.

## Cancellation for any reason
- 70% of non-refundable costs for trips or events cancelled, regardless of the reason you cancel 
- A 30% excess applies to the total non-refundable costs you claim for
- The trip or event must be worth at least £25 (after the deductible is applied) to be eligible for cover
- Maximum claim limit of £2,500 per claim, with a total annual limit of £5,000

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of coverage. Revolut's cancel for any reason insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select 'Cancel for any reason' and tap 'Documents'

title: Documents needed for an Ultra trip and event cancellation protection claim
content_text:
When submitting an Ultra trip and event cancellation protection claim, our insurance provider will request some documents to confirm your eligibility for cover. 
You must provide the following when submitting a claim for Ultra trip and event cancellation protection:
- Proof that you paid for the trip or event with your Revolut account
- Proof of cancellation at least one day before the trip or event and any charges
- Original receipts and invoices
If you cancel your trip for a specific reason, you need to provide additional supporting documents. Some examples include:
- Serious injury or sickness: a medical certificate with the date and details of the accident or illness
- Contagious disease: a medical certificate showing quarantine is required, or a copy of the test results
- Death: a copy of the death certificate
- Quarantine: a copy of the official document that places you or your travel companion in quarantine due to an epidemic or pandemic
- Childbirth: a copy of the birth certificate
- Redundancy: a copy of your company's redundancy notice, proof of your employment contract, and the exact start date of your employment
- Traffic accident: a copy of the breakdown, towing invoice, or report, and a medical report if any medical attention was needed
- Theft of identity papers or tickets: a copy of the police report and any other documents showing your efforts to replace the stolen items

## Important things to remember
- File types: Accepted formats are GIF, JPEG, PDF, and PNG. If one format doesn't work, try another from the list
- Max documents accepted: A maximum of 3 documents can be processed when submitting your claim, so merge files if you have more than this to avoid any problems

title: What is a trip?
content_text:
In the context of Ultra cancel for any reason protection, a 'trip' refers to your travel (for leisure purposes only) scheduled to begin from your primary residence on your departure date and end on your return date to or within a location:
- at least 100km away from your primary residence, or
- abroad, or
- outside your city or town of residence, if your travel includes an overnight stay
Cancel for any reason is for cancellations made before you leave your home. If you cancel after departing your primary residence, this becomes a trip interruption. This may be covered under the Ultra travel insurance policy. Consult this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/travel-insurance/cover-for-cancelled-and-interrupted-trips/?geo-redirect=true for details on interruption coverage.

## Trip expenses covered under cancel for any reason protection
The cover reimburses non-refundable expenses like trip payments, deposits, cancellation fees, and change fees. Reimbursement is up to the maximum benefit, minus any refund you already received.
- Transportation tickets: plane, train, bus, boat
- Accommodation: hotels, all-in-one holidays, retreats, Airbnb, and short-term holiday rentals, eligible Revolut Stays bookings
- Rentals: transportation and sports equipment
Need to cancel a future trip? Learn more in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/how-do-i-make-a-trip-or-event-cancellation-protection-claim/?geo-redirect=true.

## Key exclusions:
- Cancellations less than 1 day before the trip's start date, unless you're cancelling for a reason eligible for 100% cover. Visit this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/what-are-the-covered-reasons-to-get-100-reimbursement-for-trip-event-cancellation/?geo-redirect=true
- Trips purchased less than 3 days or more than 12 months before the start date
- Trips that are longer than 90 consecutive days
- Cancelled trips that the provider already reimbursed
- Trips not purchased with your Revolut account on the Ultra plan
- Trips purchased from private sellers or peer-to-peer transactions
- Trips booked for business purposes
- Trips cancelled by the provider, retailer, or organiser

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of coverage. Revolut's cancel for any reason insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Cancel for any reason' and tap 'Documents'

title: What is an event?
content_text:
An event in the context of Ultra trip and event cancellation protection refers to a planned occasion where bookings or tickets are sold in advance that you plan to attend within the next 12 months and that was paid for through your Ultra plan. 
Event cancellation protection covers you for the following activities if you need to cancel, as long as you meet the eligibility criteria: 
- Sports events
- Music festivals
- Concerts
- Shows
- Tours
- Tourist attractions
- Exhibitions

## Key exclusions:
- Cancellations less than 1 day before the event
- Events purchased less than 3 days or more than 12 months before the event
- Event carrier bookings or tickets that don't show event date(s)
- Cancelled events that the provider has already reimbursed
- Events not purchased with your Revolut account on the Ultra plan
- Events purchased from private sellers or peer-to-peer transactions
- Events booked for business purposes
- Events cancelled by the provider, retailer, or organiser
This is just a summary of how the cover works. For the full details regarding the cover in your Revolut app: 
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Insurance'
- Choose 'Travel' and select the cover type 'Cancel for any reason'
- Tap 'All documents' 
Need to cancel a future trip? Learn more in this FAQ/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/how-do-i-make-a-trip-or-event-cancellation-protection-claim in our Help Centre.

title: Who provides Ultra trip and event cancellation protection?
content_text:
We’re not an insurer, but we currently work exclusively with Qover to provide trip and cancellation insurance for all of our Ultra customers. Qover arranges and administers the policy for the insurer, Wakam.
We’re responsible to you for arranging trip and cancellation insurance as part of your subscription. Qover is responsible to you for handling any claim you make under that insurance, and for making any payments to you after a successful claim.
For more information, check the official Qover website.https://www.qover.com/

title: How do I link an external account to Revolut?
content_text:
You can link external bank accounts to your Revolut account for easier transfers and visibility across all of your balances from within the Revolut app. Linked accounts should be a personal or savings account in your own name.

## How to link a bank account
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Tap 'Add new'
- Tap the 'Linked' tab
- Select Linked account
- Choose a supported bank
- Tap 'Confirm' and complete the login on your bank's website or app
Once linked, your approval is valid for 90 days unless you revoke permission earlier. 

## Difficulty linking a bank account
A poor network connection can cause problems when linking your account. Close both your Revolut app and the external banking website or app, and try again once your connection is stronger.
If you still encounter an error in the Revolut app, request live support by typing 'Live agent' in the in-app chat for more assistance.
If the error only appears after leaving the Revolut app (i.e. in the verification screen from your bank, or when you don't get redirected correctly), it's best to reach out to the external bank for further support.

## How to add money from a linked account
You can add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup normally from your external banking app's 'Home' screen, or you can also initiate a transfer from your linked bank account within the Revolut app.
Your linked accounts and other saved deposit methods will also be displayed in the 'My methods' section of the 'Adding money' screen for ease of access.
For more help with a deposit from your linked bank account, read these FAQs/help/adding-money/add-money-with-a-bank-transfer/add-money-with-an-easy-bank-transfer.

## Timeframes
If your linked account supports instant transfers, your deposit should arrive instantly after authorisation, but may take several minutes. If not, transfers may take several hours up to 2 business days (if initiated on a weekend).
Once we receive the money, we'll settle it in the background. If your bank is unable to settle it, we'll revert the money within 5 business days. If the transfers made from your linked account aren't going through, read this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/with-linked-accounts/why-am-i-unable-to-add-money-with-a-linked-account.

title: Manage my external linked accounts in Revolut
content_text:
## How to unlink an external account
If you wish to revoke access to your external linked account, you'll need to do it from the Revolut app. You can do this at any time.
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select a linked account
- Tap 'Details'
- Scroll down and tap 'Revoke' 

## Analytics for linked accounts
Our analytics and budgeting tools for your linked accounts work automatically. You should see the app starting to track your spending as soon as your account is linked. To set a budget:
- In the Revolut app, go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap on the 'Analytics' icon in the top-right corner
- Select your linked account

title: Can I accept multiple paid plan gifts?
content_text:
If the same Revolut friend has sent you multiple gifts, you can only accept one gift sent by this friend at any given time.
The rest of the gifts will remain unclaimed, and they can be cancelled, declined, or you can accept the gift once your current gifted plan duration ends if the gift hasn't expired.
Gifts expire within 1 year of being received.

## I have received gifts from multiple Revolut friends
If multiple Revolut friends have sent you paid plan gifts, you can accept more than one gift. However, your gifted plan will be the latest gift you accepted.
You can also choose to accept one gift first and keep the other gifts as unaccepted. Paid plan gifts are valid for 1 year if they are not accepted/rejected, so you can also wait to accept the paid plan gifts sent to you, and redeem once your current paid plan gift has expired.

## For example:
Friend A sends you a gift of a Premium plan and you accept. Your current gifted plan will then be the Premium plan. A few days later, friend B sends you a gift for the Metal plan. If you accept the gift from friend B, then your current gifted plan changes to the Metal plan for the gift duration.
In this case, the initial Premium plan gift sent to you has already been redeemed, so it can no longer be used again, cancelled, or refunded to your friend. So you may want to wait for the Friend A Premium plan gift to expire before you redeem the Friend B Metal plan gift.
Make sure to redeem the Metal plan gift within the one year validity period.

title: Gifting a paid plan
content_text:
As a Revolut customer, you have the option to buy a paid plan for a Revolut friend if this feature is available in your country. We call this 'gifting' a paid plan. The price of a gifted paid plan is equal to the price of our annual billing plan options on our Fees pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/fees/?geo-redirect=true. 
Your Revolut friend will need to meet the following criteria:
- They're an existing Revolut customer
- They've activated the 'Show my plan' toggle in the 'Privacyhttps://app.revolut.com/settings/security/privacyrevolut://app/privacy' section of their app
- Additionally to be eligible for travel insurance cover, a friend receiving a Metal or Ultra plan must be at least 18 years of age and not older than 76

title: How to share your birthday on Revolut
content_text:
## Sharing your birthday with your friends
You can now share your birthday with your friends on Revolut to receive gifts. In turn, you’ll also receive reminders about their birthdays and have the option to send them gifts. To enable birthday sharing:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Privacy'
- Scroll down and enable the 'Show my birthday' toggle
Your friends will get a birthday reminder and have the option to send you a gift via Revolut. To stop sharing your birthday, simply disable the toggle anytime.

title: Receiving paid plan gifts
content_text:
## How to accept a paid plan gift
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Open the chat with your Revolut friend
- In the transaction description, you'll see the details of the gift you've received
- Choose 'Claim' to accept the paid plan gift
To receive paid plan gifts, your 'Show my plan' privacy toggle needs to be activated in your app. You can find this section by tapping your profile icon in the top-left corner → selecting 'Privacyhttps://app.revolut.com/settings/security/privacyrevolut://app/privacy' → scrolling down and enabling the 'Show my plan' toggle.
Once you claim a paid plan gift, it will become effective immediately. You can cancel the paid plan at any time without incurring any fees. However, if you cancel the plan early, your friend who sent you the gift won't receive a refund.

## What if I am on a paid plan already?
If you're already on a paid plan, and accept a paid plan gift, we'll refund you the amount of what you had paid (annual or monthly) to compensate for the time you did not spend on the paid plan. We'll then update your account to reflect the paid plan gift.
For example, if you're currently 7 months into an annual billing period for a Premium plan and your friend sends you a gift for the Metal plan, which you accept, we'll give you a partial refund of the annual plan fees you paid.
The refund is proportional to the 5 months you have remaining on your current annual Premium plan.

## What if I'm on an annual plan and get gifted an annual plan?
If you are currently on a paid plan subscription with annual billing and receive a paid plan gift for the same paid plan, we will add an additional year to your existing annual subscription. You will not receive a refund in this scenario. 
For example, You're currently in the 5th month of a 12 month subscription of a Premium paid plan with annual billing. You receive a paid plan gift for an annual Premium paid plan and accept the paid plan gift.
We will extend the duration of your annual Premium paid plan subscription by 1 year once your current annual Premium paid plan subscription ends (7 months from now).

title: Sending paid plan gifts
content_text:
## How to send a paid plan gift
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Open a chat with your Revolut friend who you want to send the paid plan gift to
- Tap your friend's profile icon in the top-right corner
- Choose 'Gift a plan' and follow the prompts
To receive the gift, your friend needs to have their 'Show my plan' privacy toggle in the 'Privacyhttps://app.revolut.com/settings/security/privacyrevolut://app/privacy' section of their app activated. They can find this section by:
- Tapping their profile icon in the top-left corner 
- Selecting 'Account'
- Selecting 'Privacyhttps://app.revolut.com/settings/security/privacyrevolut://app/privacy'
- Scrolling down to enable the 'Show my plan' toggle

## Which plans can I choose?
You can choose which Revolut paid plan you'd like to gift to your friend, but it must be at least on the same level as their current paid plan, or a higher paid plan.
For example, if your friend has a Premium plan, you can gift your Revolut friend a Premium, Metal, or Ultra plan.
You can only gift a paid plan to your friends on Revolut by using their phone number or Revtag. You can send a maximum of 50 gifts per week. You can send the same Revolut friend multiple gifts, however, they can only accept one gift at any given time from the same sender.

## How long is the gift valid for?
Your friend has one year to redeem the gift before it expires. Once the gift expires, it can't be redeemed, and you'll receive a refund for the price of the gift back into your account.
You can cancel the gift anytime until your friend accepts the gift. Once your friend accepts the gift, there is no option to cancel the gift and get it refunded.

title: What happens once the gifted paid plan duration ends?
content_text:
## If the gifted plan was different from your prior plan
Towards the end of the duration of your gifted paid plan, you can choose to stay on the same paid plan by automatically switching to paying the subscription fees for the plan (refer to our Paid Plan Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/paid-plans/?geo-redirect=true, Plan Gifting Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/paid-plan-gifts/?geo-redirect=true and Feeshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/fees/?geo-redirect=true page for more information).
If you don't opt for this, you'll be moved back to the type of plan you had before accepting the gift.
For example, if you were on the Standard plan and accepted a Premium plan gift, you'll be notified towards the end and given the option to pay for Premium and stay on the plan after the gift duration ends. If you don't opt for it, you'll be moved back to the Standard plan.

## If you were gifted the same plan that you had previously
If you were gifted the same plan that you had previously, and accepted the gift, you'll automatically be switched to the regular billing plan for the type of paid plan you had before you accepted the gift (refer to our Paid Plan Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/paid-plans/?geo-redirect=true, Plan Gifting Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/paid-plan-gifts/?geo-redirect=true and Feeshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/fees/?geo-redirect=true page for more information)
For example: If you were on the Premium plan and accepted a gift for another Premium plan, you'll automatically be switched back to a Premium plan once the gift duration expires.

## What will happen to the insurance benefit if I do not renew?
As a result of switching to a lower plan type after your gifted plan expires, any insurance that applied to your gifted plan will immediately come to an end, including travel, purchase protection, accidental damage, and cancellation insurance coverage. This depends on the new plan chosen.

## Can I re-upgrade?
Yes, you can. However, your insurance benefits, depending on your previous plan, will start as brand new, and you need to be on your plan continuously for your insurance to cover you.
This also includes travel insurance, read your insurance terms and conditions to make sure you have valid insurance cover before your next trip.

title: Earning interest with Savings
content_text:
## What is the interest rate?
The interest rate on your Savings account depends on your plan. If you change your plan, your rate will change accordingly when the plan change comes into effect. For more info, see our Instant Access Savings (powered by ClearBank) Terms https://www.revolut.com/legal/clearbank-savings-terms/and Savings Vaults Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/savings-vaults/.
The Annual Equivalent Rate (AER) shows the amount of interest you'd earn in a year, taking into account daily compounding. This allows you to compare rates with a different compounding schedule.
You can see the AER under your savings account balance in-app by tapping 'Info' and viewing the 'Deposit Information' or 'Summary Box' document.

## Why is the interest rate different depending on the partner?
We always negotiate the best possible rates for our customers. However, our bank partners offer their own competitive rates, which may differ. Customers are able to choose the best rates — see this article — while keeping other factors such as maximum deposit amounts in mind. See this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/savings-vaults/getting-started-with-instant-access-savings/ for more.

## How is the interest calculated and paid?
Interest is accrued and paid daily. While interest accrues daily, the minimum threshold for interest payable is GBP 0.01; this means interest may not be paid to your savings every day (until net interest becomes at least GBP 0.01). This is the only case where the interest payout would be postponed.
Funds must arrive at the partner bank before starting to earn interest. Funds usually arrive on the next business day after sending.
For example, a deposit made on Friday settles by Monday, and interest begins to accrue from Tuesday. For GBP savings with Clearbank, your funds settle immediately in most cases and start accruing interest from the next calendar day.

## Can the interest rate change?
We may adjust interest rates on your Savings account as explained in our Instant Access Savings T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/clearbank-savings-terms/ and Savings Vaults T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/savings-vaults/. We'll notify you before any decrease, but we may not notify you before increases happen. To view notice periods in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap Accounts
- Select your Savings account
- Tap 'Info'
- Tap 'Documents' or 'Summary Box'
- Select 'Deposit Information'
You can also review our partners terms for further information on your Savings accounts (ClearBank T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/clearbank-savings-partner-terms/ and Flagstone T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/savings-vaults-partner-terms/).

title: Getting started with Savings
content_text:
Revolut savings accounts are available for personal accounts, and are free to open and manage. Instant Access Savings are available in the following currencies: GBP, EUR, USD

## Eligibility criteria
- You’re a resident of the country where your Revolut account is registered and also hold tax residency there
- You’re not a US tax resident
- For GBP Savings, you also need to have a valid identity document

## How to access a Savings account
In the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap Accounts
- Tap 'Add new' and 'Savings'
- Follow the prompts to set up your account
 Or, simply use this link to create a Savings account.

## Are there any minimum or maximum deposit amounts?
No minimum deposit is required. If you hold deposits in either or both US dollars and euros with Investec, the total (across both currencies) must not exceed the £120,000 equivalent. British pounds savings accounts with ClearBank have a higher maximum limit of £5,000,000.
For more information, see our Instant Access Savings (powered by ClearBank) Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/clearbank-savings-terms/. Additionally, see our Savings Vaults Termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/savings-vaults/.

title: How to calculate interest on Savings accounts
content_text:
## Interest accrual and multiple Savings accounts
Interest accrues daily, but if it's less than 0.01 EUR/GBP/USD, it won't be paid until the cumulative interest exceeds this amount. Interest accrues on a single deposit per Savings account product for every partner bank. You can create multiple Savings accounts in-app for each partner bank product.
If you hold a GBP Savings account with our partner, Clearbank, each of these multiple Savings accounts corresponds to a single Savings account with Clearbank. This means that interest is paid individually on each of the accounts.
Our other partners, though, view your separate Savings accounts in the Revolut app as a single deposit. This means that interest is paid on the total balance in a single payment. When we receive it, we distribute it across your accounts based on their balances when the interest was calculated.

## Here's an interest accrual example
Consider a balance of £1,000 with a 2.5% annual interest rate, compounded daily:

## Day 2
- Interest accrued: £1,000 * 2.50%/365 = £0.0684932
- Interest paid: £0.06 (remaining amount keeps accruing until it becomes £0.01)
- New balance: £1,000 + £0.06 = £1,000.06

## Day 3
- Interest accrued: £1,000.06 * 2.50%/365 = £0.0684973
- Interest paid: £0.06 + £0.01 = £0.07 (accrued interest of Day 1: £0.0084932 and Day 2: £0.0084973)
- New balance: £1,000.06 + £0.07 = £1,000.13

title: How is interest calculated on tiered Instant Access Savings?
content_text:
## Savings in local currency
A tiered rate model follows the below structure:
- Balances up to £200,000 earn the regular plan rate
- Balances over £200,000 and up to £5,000,000 will earn a lowered rate — only for customers on eligible plans. Eligible plans: Ultra
- Balances on other plans continue to earn the regular plan rate on their full balance up to £5,000,000
A blended rate is calculated using the 2 rates, and the resulting rate is applied to your Instant Access Savings balance (across all personal Instant Access Savings balance in the same local currency).

## Example for Ultra plan in GBP
If you have £300,000 saved across all your personal Instant Access Savings balance (in the same local currency):
- £200,000 earns 4.00% AER — tier 1
- £100,000 earns 3.51% AER — tier 2
- 3.92% (expressed as AER) is the blended rate applied to the full balance

## Blended rate calculation (for above example)
- Step 1: Multiply the amount below and above the £200,000 threshold by their respective per annum (p.a.) rates, then add the results. Divide this total by the full balance to get the blended rate in p.a.: (£200,000 times 3.93% p.a. + £100,000 times 3.69% p.a.) ÷ £300,000 = 3.85% p.a
- Step 2: Convert to AER to reflect compounding
This blended rate of 3.92% AER is applied to the full balance.

## Savings in non-local currencies
If you hold deposits in either or both USD and EUR with Investec, the total (across both currencies) must not exceed the £120,000 equivalent.

title: Rate decrease on my Instant Access Savings
content_text:
## Why is the rate decreasing on my Instant Access Savings?
The way it works for banks is that interest rates are influenced by the rates set by the Bank of England (BoE), along with other macroeconomic factors. Recently, the BoE announced a decrease in their interest rate, impacting the way we bring Instant Access Savings to you.

## What will the new interest rate be?
Details on latest interest rates for GBP Instant Access Savings — offered by ClearBank — can be found on this pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/clearbank-savings-summary-box/.

## When will the interest rate decrease be effective?
Your Instant Access Savings will continue to earn the interest at the existing interest rate until the notice period ends. The notice period starts from the day Revolut lets you know about the rate reduction via email. You can learn more in our Instant Access Savings Terms.https://www.revolut.com/legal/clearbank-savings-terms/
For information on notice periods, follow these steps in-app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your 'Instant Access Savings'
- Tap 'Info' and 'Terms & Conditions'

## More information
Any new Instant Access Savings created in the meantime will earn interest at the new interest rate. If you switch to a different Revolut plan, the rate that applies to your savings will be the updated rate available on your  new plan. Your next interest accrual would reflect your new rate.
If you don't agree to the new rate and wish to close your savings, you can terminate your account in line with our Instant Access Savings Terms.https://www.revolut.com/legal/clearbank-savings-terms/

title: Why is my withdrawal pending?
content_text:
When you withdraw money from your savings account, which is held with one of our partner banks — Allica, Paragon, or Investec Bank — we may credit some or all of the amount to your Revolut account before receiving the funds from the partner bank. This allows you to access your money faster.
In rare cases when this isn’t possible, we’ll credit your withdrawal by the next business day, after we receive the funds from the partner bank.

title: Savings Challenge
content_text:
## What is the Savings Challenge?
A 52-week challenge where you can earn entries into monthly and annual prize draws. Choose a weekly amount between £10 and £50 (in multiples of £10), and we automatically move it to your savings. Save what you can. Essential expenses come first. Every £1 saved counts as 1 entry.
You can join between 1 May 2026 and 31 May 2026 by opening a Smart Saver Challenge Savings Account in the Revolut app. If you join later in the month, you’ll need to make a one-off payment to cover any missed weeks so you stay aligned with the current challenge week.
If you don’t join within this window, you won’t be able to participate. Once joined, your weekly amount is fixed for the full challenge and cannot be changed. You will earn the rate according to your plan, from standard 3.00% AER to Ultra 4.00% AER, and not any promotional rate if available. 

## How do weekly savings and payments work?
Your selected amount is transferred weekly. Weeks are tracked as completed, at risk, or missed. Only completed weeks count towards prize eligibility. Each week, your selected amount is added automatically or manually. A week is completed once the full amount is added by Sunday 23:59.
If a transfer fails, we retry once automatically. If it still doesn’t go through, the week becomes at risk and you’ll be notified. You can manually add funds before Sunday to restore the week to completed.
Use the in-app 'Catch-up' button to catch up within the same week if you have available funds. If the full amount isn’t added by Sunday, the week is missed, can’t be recovered, and won’t count towards prize eligibility but you can continue saving towards your goal and complete future weeks as usual.

## How do prizes and eligibility work?
There are monthly and annual prize draws. To qualify for a monthly prize of £10,000, all weeks in that month must be completed. Missing one week means you won’t be eligible for that month’s prize but you can still be eligible for future months — save in a way that works for you.
To qualify for the annual prize of £100,000, you must have at least 50 completed weeks out of 52. Missing 3 or more weeks makes you ineligible for the annual prize.
Each £1 saved gives you 1 entry into the prize draws. Higher weekly savings increase your number of entries and improve your chances of winning. If a week is missed or becomes invalid, it won’t count towards eligibility for monthly or annual prizes. Prizes are paid to your main account.

## How can I manage or exit the challenge?
Withdraw, pause, or close the challenge — you're in control of your finances. Withdraw money anytime. If your balance drops below your expected savings, previously completed weeks may become at risk. You must add back the full amount before Sunday 23:59 or those weeks will be marked as missed.
Pause the challenge at any time to stop future transfers. However, weeks during the pause may not be completed unless you manually add funds, which can affect your prize eligibility.
Close the challenge anytime and keep your savings, but you can't join/enter future prize draws. After 52 weeks, eligible customers enter the annual prize draw and convert this account to a regular Savings Account or withdraw money. Take the challenge at your pace — financial wellbeing comes first.

title: Instant Access Savings promotion
content_text:
We’re offering eligible new and existing Revolut UK customers who have not previously opened a Savings account before 10 December 2025 the possibility to earn a boosted 4.50% AER (variable) when they open an Instant Access Savings account.
The promotional campaign will run from 10 December 2025 to 22 January 2026. Eligible customers who set up an Instant Access Savings account during this period will benefit from a boosted 4.50% AER (variable) until 31 July 2026.
This rate will apply up to £20,000. For any amount over that, a blended rate will apply between the boosted rate and the applicable plan rate (see the Promo Rate Instant Access Savings (powered by ClearBank) - Product Terms and Summary Boxhttps://www.revolut.com/legal/promo-rate-savings-25-terms-and-summary-box-clearbank for more info on rates and promo conditions).

## You must meet the following eligibility criteria:
- You have not previously opened a Savings account with any partner bank in the Revolut app
- You opened your Instant Access Savings from 10 December 2025 to 22 January 2026
- You have a registered address in the UK
- You're at least 18 years old

## You're not eligible for this promotion if you:
- opened any Revolut Savings account before 10 December 2025 (regardless of the partner bank)
- are a US person
Ensure your Revolut app is updated to iOS version 10.106 or above, or Android version 10.107 or above, to open an Instant Access Savings account with this promotion.

## What happens after 31 July 2026?
On the expiry of this promotion, your Instant Access Savings rate will revert to the rate determined by your plan.
We’ll let you know about the change one month before the expiry of the promotion. If you’d prefer not to move to the new account, you can close your Instant Access Savings before the change takes effect.

title: Tax on Savings
content_text:
Revolut doesn't withhold any tax on your interest income earned. You have sole responsibility for the management of your legal obligations and tax affairs, including making any applicable filings and tax payments, and complying with any applicable laws and regulations.
Your personal tax status and local rules and regulations (which can change from time to time) will impact what you are required to declare. 
In particular, you'll be responsible for any reduction or exemption claims regarding withholding taxes. You should seek independent advice from a professionally qualified tax adviser if you have any questions.

## How can I determine the tax I have to pay?
You can download your Savings accounts statement in-app. This statement provides an overview of earned interest, which can help you report your income.

title: How are the funds in my Savings accounts protected?
content_text:
## How does Revolut safeguard my account with ClearBank?
For GBP savings with ClearBank, we ensure that all your deposits in your Savings accounts are held with ClearBank and protected by the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) up to a limit of £120,000 across all your ClearBank-powered accounts.
To learn more about this in-app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Under your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your Savings account
- Tap 'Info'
- Tap the information button near 'FSCS protection'
- Select 'Open protection document'

## How does Revolut ensure my other Savings accounts are safe?
Revolut ensures that all deposits in your other Savings accounts are held in a trust, adhering to our Savings accounts termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/savings-vaults/?geo-redirect=true, Trustee Terms and the Trust Deedhttps://www.revolut.com/legal/savings-vaults-partner-terms/?geo-redirect=true. To gain a deeper understanding of these protections and how we keep your savings safe, and to learn more about the trustee:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu of the main Revolut app
- Under your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your Savings account
- Tap 'Info'

## How Revolut handles your deposits
For GBP savings with ClearBank, the money you deposit to your Savings account is transferred instantly to ClearBank in most of the cases. Money you deposit in other Savings accounts is placed (by the trustee) with a partner bank on your behalf.
It's always held in a segregated trust account operated by the trustee, ensuring your funds are secure. The bank acts as the deposit taker, not the trustee or Revolut, providing an added layer of security.

## Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS)
Revolut has partnered with banks that are part of the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS). If eligible, your Savings accounts deposits are protected up to £120,000 per person, per bank, by the FSCS.
If you have another account with the same bank outside of Revolut, that account will also count towards the total FSCS cover. You can learn more on the FSCS websitehttps://www.fscs.org.uk/.

title: Why was my Savings Accounts restricted or closed?
content_text:
In exceptional circumstances, we may need to close or restrict your Savings Account, as per our T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/savings-vaults/?geo-redirect=true. This can happen if we're reasonably concerned about your account security, or if we're required to take action by law. 
If your account is restricted, we'll inform you of the reason as long as we can do so without any security risk or legal impediment. We'll also let you know as soon as your account is unrestricted.
If your account is closed, your Savings Account balance will immediately be moved to your current account. You'll also receive a document within 30 days of closure, which will include any deposits, withdrawals, and interest earned on your account in the previous 12 months.

title: Add money to your savings account
content_text:
You can set up one-time or recurring deposits into your savings account from your main Revolut currency account.
We'll always show you any fees on the deposit screen before you add money.
To deposit funds, you need to set up a Savings account and accept the T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/savings-vaults/. Learn more in this FAQ/help/app-features/savings-vaults/getting-started-with-instant-access-savings.

## Make a one-time deposit
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your savings account
- Tap 'Move'
- Set the deposit amount and tap 'Add money'

## Set up recurring deposits
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your savings account
- Scroll down and select 'Recurring transfer' or tap on the calendar icon on the bottom left and tap 'Move'
- Set the start date and frequency
- Follow the prompts to set the amount and deposit method
Once that's done, you can follow the same steps to edit, deactivate, or delete your recurring deposit.

## Round up everyday card purchases
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your savings account
- Scroll down and select 'Spare change'
- Set the multiplier and tap 'Save'
Once that's done, you can follow the same steps to edit or remove spare change/help/app-features/vaults/how-do-spare-change-round-ups-work.

title: Close my Savings account
content_text:
To close a Savings account in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your Savings account
- Tap 'More' and 'Close account'
Your Savings account balance should immediately be moved to your current account.

## Interest handling after closure
If you close one or more Savings accounts, any interest that was accrued before this day on your balance will be paid to your main Revolut account the following day.
To ensure you receive all accrued interest, keep your main Revolut account open until the interest is paid. Any interest that was accrued but not yet paid (below 0.01) will be lost.

title: Withdraw from your Savings account
content_text:
To withdraw from a Savings account in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your Savings account
- Tap 'Move'
- Set the amount and confirm your withdrawal
Withdrawals to your main currency account should always be free and instant. We’ll show you any fees that may apply on the 'Transfer' screen.

title: Documents and statements for my Savings accounts
content_text:
## How to download a statement for an active account
All documents can be found in-app.
To download a statement for an active Savings account from the app:
- Go to 'Savings'
- Tap 'More'
- Select 'Statement' 
- Choose the timeframe you want to generate a statement for
- Tap 'Generate'

## How to download a statement for a closed account
All documents can be found in-app.
To download a statement for a closed Savings account from the app:
- Tap the profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Documents & statements'
- Choose 'Savings'

## How to view the T&Cs
In the app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your Savings account
- Select 'Info' and select 'Documents'
- Choose 'Terms and conditions'

## More info about Savings accounts and interest rates
In the app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Select your Savings account
- Tap 'Info' and select 'Documents'
- Choose 'Deposit information'

title: Cover for cancelled and interrupted trips
content_text:
## Trip cancellation
If you have the Metal plan, your travel insurance covers trip cancellations if your trip is eligible and the cancellation reason is covered by the policy's T&Cs. 
Cover is available for non-refundable costs and cancellation fees up to £5,000 per insured person.
If you have the Ultra plan, trip and event cancellations are covered separately under 'cancel for any reason' insurance. Learn more in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/what-does-the-trip-event-cancellation-protection-cover-me-for/?geo-redirect=true.

## Trip interruption
If your trip is interrupted for a reason covered by the T&Cs, such as an injury or illness, you can submit a claim for:
- Unused trip costs up to £5,000
- Emergency accommodation up to £1,000
- The cost of an economy-class ticket

## Key exclusions
- You fail to obtain required passports, visas, or travel documents
- You change your mind or can't afford the trip
- You can recover the costs from any other source, including if you're refunded with a voucher
- You travel to sanctioned countries or against government advice
If you're eligible for cover, you can raise a claim directly with the insurance provider. Learn more in this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/how-to-make-a-travel-insurance-claim/making-a-travel-insurance-claim/?geo-redirect=true. 
Have a trip coming up? Check whether it might be covered with this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.cover-for-cancelled-and-interrupted-tripsrevolut://app/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.cover-for-cancelled-and-interrupted-trips. 

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions about the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Travel insurance' and tap 'Get help'

title: Do I have to pay for my trip using my Revolut account?
content_text:
To be protected, you must have an active Revolut Metal or Ultra plan at the time of booking your trip and use your account to pay for at least 75% of your primary transport and accommodation expenses. Payments eligible for cover include: 
- Revolut debit or virtual card payments
- A transfer from your Revolut account to a travel supplier
- Use of RevPoints or Airline Miles purchased with RevPoints
- Payment using a Joint account 
Your plan must remain active (without interruption) from when you book your trip to when you travel. If you downgrade your plan in the meantime, or booked the trip before upgrading, it will not be covered. Have a trip coming up? Check whether it might be covered with this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.do-i-have-to-pay-for-my-trip-using-my-revolut-accountrevolut://app/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.do-i-have-to-pay-for-my-trip-using-my-revolut-account. 

## What does primary transport mean?
Primary transport is the main means of travel to your destination. This doesn't include transfers to and from the primary means of travel. 
For example, for a trip from the UK to Spain, flights and accommodation must be paid using your Revolut account but the taxi transfers between the airport and your accommodation don't have to be paid using your Revolut account.

## Is my trip covered if I don't pay with Revolut?
You must use your Revolut account to pay for at least 75% of your combined primary transport and accommodation expenses to be covered. If you don't pay for either of these with your Revolut account, your trip will not be eligible for cover.
Cover may be extended in some circumstances at the discretion of our insurance partner. For example, if you're driving to your destination but paid for your accommodation with your Revolut account, or if you're staying with a friend but paid for your flights with your Revolut account. 
If you have any doubts regarding the cover, contact Chubb by phone at +44 (0) 203 964 3004. Lines are open 9am to 5pm, Monday to Friday, or you can contact them by email at revoluttravelinsurance@chubb.com. 

## Is my trip covered if someone else booked the trip? 
You'll need to provide proof of payment for the trip from your Revolut account. This can include a Revolut card payment or transfer from you to the trip organiser for your primary transport and accommodation costs.

title: Claiming after Premium travel insurance removal
content_text:
Since 25 July 2024, travel insurance is no longer included as a Premium plan benefit. Trips taking place on or after this cut-off date are not covered.
If your claim is for an event that occurred before this date, you can contact Chubb directly using the below details. 
- Online: submit your claim on this websitehttps://intake.sedgwick.com/u/ChubbTravel/RevolutTravelClaims
- Phone: 0203 964 3004. Lines are open 9am to 5pm, Monday to Friday. Please have your policy number ready before calling
- Email: revolutclaims@ie.sedgwick.com 
- Post: Revolut Travel Insurance Claims Team, Chubb European Group SE, PO BOX 1086, Belfast BT1 9ES

title: Does moving home impact my travel insurance?
content_text:
Your travel insurance is based on the address and country of residence linked to your Revolut account. If you move home (or to another country) and don't update your address in your account, you may not be eligible for cover and future travel insurance claims could be declined.
Trips must start and end at your primary residence, which is the address linked to your Revolut account. You can read more about how trips are defined in this article./help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/before-going-abroad/what-is-the-maximum-trip-duration-to-get-covered-by-the-travel-insurance 
Make sure to update your address in your Revolut account whenever you move. To find instructions on how to do this, read this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/how-do-i-change-my-country-of-residence.

title: Travel insurance coverage
content_text:
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions about the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance', choose 'Travel insurance', and tap 'Documents'

## Trips covered by travel insurance
- Personal and business-related trips
- Worldwide, except for travel to countries or areas where the government's Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) advises against 'all travel' or 'all but essential' travel
- Trips in the UK, more than 75 miles away from your home, that include an overnight stay
The Metal plan covers up to 30 days per trip, while Ultra covers up to 90 days. Read more on this pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/before-going-abroad/what-is-the-maximum-trip-duration-to-get-covered-by-the-travel-insurance/?geo-redirect=true. Have a trip coming up? Check whether it might be covered with this linkhttps://app.revolut.com/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.travel-insurance-coveragerevolut://app/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.travel-insurance-coverage.

## Overview of benefits 
- Emergency medical expenses: if you're sick or injured abroad. Learn more in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/what-am-i-covered-for/travel-insurance-features/does-my-insurance-cover-me-for-medical-expenses-abroad/?geo-redirect=true
- Trip delay: if your public transport is delayed. Learn more in this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/what-am-i-covered-for/travel-insurance-features/does-my-insurance-cover-me-for-cancelled-or-delayed-flights-and-airport-delays/?geo-redirect=true
- Baggage protection: if your baggage is damaged or stolen or if it's lost or delayed in transit. Learn more on this pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/what-am-i-covered-for/travel-insurance-features/does-my-insurance-cover-me-for-damaged-and-lost-baggage-or-baggage-delay/?geo-redirect=true
- Car hire excess: if your rental car is stolen or damaged. Learn more in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/what-am-i-covered-for/travel-insurance-features/car-hire-excess-protection/?geo-redirect=true
- Trip interruption: if you have to cut your trip short. Learn more in this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/travel-insurance/cover-for-cancelled-and-interrupted-trips/?geo-redirect=true
- Trip cancellation: if you have to cancel your trip (Metal plan only). Learn more on this pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/travel-insurance/cover-for-cancelled-and-interrupted-trips/?geo-redirect=true
Ultra plan holders are covered for trip cancellation under cancel for any reason protection. Learn more in this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/what-does-the-trip-event-cancellation-protection-cover-me-for/?geo-redirect=true.

## Winter sports cover
Revolut travel insurance offers cover if you (or your insured travelling companions) are injured while skiing, snowboarding, or taking part in other winter sports. Learn more in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/what-am-i-covered-for/travel-insurance-features/does-my-travel-insurance-cover-me-for-winter-or-extreme-sports/?geo-redirect=true.

title: Travel insurance for areas affected by conflict
content_text:
## What to do if you’re in a conflict area
If you’re currently in a region experiencing conflict, be aware of how your cover works:
- Your travel insurance doesn't cover any claims caused by a war or act of war, whether or not one has been declared
- If your flight is cancelled by the airline, contact them directly for a refund or to rebook travel as these types of cancellations should be handled by the carrier
- For other medical emergencies abroad, read more in this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/what-am-i-covered-for/im-abroad-and-i-need-medical-assistance-what-should-i-do/?geo-redirect=true
- For British nationals in a crisis abroad, contact the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO)
- Other nationalities: contact your country’s department of foreign affairs or embassy

## If your trip hasn't started yet
If you're planning or have a trip coming up in a region currently experiencing conflict:
- Your policy doesn't cover travel to a destination where the FCDO has advised against ‘all travel’ or ‘all but essential travel’ including trip cancellation if the FCDO changes their advice after you’ve booked your trip
- Cover doesn't apply if you cancel for a reason that's not specifically insured by your policy
- If your trip is cancelled by your airline or the airport, contact them directly for a refund or to reschedule, these types of cancellations should be handled by the carrier and not by your insurance policy

## Travel insurance help
This is only a summary of your cover. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for the policy terms and claim handling. If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Chubb directly: 
- By phone: +44 20 3964 3004. Lines are open 09:00–17:00, Monday to Friday
- By email: revoluttravelinsurance@chubb.com

title: Close a Pocket
content_text:
If you still have money in the Pocket you want to close, it'll be automatically added to your main balance once you close it. If it’s a Group Pocket, only the admin can close it. You won't be able to close a Pocket until you unlink any cards connected to it.
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Choose your Pocket
- Tap 'Info'
- Select 'Close Pocket' and 'Confirm'

title: How do I manage a Group Pocket?
content_text:
With a Group Pocket, you can save with family and friends for a common goal. The management permissions for your Pocket will vary based on whether you're an admin or a member.
You should only join a Group Pocket or send money to it if you trust the admin. If the owner closes their Revolut account, or their account is restricted, you won't be able to access the money in their Group Pocket.

## Create a Group Pocket in-app
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Tap 'Add new' and 'Pockets'
- Choose 'Group'
- Set the Pocket name and currency
- Select friends and tap 'Invite'
- Define the withdrawal permissions
- Tap 'Continue' to complete setup

## Admin permissions
Group Pocket admins can:
- Add members: scroll down in the Group Pocket and tap 'Members', then select ‘Add’
- Remove members: scroll down in the Group Pocket and tap 'Members', select a member, and select 'Remove'
- Set withdrawal permissions for each member
- Add money via once-off transfers, recurring transfers, or spare change
- Close a Group Pocket

## Member permissions
- Accept or decline Group Pocket invitations
- Add money via once-off transfers, recurring transfers, or spare change
- Withdraw money only if permission is granted by the admin
- Leave a Group Pocket

title: Adding money to Pockets
content_text:
You can set up one-time or recurring transfers into your Pockets from your main currency account in the Revolut app.

## Make a one-time deposit
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Choose your Pocket
- Tap 'Add money'
- Select the Revolut currency or crypto account you want to deposit from, and how much you'd like to deposit
- Tap 'Add money'

## Set up recurring transfers
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Choose your Pocket
- Scroll down and tap 'Recurring transfer'
- Set the start date and frequency
Once that's done, you can follow the same steps to edit, deactivate, or delete your recurring transfer.

## Round up your spare change
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Choose your Pocket 
- Scroll down and tap 'Spare change'
- Set the multiplier and tap 'Save'
Once that's done, you can follow the same steps to edit or remove spare change.
You can round up your spare change for Regular Pockets and Group Pockets.

title: Withdrawing from Pockets
content_text:
You can only withdraw money from a Pocket in-app if you're the admin or if the admin of a Group Pocket has given you permission to withdraw money from it.
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Choose your Pocket
- Tap 'Withdraw'
- Enter the amount you wish to withdraw
- Tap 'Withdraw' and the money will be added to your main balance

title: Spare change round ups
content_text:
## How do spare change round ups work?
With spare change round ups, we automatically round up your transactions and transfer the difference into your chosen Revolut Pocket or Savings Account, or to purchase cryptocurrencies/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/whats-crypto-spare-change or earn RevPoints.
Rounding rules vary depending on the currency:
- PLN and RON: rounded to the nearest multiple of 5
- CZK, DKK, NOK, SEK, and ZAR: rounded to the nearest multiple of 10
- HUF and JPY: rounded to the nearest multiple of 100
- All other supported currencies: transactions are rounded to the nearest whole number

## Enable or disable spare change in RevPoints
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap the RevPoints icon in the top-right corner
- Under 'How you earn' enable or disable the 'Spare change' toggle as desired
If you've enabled spare change to contribute towards RevPoints, it will need to be deactivated if you choose to switch it back to a Pocket or Savings Account.

## Enable or disable spare change in Pockets or Savings Account
In the Revolut mobile app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Choose your Pocket or Savings Account
- Scroll down and enable or disable the 'Spare change' toggle as desired
You’ll see an option to accelerate your round ups by x1, x2, x3, x4, x5, and x10. This means you can increase your spare change contributions to save even quicker. Round ups are applied to all transactions made using any card linked to your account, even virtual cards.

## Different currencies
Spare change for Pockets and Savings Account use one currency. If you spend in a different currency, we'll convert the spare change to your Pocket's currency. Weekend conversions include a fee, and other standard Revolut fees may apply.
For more information, visit this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies on weekend fees.

title: I can't find my Vaults
content_text:
For Revolut app versions 10 and above, Vaults have been removed. You can now use our Pockets, and Savings Account.

## Pockets
Pockets are money management tools that can simplify managing your expenses. You can choose between our Regular and Group Pockets.
For more details, visit our Pockets FAQs/help/app-features/vaults.

## Savings Account
Savings Account allows you to earn interest on your deposits or spare change, without any risk on your capital.
For more details, visit our Savings Account FAQs/help/app-features/savings-vaults.

## View new account options in-app
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Accounts' and 'Add new'
- Choose an option from the list

title: How to link a card to a Pocket and manage it
content_text:
## From your card's settings
- Go to ‘Home’ on the bottom menu
- Tap the card icon in the top-right corner
- Select the card you want to link to a Pocket
- Tap 'Settings'
- Select 'Spend from' 
- Choose the Pocket you want to spend from 
To change or unlink, follow the same steps and then select another Pocket you want to spend from, or select one of the cash options to unlink the card from its current Pocket.

## From your Pocket's settings
- Open the Pocket you want to link a card to 
- Scroll down to 'Linked card' and tap 'Link a card'
- Choose a card from the list or order a new one
To change or unlink a card, follow the same steps and tap 'Unlink card'. You can leave it unlinked or choose a new card to link.

## Using a linked card
If you attempt to make a card payment without sufficient funds in the Pocket linked to your card, the transaction will be declined. No other Pockets or accounts will be considered. Keep in mind the following:
- You can only link your card to personal Pockets
- You can link active physical and virtual cards
- You can't link multiple cards to your Pocket
- Cards can only be linked to one Pocket at a time

title: Getting started with Pockets
content_text:
Pockets are short-term money management tools.

## Create a Pocket in-app
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap Accounts
- Select 'Add new' and choose 'Pockets'
- Choose your Pocket type and follow the instructions

## Types of Pockets
- Personal Pockets: to save for a personal goal or expense. This could be for anything, such as your rent, a new car, or your weekly grocery shopping
- Group Pockets: you can save with family and friends for a common goal. This could be for a holiday or for covering your next meal together

title: What if I don't have enough money in my bills Pocket?
content_text:
We'll send you a notification 24 hours before the payment, so you'll have enough time to top-up your account in order for the payment to go through.
If you don’t have enough money for the transaction in your Pocket, we’ll take it from your main account instead. If there's not enough money in the currency account or assigned Pocket, the transaction will fail.
- For Direct Debits: contact the merchant to trigger the transaction again, or make a one-time bank transfer
- For subscriptions: add money to your Pocket or account. The merchant should automatically re-attempt to charge the amount
- If the merchant doesn't charge you again, contact them directly

title: What is Revolut Pay?
content_text:
Revolut Pay is a one-click payment method that allows you to make online and in-person payments directly from your Revolut account, without entering card or bank details. 
For online payments, look for the Revolut Pay button when confirming a purchase in-app. For in-person payments, look for the Revolut Pay QR code on the Revolut Terminal. 
There's no limit to the amount of payments you can make with Revolut Pay.

## Benefits of Revolut Pay
- Turn your RevPoints into full or partial discounts on Revolut Pay transactions. You also earn RevPoints on each Revolut Pay purchase
- Enjoy a one-tap checkout by authorising payments directly in-app. Your details are protected by Revolut Secure, which encrypts and stores them safely, meaning you don't need to share or save your details across various merchants or external sites
- Revolut also offers buyer protection for Revolut Pay transactions‌ — ‌if a merchant fails to fulfil your order, you can raise a claim. To do this, you need to contact our customer support team

## How does it work for online payments?
- When shopping online, look for the 'Revolut Pay' button at checkout
- Select 'Revolut Pay' to be redirected to your Revolut app for payment confirmation
- If you have RevPoints, select the amount you'd like to redeem as a discount
- Approve the payment and earn RevPoints for every transaction
In most cases, Revolut Pay transactions are completed immediately. However, if you see a transaction marked as 'pending', it means the merchant has not yet processed the payment. If the merchant doesn't process the payment within 7 days, the funds will be automatically returned to your account.
Revolut Pay T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/pay-with-revolut-checkout/?geo-redirect=true and RevPoints T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RevPoints/?geo-redirect=true apply.

## How does it work for in-person payments?
Customers with a personal Revolut account can enjoy easy, straight-forward payments with Revolut Pay through the Revolut Terminal. To do so:
- Customers can scan the Revolut Pay QR Code shown on the payment screen at the terminal. This will lead them to the Revolut app
- If they have RevPoints, they can select the amount to redeem as a discount (optional)
- They can then approve the payment, earning RevPoints for every  Revolut Pay transaction
If you're looking into accepting payments through Revolut Pro (designed for freelancers, sole traders, and professional/business), visit this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/accepting-customer-payments/accepting-payments-with-pro/.

title: How to withdraw cash from an ATM
content_text:
## How to withdraw cash
- Make sure you have sufficient funds in your account
- Check that the card you want to use is active, cash withdrawals are enabled, and you know your card's PIN
- Locate an ATM and follow the on-screen instructions to complete your withdrawal
If the ATM supports contactless withdrawals, you won't be able to use a virtual card. You'll need a physical card, or you can use your physical card from your digital wallet.
If your withdrawal was declined, learn more in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/withdrawing-cash-issues/why-was-my-cash-withdrawal-declined.

## Limits and fees
Cash withdrawal limits and fees may apply depending on your plan. Read about cash withdrawal fees and limits in this FAQ/help/inactive-faqs/why-did-the-atm-charge-a-fee-for-my-cash-withdrawal and check the information about withdrawing in other currencies in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/can-i-pay-in-a-specific-currency.
Each ATM can charge fees based on their own policies. If you’re unsure if Revolut charged a fee, tap on the withdrawal in your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to see the details. If no fee is shown, we didn’t charge one.

## How to find an ATM
From your home screen in the app, tap the cards icon in the top-right corner, then tap 'Find ATMs nearby' at the bottom.

title: Fees for cash withdrawals
content_text:
Whether you convert your funds before or at the time of a withdrawal, you are subject to currency exchange fees and weekend fees/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies.

## Cash withdrawal fees
All ATM withdrawals are subject to our cash withdrawal fee. As part of our plan benefits, this fee is waived for a certain amount of withdrawals each rolling month, depending on your plan. You can increase your fee-free limit by upgrading to a higher-tier plan.
- Standard: £200 (or 5 withdrawals, whichever comes first)
- Plus: £200
- Premium: £400 
- Metal: £800 
- Ultra: £2,000
A 2% or £1 fair usage fee applies thereafter. 
These fees do not reflect any fees that may be charged by the ATM.
If you're unsure if Revolut charged a fee, tap the ATM withdrawal in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to see the details. If no fee is shown, we didn't charge one.

title: My card payment is pending
content_text:
A pending card payment happens when your transaction is authorised but hasn’t been fully processed yet. Typically, pending transactions are cleared within a few business days. 

## How to check the estimated reversal date
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Open your in-app transactionshttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap the card payment
- In the 'Status' section, you'll see the reversal date

## Can I cancel a pending card payment?
We can't cancel pending payments on our end before the date mentioned in-app. We recommend allowing a few days for the merchant to take action.
The transaction amount is temporarily blocked in your account to fund the transaction and cannot be accessed by anyone until the merchant completes the payment, cancels the payment, or the transaction expires (in which case the funds will become available to you again).
If your transaction has been in a pending state for longer, contact the merchant directly, as only they can progress the transaction. If they take no action until the unblock date shown in the transaction status, the blocked funds should automatically be returned to you.

title: How to disable RevPoints spare change?
content_text:
If you have RevPoints spare change enabled, then every time you make a card payment, a certain amount from the transaction is transferred to your RevPoints. For example, if you buy a coffee for GBP 2.46, you'll earn RevPoints with the spare change of GBP 0.54.
RevPoints earned with spare change payments are non-refundable. Check this FAQ/help/revpoints/what-is-revpoints/question-how-can-i-redeem-my-revpoints to learn about redeeming RevPoints.
You can redeem your RevPoints by exchanging them for air miles in airline loyalty programmes, by making bookings with our reservations platform (Stays and Experiences), and by exchanging them for a discount at checkout by using Revolut Pay.

## Disable spare change
You can disable spare change anytime by following these steps:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Earn' below your points balance
- Select 'Spare change', then 'Deactivate'

title: Why did my payment amount change?
content_text:
## Why did the merchant update my payment amount?
Merchants can update a payment's amount if the quantity of goods or services you consumed has changed since you started the payment.
This could be the addition of a tip when using a food delivery app, a change in route that affects the cost of an Uber ride, or any situation where the product or service price might change before you pay for it.

## When do payment updates usually happen?
Payment updates are common among merchants for things like transport, food, energy, accommodation, and car rentals.

## What should I do next?
If you disagree with the updated amount, contact the merchant. If this is unproductive, you can raise a chargeback request to recover your funds:
- Return to the payment screen and scroll down to 'Get help'
- Select 'I was charged incorrectly'
- Tap 'Raise a dispute'
- Follow the steps to complete a chargeback request

title: Fees for card payments in a foreign currency
content_text:
All customers can spend, withdraw, and exchange foreign currencies during the week without additional fees, up to a certain amount based on your plan. After this, you will be charged an exchange fee.

## Exchange fees
This fee is only charged if you exceed your monthly free allowance. This fee differs depending on your plan and resets on the date your plan started. Check which fee matches your plan below.
- Standard: 1% over £1,000
- Plus: 0.5% over £3,000
- Premium, Metal, and Ultra: no additional exchange fees

## Weekend fees
Depending on your plan, you may be charged a fee on all exchanges made on the weekend between 5pm Friday and 6pm Sunday, Eastern Time (ET). For Standard customers, this fee is 1%. For Plus, it is 0.5%. Premium, Metal, and Ultra customers don't have to pay an additional fee on weekends. 

## How to check my fees
You can check your fees and allowance reset dates in 'Your plan' section.

title: Setting a monthly spending limit
content_text:
We apply weekly limits for card payments based on the merchant's registered business type.
You can set a monthly spending limit so you won't over-spend. Any transaction over this limit will be declined.
You can set a monthly spending limit in the 'Cards' section, by tapping the card icon in the top right, so you won't over-spend, and any transaction over this limit will be declined. If your card is getting declined, the reason should be visible in transaction details.

title: How do regulatory limits affect my usage?
content_text:
The Payment Services Directive (PSD 2) requires us to enforce certain security measures for our card payments.
For contactless payments, you need to verify your identity by performing a chip and PIN transaction or reset your contactless allowance in the app.
For online payments, you may occasionally need to confirm the payment in the app. The merchant website will prompt you to do so when this is required during the checkout process.

title: Am I eligible for Apple Pay?
content_text:
## Supported countries
Apple Pay is currently available for Revolut customers in the below countries:
Europe: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, UK, and Ukraine.
Rest of the world: Australia, Brazil, Japan, Mexico, New Zealand, Singapore, US.

## Supported devices
You can add your Revolut cards to any of the following devices:
- iPhone SE or more recent models
- iPad 3 or newer models
- All Apple Watch models
- Mac models with Touch ID

## Eligibility requirements
To be able to use Apple Pay, you need to:
- Have passed identity verification
- Have no negative balance on the account
- Use a non-disposable (physical or virtual) card
- Be the minimum age required to use Apple Pay in your country

title: Am I eligible for Google Pay?
content_text:
## Supported countries
Google Pay is available to customers in the below countries:
Europe: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, UK, Ukraine.
Rest of the world: Australia, Brazil, Japan, Mexico, Singapore, US.

## Supported devices
Your Android device needs to support NFC (near-field communication) and HCE (host card emulation) to be compatible with Google Pay.
To find out whether your device will work with Google Pay, go to 'Settings' on your device and select 'Wireless & networks'. Tap 'More' and if you see NFC appear, your device will be able to make Google Pay payments in stores.

## Eligibility requirements
To be able to use Google Pay, you need to:
- Have passed identity verification
- Have no negative balance on your account
- Use a non-disposable (physical or virtual) card
- Be the minimum age required to use Google Pay in your country

title: Payment limits when using Apple Pay or Google Pay
content_text:
If there is a monthly spending limit on your Revolut card, the payments you make using Apple Pay or Google Pay will be subject to the same limit. Some merchants might also have their own contactless limit.
We apply weekly limits for card payments based on the merchant's registered business type. Read more about the limits in this FAQ.

title: Decline reasons on Apple Pay and Google Pay
content_text:
