If you use your card for payments via Apple or Google Pay and your transaction is declined, we’ve prepared some of the most common reasons on why that might have happened.
If you just started using your card via Apple or Google Pay, your card payments might get declined in case card authorization wasn't successful. For example, if there are insufficient funds on the account, or if your card is blocked. 

## Apple or Google Pay previously worked, but stopped working
- If you get a notification saying 'Card suspended',  remove and add the card again
- If your card has been terminated, the card will no longer be active on Apple/Google Pay. If you reactivate the terminated one, you'll need to delete the card from Apple/Google Pay and add it again after reactivation
- Check if your card is frozen in-app. If your card was frozen, any payment with Apple/Google Pay will be declined and the card will appear as suspended in Apple/Google Pay. If your card isn't working with your digital wallet, try to delete it and re-add it again or use another card
You should make sure that your card wasn't frozen or terminated due to compromised card security. We don't advise you to use that card, and in this case you'll need to use a new card.

title: Setting up Apple Pay
content_text:
To set up Apple Pay in the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Select the card you wish to add
- Tap 'Add to Apple Wallet'
To set up Apple Pay on your iPhone or Apple device:
- Open your wallet
- Tap '+' in the top-right corner
- Select 'Debit or Credit Card'
- Scan or enter the details manually
If your card isn't working with Apple Pay, try to delete it and re-add it, or use another card.

## Choose a verification method
You can choose between two authentication methods.
In-app verification:
- From your wallet’s app, you’ll be redirected to the Revolut app
- Log in into the Revolut app
- Tap 'Confirm'
Text message verification:
- Tap the notification sent to your phone where the Revolut app is installed
- You’ll be automatically redirected to log in to the Revolut app
- When you open the app, a pop-up will appear with a one-time password (OTP). Copy this OTP and paste it in your wallet’s app to complete verification

title: Setting up Google Pay
content_text:
To set up Google Pay in the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Select the card you wish to add
- Tap 'Add to G Pay'
To set up Google Pay on your phone:
- Open your Google Pay app
- Tap your profile picture
- Select 'Add to Wallet' and 'Payment card'
- Scan or enter the details manually
If your card isn't working with Google Pay, try to delete it and re-add it, or use another card.

## Choose a verification method
You can choose between two authentication methods.
In-app verification:
- From your Google Pay app, you’ll be redirected to the Revolut app
- Log in into the Revolut app
- Tap 'Confirm'
Text message verification:
- Tap the notification sent to your phone where the Revolut app is installed
- You’ll be automatically redirected to log in to the Revolut app
- When you open the app, a pop-up will appear with a one-time password (OTP). Copy the OTP and paste it in your wallet’s app to complete verification

title: Where can I pay with Apple Pay or Google Pay?
content_text: You can pay with Apple Pay or Google Pay online and at any offline merchant where the payment terminal supports contactless payments. You don't need an internet connection if you're paying in-store.

title: How do I block and unblock a merchant?
content_text:
## Block a merchant
In the Revolut app:
- Tap your profile icon in the top left corner
- Select 'Security'
- Choose 'Payments'
- Select Blocked payments
- Tap 'Add new' and tap a previous payment from the merchant you want to block

## Unblock a merchant
Select the merchant you want to unblock and tap 'Unblock payments'.
You can also do this directly for certain transactionshttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list with blocked merchants.
You can only have a maximum of 5 active blocks in place and block up to 3 different merchants within a 30-day period.

## Whitelisted merchants
Some merchants cannot be blocked as described above. This includes:
- Apple (such as App Store, iTunes, iCloud, and more)
- Google (such as Google Play, YouTube, and more)
- Others
To stop payments to these merchants, go to the respective merchant’s website or app to manage your settings or unsubscribe from their products directly (such as Apple subscriptionshttps://apps.apple.com/account/subscriptions).

title: What currencies are supported for spending?
content_text:
Revolut currently supports card payments and ATM withdrawals wherever Mastercard and Visa are accepted, and in the following currencies:

## Supported currencies
AED, AFN, ALL, AMD, ANG, AOA, ARS, AUD, AWG, AZN, BAM, BBD, BDT, BHD, BIF, BMD, BND, BOB, BRL, BSD, BWP, BZD, CAD, CDF, CHF, CLP, CNY, COP, CRC, CVE, CZK, DJF, DKK, DOP, DZD, EGP, ERN, ETB, EUR, FJD, FKP, GBP, GEL, GGP, GHS, GIP, GMD, GNF, GTQ, GYD
HKD, HNL, HTG, HUF, IDR, ILS, IMP, INR, IQD, ISK, JEP, JMD, JOD, JPY, KES, KGS, KHR, KMF, KRW, KWD, KYD, KZT, LAK, LBP, LKR, LSL, MAD, MDL, MGA, MKD, MMK, MNT, MOP, MRO, MUR, MVR, MWK, MXN, MYR, MZN, NAD, NGN, NIO, NOK, NPR, NZD
OMR, PAB, PEN, PGK, PHP, PKR, PLN, PYG, QAR, RON, RSD, RUB, RWF, SAR, SBD, SCR, SDG, SEK, SGD, SHP, SLE, SLL, SOS, SRD, SSP, STN, SVC, SZL, THB, TJS, TMT, TND, TOP, TRY, TTD, TWD, TZS, UAH, UGX, USD, UYU, UZS, VEF, VND, VUV, WST, XAF, XCD, XOF, XPF, YER, ZAR, XMW

## Unsupported currencies
If the currency you're looking for is not listed here, it's not supported. Don't worry, this list is subject to change, as more currencies will be added.
Due to international sanctions, it's not currently possible to use your Revolut card in Russia.

title: Card payment fees and limits
content_text:
## Fees
We don't charge a fee for using your Revolut card to make purchases in your base currency. However, we may charge fees if you’re using your card to withdraw cash or spend in another currency.
Read about spending in other currencies in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/can-i-pay-in-a-specific-currency or check out our withdrawal limits and fees FAQ/help/inactive-faqs/why-did-the-atm-charge-a-fee-for-my-cash-withdrawal for more info on cash withdrawals.

## Monthly spending limit
You can set a monthly limit that controls the amount that can be spent or withdrawn using a specific card. Transactions exceeding this limit will be declined. To set a card-specific limit:
- Go to 'Home' at the bottom menu
- Tap the card icon in the top-right corner
- Choose a card
- Tap 'Settings'
- Tap 'Spending limit'
- Toggle the feature on and set a limit
Follow these same steps to change or deactivate the monthly spending limit on a card.

## Weekly spending limits
Revolut sets rolling spending limits for some merchant categories, listed below. If you exceed these limits, you'll need to wait 7 days for the limit to reset.
Weekly merchant spending limits are as follows:
- Non-bank financial institutions (including ordering foreign cash, traveller’s cheques, and money orders): £10,000 or equivalent per 7 days
- Wire transfers: £25,000 or equivalent per 7 days
- Betting and gambling: £25,000 or equivalent per 7 days

## Contactless limit
Due to regulation, there is a £300 limit on contactless payments with your physical card, with a maximum of £100 per single transaction. These limits don't apply when you use Apple or Google Pay or unattended transport terminals, such as tapping to pay for the London Tube.
Every time you use chip and pin, the limit will reset. To reset the limit yourself, go to your card's 'Settings' and tap 'Reset' next to contactless payments.

title: Card verification and pre-authorisation
content_text:
## Card verification
When you add a new card to a payment service like PayPal, Google Pay, or Apple Pay, the payment processor may initiate a small transaction to verify your card details. These verification transactions usually charge nothing or a very small amount (a few cents).
They also revert back to your account once your card has been verified by the payment processor. You can check the reversal date in-app by tapping the transactionhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. The reversal date is in the 'Status' section. These transactions will appear as 'Card Verification' in your app.

## Pre-authorisation
A pre-authorization is a temporary hold on your card, often used by hotels or car rental companies to ensure sufficient funds. We can’t cancel a pre-authorisation hold from our side. It can only be lifted when the authorization expires or if the merchant releases the funds. 
If there's a duplicate transaction, contact the merchant to resolve it.

title: What is Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC)?
content_text:
Sometimes, an ATM or card terminal may ask you if you want to be charged in a different currency than the one you are trying to transact in. For example, an ATM in the UK may offer to give you cash in GBP but charge you in USD.
To make sure you always get the best rate, you should always choose to be charged in the local currency of the country you’re in.
If you’re in Thailand, choose Thai Baht, in Spain, Euros or in the United States, US Dollars. If you choose 'With conversion', the ATM provider can apply their own exchange rate.

## I rejected ATM currency conversion but it was still applied
If you reject the currency conversion at the time of making a payment but it was still applied, we can raise a dispute chargeback on your behalf. To submit a chargeback form, go to your transactions' historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list, choose the transaction you'd like to report and tap on 'Get help'.
ATM withdrawals and conversions are subject to fair-usage limits (see this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies to learn more about them) and weekend markups.

title: Which merchants are not supported by Revolut?
content_text:
All customers can spend, withdraw, and exchange foreign currencies during the week without additional fees, up to a certain amount based on your plan. After this, you'll be charged an exchange fee.
Age-restricted vendors, such as gambling establishments or adult content providers, are not available for customers aged 16–17.
All merchants have a 'merchant category code', and this is how we recognise what type of merchant they are. If you receive a notification saying 'This merchant isn't supported', it’s likely that they have an incorrect category code.

## More information
We apply weekly limits for card payments based on the merchant's registered business type. Read more about the limits in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/what-fees-will-i-be-charged-for-making-card-payments.
For more information related to transactions, read this article/help/card-payments-withdrawals/more-help-with-transactions-atms.

title: What is a virtual card?
content_text:
A virtual card is a digital payment card. Virtual cards don't cost anything to create and can be used instantly. We offer 2 types of virtual cards:

## Multi-use cards
- Can be used for any online card payments, or via Apple Pay or Google Pay
- Can't be used for cash withdrawals
To learn more about how to use your multi-use virtual card with Apple Pay, read these FAQs/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/paying-with-apple-or-google-pay.

## Single-use cards
- Card number regenerates after each use for extra security
- Can only be used for one-time online card payments
- Can't be used via Apple Pay or Google Pay
- Can't be used for cash withdrawals or pre-authorisations, including subscriptions, hotel reservations, and car rentals

## How to view your virtual card details
- From your home screen, tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Tap a virtual card, then tap 'Show details'
- Enter your passcode. You may also be asked for a one-time passcode
If you don’t use your single-use card within 24 hours of viewing the card details, the number will be regenerated.

## Refunds to virtual cards
You can always receive a refund, even if your virtual card is no longer active or the card number has changed.
If the merchant asks for a card number, you can let them know the card is no longer active and share an active Revolut card number instead.
If card payments with a virtual card are getting declined, check this article/help/card-payments-withdrawals/paying-by-card-issues/declined-card-payments/why-has-my-card-payment-been-declined.

title: My virtual card is not working
content_text:
If you're trying to use your virtual card, and it's not working, you may be trying to carry out an action that virtual cards don't support. See below for more details.

## What you can't do with a virtual card
- Pay at terminals that ask for a PIN
- Pay via contactless above the allowed limit, usually £50 or equivalent
- Withdraw cash
- You can't create  more than 4 cards per month

## What you can't do with a single-use virtual card
- Set up recurring card payments, subscriptions, or deposits (e.g. car rentals, hotels)
- Pay if we suspect the merchant will charge you again
- Withdraw cash
- Pay at any terminal that asks for a PIN
- Add it to Apple Pay or Google Pay
Single-use virtual cards are designed for one-time online transactions. Your single-use card will automatically generate new card details after one use, or after 24 hours. Single-use virtual cards are automatically terminated after making a payment.
You can still receive refunds to your Revolut account even if the single-use virtual card number or regular virtual card  is no longer active.

title: Problems with my card PIN
content_text:
A PIN code is a 4-digit number used to authorise transactions at ATMs and payment terminals. If needed, you can change your PIN at any ATM with the 'PIN services' feature. Remember, your 4-digit card PIN is different from your Revolut app's 6-digit passcode.

## Transaction declined
To see why your transaction was declined and the relevant troubleshooting steps, tap the transaction in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list.

## PIN attempts exceeded
If you've exceeded your PIN attempts, you can unblock your card in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top right corner
- Select the card you need to unblock
- Tap 'Settings'
- Select 'Unblock PIN/CVV'
If you still can't use your card after following these steps, get in touch with us.

title: Why was my card payment declined if I have enough funds?
content_text:
## Check if the card is linked to a currency
If the card is linked to a currency account with a low balance, the payment will be declined even if you have enough money across your other accounts. To choose what currency your card spends from, select the card → tap 'Settings' → 'Spend from'.

## Low balance in a specific currency account
Make sure you have enough money in the individual currency account you want to pay from (or that your card is linked to) to avoid transaction rejections — even if your total balance across all accounts is enough.

## Check if the card is linked to a Pocket
If the card is linked to a Pocket with a low balance, the payment will be declined even if you have enough money across other Pockets and accounts. To see your Pocket balance or check transaction details, tap on 'Accounts' below your balance and go to 'Pockets', or tap on the transactionhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list in-app.

## Check future payments
Check scheduled payments to make sure your account balance is enough.
If a subscription payment was declined, add money to your account, as the merchant will probably try to charge the amount again after some time. If not, please contact the merchant. For other subscription-related issues, read these FAQs on scheduled payments.

title: What is 3D Secure?
content_text:
## 3D Secure payment authorisation
Revolut cards support 3D Secure (3DS) — an extra layer of authorisation for online card payments that helps fight against fraud. If a merchant requests it, you'll receive a push notification asking you to confirm a payment you’re about to make to them.

## Issues with 3DS
If you didn't receive a push notification, you can open the Revolut mobile app and an authorisation prompt should appear there automatically. You must be logged into the mobile app to complete the authorisation.
We have no control over which merchants require you to go through the 3DS process. If 3DS didn't appear and your payment got declined, you can add the merchant as a 'trusted merchant' and try the payment again.

## What are trusted merchants?
If you trust a merchant, you can add them to your list of trusted merchants in-app. 3DS checks aren't required for trusted merchants, so any payments to/from them will be instantly approved.
If your transaction is declined due to a lack of 3DS, open the transaction and choose the option to trust that merchant. You'll also be notified in real-time when this option will be available.

## Where can I see my trusted merchants?
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner 
- Select 'Security'
- Choose 'Merchants and payments'
- Select Trusted merchants

title: Delayed (offline) card payments
content_text:
## If you see a delayed transaction in-app
- Check previous transactions: review your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to see if you previously made a payment to the same merchant. Sometimes, a merchant will retry a transaction that didn't work at a later time. If it's successful, you'll see both a reverted transaction and the successful transaction in-app
- Watch for duplicate charges: offline payments can sometimes cause duplicate transactions, and merchants may mistakenly charge you twice for a single transaction. To report unrecognised or incorrect charges, tap the payment in your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list and select 'Get help'

## If a delayed transaction causes your balance to go negative
Most likely, the negative balance is caused by the original transaction being in a pending state. The pending payment should be sent back to your account within a few hours or days and your balance should adjust.
You can check when the transaction is due to be sent back by tapping the transaction in your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list and checking its status.

title: Where can I use my Revolut card?
content_text:
You can use your physical and virtual Revolut card in any country where Visa or Mastercard are supported, except for the following countries and regions:
Belarus, Crimea, Cuba, Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), Iran, Libya, Myanmar, Russian Federation, Syria, Luhansk, Donetsk, Russian controlled parts of Zaporizhzhia and Kherson, Venezuela, and Zimbabwe

## Spending in another currency
If you can't select a currency in-app to exchange your money to, it means it's not currently supported for holding (keeping in your account). However, you might still be able to make purchases if it's supported for spending.
To see the list of currencies supported for spending, visit this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/what-currencies-are-supported-for-spending in our Help Centre.
To learn more about fees for card payments in a foreign currency, check this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/can-i-pay-in-a-specific-currency.

## Declined card payments
Virtual or disposable cards can't be used for ATM withdrawals.
If your card payment was declined in countries that are not mentioned in the list above, check this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/paying-by-card-issues/declined-card-payments/why-has-my-card-payment-been-declined.

title: Am I eligible for Click to Pay?
content_text:
## Supported countries
Click to Pay is currently available globally, excluding India and USA.

## Supported customers
Click to Pay is currently not available to customers below 18 years old.

## Supported cards
You can add any card to Click to Pay, excluding disposable cards.

title: How do I add cards to Click to Pay?
content_text:
## To add an individual card to Click to Pay:
- In your Revolut app, select a card, and tap 'Settings'
- Under 'Security', select 'Linked wallets'
- Next to 'Click to Pay', tap 'Add'
- Tap 'Add to Click to Pay'
For security reasons, we may require you to authenticate yourself, and then we'll add your card to Click to Pay.

## To add all new cards automatically to Click to Pay:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Security' and 'Linked wallets'
- Enable the toggle for 'Automatically add new cards to Click to Pay'
For security reasons, we may require you to authenticate yourself. 
Whenever we issue a new card, we'll automatically add it to Click to Pay.

title: How can I remove my card from Click to Pay?
content_text:
## To remove any card from Click to Pay:
- Log in and go to 'Cards'
- Select the card and tap 'Settings'
- Tap 'Linked wallets' and tap 'Click to Pay'
- Select 'Remove card from wallet'
You can also view and remove your linked wallets for all your cards by selecting 'Security' in your profile, and then selecting 'Linked wallets'.
If the card is terminated for any reason, we will automatically delete it from Click to Pay.

title: What is Click to Pay?
content_text:
## Click to Pay as a payment method
Click to Pay is a new method of payment at online stores. You can link your cards to Click to Pay using your email and mobile number, and then you don't have to type your card number every time you make a purchase at online merchants.

## Benefits of Click to Pay
- Make online payments at participating merchants using only your phone number or email address
- No need to remember any card details
- Supported by both Mastercard and Visa

## Using Click to Pay at online stores
- Log in using the same email and mobile number you used to link your cards to Click to Pay
- Enter the one-time passcode sent to your email or mobile number to access the linked cards
- Select the card you wish to use for payment
- Complete payment by following the on-screen instructions

title: Why is my card added to Click to Pay not shown at checkout?
content_text:
## Using different email or mobile
Your cards are linked to Click to Pay via email and mobile number. Ensure you use the same email and mobile at merchant checkout which you used to link your cards to Click to Pay.
If you added your card via the Revolut app, you can find this information in-app as follows:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner 
- Select 'Security' and 'Linked wallets'
- Tap the card icon to view the email and mobile details

title: Declined card payment isn't visible
content_text:
If your card is declined, but the declined transaction isn't showing in your app, it's probably because:
- the merchant is experiencing a technical disruption
- the merchant declined the transaction because of regional restrictions or security controls on their side
- the merchant couldn't accept your card because of the format (e.g. single-use or virtual card) or because it's a Revolut card
- your card is physically damaged (only affects physical card payments)

## Troubleshooting
Since we have no visibility of the decline reason on our side, we recommend reaching out to the merchant first.
If the merchant is unable to help, tap the transaction and select 'Troubleshoot my declined transaction' to answer a few questions about the declined transaction. We'll do our best to help you resolve the problem.
You can also fill out this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.declined-payment-is-not-visible-in-transactions-listrevolut://app/form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.declined-payment-is-not-visible-in-transactions-list to help us raise your case directly with the merchant.

## Card damage
If you're trying to pay with your physical card and the same method (chip and PIN, contactless, or swipe) continues to fail, check for damage and consider ordering a replacement card in-app. Fees may apply.

title: My card payment was declined by the security system
content_text:
Your transaction may be declined if our security system identifies potential fraud. To keep your money safe, we need you to review the payment by completing our in-app questionnaire.
You can access the questionnaire by going to the home page of your Revolut app and tapping the relevant tile. You can also access the questionnaire through the push notification and email sent to you.

## If your transaction was identified as a potential scam
The merchant will be blocked and all card payments to the merchant will be declined until you complete the in-app questionnaire. 
If you choose to unblock the merchant, you can then retry the payment.
If you choose to keep the merchant blocked, all card payments to the merchant and similar merchants will be blocked for the next 7 days. This block can’t be removed during this timeframe.

## If your card is frozen
All payments made with the frozen card will be declined until you complete the in-app questionnaire. 
If you mark that the payment was made by you, your card will be unfrozen — you can then retry the payment. If you don't recognise the payment, your card will be permanently terminated. You can order a new card to replace the terminated card.
If you accidentally terminate your card, you can reactivate it: Go to ‘Home’ on the bottom menu → tap the card icon in the top-right corner

title: What is the location-based security feature?
content_text:
Location-based security is a feature available on all plans that compares your mobile device's last known location with the location of the card terminal at the time of a payment. 
If the 2 locations don't match, the transaction may be declined to protect your money in the event your card has been stolen.

## How to activate this feature in the Revolut app
- From your home screen, go to Cards (tap the card icon in the top-right corner)
- Select a card
- Tap 'Settings'
- Under 'Security', enable the 'Location-based security' toggle
Location-based security feature only works for physical cards, not virtual cards.

title: My card payment was declined
content_text:
## Why can't I see a declined payment in the app?
- Because of restrictions set by the merchant
- Technical issues on the merchant's side
We can't see the declined payment from our side, but you can fill out this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.why-has-my-card-payment-been-declinedrevolut://app/form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.why-has-my-card-payment-been-declined so that we can reach out to the merchant and resolve the issue as soon as possible.
Alternatively, use this linkhttps://app.revolut.com/form/44f82e23-76fc-44db-859e-8bad0f51a56e?faqEntryPoint=question.why-has-my-card-payment-been-declined to try to identify the issue and find a solution for the declined withdrawal yourself.

## How do I check why a transaction was declined?
- From your home screen, open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list by tapping 'See all' below your most recent transactions
- Tap the declined payment to see the transaction's details
- The reason for the declined payment should be visible in the 'Status' section
- For more support, tap 'Get help'

## What do I do if my card isn't working?
If you're trying to pay with your physical card and the same method (chip and PIN, contactless, or swipe) fails multiple times, check your card for damage and consider ordering a replacement card in-app/help/cards/get-a-card. Fees may apply.

title: Why has my card payment been reverted?
content_text:
If you see a reverted payment, or a transaction state changes from pending to reverted, it means a merchant has decided to cancel a payment, or that they didn't claim the funds within the allocated time. You'll need to get in touch with the merchant to understand the reason for cancellation.
The payment cannot be re-attempted from our system. If you've already received the goods or services, then don't worry — they can still claim the money via a separate transaction or charge you with a delay a few days later.
If that happens, the payment will appear in your app as a delayed payment.  Learn more about delayed payments in this FAQ. If you’re expecting a refund and the transaction was reverted, there won't be a separate refund transaction as the original transaction was reverted.

title: Report a card payment as a scam or fraud
content_text:
If you believe you've been the victim of a scam or fraud, immediately end all communication with the fraudster or scammer.

## Report a card transaction as fraudulent
- Tap the transaction in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. If you have multiple to report, you'll be able to add them in the form later
- Tap 'Get help'
- Choose 'Report this payment as fraudulent'
- Follow the steps to raise a claim
- Select the option that best describes your situation
- After you submit the form, your card will be terminated
We'll get in touch with you as soon as possible regarding your claim. You can also track the progress of your claim in-app by tapping the transactionhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list you have reported. Learn more about the dispute process and timelines in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/refunds/how-does-the-chargeback-process-work.

title: Card security settings
content_text:
Managing your Revolut card security settings allows you to adjust your card functionality based on your needs. This applies to physical cards only and is not applicable to virtual cards. To get started:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Choose your card and select 'Settings'
- Scroll down to 'Security'

## Security settings available in Revolut
- Online transactions: lock online transactions for added security. In-person, Apple Pay, or Google Pay payments remain unaffected
- Location-based security: enable this feature to use your phone's GPS to prevent fraud. Ensure location sharing is allowed. For more details, check this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/paying-by-card-issues/declined-card-payments/payment-declined-due-to-geolocation-mismatch
- Swipe payments: disable magstripe and swipe payments to prevent fraud or cloning. This limits card use to chip and PIN or contactless
- ATM withdrawal: disable ATM withdrawals to block all cash withdrawal attempts
- Contactless payments: turn off contactless payments for your physical card. Apple Pay and Google Pay will still work
- Contactless payment limit: Revolut automatically sets a limit for contactless payments. You can reset this limit by using chip and PIN or tapping 'Reset limit' in your card settings

title: Freeze a card
content_text:
If you've lost your card, or you suspect it was compromised or notice suspicious transactions, freezing your card can protect your money and account. It prevents unauthorised use and allows us to look into the situation while knowing your account is secure.

## How to freeze a card
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu of the Revolut mobile app
- Tap the card icon in the top-right corner
- Choose the card you wish to freeze
- Tap 'Freeze'
While the card is frozen, any card payments attempted with this card will fail. To unfreeze the card, tap 'Unfreeze' by following the same steps you used to freeze it.

## Alternative steps
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select Security and 'Lost card'
- Select a card and tap 'Freeze'

## Revolut web app steps
- Go to the home screen
- Click the card icon in the top-right corner
- Choose the card you wish to freeze
- Click 'Freeze'
You can also block your card by calling our automated phone line on +44 20 3322 8352. If you need help blocking or unblocking a merchant, read this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/scheduled-payments/how-to-block-and-unblock-a-merchant in our Help Centre.

title: How to cancel a dispute
content_text:
You can only cancel a dispute if we haven't yet shared the case with the merchant.

## To check if you can cancel a dispute:
- From your home screen, tap 'See all' to open your transactions list
- Tap the transactionhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- 'Cancel' will appear at the top of the dispute tracker

title: Dispute a card payment
content_text:
## Contact the merchant
Most issues like cancellations, refunds, or wrong charges are best resolved directly with the merchant. It’s faster and usually required before submitting a dispute. 
If you still need to dispute the transaction, make sure you gather evidence of the merchant's response, as this can significantly help your case.

## How to dispute a card transaction
If your direct contact with the merchant is unsuccessful, follow these steps to dispute the card transaction in your Revolut app:
- Tap the transaction in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. If you have multiple to report, you'll be able to add them in the form later
- Tap 'Get help'
- Select an issue
- Complete and submit the form
We'll review your case and get back to you with updates. You can also track the progress of your dispute in-app by tapping the disputed transaction on the transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. Learn more about the disputes process and timeliness in this FAQ.

title: Dispute process and timelines
content_text:
After you submit a dispute, we'll review your case and decide whether there's sufficient evidence to initiate a chargeback. A chargeback is a formal process to reverse the transaction through the card network (e.g. Visa, Mastercard).
This process is regulated by strict rules and evidence requirements. 
Up to 85% of disputes are resolved within 5 weeks, but complex cases may take up to 12 weeks. Since the process is regulated, we can't speed this process up. To track your dispute status, tap the transactionhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list in your Revolut app.

## Initial assessment
- You fill out a form to initiate the dispute
- Once the transaction is completed, we review your dispute case usually within 8 days, and if there is sufficient evidence, we will proceed to the next stage
- If we need more information, we'll reach out to you
- After this, we'll come to an initial decision. Depending on the type of dispute, we may be obliged to wait a minimum period of 15 days before raising with the merchant, as per card network rules
If your dispute is successful, you'll be refunded by us or the merchant. If your dispute is declined or cancelled, you're not refunded and your case is closed. If we raise your case to the merchant, your case is still open and proceeds to the next step. We'll keep you updated every step of the way.

## Merchant review
If we decide to raise the dispute to the merchant, we reach out to them. The merchant has 30 days to respond for Visa or 45 days for Mastercard transactions.
- If the merchant agrees or doesn't respond, you'll receive a refund for the associated transaction(s)
- If the merchant disagrees, they'll provide supporting documentation and the case will proceed to the next step

## Second assessment
We receive and review the merchant's evidence within 5 days and reach a decision. 
- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund
- If the evidence is insufficient, the dispute process continues

## Final assessment before resolution
Once the case is raised to the merchant for a second time (if needed), they have 30 days to respond, after which we'll make a final decision.
- If the merchant agrees, you'll receive a refund
- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund
If it's unresolved and the merchant decides to keep disputing, we may escalate the case to Visa or Mastercard for review, which may take approximately 40 additional days.

title: Update on my chargeback case
content_text:
## Can I get an update on my case?
Not to worry, we'll update you during each stage of the dispute process and let you know the expected date for an outcome. We'll also provide an explanation when any decision has been reached via email and in-app.

## How to check the status of a dispute
In the Revolut mobile app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'See all' to open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap the disputed transaction
- View the dispute tracker

## I need to provide updated information to Revolut
If we need any additional information to support your case, we'll let you know in-app. We'll wait up to a maximum of 15 days to hear back from you. If you fail to reply within this timeframe, we'll proceed to cancel your case.

## Chargeback timeframes
How long you'll likely need to wait for an outcome depends on the case type:
- Fraud cases: within 10 days after you report the matter to us
- ATM cases: up to 45 days after you report the matter to us
- Disputes: up to 120 days after you report the matter to us

title: Problems with a refund
content_text:
## Refund is 'Waiting for funds'
A refund may take up to 5 days to be processed and credited to your account from the moment it's initiated by the merchant. The funds will appear in the Revolut app once the merchant completes the transaction on their side.
Once we're notified, the refund will appear in your upcoming payments in your Revolut app. You can view it at the top of your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. If the refund is 'Waiting for funds', it means that the merchant is processing it, and it should be completed by the due date.
To view the due date, tap the transaction in your 'Upcoming payments' or 'Transactions list'.

## Refund is declined or reverted
If your refund was declined or reverted, reach out to the merchant and request them to process the refund again.

## Refunds on inactive or single-use cards, or a closed account
These refunds will be processed as normal, even if you used an inactive or single-use card. If the merchant requests a card, let them know that the card is no longer active and provide them with active Revolut card details.

## I received less than expected
If your refund amount is less than expected, contact the merchant directly and clarify it with them. If they aren't responding, you can raise a dispute in-app. To learn how, see this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/refunds/dispute-a-card-transaction.

title: Revolut Chrome Extension
content_text:
Revolut Shopper is a free Google Chrome extension for fast and secure online shopping. With Revolut Shopper, you can create new virtual cards from your computer and autofill card details while checking out. 

## How to install Revolut Shopper
- Open Google Chrome on your laptop
- Click the three dots in the top-right
- Hover over 'Extensions' and tap 'Visit Chrome Web Store'
- Search for Revolut Shopper
- Tap 'Add to Chrome'
- Once the extension is installed, sign in to your Revolut account

## Autofill card details
- When you reach checkout, click on the card number input field
- After a few seconds, your list of cards will appear
- Choose a Revolut card
- Tap the field again to change the card

## Manage cards and copy card details
- Tap the Extensions puzzle icon at the top of your Chrome browser, or tap the Revolut icon if you have it pinned
- Hover over a card to copy the card number, reveal card details, and more options
- Tap 'Add new' next to 'Multi-use virtual' to create a new virtual card

title: How to withdraw a foreign currency
content_text:
You can withdraw money from almost any ATM that supports Visa or Mastercard worldwide. You must use your physical card or digital wallet version of a physical card. To withdraw the local currency, you must withdraw cash from a local ATM. All withdrawals are subject to fees and limits/help/inactive-faqs/why-did-the-atm-charge-a-fee-for-my-cash-withdrawal.
If the local currency is not supported for holding in the Revolut app, you can still withdraw money as long as it’s not in a restricted region/help/card-payments-withdrawals/help-with-a-transaction/which-countries-can-i-use-my-revolut-cards-in and is supported for spending/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/what-currencies-are-supported-for-spending.
Use our ATM map to locate an ATM near you.

## Tips to avoid fees
Although it’s not required, we recommend that you convert your funds to the local currency before you make the withdrawal. This will help avoid unexpected fees.
When the ATM asks, decline the option to convert the money. If you already have enough of the chosen currency in your Revolut account, there is no need for the ATM to make an exchange for you.
If you don't have enough of the chosen currency, we will exchange money from your other currency accounts. Our exchange rates are generally better than the ATM provider’s.

title: Limits for using my card abroad
content_text:
## Limits for exchanging
You can perform up to 100 exchanges within a 24-hour period. This applies to all currency, crypto, and commodity exchanges.

## Limits for withdrawing
You can withdraw up to £3,000 (or another currency equivalent) in other currencies within 24 hours.

## How to check my limits
You can check your fees and limit reset dates in 'Your plan' section. You can also preview upgrade options to reduce fees. Plan upgrades won’t increase your daily exchange or withdrawal limits.

title: Tips for spending in a foreign currency
content_text:
## Review your currency exchange allowance and fees
You will be charged a fee if you:
- Make an exchange or transaction that forces an exchange and, as a result, you exceed your monthly exchange allowance
- Make an exchange or transaction that forces an exchange on the weekend, when weekend exchange fees apply
For more information, read this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies.

## Review your account balances
If you don’t have enough of the transaction currency, other currency accounts may be charged and result in a fee. Learn more in this article/help/card-payments-withdrawals/spending-abroad-or-in-different-currencies/what-currency-will-i-be-charged-in.
You can open a currency account/help/wealth/exchanging-money/what-currencies-are-available/how-do-i-open-a-currency-account and exchange funds in advance of spending to preview fees. However, if you then have unused funds in that currency and wish to exchange it back to your base currency, exchange fees/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies may apply.

## Always pay in the local currency
If prompted, choose to pay in the local currency and reject any exchanges. If you have enough of the local currency, you shouldn't be charged any additional fees for exchanges by Revolut. If you do not have enough of the local currency, Revolut’s exchange rates and fees are typically better.

title: What currency will I be charged in?
content_text:
## How Revolut determines the currency you'll be charged in
We automatically convert your balance to the currency you're paying in, so there's no need to buy or exchange currency before your trip. We won't combine multiple currencies to complete a transaction. Read more about our exchange rates in this FAQ.
When you make a payment in-app, we'll check your currency balances in the below order (and complete the transaction in the first currency that has enough funds to complete the transaction):
- Transaction currency
- Your base currency (GBP)
- The active currency with the highest balance

## How to control the currency used for a transaction
You can assign a specific currency to a card by following these steps in-app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Select the card you want to use
- Tap 'Settings'
- Under 'Spend from', choose a currency
- Select 'All cash accounts' or any currency you want to be charged in. After that, card payments will be charged in the same order as your selected currencies

## Declined transactions
The transaction will be declined in the following scenarios:
- Insufficient funds in a single currency to cover the transaction amount plus fees
- Insufficient funds in the currency assigned to the card
- Normal decline reasons, unrelated to the currency. For more info, read this FAQ

title: Manage subscription payments to merchants
content_text:
## View and manage my subscriptions
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu 
- Tap the calendar icon in the top right corner
- Select a subscription to view its details
If you don't see your subscription when you tap the calendar icon, you can mark a payment as a subscription manually instead. To do so:
- Go to your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap on the payment to open its details
- Toggle 'Subscription payment'

## How to prevent a subscription from happening again
- Block a specific subscription payment to a merchant: only subscription payments of the specified amount of that merchant will be blocked
- Block the merchant: all payments to this merchant will be blocked

## How to block a subscription in-app
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner 
- Select 'Security'
- Choose 'Payments' and 'Blocked payments'
- Tap 'Add new' above 'Subscriptions' or 'Merchants' sections and select a previous payment from the merchant you want to block
To unblock the merchant, select the merchant you want to unblock and tap 'Unblock payments'. If you still don't see the subscription as unblocked, follow these steps:
- Go to your transaction list https://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap the payment to the merchant you want to unblock
- Tap 'Unblock payments'

## How to cancel a subscription
To cancel a subscription, you'll need to contact the merchant. Blocking a subscription or a merchant  in-app doesn't cancel the subscription.
Blocking future payments to a merchant on our platform prevents them from collecting subscription payments based on their merchant ID and specific amount. 
However, some may attempt to charge with a different merchant ID. To fully deactivate the subscription, contact the merchant directly and request cancellation.

title: Report a fraudulent ATM withdrawal
content_text:
If you don't recognise an ATM withdrawal, follow the steps below to secure your account and report the fraudulent transaction.

## Freeze or terminate your card
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the card icon in the top-right corner
- Select the card you want to freeze, and tap 'Freeze'. Alternatively, you can terminate it by going to 'Settings' and tapping 'Terminate card'

## Check if you made a withdrawal at the same ATM in the past
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Type 'ATM' in the search bar of your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. This may help you recognise it

## Report the fraudulent ATM withdrawal
If you're sure the ATM withdrawal wasn't made by you, report it by following these steps in the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Select the transaction from your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap 'Get help'
- Tap 'Report this payment as fraudulent'
We also recommend reporting this to the police.

title: When does my ATM withdrawal limit reset?
content_text:
The limit resets on a rolling monthly basis. This means that the reset date is not fixed to the last day of each month, but instead is based on the day your current price plan began.
Here’s an example:
If your plan started on the fifteenth of the month, your withdrawal limit will reset on the fifteenth of each following month.

## How to check your ATM withdrawal limit and reset date
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select Your plan
- Under the 'Your limits' section, you'll see ATM withdrawals
You can check the exact limit refresh date and the remaining allowance for the current rolling month.
For more information about the ATM withdrawal limits, check this FAQ.

title: Why is my ATM withdrawal pending?
content_text:
A cash withdrawal will be pending once the transaction is authorised. If you received the cash, it should be completed within a few days. If you didn't receive the cash, the transaction should automatically be reverted after the date specified in-app.
We can’t revert or complete a pending withdrawal any sooner than this date.

## How to check the reversal date
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'See all' to open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Select the withdrawal
- Check the 'Status' section to see the reversal date
If you have any issues with a withdrawal, you should always contact the ATM provider first.

title: Why was my ATM withdrawal declined?
content_text:
## If you can't see the withdrawal in-app
- Restrictions may have been applied by the external financial institution
- There may be technical issues on the external financial institution's side
Since we have no visibility of the declined withdrawal from our side, we ask that you fill out this formhttps://revolut.com/app-form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.why-was-my-cash-withdrawal-declined so we can get in touch with the financial institution and resolve the matter as soon as possible.
You can also try to troubleshoot your declined withdrawal by using this link.https://app.revolut.com/form/44f82e23-76fc-44db-859e-8bad0f51a56e?faqEntryPoint=question.why-was-my-cash-withdrawal-declined

## Check the withdrawal in your history
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'See all' to open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Select the transaction that was declined to view its details. You'll see the reason for the declined withdrawal in the 'Status' section
- For more help, tap 'Get help' 
If you've been charged incorrectly, you'll see support options after you tap 'Get help'.

## My cash withdrawal was reverted
If an ATM withdrawal transaction is reverted, it means that the transaction was cancelled and the funds were released back to your available balance immediately.
This can happen if the ATM provider decided to cancel a payment, or if they didn't claim the funds within their allocated time.
To find out why this may have happened, contact the ATM provider directly.

title: Activate a physical card
content_text:
Card activation is an extra security measure we've added to make sure you've received your new physical Revolut card as expected. To activate your card in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the card iconhttps://app.revolut.com/cards/overviewrevolut://app/cards in the top-right corner
- Choose the card you wish to activate
- Tap 'Activate now'
- Select 'I have my card'
Your card is now ready to use. You can also activate your Revolut card in person. Simply make a payment by inserting the card into a physical terminal and entering your PIN. For added security, contactless and online payments are disabled until the card is activated.

title: Reactivate a card
content_text:
## How to reactivate a terminated card
- Go to ‘Home’ on the bottom menu
- Tap the card icon in the top-right corner
- Tap 'Want to reactivate a terminated card?' at the bottom
- Choose a card to reactivate

## Frozen, blocked or physical card activation
If your card is active but frozen, you can unfreeze it in the app. Just tap the card in your card list, then select 'Unfreeze'.
If you received a notification that your card has been blocked, it will remain frozen until you review and confirm the flagged activity.
If you need to activate a new physical card, follow the steps in this FAQ/help/cards/card-activation/how-do-i-activate-a-card.

## Cards that are ineligible for reactivation
- Expired cards
- Single-use cards
- Cards terminated over 30 days ago
- Cards terminated by Revolut for security reasons
- You’ve reached the maximum number of active cards
- Cards that got replaced following a new card order

## After reactivation
If you reactivate your card, you may need to remove it and re-add it to Apple Pay or Google Pay before using it again.

title: I can't add my card to Apple Pay
content_text:
To successfully add your card to your Apple Pay:
- Make sure your account is fully verified. Cards cannot be added without completing the full verification of your account
- Make sure your account balance isn't negative
- Make sure you're using a supported card type. Only Visa and Mastercard are supported
- Make sure you don't have the same card digitalized in too many devices
- The card you're trying to add can't be a frozen or terminated

## Why is my Apple Pay card number different from Revolut's?
For security reasons, the last four digits shown for Apple Pay transactions on your statement are not the actual last four digits of your card.
Instead, they represent an encrypted number. This Apple-developed feature ensures that even if a payment terminal or database is breached, hackers would only get a one-time dynamic security code, which is useless to them. If you need to provide your card details to a merchant for a refund:
- Open your digital wallet on your iPhone
- Select your Revolut card 
- Tap the three dots in the top-right corner
- Choose 'Card Number' to view the masked number Apple uses for transactions

title: My card was stolen. What should I do?
content_text:
If your card was lost or stolen, replace it as soon as possible. To do this from your Revolut app:
- From 'Home', tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Select the card
- Tap 'Settings'
- Scroll down and tap 'Replace card'
- Choose the relevant replacement reason
- Follow the steps on-screen to terminate the lost card and order a new one
While waiting for your new card to arrive, you can add it to your Apple Pay/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/paying-with-apple-or-google-pay/setting-up-apple-pay or Google Pay/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/paying-with-apple-or-google-pay/setting-up-google-pay wallet to start using it immediately. If your Kids & Teens card was lost or stolen, read this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/revolut-junior/revolut-18-card-management.

title: The name on my card is incorrect
content_text:
The name on your card follows specific rules due to a 20-character limit (including spaces). If your full name exceeds this limit, names are shortened as follows:
- First name and last surname. Middle names will be shortened
- Middle names may be removed if necessary
- First name may be shortened to an initial if necessary
- If all else fails, only the last surname will be printed
- Special characters (e.g. umlauts, accents) are replaced with standard Latin equivalents. For example: Müller will appear as Muller

## Why can't my card have non-Latin characters?
Revolut cards only support Latin characters. Special characters, such as Ś/ę/Ą, are replaced with their closest Latin equivalent (S/e/A).
It's not possible to customise the name on your card. To change the name associated with your account, go to your profile and tap Personal details. If you wish to proceed with the name change, you'll need to provide a document proving that the updated name is your legal name.

title: Why is my Apple Pay card number different from my Revolut's?
content_text:
For security reasons, the last four digits shown for Apple Pay transactions on your statement are not the actual last four digits of your card. Instead, they represent an encrypted number. 
This Apple-developed feature ensures that even if a payment terminal or database is breached, hackers would only get a one-time dynamic security code, which is useless to them. If you need to provide your card details to a merchant for a refund:
- Open your digital wallet on your iPhone
- Select your Revolut card 
- Tap the three dots in the top-right corner
- Choose 'Card Number' to view the masked number Apple uses for transactions

title: My card has been swallowed by an ATM
content_text:
If your card is swallowed by an ATM terminal, freeze your card to prevent any unauthorised transactions. Then, in the app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Choose your card and tap 'Freeze'
Contact the ATM provider and ask for instructions on how to retrieve your card. Most ATMs should display the contact details on the machine.

## Help with a pending transaction
If you tried to withdraw funds, but the card was swallowed, and you see a pending transaction, it should be returned automatically.
You can check the date of the reversal in the 'Status' section of the payment. If the transaction is completed, select the payment from the transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list and tap 'Get help' to submit a chargeback request.

title: Why aren’t my contactless payments working?
content_text:
## Reset your card's contactless limit
You may have reached the contactless limit for a certain card. To reset it in the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards iconhttps://app.revolut.com/cards/overviewrevolut://app/cards in the top-right corner
- Select your card
- Tap 'Settings'
- Scroll down and make sure 'Contactless payments' are enabled
- If the 'Contactless limit' is reached, tap 'Reset'

## If resetting the limit doesn’t work
- Check if the card is activated: select the card in the cards section and tap 'Activate now', choose 'I have my card', or make a PIN purchase and then attempt the payment again
- If you used Apple Pay or Google Pay: re-add the card to your wallet or create a new virtual card, then retry the payment
- Check for card damage: if the physical card is damaged or worn out, it may not work. You can order a replacement by selecting the damaged card in the cards sectionhttps://app.revolut.com/cards/overviewrevolut://app/cards and tapping 'Replace card'

title: My single-use virtual card isn't working
content_text:
Single-use virtual cards are designed for one-time online transactions. Your single-use card will automatically generate new card details after one use, or after 24 hours.

## Your single-use virtual card won’t work if:
- The card is used for recurring card payments, subscriptions, or deposits (e.g. car rentals, hotels)
- We suspect the merchant will charge your card again
- You try to withdraw cash with the disposable virtual card
- The terminal asks for a PIN
- You try to add it to Apple Pay or Google Pay
Single-use virtual cards are automatically terminated after making a payment. You can still receive refunds to your Revolut account even if the single-use card number is no longer active.

## Card details verification
To protect your card details, we may ask you to confirm your login information to view your single-use virtual card details. We’ll either send you an authentication code via SMS or email, or ask you to confirm your information with Face ID or Touch ID. 
To switch to a different verification method, simply tap 'Choose another method'.

title: My physical card isn't working
content_text:
If your issue is related to a card payment, simply tap the failed transaction in your in-app transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to see the decline reason and find the relevant troubleshooting tips.

## My card is damaged
If your physical card is peeling, or you’re unable to make a payment with it, you can order a replacement (fees may apply). To order a new card in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Select the card and tap 'Settings'
- Scroll down and tap 'Replace card'
While you wait for your new card to arrive, you can add it to Apple Pay or Google Pay, and even make payments using your phone. To learn how, visit these FAQs/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/paying-with-apple-or-google-pay in our Help Centre. 

## My card isn't working with contactless
Make sure the contactless feature is enabled for your card in-app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Select the card and tap 'Settings'
- Scroll down and enable the 'Contactless payments' toggle
If the feature is enabled and the issue persists, check if the problem is being caused on the merchant’s side by trying to pay using another payment terminal.

## My card isn't working for chip and PIN payments
The reason why your card isn’t working should be visible in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. Some other reasons could be:
- PIN tries exceeded
- Card blocked due to location-based security
- Spending limit reached
If your card isn't working for ATM withdrawals, the issue may be with the ATM itself and possibly due to temporary or connectivity problems. You can try using a different machine by checking nearby ATMs with our in-app ATM locator.

title: What should I do if my card is expiring soon?
content_text:
Your card will work until midnight of the last day of the expiry month. If you’re using a physical card, we’ll send a notification 28 days prior to the expiry date to replace your expiring card free of charge using standard shipping.

## Replace your card
In the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Choose the card that’s expiring soon
- Tap 'Replace card'

title: Can I cancel or edit a card order?
content_text:
You can cancel or edit your card order within 1 hour of placing it.

## Cancel a card order
To cancel a card order in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Choose the card you wish to cancel
- Tap 'Edit or cancel'
- Select 'Cancel order' and 'Yes, cancel'
The card order fees will be returned to your account in 1 business day.

## Edit the delivery details of your card order
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top right corner
- Choose the card you wish to cancel
- Tap 'Edit or cancel'
- Next to 'Delivery address', tap 'Edit' and update your delivery address
After the 1 hour ordering window, you won't be able to edit or cancel the card order.

title: Can I choose between Mastercard and Visa?
content_text:
When you order a new card in the Revolut app, you'll be shown the applicable card brand (Mastercard or Visa).

## Available brands for physical cards
At the moment, you aren't able to choose the brand for physical cards. Physical card brands are assigned automatically based on availability regardless of the plan.

## Available brands for virtual cards
For virtual cards, you can order your preferred card brand in-app:
- Order a virtual Visa card
- Order a virtual Mastercard card
The option to choose a virtual card brand may not always be available, and isn't possible for single-use cards.

title: Maximum active cards
content_text:
The number of active cards you can have at any one time depends on the card type:
- Physical: 6 active cards (each account type can have up to 6 separate physical cards. For example, if you have a joint account, you can hold 6 physical cards for your main account and an additional 6 physical cards for the joint account)
- Virtual: 20 active cards
- Virtual single-use: 1 active card

## Limit on getting new cards
You can only create 4 new virtual cards a month, including single-use cards. After you exceed this limit, you'll need to wait 30 days before you can create a new virtual card, even if you haven't exceeded your active limit.
When you order a new physical card, your current card will remain active until the last business day of its expiration month. When you are ready to destroy the card, always cut through the magnetic strip and chip when disposing of old cards and deactivate the card in-app:
- Choose the card in your cards list
- Tap 'Settings'
- Scroll down and select 'Terminate card'

title: What are the fees for ordering a card?
content_text:
For physical card orders, a fee is charged based on your plan, card type, and delivery method. We'll show you all applicable fees in-app before you order. Virtual cards are available at no cost. Paid plan customers can opt to order a card from a lower-tier plan. 
- Card fees are a one-time charge for each additional card ordered
- There are no monthly charges for having a physical card
- Special edition cards are available to each plan, upgrading to a higher plan will not decrease the fee for these cards. These cards cannot be provided for free
- There are no special or exclusive benefits associated with special edition cards beyond any associated plan benefits

## Card fees per plan
- First Standard card: no fee for the card, but delivery fees apply
- First Plus, Premium, Metal, and Ultra card: free of charge, once per 12-month billing cycle. Includes one replacement non-Metal card, and additional Metal cards incur a fee
Additional cards
- Standard and Plus: GBP 4.99
- Premium and Metal: GBP 39.99
- Ultra: GBP 49.99
- Special edition: check in-app

## Delivery fees
Standard customers need to pay for delivery. Delivery fees are as follows:
- Local standard untracked: GBP 4.99
- International standard untracked: GBP 5.99
- Express tracked: GBP 16.99
Plus customers get standard delivery at no cost, but must pay for express delivery. Premium, Metal, and Ultra customers get complimentary express tracked delivery.

## Customisation fees
- Standard: check in-app (if available)
- Plus, Premium, Metal, and Ultra: free customisation (if available)
Replacement fees may apply. While extra cards always incur a cost, replacement cards may also be subject to additional fees depending on the circumstances.
To order a new physical card in-app, use this link.

title: Creating a virtual card
content_text:
## How to create a virtual card
In the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Tap 'Add new'
- Choose 'Debit', 'Joint', or 'Pro' at the top
- Select 'Virtual'
- Choose a colour
- Tap 'Get card for free' to instantly create your virtual card

## Ordering specific card brands
You can order a specific virtual card brand in the app or using these links:
- Visa virtual card
- Mastercard virtual card
Go to this FAQ/help/cards/get-a-card/how-to-use-virtual-cards to learn more about using a virtual card.

title: I ordered a card and it didn't arrive
content_text:
Your card order may take up to 5 business days to process before being shipped. Once your card is ready for shipping, you'll see an estimated delivery date in the cards section.

## If the estimated delivery date has passed
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top right corner
- Select the card you are expecting
- Tap 'Track delivery' to view the estimated delivery date
- If the delivery date has passed, select 'Re-order this card' to request a replacement
Make sure the delivery address is correct. You can replace the card for free. While you're waiting for the new card, the old card you've replaced can't be used any more, but you can create a virtual card for free and add it to a digital wallet to keep spending.

## If you need to reorder the card, confirm your address
Before placing your order, verify your address to avoid delivery issues. Common mistakes include incorrect postal code, missing door number, or a missing apartment number.
Tracking information may not be available immediately after shipping. Allow an extra business day for the details to update in the app.
- Standard and Plus plan card delivery doesn't include a tracking number, but we provide an estimated delivery date
- Premium, Metal, and Ultra plan customers can choose priority delivery and receive a tracking number once their card is dispatched

title: Card customisation
content_text:
When ordering a physical card in-app, any card with the word 'Customisable' above it can be customised. Once you finish your design, tap 'Learn more' to review the guidelines.
You'll receive a notification when your design is approved or declined. Virtual cards cannot be customised. Any applicable fees will be clearly displayed during checkout.

## Customisation guidelines
Certain card designs are prohibited and will be rejected. These include, but aren't limited to, designs that show:
- Profanity, offensive racial/prejudicial material
- Nudity or content of a sexual nature
- Political subject of any nature (flags, state symbols, etc)
- Copyrighted content
- Branded or trademarked products/services/logos including abbreviations, acronyms and symbols of any nature
- Non-latin alphabet symbols
- Depiction of drugs, alcohol, tobacco, firearms, weapons, military equipment
- Content which may be determined to be socially unacceptable or illegal
- Solicitations, including telephone numbers or services of any nature, URLs
- Depiction of or reproduction of any currency
- Religious content

## Card received without customisation
If you received your card with the incorrect design, terminate it and order a new one in-app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the card iconhttps://app.revolut.com/cards/overviewrevolut://app/cards in the top-right corner
- Select the card and tap 'Settings'
- Scroll down and select 'Terminate card'
- Once the card is terminated, you can order your new customised card
If you want to order a special edition or limited design card, read these FAQs to view the available options and ordering T&Cs. When adding your card to your Apple Wallet, Google Wallet, or similar, the customisation won't be visible. Only your physical card will display the design you selected.

## Card customisation rejected
If your card design is rejected, you'll see a 'Rejected' status in the cards section of the app under the ordered card. You'll also receive a push notification about the card status. 
To learn more, tap the rejected card. Below it, you'll see the message: 'Unfortunately, your card design does not follow our guidelines.' To proceed, tap 'Create new design'. On the next screen, you'll see the reasons for rejection, and you can choose between two options: 
- 'Create a new design': this will redirect you to the design area
- 'Cancel your order': if you do cancel, you'll receive an automatic refund, and the card will be removed from the cards section

title: How does the Clear Sky debit card support Ukraine?
content_text:
UK for UNHCR is an international organisation dedicated to protecting refugees.
The organisation will receive 100% of your donation after you order the Clear Sky debit card in your Revolut app, and it'll go directly to providing essential services and support to those in need. We'll also match donations up to £200,000.
UNHCR aims to help families affected by the war in Ukraine, including millions of displaced people inside Ukraine and refugees across the region, by providing essential aid (such as emergency cash assistance and shelter repairs).

title: Order a physical card
content_text:
## How to order a new card
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the card icon in the top right corner
- Tap 'Add new'
- Choose the account you wish to order a card for
- Tap 'Physical card'
- Swipe between cards to choose the card type and design
- Follow the prompts to complete your order
Each card type has a fixed card brand — Visa or Mastercard — which can't be changed. Learn more about choosing a card brand in this FAQ/help/cards/card-order/can-i-choose-between-visa-and-mastercard.

## Delivery fees
- Standard and Plus customers receive standard delivery, unless they upgrade to priority tracked delivery for an additional fee
- Premium, Metal and Ultra customers enjoy free express tracked delivery
If you're experiencing issues with your card delivery, such as the card not being delivered or delivery to the wrong address, read these FAQs for assistance.

## Fees when adding new cards
- Standard: customers get their first card at no cost, but there is a delivery fee
- Plus and Premium: first 2 cards are complimentary
- Metal: 2 complimentary cards (1 Metal design and 1 Premium, Plus, or Standard design)
- Ultra: 2 free cards (1 Ultra and 1 Premium, Plus, or Standard design)
However, the final cost of your card depends on the card type, customisation, and delivery. We'll always show you any applicable fees before you complete your order. To learn more about fees when ordering cards, read this FAQ/help/cards/card-order/fees-for-ordering-a-card.
For Plus, Premium, Metal, and Ultra subscribers, the cards allowance refreshes each 12-month billing cycle.

## Lost or compromised card
If a card was lost or stolen, read this FAQ/help/cards/card-issue/issues-with-a-card/i-found-someone-elses-revolut-card before ordering a new one and see if it covers your circumstances. If your card has been used fraudulently, freeze the card immediately in the Revolut app. To do so:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the card icon in the top right corner
- Tap your card and select 'Freeze'
Once your card is secured, you can proceed with ordering a new physical card through the app.

title: How do I get the Clear Sky debit card?
content_text:
The Clear Sky debit card is available in conjunction with our Donations feature in the UK and all EEA countries. Customers in Ukraine can get the card without making a donation.
Only one Clear Sky debit card can be ordered per Revolut account. Cards are available while stocks last and on a first-come, first-served basis.

## To unlock and order the card in-app:
- Go to ‘RevPoints’ on the bottom menu
- Tap ‘Donations’
- Make a donation of at least £5 to UK for UNHCR to unlock the card
- To order the card, go to ‘Home’ on the bottom menu
- Tap the card icon in the top-right corner and ‘+ Add new’ 
- Select ‘Physical card’ and ‘Clear Sky card’, and complete your order
Card fees and T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RevolutClearSky apply. 
Make sure you have the latest version of the Revolut app installed on your device.

title: What is a special edition card?
content_text:
Special edition cards are card designs which will be available for limited time only. There will be an additional fee to order a special edition card and a delivery fee may also be charged.
You can have up to 6 active physical cards. Any cards you may already own will continue to work as normal. If you can't find the special edition card you want to order:
- Make sure you have the latest version of the app installed
- Go to the Cards section by tapping the card icon in the top right
- Tap 'Add new'
- Choose 'Physical card'
- If the special edition card is linked to a charity, you'll need to donate a minimum amount for the card design to be available in the app

title: What card brands and types can I get?
content_text:
## Card brands
- We currently offer Visa and Mastercard cards to our customers. The specific card brand you receive will depend on the registered location of your account
- We don’t support American Express, UnionPay, PayPal, Cirrus, or Discover. Maestro cards have been discontinued

## Available card types
We only support debit cards.

title: Finding the card PIN
content_text:
In the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Select the card you want to view the PIN for, and tap 'Settings'
- Tap 'View PIN'
Virtual cards have no PIN and there's no possibility to add one.

title: Linking an existing card
content_text:
## Link an existing card in-app
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu and tap the cards icon in the top-right
- Tap 'Add new'
- Select 'Already have a Revolut card? Link it now'
- Enter the card number manually
- Press 'Link card' to complete the process
The card will now be linked to your account, and you'll be able to start using it.

## Potential difficulties when linking a card
While the linking process is usually seamless, there are some common difficulties you may encounter. Here’s how to troubleshoot them:
- Double-check the card number is correct and re-enter it if there was a mistake
- Check that the card was not previously terminated
- Make sure the card is not already linked to a Revolut account. Only two external physical cards can be linked to any Revolut account
- You can’t link a card from another entity. If the card belongs to a different Revolut entity (e.g. a Swiss customer trying to link a card from a European entity), linking the card will not be possible

title: Can I order my card to a different address?
content_text:
You can have your physical card delivered to a different address or country, but depending on where you choose to have it sent to, additional fees may apply.

## To order a physical card
In the Revolut app:
- From the home screen, tap the cards icon in the top-right corner 
- Tap 'Add new'
- Choose the card type at the top and select 'Physical'
- Follow the prompts to create your card
- When asked to confirm the delivery address, tap 'No, edit address'
- Select the country of delivery, find the address, or enter it manually
- Tap 'Continue' to confirm your card order

title: How do I use a Revolut virtual card?
content_text:
Our virtual card is a digital-only payment card you can create directly in the Revolut app, for no fee. Virtual cards are more secure than physical ones, as they can't be lost or stolen and use advanced security measures, like two-factor authentication, to prevent fraud.
With single-use virtual cards, your details are immediately deleted and refreshed after each purchase, so they can't be reused or skimmed by criminals.

## How do I pay with a virtual card?
- Pay in-store using Apple Pay or Google Pay: link your virtual card to Apple Pay or Google Pay, depending on your device. You can learn about adding your virtual card to your wallet in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/paying-with-apple-or-google-pay in our Help Centre
- Make online purchases: view your virtual card details in-app, such as the card number, expiry date (MM/YY), and CVV number
- Set spending limits: monthly spending limits allow you to control your budget and avoid overspending
- Set up recurring payments: automate charges and never miss a payment

## Can I add or withdraw money from a virtual card?
- It isn't possible to directly add funds to virtual cards. Instead, the funds will be automatically drawn from your available Revolut accounts
- Virtual cards can't be used for ATM withdrawals 

## Manage your virtual card
- Freeze/unfreeze: prevent unauthorised use and unfreeze your card when you're ready to use it again
- Customise: label your card to help you organise multiple cards for specific payments
