A virtual cardhttps://www.revolut.com/cards/virtual-card/?geo-redirect=true is ideal for subscriptions, digital wallets, and online shopping because you can save its details and use it again.
Single-use virtual cards are useful for one-time payments, especially if you have doubts about the merchant's security, or you prefer not to have your card details saved and reused. If you’re having issues with card payments, visit these FAQs/help/card-payments-withdrawals/card-payments for troubleshooting tips.

title: I received a card that I had marked as undelivered
content_text:
## What should I do?
If you previously marked a card as undelivered and ordered a replacement for that card instead, the original card (which you marked as undelivered) is no longer valid for use. Even if the card eventually arrives, it can't be used.
We recommend you to destroy the original card. You can check the expected delivery date of your new replacement card in the Cards section.https://app.revolut.com/cards/overviewrevolut://app/cards
While you wait for your replacement physical card to be delivered, you can link it to Google Pay or Apple Pay to start using it instantly. You can use Google Pay or Apple Pay to shop online, or wherever you see contactless symbol.

title: I found my lost card
content_text:
## Reactivate your card
If you’ve found your card and need help reactivating it, read this FAQ/help/cards/card-issue/i-ve-found-my-lost-card-how-can-i-re-link-it for troubleshooting methods. You can also check if the card can be reactivated by tapping the cards icon in the top-right corner and selecting the relevant card.

## I've found someone else's Revolut card
Let us know about the card you've found by contacting us via the in-app chat.

title: My card has been used fraudulently
content_text:
## Report a fraudulent transaction and replace your card
If you see that unauthorised charges have been made with your lost or stolen card, report them as fraudulent payments. To do this from your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Go to transactionshttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list 
- Tap the relevant fraudulent transaction and 'Get help'
- Select 'Report this payment as fraudulent' and 'Raise claim'
- Follow the prompts to enter the required information
- Tap 'Yes, order a card now' after submitting the claim and replace your card
Raising a claim will terminate your card so that it can no longer be used fraudulently. Once your claim is submitted, you don't need to take any further action. We'll get in touch about the progress of your claim. To temporarily freeze your card, follow the steps outlined in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/ping-other-card-security-settings/freezing-or-blocking-your-card.

title: Glossary of onboarding terms
content_text:
## Restricted investor
You are considered a restricted investor if in the past 12 months you've invested less than 10% of your net assets in high-risk investments, and in the next 12 months you intend to invest less than 10% of your net assets in high-risk investments.

## High-net-worth individual
You're considered a high-net-worth individual if you've earned more than £100,000 in the past 12 months, and/or have net assets of more than £250,000.

## High-risk investments
For the purpose of this statement, high-risk investments are: 
- Peer-to-peer (P2P) loans
- Investment-based crowdfunding
- Unlisted debt and equity (such as in companies not listed on an exchange like the London Stock Exchange)

## Net assets
For the purpose of categorising, 'net assets' refers to the total value of things like savings, investments. It doesn't include your home (primary residence), your pension (or any pension withdrawals), or any rights under qualifying contracts of insurance. 

## Income
You can include salary such as money/earnings from employment or self-employment, UK company dividends, savings and investments income profit from a property you own or rent out in the UK.  
Income doesn't include any one-off pension withdrawals.

title: Manage scheduled transfers
content_text:
## Where to view scheduled transfers
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu of your Revolut app
- Tap the calendar icon in the top-right corner
- View active and paused scheduled transfers

## Change a scheduled transfer
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu of your Revolut app
- Tap the calendar icon in the top-right corner
- Select an active scheduled transfer
- Tap 'Edit' and follow the prompts to change the amount, date, or frequency

## Cancel a scheduled transfer
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu of your Revolut app
- Tap the calendar icon in the top-right corner
- Select an active scheduled transfer
- Tap 'Pause' to deactivate the scheduled transfer, or tap 'Delete' at the bottom to permanently delete it
Once you delete a scheduled transfer, you won't be able to get it back. You’ll need to set it up all over again if you still need it.

## Difficulty with a scheduled transfer
To get specialised support for your transfer, open your 'Scheduled payments' or tap a completed transfer in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list.
If you need more help with a scheduled transfer, read this FAQ/help/future-payments-subscriptions/scheduled-and-recurring-transfers/more-help-with-a-scheduled-transfer.

title: More help with a scheduled transfer
content_text:
## Transfer hasn't arrived
Tap on any transfer you’ve sent in your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to view its estimated arrival date or track it.

## Cancel a transfer
You can cancel or change the date for a scheduled transfer anytime before it executes by tapping on it in your scheduled payments. You can find your scheduled payments by tapping the calendar icon in the Payments tab.
If the transfer was already sent, select the transfer in your transaction history for cancellation options.

## Transfer failed or reverted
To check the reason your transfer failed or was reverted, tap the transfer in your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list.

## Insufficient funds for a scheduled transfer
If there's not enough money in your account or assigned Pocket, the transfer will fail. We won't re-attempt the transfer, you'll need to top-up and then initiate a one-time transfer.

title: Schedule a transfer
content_text:
## How to schedule a transfer
- Go to Payments on the bottom menu
- Tap the calendar icon in the top-right corner
- Tap 'New'
- Choose 'Transfer'
- Choose a past recipient or tap 'New'
- Enter the amount and 'Continue'
- Set the date and frequency

## Fees
The fees for a scheduled transfer are the same as for one-time transfers. We'll always show any applicable fees before you set up a transfer.

## Issues with a scheduled transfer
To manage your scheduled transfer, open your scheduled payments (calendar icon in top-right corner of the Payments tab). Read more about managing scheduled transfers in this FAQ/help/future-payments-subscriptions/scheduled-and-recurring-transfers/manage-scheduled-transfers.
If you have a problem with your scheduled transfer, open your 'Scheduled payments' tab or select a completed scheduled transfer in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to get specialised support.
If you need more help with a scheduled transfer, read this FAQ/help/future-payments-subscriptions/scheduled-and-recurring-transfers/more-help-with-a-scheduled-transfer.

title: Cash withdrawal limits and fees
content_text:
Cash withdrawals are limited to £3,000 (or other currency equivalent) within 24 hours. The daily limit can't be increased.
You can make a certain amount of withdrawals each rolling month without withdrawal fees charged by Revolut. Check your withdrawal allowance status and reset date in the your plan section in-app.
If you convert money before you withdraw, or if we convert it for you at the time of the withdrawal, you're subject to currency exchange and weekend fees.

## Withdrawal limits and fees
If you exceed your withdrawal allowance, you'll be charged 2% of the withdrawal amount or a £1 fee (whichever is greater) for each transaction in each rolling month. Here are the withdrawal allowances by plan:
- Standard: £200 (or 5 withdrawals, whichever comes first)
- Plus: £200
- Premium: £400
- Metal: £800
- Ultra: £2,000
The ATM may charge its own third-party fees, which should be shown to you before you complete a withdrawal. Third-party fees apply to the withdrawal amount, which are collectively subject to Revolut fees. To check whether you were charged a fee, tap the 'ATM withdrawal' in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list.

## Exchange fees
Whether you convert money before you withdraw or we convert it for you at withdrawal time, you’re subject to currency exchange fees if you exceed your fair usage allowance, and weekend fees depending on plan. Your fair usage allowance includes all currency exchanges. Exchange fees per plan:
- Standard: 1% over £1,000 (each rolling month billing cycle)
- Plus: 0.5% over £3,000 (each rolling month billing cycle)
- Premium, Metal, and Ultra: no additional exchange fees
This limit resets on the date your current plan started. Check your limits in the your plan section in-app.

## Weekend fees
Depending on your plan, you may be charged a fee on all exchanges made on the weekend from 17:00 Friday to 18:00 Sunday, Eastern Time (ET). For Standard customers, this fee is 1%. For Plus, it is 0.5%. Premium, Metal, and Ultra customers don't have to pay an additional fee on weekends.

title: Getting started with Pet Insurance
content_text:
Revolut Pet Insurance protects you from unexpected expenses related to your pet and additional benefits, including:
- Vet fees and medical treatments
- Missing/lost pets, including advertising and rewards
- Pass away cover, including cremation and burial
- Holiday cancellation and cutting your trip short for a pet's unexpected illness or injury
- Public liability cover (dogs only)
- Unlimited 24/7 access to video consultations with experienced, UK-registered vets
- Complementary therapy includes hydrotherapy, osteopathy, massage, laser treatment, electrical muscle stimulation, acupuncture, or chiropractic treatment

## Key exclusions
- Any cats or dogs under 8 weeks of age
- Dog breeds considered to match the description of a prohibited type defined in the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1991/65/contents and any amendments to it or replacement legislation
- Pre-existing conditions
- Failure to take precautions, advice, and treatment recommended by a vet
- Any circumstances that were known or could reasonably have been anticipated at the time the policy or cover was purchased

## How to get a quote
You won't be able to purchase a new Revolut Pet Insurance policy from 10 December 2025. This change does not affect currently active policies — your existing cover remains valid. We'll send further communications soon to advise you on the next steps for maintaining your pet's cover.

title: How do I make a protection insurance claim?
content_text:
You can file a claim under purchase protection, refund protection, or event ticket cancellation insurance from your Revolut app: 
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select ‘Purchase’ and tap ‘Claim’
If you're having trouble accessing the app, you can also submit your claim on Qover's website directlyhttps://revolut.qover.com/.

## What do I need to submit a claim?
- Your Revolut policy number (found within the 'Purchase insurance' section of your app)
- All documents that support your claim

## What happens after I submit my claim?
You'll get an email from Qover confirming they have received your claim. If not, check your spam and contact details. They'll then review the claim and documents, check its validity, and calculate the eligible amount.
Qover might request additional claim information directly. Keep all relevant documents readily available. Qover will either approve your claim and pay the eligible amount, or explain why it was denied.

title: What's the excess for purchase insurance claims?
content_text:
There is no excess for purchase insurance claims. However, items with a purchase cost of less than £50 aren't covered for damage and theft protection or return protection.

## How to check your coverage
Revolut's purchase insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions about the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Purchase insurance' and tap 'Documents'

title: Submitting documents for a purchase insurance claim
content_text:
When you make a damage and theft protection, return protection, or event ticket cancellation protection claim with Qover, it's important to provide all the necessary documents to ensure your claim is assessed quickly and accurately.

## What documents are needed?
Refer to the policy's T&Cs for a full list of required documents, which will vary according to the nature of your claim and will be communicated with you by Qover. However, the documents may include the following:
- Police report for stolen belongings
- Repair invoice for damaged, repairable belongings
- Proof of inability to attend a covered event for ticket cancellation protection

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of the coverage. Revolut's purchase insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions about the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/ or read the T&Cs by following these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Purchase insurance' and tap 'Documents'

title: Is my mobile device covered by Revolut purchase protection?
content_text:
Mobile devices that are purchased in full with your Revolut Plus, Premium, Metal, or Ultra account may be covered under purchase protection in cases of accidental damage or theft (depreciation applies), up to 12 months after purchase. Read more about purchase protection cover on this pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/everyday-protection-insurance/what-is-not-covered/when-does-the-purchase-protection-not-cover-me?geo-redirect=true. 
In addition to the coverage requirements and conditions under purchase protection, only one incident with a mobile phone may be covered within any 12-month period. This 12-month period starts from the date you previously filed a mobile phone claim.

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of coverage. Revolut's everyday protection insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select ‘Purchase’ and tap 'Documents'

title: Getting started with purchase insurance
content_text:
Revolut purchase insurance is a benefit of Plus, Premium, Metal, and Ultra plans. This insurance includes damage and theft protection, return protection, and event ticket cancellation protection.
Not every incident is covered under purchase insurance. Make sure your item or event fits the criteria outlined in the T&Cs to ensure it’s covered.

## What’s covered under purchase insurance
Damage and theft protection offers cover for repair or replacement costs if an item bought with your Revolut account is stolen or accidentally damaged. More details can be found in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/everyday-protection-insurance/what-is-not-covered/when-does-the-purchase-protection-not-cover-me?geo-redirect=true.
Return protection covers you when you want to return an item bought with your Revolut account and the retailer doesn't accept it. To be eligible, the item must be unused and purchased within the last 90 days. More details can be found in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/everyday-protection-insurance/what-is-not-covered/when-does-the-refund-protection-not-cover-me?geo-redirect=true.
Event ticket cancellation covers you if you can't attend an event due to specific unforeseen circumstances, as outlined in the T&Cs. Read more in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/everyday-protection-insurance/what-is-not-covered/when-does-the-ticket-cancellation-protection-not-cover-me?geo-redirect=true.

## What are the conditions and limitations?
- Cover is only for new items purchased from authorised retailers, not private sellers
- Cover excludes pre-owned or refurbished items
- Items must be paid for in full using your Revolut account. Items bought through instalment plans, or a non-Revolut account, aren't covered
- Mobile phone coverage is limited to one claim per year
- Eligible purchases made with your Revolut account are automatically covered, according to the T&Cs. You don't need to declare purchases
Purchase insurance doesn't cover transportation disruptions. Items that are lost or not delivered, or broken or defective when they arrive, aren't covered.

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of the coverage. Revolut's purchase insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions about the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/ or read the T&Cs by following these steps:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Purchase insurance' and tap 'Get help'

title: What's not covered under purchase insurance?
content_text:
Purchase insurance doesn't cover all incidents. Read the Qover T&Cs to confirm if your item or event meets the coverage criteria. It's important to understand the specific exclusions explained in each section.
Revolut's purchase insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions about the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Purchase insurance' and tap 'Documents'

title: What items are eligible for purchase insurance?
content_text:
Purchase insurance doesn't cover all items that you purchase with your Plus, Premium, Metal, or Ultra account.

## Examples of items excluded from cover
- Precious stones
- Rare and precious coins or stamps
- One-of-a-kind items, including antiques, artwork, and furs
- Cash or its equivalents (including travellers cheques)
- Stocks, bonds, coupons, securities of all species, and equivalent papers
- Tickets
- Services
- Books
- Animals and plants
- Consumable and perishable goods
- Food and beverages
- Healthcare items
- Rebuilt and refurbished items
- Closing down sale items
- Vehicles and their parts
- Land and buildings
- Items permanently affixed to a home, office, or vehicles
- Digital data to view or download online (including files, music, films, photos)
- Goods purchased to be sold onwards or used for professional purposes
- Weapons, illegal drugs, counterfeit goods, and other goods subject to customs confiscation
- Purchases on peer-to-peer sites unless from a commercial seller

## How to check your coverage
Revolut's purchase insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions about the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Purchase insurance' and tap 'Documents'

title: Getting an update on an everyday protection claim
content_text:
Revolut doesn't have access to your claim and can't confirm your eligibility for cover. You’ll need to contact Qover directly to check up on an existing claim.

## If you have an ongoing claim with Qover
Contact Qover using this link,https://revolut.qover.com/ or find their claims contact details by following these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select ‘Purchase’ and tap 'Claim'

title: Who provides Revolut's everyday protection insurance?
content_text:
Revolut is not an insurer, but we currently work with Qover SA (“Qover”) to provide purchase, refund, and cancellation insurance for eligible paid plan customers. 
Qover arranges and administers the policy for its co-insurers, Chubb European Group SE and/or Wakam, depending on your country of residence. If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select ‘Purchase’ and tap 'Documents'

title: Purchase protection coverage
content_text:
Purchase protection may offer coverage for the cost of repair or replacement of eligible items you buy with your Revolut Plus, Premium, Metal, or Ultra account. If your purchased item is stolen or accidentally damaged beyond repair within 365 days of purchase (or delivery, whichever is later). 
Purchase protection doesn't cover undelivered or damaged-on-arrival items. In other words, to be‌ eligible for a claim, you must have received the item. 

## Eligibility requirements
You must be a Revolut Plus, Premium, Metal, or Ultra plan holder and have held the paid plan continuously from the time you purchased the item until you submit a claim. To be eligible for purchase protection insurance, an item must also be:
- Paid for in full with an eligible Revolut plan
- For personal use
- Purchased from a retailer (not privately) in your Revolut account’s registered country or within Europe
- Meet European Conformity safety and environmental certification requirements (if applicable)
Purchase protection only applies to items costing over £50 that are not specifically excluded in the terms and conditions. Other criteria may also apply. Read more about excluded items on this pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/everyday-protection-insurance/what-is-covered/which-items-are-eligible-for-purchase-and-refund-protection-insurance?geo-redirect=true. 

## How much can I claim?
The most you can claim in total is the item's original purchase price, up to a limit that depends on the plan you hold:
- £1,000 for Plus customers
- £2,500 for Premium customers
- £10,000 for Metal or Ultra customers
If you file a claim more than 90 days after purchasing the item, depreciation will be applied by the insurance provider.

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of coverage. Revolut's everyday protection insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select ‘Purchase’ and tap 'Documents'

title: Refund protection coverage
content_text:
If a retailer denies a return within 90 days of purchase, items bought with Revolut Plus, Premium, Metal, or Ultra may be eligible for refund protection. This benefit could cover the item's purchase price and applies to eligible items only.
Only items may be covered by refund protection, services of any kind are not covered. Upon request from your claim handler, you might be required to send the item to Qover's offices in Belgium.

## Eligibility requirements
You must be a Revolut Plus, Premium, Metal, or Ultra plan holder and have held the paid plan continuously from the time you purchased the item until you submit a claim. To be eligible for Refund Protection Insurance, an item must also be:
- Paid for in full with Revolut
- For personal use
- Purchased from a retailer (not privately) in your Revolut account’s registered country or within Europe
- Brand new, suitable for resale, without any flaws, and fully functional
- Meet CE safety and environmental certification requirements (if applicable)
Refund protection applies to items costing over £50 that are not specifically excluded in the terms and conditions. Other criteria may also apply. 

## How much can I claim?
Individual item coverage is capped at the item's purchase price or £300, whichever is lower. This limit includes any discounts, vouchers, or credits used for the purchase. 
Within a rolling 12-month period, the maximum coverage provided by the Refund Protection benefit is £600. This period begins from the date of your Revolut account upgrade.

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of coverage. Revolut's everyday protection insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select ‘Purchase’ and tap 'Documents'

title: Event ticket cancellation protection coverage
content_text:
With event ticket cancellation protection, you could get reimbursed for event tickets purchased using your Revolut Plus, Premium, Metal, or Ultra account. Reimbursement is possible if you can’t attend due to unforeseen, covered circumstances, which include (but aren't limited to): 
- Death, serious bodily injury or illness of you, an event companion, close relative, or childcare provider
- Traffic accidents or carrier delays while traveling to the event
- Jury duty, court orders, or subpoenas on the event date which are issued to you after you bought the tickets
Ultra plan holders are also covered for event ticket cancellations for any reason under Ultra trip and event cancellation protection. Read more about the cover on this pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/insurance/trip-event-cancellation-protection/what-does-the-trip-event-cancellation-protection-cover-me-for?geo-redirect=true. 

## Eligibility requirements for coverage
To qualify for event ticket cancellation protection, you must be a Revolut Plus, Premium, Metal, or Ultra subscriber, maintaining this paid plan from the ticket purchase until claim submission. Eligible tickets must be newly purchased tickets paid for before the event with no prior ownership and be: 
- Paid for in full with an eligible Revolut plan
- For personal use
- Purchased from a retailer (not privately) for an event that takes place in your Revolut account’s registered country or within Europe
Ticket cancellation protection may cover pre-purchased tickets for events like cultural, sporting, leisure activities, shows, or amusement park visits at specific venues. It excludes transportation tickets such as flight or train tickets.

## How much can I claim?
The most you can claim is the lower of the face value of the ticket item or up to £1,000 in any 12-month period.

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of coverage. Revolut's everyday protection insurance is provided through Qover, not Revolut itself. Qover is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. 
If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Qover through this pagehttps://revolut.qover.com/. To read the T&Cs, follow these steps:
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'Insurance'
- Then, select ‘Purchase’ and tap 'Documents'

title: Getting started with travel insurance
content_text:
Travel insurance is included as a benefit with our Metal and Ultra plans. It's automatically activated when you sign up for Metal or Ultra — you don't need to do anything else to activate it.
If you and your trip meet the eligibility requirements, you'll automatically be covered.
Have a trip coming up? Check whether it might be covered with this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.do-i-need-to-tell-you-when-i-go-away-on-holidayrevolut://app/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.do-i-need-to-tell-you-when-i-go-away-on-holiday. 

## Eligibility and people covered
- Trips must be paid with your Metal or Ultra Revolut account. See more in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/travel-insurance/do-i-have-to-pay-for-my-trip-using-my-revolut-account/?geo-redirect=true
- Your Metal or Ultra plan must be active at the time of booking, and remain active until the trip ends
- You must reside in the UK, with your primary address matching your Revolut account
- Your trip must begin and end at the address your Revolut account is registered to
- You and other beneficiaries must meet the eligibility criteria outlined in this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/what-is-travel-insurance-am-i-eligible/am-i-eligible-for-revolut-travel-insurance/?geo-redirect=true
Moving home, or moving abroad, can change your eligibility for cover. Insurance availability varies by country, make sure to update your Revolut profile as soon as you move so you can see exactly what’s available in your new home. See how moving affects your policy in this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/travel-insurance/question-does-moving-home-impact-my-trav/?geo-redirect=true.

## Regions covered 
- Worldwide, except for travel to countries or areas where the government's Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) advises against 'all travel' or 'all but essential' travel
- Trips in the UK, more than 75 miles away from your home, that include an overnight stay
Read more about trips that are covered in this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/before-going-abroad/what-is-the-maximum-trip-duration-to-get-covered-by-the-travel-insurance/?geo-redirect=true. 
Learn more about what’s covered by travel insurance on this help pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/travel-insurance/travel-insurance-coverage/?geo-redirect=true. 

## How to check your policy
In the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Travel insurance' and tap 'Get help'

title: What is the excess for travel insurance claims?
content_text:
Depending on the benefit you claim under, an excess may be deducted from the claim amount. This means you'll receive the claim amount minus the excess amount. For some types of claims, this excess doesn’t apply (for example, for overseas emergency medical expense claims, an excess doesn’t apply).
It's important to check your coverage to understand if an excess applies to your specific claim and will be deducted from the claim amount.
Remember, Revolut doesn't have access to your claim, so you’ll need to contact the travel insurance provider directly if you have any doubts regarding a payment received from a claim. To find out more about how to contact the provider, read this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/how-to-make-a-travel-insurance-claim/how-do-i-get-an-update-on-my-existing-claim/?geo-redirect=true. 

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions about the cover, contact Chubb. Or read the T&Cs by following these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Travel insurance' and tap 'Get help'

title: Trips covered by travel insurance
content_text:
A trip means a holiday or journey which starts when you leave your country of residence and ends when you return. A trip doesn't include travel to and from your normal place of work.
Revolut travel insurance includes coverage for pre-booked personal and business international and domestic trips that are at least 75 miles away from home. Certain coverage aspects, such as medical expenses, do not apply when travelling within the UK.
Metal plans cover a maximum of 30 days per trip, whereas Ultra plans cover for maximum of 90 days. If your trip exceeds these limits, cover will apply for the first 30 (Metal) or 90 days (Ultra). It is not possible to extend coverage beyond these maximum durations.

## Important reminder
Remember, you must have an active Revolut Metal or Ultra plan at the time of booking and use this account to pay for at least 75% of your primary transport and accommodation expenses in order for a trip to be covered. Read more on this page.https://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/travel-insurance/do-i-have-to-pay-for-my-trip-using-my-revolut-account/?geo-redirect=true
Have a trip coming up? Check whether it might be covered with this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.what-is-the-maximum-trip-duration-to-get-covered-by-the-travel-insurance. 

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps:  
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select 'Travel' and tap 'Documents'

title: Why is my travel insurance coverage ending?
content_text:
## Understanding the Revolut travel insurance age limit
Revolut travel insurance has an eligibility age limit of 76 years old.
Your coverage will automatically end on your 76th birthday. After that date, you won't be able to access the travel insurance section in your app, and the benefit will no longer be included with any future renewal of your paid plan.
If you plan to travel after you turn 76, you'll need to arrange alternative travel insurance for your trip.

## What happens to my existing claims?
Any claims you have already made will not be affected by the expiration of your eligibility. These claims will continue to be processed as usual according to the terms of your policy at the time of the claim.

## How can I get help or ask questions?
If you have any questions about a claim you've already submitted, or the claims process itself, contact Chubb's claims team at +44 352 123 9483. They are available from Monday to Friday, 8am to 4pm GMT.

title: Submitting documents for a travel insurance claim
content_text:
To make sure your claim is assessed quickly and accurately, you'll need to provide all the necessary documents.

## What documents are needed?
The exact documents will vary depending on your claim type, but may include:
- Medical documents: receipts, doctor’s notes, or proof of treatment
- Flight delay or cancellation proof: confirmation from airlines or proof of expenses incurred
- Evidence of lost or stolen items: police reports, proof of purchase, or photos of the items

## How to upload documents
You'll need to upload your documents to Chubb's online claims portalhttps://intake.sedgwick.com/u/ChubbTravel/RevolutTravelClaims so that Chubb can evaluate your claim. Missing or incorrect information could lead to delays or affect the outcome of your claim.
If you’ve already submitted a claim and Chubb’s claims team asks for additional or corrected documents, or you'd like to add supporting evidence to your claim, you can do so by emailing them at revolutclaims@ie.sedgwick.com.

## Stay alert for communication about your claim
Depending on the type of claim, Chubb may ask for specific documents through their claims portal or via email. 
Stay alert for these requests and respond quickly so your claim isn't delayed.
Find out how to speak with Chubb regarding an existing claim in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/how-to-make-a-travel-insurance-claim/how-do-i-get-an-update-on-my-existing-claim/?geo-redirect=true.

title: How can I view my travel insurance policy documents?
content_text:
To view or download your policy documents, follow these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Travel insurance' and tap 'Documents'

title: How do I get an update on my existing claim?
content_text:
We don't have access to your claim and can't confirm your eligibility for cover, so you’ll need to contact the provider directly to check up on an existing claim.

## Insurance support in the Revolut app
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'

## If you have an ongoing claim with Chubb
You can contact their customer support via:

- Phone: +44 (0)203 964 3004 (9:00–17:00, Mon to Fri)

title: How do I make a travel insurance claim?
content_text:
For medical and other emergencies abroad, the Chubb assistance helpline is open 24/7 at +44 (0) 203 964 3005. 

## Submit a claim for non-emergency cases
- Online: submit your claim on this websitehttps://intake.sedgwick.com/u/ChubbTravel/RevolutTravelClaims
- Phone: call +44 (0) 203 964 3004 and have your policy number ready. Lines are open 9am to 5pm, Monday to Friday 
- Email: revolutclaims@ie.sedgwick.com 
- Post: Revolut Travel Insurance Claims Team, Chubb European Group SE, PO BOX 1086, Belfast BT1 9ES

title: Emergency medical assistance abroad
content_text:
If you need urgent medical assistance while abroad due to a serious illness or injury, the Chubb Assistance Helpline is available 24/7 at +44 (0) 203 964 3005. To ensure your call connects, we recommend enabling international roaming on your phone.
If you or another insured traveller is admitted to the hospital as an inpatient for at least one night, you or someone on your behalf must contact the Chubb Assistance Helpline as soon as possible.
For non-urgent medical concerns, make sure to save all receipts, documents, and details to file a claim online. You can find out how to submit a claim in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/how-to-make-a-travel-insurance-claim/making-a-travel-insurance-claim/?geo-redirect=true. For more information about medical expense coverage while abroad, refer to this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/what-am-i-covered-for/travel-insurance-features/does-my-insurance-cover-me-for-medical-expenses-abroad/?geo-redirect=true.

## Finding your insurance number
You may need your travel insurance policy number when calling the Chubb Assistance Helpline. To find this number in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Travel insurance'

title: Cover for car hire excess
content_text:
Car hire excess insurance is included with Metal and Ultra plans.
This insurance covers the excess or damage liability fee for stolen or damaged rental cars when travelling, up to £2,000. It doesn't replace vehicle insurance.

## Eligibility criteria
Your car rental is protected if it’s part of a trip that qualifies for cover. It can’t be a standalone rental and must be tied to your insured travel. Learn more about which trips are eligible in this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/before-going-abroad/what-is-the-maximum-trip-duration-to-get-covered-by-the-travel-insurance/?geo-redirect=true. To stay protected, you’ll also need to meet the following criteria:
- Your name must be on the rental agreement, as well as your signature
- You must take reasonable care to protect the rental car and avoid an accident or collision which may cause damage
- You must have purchased at least the most basic collision damage waiver (CDW) coverage offered by the rental company
- Your Metal or Ultra plan must be active before signing the rental car agreement
- If the rental car is your primary method of transport to your destination, you must pay with your Revolut account. If it's not your main method of transport, you don't need to pay with Revolut

## Key exclusions
If you're eligible for cover, you can raise a claim directly with the insurance provider. Learn more in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/how-to-make-a-travel-insurance-claim/making-a-travel-insurance-claim/?geo-redirect=true. Your cover may not apply if:
- You violate the rental car agreement, including if someone who isn’t named as a driver on the agreement drives the hired car
- You've leased or rented a car for 31 consecutive days or longer
- You accept liability, make a payment, or agree to make a payment without written consent from the insurer
- There's mechanical breakdown or wear and tear of a rental car
- You rent a vehicle type that's excluded from cover

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions about the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Travel insurance' and tap 'Get help'

title: Cover for delayed trips
content_text:
## Delayed Trips
If your trip is delayed due to a reason covered by the policy, you may be entitled to compensation for expenses incurred.
- Metal plan holders can claim for delays of eight hours or more
- Ultra plan holders can claim for delays of four hours or more
You can get up to a maximum of £500 to £100 per hour starting after the minimum delay time (if you have expenses and can provide receipts).

## Missed departure
If you miss your departure for a trip abroad due to a reason covered by the policy, you may be covered for your travel and any extra accommodation costs needed to get to your destination. This cover is up to £200 per night, per insured person, for up to five nights. 
This must be of the same standard as you had originally bought.

## Key exclusions
If you're eligible for cover, you can raise a claim directly with the insurance provider. Learn more in this FAQ/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/how-to-make-a-travel-insurance-claim/making-a-travel-insurance-claim. Your cover may not apply if:
- You can recover the costs from any other source (including if you are refunded with a voucher)
- You didn't check in before the departure time shown on your travel itinerary
- You knew there will be a strike before booking your trip 
- You travel against government advice

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps:  
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select 'Travel' and tap 'Documents'

title: Cover for damaged, lost, or delayed baggage
content_text:
## Damaged, lost, or stolen luggage
Revolut travel insurance provides coverage for damaged, lost, or stolen baggage up to £1,000 in repair or replacement costs. Individual items are covered up to a maximum of £250, but this amount may be reduced based on the item's age or lack of an original receipt. 
Lost, stolen, or damaged travel documents (such as your passport, Visa, or driving licence) may be covered up to a maximum benefit of £500. 
This coverage is subject to the policy's terms and conditions and doesn’t apply to items left unattended in vehicles or public spaces. In the event of theft or loss of belongings or documents, a report must be filed with the police (and hotel management, if applicable) within 24 hours of discovery.

## Delayed luggage
If your baggage is delayed for a minimum of 4 hours or more, you can claim up to £400 per insured traveller. This benefit doesn’t apply if your baggage is delayed on your way home.
If you're eligible for cover, you can raise a claim directly with the insurance provider. Learn more in this FAQ/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/how-to-make-a-travel-insurance-claim/making-a-travel-insurance-claim. 

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps:  
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select 'Travel' and tap 'Documents'

title: Cover for medical expenses abroad
content_text:
## Emergency medical assistance
Revolut travel insurance covers medically necessary costs up to the maximum benefit of £10,000,000 for emergency medical care (including emergency transportation and medical repatriation) and up to £300 for emergency dental care overseas.
Cover only applies if you're travelling outside of your home country and you have a sudden, unexpected illness or injury that could cause serious harm if it isn't treated before going home, or you need emergency dental treatment to relieve pain.
In case of a medical emergency, you should contact Chubb Assistance immediately on 0044 203 964 3005. Their customer support is available 24/7, 365 days a year.

## Key exclusions
- Any claims if your doctor has advised you not to travel
- Any medical treatment abroad if that was the reason for your trip
- Any surgery, medical, or preventative treatment, which can be delayed until you return to your home country
- Any claims caused by pre-existing medical conditions
- For Metal customers, if your trip exceeds the first consecutive 30-day limit, we’ll provide cover up until the thirtieth day
- For Ultra customers, if your trip exceeds the first consecutive 90-day limit, we'll provide cover up until the ninetieth day
If you're eligible for cover, you can raise a claim directly with the insurance provider. Learn more in this FAQ/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/how-to-make-a-travel-insurance-claim/making-a-travel-insurance-claim. 

## Check your full policy in-app
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps:  
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Travel insurance' and tap 'Documents'

title: Cover for sports and activities
content_text:
Revolut travel insurance offers cover if you (or your insured travelling companions) are injured while participating in an activity or sport during your trip.  
Not all activities are covered, and some activities have specific requirements to ensure you are covered.

## Examples of covered activities
- Canoeing, kayaking, sailing, or yachting — as long as you travel less than 12 nautical miles
- Cycling, not BMX or mountain biking
- Trekking, hiking, or hill walking up to max 1,000 metres above sea level and once guides or ropes aren't needed
- Motorcycling up to 125cc. You must wear a crash helmet if you’re in control of the bike and have a full UK motorcycle licence
- Safari — only professionally organised camera safaris
- Skiing or snowboarding. You must be accompanied by a qualified guide if you go off-piste
If the activity you want to participate in isn't listed in your policy wording or is excluded, you won't be covered if you’re injured while doing the activity.

## Key exclusions
- The activity is the main reason for your trip, except for winter sports activities
- You don't follow all local laws, regulations, and guidelines
- You don't use any recommended safety equipment
- You're taking part in any competitions, races, or practising for speed or time trials
- You're participating in an activity as a professional
- A doctor has advised you against participating in the activity

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions about the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps in the Revolut app:
- In the search bar at the top of the screen, search for 'Insurance'
- Select 'Insurance'
- Select 'Travel insurance' and tap 'Get help'

title: Who is covered by travel insurance?
content_text:
In addition to having a Metal or Ultra Revolut plan, the following requirements must be met for you and your trip to be covered by Revolut travel insurance:
- You're a resident of the UK
- You're at least 18 years of age and not older than 75
- Your primary residence matches the address on your Revolut account and your trip starts and ends at your primary residence. Read more about why this is important on this pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/travel-insurance/question-does-moving-home-impact-my-trav/?geo-redirect=true
- Your journey meets the definition of a triphttps://help.revolut.com/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/before-going-abroad/what-is-the-maximum-trip-duration-to-get-covered-by-the-travel-insurance/?geo-redirect=true. Have a trip coming up? Check whether it might be covered with this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.am-i-eligible-for-revolut-travel-insurancerevolut://app/form/e5bc5e23-4550-4593-9dc3-32e99055d1df?faqEntryPoint=question.am-i-eligible-for-revolut-travel-insurance.
- Your trip was paid for with your Revolut account. Read more herehttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/travel-insurance/do-i-have-to-pay-for-my-trip-using-my-revolut-account/?geo-redirect=true

## Are children covered?
If you and your trip meet the eligibility requirements for cover, your children (including fostered, adopted, and step-children) are also covered by the travel insurance, so long as they're:
- Under 18 years of age (or under 23 if they're in full-time education)
- Financially dependent on you or your partner (even if they don't live in the same residence)
- Unmarried

## Are travelling companions covered?
Your spouse or partner is covered under this policy if you and your trip meet the eligibility requirements for cover, so long as they're:
- A spouse by marriage, domestic partnership, or civil union
- Living with you for at least 12 consecutive months prior to travel and at least 18 years of age
- Travelling with you
When you make a claim, to verify your eligibility, the insurance provider may request documentation proving that you and your partner live together. This could include a utility bill or an official letter from a government agency or bank.

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps:  
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select 'Travel' and tap 'Documents'

title: Who is Revolut's travel insurance provider?
content_text: We’re not an insurer, but we work with the UK branch of Chubb European Group SE, simply known as Chubb, to provide travel insurance for Metal and Ultra customers.

title: Pet Insurance payments and renewals
content_text:
## How do I pay for Pet Insurance?
- Pet Insurance payments can only be made monthly
- The first payment will be taken on the date the policy is purchased from Revolut
- Subsequent monthly payments will be taken the day before the next coverage period starts (one day earlier than the first payment date). This date will be consistent each month, it is not possible to change the monthly payment date
- We'll notify you if you miss a monthly payment. If you do not complete a monthly payment after 25 days of it becoming due, your Pet Insurance policy will be cancelled effective from the end of your last paid month of cover. No claims will be paid until a missed monthly payment is completed

## Renewals
Your Pet Insurance policy will automatically renew at the end of your current 12-month policy year. We'll begin notifying you of your policy's upcoming renewal around 1 month before the renewal date. You can manage your next year of cover in-app and change your plan type, excess and co-pay options prior to renewal in-app. 
At renewal, you should read through your policy and edit details if they have changed so that your renewal quote is accurate, including:
- Spayed/neutered status: Update the spayed or neutered status of your pet
- Excess and co-payment option: Adjust the excess and co-payment options
- Plan changes: Change the plan between Bronze, Silver and Gold
To manage your renewal in-app: 
- Go to 'Lifestyle' on the bottom menu of the Revolut app
- Tap 'Insurance' and select 'Pet Insurance'
- Choose 'Details' under 'Plan renewal'
- Select 'Manage plan'

title: How do I complain about my pet insurance?
content_text:
We always work to provide you with the best customer experience and products possible. However, we understand that sometimes things don't go to plan and we really value your feedback. To make a complaint, contact Revolut Support. 
We'll make sure your complaint is received and carefully reviewed by our Complaints team. If you're not satisfied with the outcome of your complaint investigation, you can contact the Financial Ombudsman Servicehttps://www.financial-ombudsman.org.uk/. You can do so:
- By post: Exchange Tower, London E14 9SR
- By phone: 0300 123 9123 or 0800 023 4567
- By email: complaint.info@financial-ombudsman.org.uk

## For more details
Full details of their impartial complaints procedure can be found on the Financial Ombudsman websitehttps://www.financial-ombudsman.org.uk/. 
The Financial Ombudsman Service can only deal with your complaint after you've followed our full complaints procedure. Using our complaints procedure or complaining to the Financial Services Ombudsman won't affect your right to take legal action against us.

title: How can I view or manage my Revolut Pet Insurance policy?
content_text:
Your Revolut Pet Insurance policy can be viewed and managed by following these steps: 
- Tap to go to the Pet insurance section, or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'Pet insurance'
- Select ‘Pet’
Go to the Personal detailshttps://help.revolut.com/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/update-personal-details/?geo-redirect=true section for more information on how to update your address or contact details on your Revolut account.

## How do I cancel my policy? 
Your Revolut Pet Insurance policy can be cancelled in-app by following these steps:
- Tap to go to the Pet insurance section, or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'Pet insurance'
- Select ‘Pet’, then tap 'Get help', then follow the steps in-app to cancel your policy
If you have any difficulties with cancelling your policy, or need help with managing your Revolut Pet Insurance policy, contact us via our in-app chat.

## Will I get a refund if I cancel my policy?
If you cancel your policy within the 14-day cooling-off period, you'll receive a full refund, provided you've not made or intend to make a claim. 
If you cancel your policy outside your 14-day cooling-off period, and you haven't made or intend to make a claim, you may be eligible for a pro-rata refund, and we won't collect any further monthly payments from you. 
If the only claim you've made or intend to make in the policy year is due to the death, loss, theft or straying of your pet, resulting in the cancellation of your policy, this won't affect your rights under the cooling-off period.

title: Free 24/7 FirstVet Video Consultations
content_text:
As a Revolut customer, one of the benefits on your policy is for you to have free, unlimited 24/7 access to video consultations with experienced, UK-registered Vets, through our partnership with FirstVet. You can video call with a Vet at any time of day or night if you are worried about Your Pet’s health but are not sure if you need to visit a vet. FirstVet’s experienced vets can answer your questions and help to put Your mind at ease. Many conditions can be solved over video call, in the comfort of your own home, without the need to visit your local practice. FirstVet’s friendly and experienced vets can provide advice, information, treatment recommendations or refer you to your local practice if necessary.
Using FirstVet will not affect your premium and there is no excess to pay for this service either.
Here are some issues FirstVet can assist with:
- Vomiting and diarrhoea
- Poisoning
- Eye and ear problems
- Skin problems and itching
- Coughing and sneezing
- Minor injuries
- Bereavement counselling
How it works:
1. Register for FirstVet: Follow this linkhttps://account.firstvet.com/uk?insurance=revolut. to register for FirstVet as a Revolut customer. After you have registered, download the FirstVet app and log in. Add your pet's details now so that when you need to speak to a vet in the future, you are ready to go.
2. Make an appointment: When you need an appointment, select the pet you want to speak to a vet about, describe their symptoms and choose a time that suits you.
3. Veterinarian visit: The visit is via video call on your smart phone, tablet or computer. FirstVet will send a text reminder 5 minutes before the consultation starts. Open the app when it is time for your appointment and the vet will begin the consultation.
4. After the visit: After the visit an email will be sent to you containing the vet’s journal notes, with a summary of the vet’s advice and recommendations. The journal will be forwarded to your local practice if a Vet visit is needed.
If your pet is very sick or badly injured, you should seek in-person veterinary care immediately.

title: What are the main pet insurance policy conditions I should be aware of?
content_text:
In order to purchase our insurance, you will need to confirm that:
- You are a resident in the United Kingdom (excludes Channel Islands and the Isle of Man)
- Your pet is 8 weeks old, fully vaccinated, microchipped, and had a full Vet check-up in the last year.
- Your pet is not used for commercial, hunting, or breeding purposes.
- You have never had any insurance declined or cancelled by a pet insurer.
- You do not have any unspent fraud convictions or crime relating to animal welfare.
For dogs only:
- Your dog has never previously shown aggressive behaviour, attacked or bitten another person or animal or damaged others’ property.
- Your dog is not on or crossed with a prohibited breed as defined in the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991.
- Your dog does not live on a premise that sells alcohol.
It is the law to get your dog microchipped and under a new animal welfare plan, cats will soon be required to be microchipped by law. It is important to keep your information up to date so you can be contacted if your cat or dog is lost. It is also a condition of our cover that your cat/dog is microchipped.
There are some breeds that we simply can’t provide cover for. Those are dogs listed on the Dangerous Dogs Act and a number of additional breeds listed below:
- Akita

Any dog which is or is crossed with one of the breeds listed, or any wolf hybrid, or which is considered to match the description of a prohibited “type” as defined in the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991 and any amendments to it or replacement legislation, or deemed to be dangerous by the Secretary of State, are excluded from policy cover.
In addition, no pets used in commercial, hunting, or breeding purposes (with the exception of Assistance pets) are covered under this policy.
There are also some cat breeds that we simply can’t provide cover for. Please note the excluded cat breeds listed below:

title: Can I cancel an Airport Fast Track pass?
content_text:
Not all lanes allow free cancellation. When booking your Fast Track pass in-app, before checkout, you'll be able to see whether or not free cancellation is possible. You’ll also see the time limits to request a cancellation.
Free cancellations and refunds are only available if you cancel before the deadline. After this window passes, your pass becomes non-refundable.

## Cancellation steps
If the lane you have booked a Fast Track pass for allows you to cancel your pass, you can do so in the Revolut app:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu of the Revolut app
- Select Fast Trackhttps://www.revolut.com/app/asft
- Select the pass you want to cancel under 'Active passes'
- Select 'Manage booking', then 'Cancel booking'
If you don't see this option, it's because the lane you booked does not allow for cancellation.

title: How do I purchase an Airport Fast Track pass?
content_text:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu of the Revolut app
- Select Fast Trackhttps://www.revolut.com/app/asft
- Choose your airport and terminal, then add your travel date and time slot
- Select how many passes you need. If you’re buying passes for travel companions, add their details too
- Complete checkout 

## Airport Fast Track pass terms
- A single pass is valid for one chosen time slot, for one person
- All customers can buy passes for themselves or travel companions

## Airport Fast Track passes for travel companions
- Your guests can only use the fast track slot you’ve booked for them while they’re with you
- You can't send your pass to someone else. A pass must be used by the person named on it

title: How do I use my airport fast track pass?
content_text:
## Using an airport fast track pass
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu of the Revolut app
- Select Fast Trackhttps://www.revolut.com/app/asft
- Select the pass you want to use under 'Active passes'
- Tap to reveal your QR code. This will be available from 3 hours before your chosen time slot
- Show your QR code at the airport fast track lane
If you have fast track passes for travel companions, just swipe on your fast track QR code to reveal the QR codes for your guest passes.

## Rules for using an airport fast track pass
- You need to go to the fast track lane for the time slot you’ve chosen
- You can't send your pass to someone else. Each pass is personal, and you need to show your QR code and ID to use the pass
- If you’ve bought passes for travel companions, they can't use the pass without you
Fast track passes are provided in partnership with DragonPass. Access to the lanes is subject to the conditions of use of each lane and our Airport Fast Track Pass Terms.https://www.revolut.com/legal/airport-fast-track-pass

title: Reasons you can trust Revolut
content_text:
## Revolut is legitimate
Revolut is regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) and has recently been authorised as a bank in the UK with certain restrictions. We’re now in a 'mobilisation' phase, where we’re taking the final steps to set up our banking processes to operate as a bank in the UK.
Over 45 million customers in 38 countries use Revolut.

## Revolut is safe
Your safety is our priorityhttps://www.revolut.com/blog/post/is-revolut-safe/?geo-redirect=true. We use advanced security measures, including strict identity checks, biometrics, and instant card freezing. Our robust in-app fraud detection system monitors suspicious activity and proactively warns against scams.
We trigger proactive warnings that detect most attempted scams. Read more about our security measures on this pagehttps://www.revolut.com/how-we-keep-your-money-safe/.

## Revolut protects your data
We take the security and confidentiality of your personal information seriously. We use secure data centres with restricted access to store and process your data. Data transmissions between our mobile apps, servers, and third parties are encrypted. Learn more in our Privacy Policyhttps://www.revolut.com/privacy-policy/?geo-redirect=true

## Revolut is committed to continuous improvement
At Revolut, we’re committed to continuous improvement by enhancing services, expanding to new markets/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/what-countries-are-supported, and introducing new features. We're always reviewing feedback/help/more/how-do-i-share-feedback-with-revolut and analysing market trends to keep our services relevant and valuable.

title: Can I contact Revolut using a Power of Attorney?
content_text:
In some situations, you may wish to give access to your account to someone else. To make this possible, you or the person to whom you may wish to give access must submit a Power of Attorney (POA) to us.
A POA is a signed document prepared by one individual (the Donor) allowing another (the Attorney) to act on their behalf in certain defined situations. 
Although the 'Attorney' can be, and often is, a lawyer of some kind, almost anyone can be an 'Attorney' for these purposes (as long as they're capable of making decisions and a legal adult in the relevant jurisdiction).

## How to obtain a Power of Attorney and what does it contain?
The easiest way to get a POA is by contacting a lawyer, notary, or your local citizens advice office. They'll be able to advise you on the type of POA you need, and how to obtain one.
What should be contained in a POA depends on its type and your jurisdiction of your country. In all circumstances, the POA should state:
- Your name, as the donor (the POA should include your address, so we know it's related to you)
- The name of the Attorney (and their address)
- The date on which the POA was signed
- If the POA is requested for a limited time, the date of its expiry
- The scope of the Attorney's authority under the POA

## How can I submit a Power of Attorney to Revolut?
You need to contact our support team, you can do this by requesting live support by typing 'Live Agent' in the in-app chat. We'll then verify your request to have a POA added to your account, and, when relevant, act on your instructions in accordance with the POA.
 Alternatively, you can send an email to support@revolut.com

## Submitting a POA as the Attorney
- If you have a Revolut account, log in and send a selfie with your ID via chat for verification 
- If you don't have an account, contact Support at support@revolut.com. Our team will request your full name, a selfie with your ID, and your contact details to assist you
After you share this information with our Support team, we will review your request and then contact you regarding the next steps.
Revolut does not provide legal advice. This page contains general information. We recommend you check the legal requirements in your jurisdiction or obtain specialist advice.

title: Revolut Business

You can use the same phone number and email address as your personal Revolut account.

With Revolut Business, you can send, receive, and exchange in 30+ currencies at competitive rates (T&Cs apply).
You can also control team spending with physical or virtual cards. Accept payments online or in-person, with fast settlement. Payment Processing Terms and Fees apply.https://www.revolut.com/legal/business-acquiring/

title: Difference between limited and full access to the web app
content_text:
With limited access to the web app, you’re able to view up to three of your most recent transactions and freeze your existing Revolut cards. 
When you get full access to the web app, you'll be able to use even more transactional features, such as viewing all transactions, freezing your card settings, unfreezing your card, adjusting other card settings, and separating transactions by account type (e.g. different currencies).
Freezing your card allows you to secure your Revolut account if you lose access to your Revolut mobile app for any reason, e.g. a stolen device or fraudulent activity.

title: Does Revolut offer services in Russia?
content_text:
## We don't currently offer or support services in Russia
Our stance on the conflict aligns with international regulatory rules, and we’re committed to complying with all applicable laws and sanctions. Read our response to the war on Ukrainehttps://www.revolut.com/blog/post/the-war-on-ukraine-our-response/. Currently, Revolut has no plans to expand our services to Russia.

title: Reporting vulnerable circumstances to Revolut
content_text:
At Revolut, we understand how certain circumstances in life can impact your finances and the way you interact with us. When we face difficult situations, we can feel vulnerable — emotionally, mentally, or physically. 

## What are vulnerable circumstances? 
Examples of vulnerable circumstances include, but aren't limited to:
- Addiction (e.g. alcoholism, gambling)
- Disability (e.g. injury, visual impairment)
- Financial difficulty
- Grief
- Mental health challenges
We want to give you the right kind of financial support, no matter what. By sharing your circumstances with us through the 'Tell us something' formhttps://revolut.onelink.me/E528/?af_channel=website_organic&af_dp=revolut%3A%2F%2Fapp%2Fform%2F13e7ebea-1153-42ce-9f4f-390c30eb1814&af_force_deeplink=true&af_sub1=%257B%2522conversion_page_url%2522%253A%2522https%253A%252F%252Fhelp.revolut.com%252Fhelp%252Fmore%252Fhow-can-i-disclose-that-there-is-something-affecting-my-finances-or-my-interaction-with%252F%2522%252C%2522cookie_consent%2522%253A%255B%2522ads%2522%252C%2522analytics%2522%255D%252C%2522landing_page_url%2522%253A%2522https%253A%252F%252Fhelp.revolut.com%252Fhelp%252Fprofile-and-plan%252Fsecurity-and-personal-data%252Fgambling-block%252Fwhat-is-gambling-block%252F%2522%252C%2522qr_code%2522%253Afalse%257D&af_web_dp=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.revolut.com%2Fform%2F13e7ebea-1153-42ce-9f4f-390c30eb1814&pid=website, we can understand you and your needs better. Filling out the form isn't mandatory, and anything you share with us is strictly confidential.

## I've already filled out the form
If you filled out the form, here are some points to consider:
- If you need to update your circumstances, we recommend completing the form again
- If you feel the information you’ve shared with us isn't relevant or no longer impacts your finances or interactions with Revolut, you can let us know via this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/01ade6a2-8ff6-4ca3-8bdc-4f2d64aa9b86?faqEntryPoint=cf1548ef-ce40-4a3f-9a91-8cda7c23a7edrevolut://app/form/01ade6a2-8ff6-4ca3-8bdc-4f2d64aa9b86?faqEntryPoint=cf1548ef-ce40-4a3f-9a91-8cda7c23a7ed. We'll take care of removing the information

## Managing preferences and security settings
If you want to change your marketing preferences, go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, tap your profile icon in the top-left corner, select 'Account', then 'Privacy'https://app.revolut.com/settings/security/privacyrevolut://app/privacy, and toggle the setting on or off to match your preferences.
To activate the in-app gambling block feature, go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, tap your profile icon in the top-left corner, select 'Security', then 'Gambling block', and enable the toggle. For more details, check our related FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/security-and-personal-data/gambling-block/what-is-gambling-block in our Help Centre.

title: How do I share feedback with Revolut?
content_text:
We encourage you to always contact us and share your ideas on how to make Revolut better. If you want to submit your feedback, you can do it anytime via email.
How to send feedback via email?
You can send your suggestion via email feedback@revolut.com. Thanks to your emails we can improve existing features and introduce new products tailored to your needs. Although it's not possible to reply to every feedback email request, our relevant teams read every single feedback.

title: I lost access to a device my passkey is stored on
content_text:
## Lost access to a device
If you lose access to a device with your passkey on it, you may still have access to your passkeys. This depends on where you stored the passkey. 
If you store a passkey on one of the following, you can access it from any other device connected to the passkey’s storage and remove it:
- Your browser
- A cloud-based system, like Google Cloud or Apple Keychain
- A password manager within your browser or a cloud-based account

## Revoke a passkey
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Tap 'Account'
- Tap 'Passkeys'
- Select the 'Passkey' from the list of passkeys
- Press the 'Revoke' button

title: How can I file a complaint?
content_text:
We put our customers at the core of everything we do and always aim to bring you the best experience possible. However, if you're ever unhappy with the level of service we've provided or an experience you've had with one of our products, we want to hear from you and try to make things right.
If you'd just like to speak to someone about something that's concerning you, you can contact us via the in-app chat (tap your profile icon in the top-left corner → select 'Help') or visit our Help pagehttps://www.revolut.com/contact-us/. We can usually settle matters quickly in this way. 
You can submit a complaint via our online complaint formhttps://forms.revolut.com/e0ed546b-2fac-462f-9578-0eaff076c17f?source=faq&faqEntryPoint=question.how-do-i-complain or by sending details of your complaint to formalcomplaints@revolut.com.

## Information to include in your complaint:
- Your name and surname
- The phone number and email address associated with your account
- An explanation of the problem
- The date when the problem started
- Your preferred resolution for the problem

## What happens next?
We'll acknowledge and review your complaint shortly after you submit it.
The resolution time to resolve a complaint may differ depending on the company providing the product or service and the nature of the complaint.
Once we receive your complaint, we'll send you a written acknowledgement that includes an estimated response time. You can find more information on complaint resolution timeframes in our Complaints Policyhttps://www.revolut.com/legal/complaints-policy/?geo-redirect=true.

## Escalating your complaint externally
If you're still unhappy with the resolution, you can get in touch with the relevant authority. This authority may vary depending on which Revolut entity provides you with the product or service your complaint relates to. Read this FAQ/help/more/legal-topics/which-revolut-companies-provide-me-with-services to find out which Revolut entity provides you with which service.
For detailed information on the external dispute resolution authorities, refer to our Complaints Policyhttps://www.revolut.com/legal/complaints-policy/?geo-redirect=true.
If you believe you've been a victim of fraud or suspect your account is at risk, follow these FAQs/help/security-logging-in for troubleshooting steps or to get in touch. If you're a Revolut customer, you can contact our specialists to report fraud via this linkhttps://revolut.com/app-form/new-fraud-reporting-filter-screen-faq?faqEntryPoint=question.how-do-i-complain. To get help with a CIFAS marker, visit this pagehttps://www.revolut.com/cifas-marker-review/.

title: Learn about my rights when making payments in Europe
content_text:
The European Commission has produced a leaflet setting out your rights when making payments in Europe. All payment service providers must share this with you.
You can find the leaflet in English herehttps://commission.europa.eu/system/files/2019-09/leaflet-your-rights-payments-eu_en.pdf, in Lithuanian here, and in all other languages herehttps://ec.europa.eu/info/files/leaflet-your-rights-payments-eu_en.

title: Public Figure Check - More Information
content_text:
## What are prominent public functions?
- Heads of state, heads of government, ministers and deputy or assistant ministers
- Members of parliament or of similar legislative bodies
- Members of the governing bodies of political parties
- Elected metropolitan mayor
- Board members of National Audit Office or central banks or equivalent functions
- Ambassadors, charges d’affaires and high-ranking officers in the armed forces
- Members of the administrative, management or supervisory bodies of State-owned enterprises
- Members of supreme courts, of constitutional courts or of any judicial body the decisions of which are not subject to further appeal except in exceptional circumstances
- Directors, deputy directors and members of the board or equivalent function of an international organisation (such as NATO or WTO)

## What is considered a family member?
- Spouse or civil partner
- Parent(s) and Parent(s)-in-law including step-parents
- Sibling and sibling-in-law including step-sibling
- Son and partner, including son-in-law, step-son, and adopted son
- Daughter and partner, including daughter-in-law, step-daughter and adopted daughter

## What is a close associate?
- An individual known to have joint beneficial ownership of a legal entity or a legal arrangement or any other close business relationship with a politically exposed person
- An individual who has sole beneficial ownership of a legal entity or a legal arrangement that is known to have been set up for the benefit of a PEP

title: What is my tax residence?
content_text:
A tax residence is any country where you are subject to tax. Typically, it's the country where you spend most of your time and/or where you work.
Criteria for tax residency status for each country can be found on the OECD websitehttps://www.oecd.org/tax/automatic-exchange/crs-implementation-and-assistance/tax-residency/.

title: What happens if the Revolut account holder passes away?
content_text:
Coping with bereavement can be very difficult. That's why we're here to help you. However, this isn't legal advice, and we advise that you seek the help of a professional adviser, such as a lawyer with expertise in wills and estates, to help you through this process.
