- Choose your desired coverage — local for one country, or regional/global for multiple countries
- Select the amount of data you need
- Proceed to check out and purchase your plan
To receive an invoice for your eSIM purchase, request live support by typing 'Live agent' in the in-app chat.

## How to check how much data you've used
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'eSIM' and select one of your active plans
- You'll see the remaining data and total data used
If you have notifications enabled on your device, you'll receive a notification when you've used 80% of your data. You'll receive another notification when your data has run out.

## Deleting an eSIM
To remove the Revolut eSIM on iOS, go to your device settings, select ‘Mobile service’, then ‘SIMs’, choose the Revolut eSIM, and select 'Delete eSIM'.
The steps to remove an eSIM on Android may vary slightly depending on the manufacturer and model, but the general process is:
- Open your settings
- Navigate to SIM or network settings
- Select the eSIM you want to remove
- Look for an option to remove or delete the SIM

## Can I transfer an eSIM data plan to another phone?
eSIM data plans can only be used on the device they were purchased from. You can't use them on another device, even if you've downloaded the Revolut app on that device.
If you need another eSIM for a second device, you'll need to purchase one directly from the Revolut app on that device.

title: Supported eSIM devices
content_text:
An eSIM is an embedded, digital SIM that’s supported by a number of different devices. eSIM allows you to activate a mobile plan or a data only plan in a specific country without having to use a physical SIM.
To use an eSIM, your device needs to be useable with any provider, and eSIM-compatible. Check the list below to see if your device supports eSIM. This list is constantly expanding as more eSIM-compatible devices become available.
The below list highlights the oldest devices that support eSIM. If your device is newer than the ones listed, it should support eSIM. Country and carrier-specific restrictions may apply. This list was last updated August 2025.

## Earliest eSIM-compatible devices by manufacturer:
- Apple: iPhone XS, iPhone XR, iPhone SE, iPhone 11, iPad Air (4th gen), iPad Pro 11-inch (1st gen), iPad (7th gen), iPad Mini (5th gen), iPad Pro 12.9-inch (4th gen)
- Samsung: Galaxy S20, Galaxy A23 5G, Galaxy A35 5G, Galaxy A54 5G, Galaxy Z Flip, Galaxy Fold, Galaxy Z Fold, Galaxy Note 20
- Google Pixel: Pixel 3, Pixel Fold
- Honor: Magic5 Pro, Magic4 Pro, Honor 90
- Huawei: P40, P40 Pro, Mate 40 Pro
- Motorola: Moto G54, Edge 40 Neo, Edge 40 Pro, Edge+, Razr 40 Ultra, Razr 2022, Razr 2019, Razr 5G, G53 5G
- Nokia: G60, XR21, X30
- OnePlus: OnePlus 13, OnePlus 12, OnePlus 11, OnePlus Open
- Oppo: Reno6 Pro 5G, A55s 5G, Find N2 Flip, Find X3 Pro, Find X5, Find X5 Pro, Reno A
- Sony: Xperia 1 V, Xperia 1 IV, Xperia 5 IV, Xperia 10 III Lite, Xperia 10 IV
- Vivo: X90 Pro, European version V29, V29 Lite 5G, V40 Lite
- Xiaomi: 14 Pro, 13T, 13T Pro, 13, 13 Pro, 13 Lite, 12T Pro, Redmi Note 11 Pro 5G

## Will my physical SIM still work with an eSIM enabled?
The Dual SIM technology in compatible iPhone and Android models lets you stay connected to your physical SIM and your eSIM simultaneously. You can select the SIM you want to use for connectivity and switch to the other SIM at any time.

title: Mobile plan access and payment
content_text:
## Getting started with mobile plans
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu and select 'Mobile' or 'eSIM'
- Or type 'Mobile' into the search bar

## Purchasing a mobile plan
- Go to 'Mobile' or 'eSIM', and select the mobile plan you want
- Choose your primary number
- Go to the checkout
- Choose to pay for your plan from RevPoints, a Pocket balance, or both
You can select from your main account and fiat Pockets.
If you want to pay with RevPoints, just toggle the RevPoints option on. This means that every month we'll prioritise using your RevPoints to pay for your plan. If you don't have enough points to cover the full amount, we'll take the remaining balance from your chosen balance.

## Purchasing secondary number add-ons
- Go to the 'Plan details' screen
- Select 'Manage'
- Tap 'Add new'
- Go to the checkout
- Choose to pay from RevPoints, a Pocket balance, or both

## Updating your payment method
You can update your payment method at any time:
- Go to your mobile plan and select 'Plan details' → 'Billing' → 'Pay with'
- Select the current payment method displayed
- Choose how you'd like to make future payments
We'll use this method to make any future monthly plan payments or secondary number add-on payments.

title: Cancelling or rescheduling a number transfer to Revolut
content_text:
To cancel a number transfer, submit a request in the app. To do so:
- Tap your mobile plan and select ‘Plan details’
- Select 'Manage’
- Select the number that shows transfer in progress
- Select ‘Cancel number transfer’
To reschedule a transfer, simply submit the cancellation request and start a new number transfer request.

## While waiting for the number transfer to complete
You can start using your mobile plan right after purchase. We'll provide you with an eSIM and a temporary phone number so you can make/receive calls and use data immediately. Once your number transfer is complete, your original number will link to the eSIM, and the temporary one will be deactivated.

title: Cancelling your old phone plan
content_text:
## Cancel using STAC
You can cancel your old plan from your existing provider free of charge. You'll just need a STAC. Text 'STAC' to 75075 from your existing phone number with your current provider to get your code.

## How to cancel your old plan in-app
- When you reach the checkout for your new plan, tap 'Cancel my old plan'
- Enter the phone number you want to close
- Enter your STAC
- You can track your account closure status via in-app banners and push notifications
Account closure usually takes 1 working day, and we’ll let you know when it’s done. When the account closure is complete, your agreement with your previous provider will be automatically terminated, and any relevant charges will be reflected in your last bill.
If you don’t use your STAC within 30 days, it’ll automatically expire and you'll need to request a new one.

title: Managing a mobile plan for a lost or stolen device
content_text:
## If you don't have access to your account
If your device was lost, stolen, or may be at risk, follow the below steps as well as you can to help secure your account.
- Tap this linkhttps://app.revolut.com/lost-device to report a lost device
- Verify your identity with a selfie
- Follow the on-screen instructions to log out from all linked devices, freeze your cards, and eSIM temporarily, and change your passcode
If you're unable to follow these steps, email us at support@revolut.com.

## If you still have access to your account
- Go to the Revolut app
- From the home screen, tap your profile icon in the top left corner
- Tap 'Security'
- Tap 'Lost device'
- Follow the on-screen instructions to log out from all linked devices, freeze your cards and eSIM temporarily, and change your passcode
You can also complete these steps in the Revolut web app:
- Sign in to the Revolut web app
- Click 'Security' in the left-hand menu
- Click 'Lost device'
- Follow the onscreen instructions to log out from all linked devices, freeze your cards and eSIM temporarily, and change your passcode

## Freezing and unfreezing your eSIM
Your number will be temporarily disconnected, preventing calls and texts. All data plans, including local and roaming, will be paused. You won't be charged for future mobile plan payments until your eSIM is unfrozen, which you can do after securing your account.
To unfreeze your eSIM, go to 'Mobile plans' and select 'Unfreeze.' This can be done on your recovered device or another device with the app. A new eSIM may be required.
If you can't get your device back, or you need to connect right away, transfer your plan. Log in to the Revolut app on a new device, select 'Transfer here' on the 'Mobile plans' screen, and install the eSIM. This will transfer your plan and disable it on your old device.

## Plan auto-cancellation
Your plan will be automatically cancelled and your number disconnected when your current billing period ends if you don't unfreeze or transfer it. You'll then have 30 days to port out your number.
After this cancellation, you can always rejoin Revolut Mobile by purchasing a new plan.

title: Managing a failed number transfer
content_text:
## Number transfer fails
If the transfer of your number from your previous provider to Revolut is unsuccessful, we will notify you within the app via a notification banner. Alternatively, you can check the status and reason for failure by going to 'Plan details' and selecting 'My numbers'. 

## Common reasons for transfer failure
- Mismatched information: details provided in your transfer request must match the information on file with your previous provider
- Incorrect switching code: the PAC you entered is wrong/expired. Reach out to your previous provider to request a new code
- Locked device: your device must be unlocked

## Re-initiate the transfer process
To restart the number transfer, request a new PAC code from your previous provider. Then, tap the notification banner or go to 'Plan details' and select 'My numbers'. For a successful transfer, ensure the PAC code is correct and the details you provide match those held by your previous provider.

title: Managing a number transfer to Revolut
content_text:
## How long does the number transfer take?
The number transfer process usually takes 1–2 working days.
Until the switch takes place, you can continue to use your new Revolut Mobile eSIM with your temporary number or your SIM from your original network. You’ll lose service on your original network on the day the switch takes place. You can dispose of your original SIM once your transfer is complete.

## Can I transfer my number from an existing fixed contract?
You can but you will need to manage the termination of the fixed term contract with your current provider, and early termination policies and fees may apply.

## Can I transfer a number from a cancelled plan to Revolut?
If your subscription is cancelled but your phone number is still connected, or your number was disconnected within the last 30 days, you might be able to transfer it. Check with your current provider whether or not you can transfer your number.

## Transferring a number not registered in your name
It's not possible to transfer a number that isn't registered in your name to Revolut.

title: Managing mobile plans and add-ons
content_text:
## Cancelling a plan
You can cancel your Revolut mobile plan at any time. Go to 'Plan details' → ‘Billing’ → ‘Cancel mobile plan’,  or text 'STAC' to 75075 from your Revolut number. Once you receive your code, share it with your new provider to switch. You can also get a STAC via our in-app chat.
Cancel your mobile plan within 14 days of purchase for a partial refund. If you do this, your plan will end immediately. You won't get a refund for the current month if you cancel after 14 days, and your plan will stay active until the cycle ends. If you paid for the next month, you'll be refunded.
Your phone number will be disconnected 60 days after the end of your current billing cycle. If you want to keep it, you'll need to transfer your number to an external mobile network provider, or purchase a new Revolut mobile plan.

## Unsuccessful payments
If you don't have enough money in your account each month, your plan payment will fail, and you'll be limited to incoming calls and texts only. To reactivate, top up your account, then go to 'Mobile plans' → 'Add plan'. Your plan will restart immediately.
If your plan isn't renewed after a month, you'll lose access to inbound calls and texts too. You have 31 days to port your number before it disconnects. To reactivate, top up and buy a new plan. It will activate on your eSIM right away, restoring your previous number.
2 months after a failed plan renewal, your service will be terminated and your number will be disconnected.

## Purchasing a plan after a plan cancellation
You can restart your plan any time by going to 'Mobile plans' -> 'Add plan'. Depending on when you cancelled your previous plan, your new plan will activate immediately, or after the current billing period. You can keep using your current phone number if it hasn't been disconnected or transferred.

## Adding a data plan
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Mobile' and 'Add more data'
- Choose your desired coverage — local for one country, or regional or global for multiple countries
- Select the amount of data you need and confirm

title: Understanding mobile plan numbers
content_text:
## Primary number
This is the primary number associated with your mobile plan. It can be a random number or a VIP number of your choosing. It's the number you'll use for calls and texts, and what people will see when you contact them. You can also choose to transfer this number to an external provider.
A random number is one that has been randomly assigned to you free of charge. You can't choose what random number you get. VIP numbers are distinctive numbers featuring specific repetitions and sequences that you can purchase for a fee.

## Secondary number
This number is for receiving calls and texts from UK phone numbers only. You can't use it to make calls or send texts. You can have a maximum of 3 active eSIM mobile plans at any given time, including per month. The annual limit is 10 eSIM mobile plan activations per calendar year.
Each secondary number incurs a monthly add-on fee. Secondary numbers can be terminated independently of your mobile plan. You can also transfer out your secondary number to an external mobile provider without terminating your Revolut mobile plan.

## Porting in a number
You can transfer your existing number from another provider to Revolut at no cost. You can do this during the plan purchase process and in the 'manage plan' section after purchase. Once the transfer is complete, this number will become the primary number linked to your Revolut mobile plan.

## Temporary number
If you choose to transfer your existing phone number to Revolut during purchase, a temporary number will be provided once you purchase your plan. This means you can start using your new plan right away while your transfer is in progress.
The temporary number remains active until your number has been successfully ported in. Once transferred, your original number will be active on your Revolut mobile plan, replacing the temporary one, which will then stop working.

title: Paying for my mobile plan
content_text:
## Monthly mobile plan
You can pay for your monthly mobile plan using funds from your Revolut main account or other currency pockets. Alternatively, you can use RevPoints. 
Simply enable the RevPoints option when purchasing your plan, or in the 'Billing' section within 'Plan details'. This prioritises your RevPoints as the payment method for the monthly plan payments. If RevPoints can't cover the full amount, the remaining balance will be taken from your chosen Pocket.
If you have insufficient funds or RevPoints before your next billing cycle, we'll prompt you to top up your account. If your account still lacks sufficient funds on the plan renewal day, your plan will not be renewed for the following month as we will not charge you to prevent a negative balance.

## Monthly secondary number add on
You can pay for your monthly secondary number add-on using funds from your Revolut main account or other currency Pockets, or with RevPoints. If you choose a VIP number as your secondary number, an additional one-off fee will apply.
Your secondary number's billing period will align with your mobile plan. If purchased mid-cycle, a one-off fee covers the first month. Subsequently, a full-month commitment is required, with the secondary number add-on fee included in your mobile plan from the next billing cycle.

## Accessing the invoice
You can find your invoice in-app:
- Access your mobile plan and go to 'Plan details'. Select 'Billing' and choose 'Invoices'
- Select the invoice you wish to view and access
Your invoice will be available as soon as you've paid for your mobile plan. All your past invoices are available for viewing, downloading, and saving.

title: Mobile plan voice support
content_text:
## Types of customer support
If your questions aren't answered in the FAQs, you can contact us through our in-app chat for support. Voice support is only available in two specific situations: submit complaint and to request for porting out credentials.

## Submit complaints by voice
If you’re unhappy with your mobile plan, we’ll try to put things right. Please contact us through the Revolut app and follow these steps:
- Start an in-app chat and request voice support to submit a complaint
- We'll send you a link to voice support
- Follow the link to be connected to a phone agent, available 24/7

## Porting out your details
To port your Revolut mobile number out to another provider, you can request a voice call. Follow these steps:
- Start an in-app chat and request voice support to request porting out your details
- We'll send you a link to voice support
- Follow the link to be connected to a phone agent, available 24/7
The phone agent won't be able to advise on anything besides porting out your details. You can contact us via our in-app chat for any other questions or concerns.

title: Setting up voicemail
content_text:
## Setting up voicemail for the first time
- Dial 121
- Follow the prompts to create a PIN and record your greeting

## Changing your voicemail greeting
- Call 121 from your phone
- Enter your voicemail PIN when prompted
- Select the option to change your greeting
- Record your new message and save it

title: Bring your number to Revolut mobile
content_text:
## Transfer your existing number to Revolut
To keep your current number when switching to Revolut, select 'Transfer to Revolut' when purchasing a plan from us. In-app instructions will guide you through transferring your number, and there's no cost to move your current number to Revolut.
Even if you have completed the purchase of a plan and already have a random or VIP number, you can still transfer your existing phone number to Revolut Mobile. To start the transfer process, go to 'Mobile plans', ‘Plan details’, ‘Add’, and select ‘Transfer to Revolut’.

## How to transfer your number
- Confirm the number to be transferred
- Text 'PAC' to 65075 from your existing provider using the number you wish to transfer. Once you receive the PAC code, share it with us
- You'll be informed of the transfer status via in-app banners and push notifications
The transfer usually takes 1 to 2 working days. We’ll notify you when it’s done. When the transfer is completed, your existing agreement will be automatically terminated, and any relevant charges will be reflected in your final invoice from your current provider.

title: Revolut mobile plans
content_text:
## About Revolut mobile plans
Revolut's prepaid mobile plans operate on a flexible monthly rolling contract, allowing for cancellation at any time. These mobile plans are eSIM-only, enabling straightforward activation. Your plan activates as soon as you complete your purchase and install your eSIM.
The introductory price for the unlimited plan is £12.50 a month. This introductory offer ends on 31 July 2026. If you join after this, the plan will cost £14.99 a month. If you're an existing customer who joined during the introductory period, you will continue to pay £12.50 until further notice.

## Your plan allowance
- Unlimited data in the UK
- 20GB data roaming in EEA/US
- Unlimited calls and texts in the UK
- 50 minutes roaming calls (made while in EEA/US to EEA/US/UK)
- 50 minutes international long distance calls (made while in UK to EEA/US)
- 100 roaming/international text messages (sent while in EEA/US/UK to EEA/US/UK)
Once you have a mobile plan with us, you can also find this information in-app. If you've run out of data or you're travelling to a country not covered by your plan, you can buy another data plan through the app. Only data add-ons are currently offered.

## Plan features
- 100 mbps maximum data speed
- 4G and 5G nationwide coverage
- No throttle
- Tethering included
- Access to voice mail, call forwarding and VoWIFI
- Get a random number or transfer in your existing number for free
- Purchase a VIP number
- Purchase up to 3 secondary numbers
- Access to NordVPN and Messaging Pass
If you're outside the EEA and US, you can stay in touch with Messaging Pass in 100+ countries. This allowance lets you use data with restricted bandwidth for messaging and other apps, so you're always connected abroad. Data limits apply.
These features may be revised periodically, so check the app for the most up-to-date list of countries, plans, features, and fees.

## Revolut's UK mobile partner
UK mobile plans are provided by Gigs. Payments are collected by Revolut Ltd on behalf of Gigs. Revolut is an agent for Gigs. You'll accept both Revolut and Gigs terms to start using UK mobile plans.
eSIM data plans purchased by current or former UK mobile plan customers are provided by Gigs as well.

title: Service disruptions during transfer to Revolut
content_text:
## Will the transfer cause service interruption?
After you transfer your number, your temporary number is replaced. In some cases, your phone (or Apple services like iMessage/FaceTime) may take a little extra time to fully switch over. This is normal and is usually resolved within a few hours, but can take up to 24 hours.
If you’ve lost connection completely (have no signal or data), try these steps in order:
- Set your Revolut eSIM as the default voice line and mobile data in your phone settings
- Restart your device. Turn it off, wait 10–20 seconds, then turn it back on
- Turn Airplane Mode on for a few seconds, then turn it off

## What if my device doesn't recognise the new number?
Try these steps if regular calls or SMS work but iMessage or FaceTime (on iPhone) doesn't recognise your new number:
- Disable iMessage and FaceTime (following the instructions by Apple, which can be found on their website)
- Restart your device
- Enable iMessage and FaceTime again

## What if I cancel my mobile plan during the transfer process?
The number transfer to Revolut will proceed. However, after the number transfer is complete, you can then transfer the number to a different provider.

## Can my old provider stop the process?
If your number transfer encounters any issues or is rejected, we'll promptly notify you within the app and provide clear instructions on how to proceed.

title: Mobile plan allowance and consumption
content_text:
## Check your mobile plan allowance and usage
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Mobile' and select your active mobile plans
- In 'Plan details', you'll see local and roaming allowances of your plan, as well as the remaining data, voice, SMS against the total allowance
If you have notifications enabled on your device, you'll receive a notification when you've used 80% of your allowance. You'll receive another notification when your allowance has run out.

title: Transfer your Revolut number to another provider
content_text:
## Transfer Revolut number to new provider
You can transfer your Revolut number to another provider free of charge using a PAC. Provide the PAC to your new provider to start the transfer. The PAC will automatically expire in 30 days. Text 'PAC' to 65075 from your Revolut phone number to receive the code. You can also find the PAC in-app:
- Go to 'Mobile plans' and select your mobile plan
- Go to 'Manage' and select the number you want to transfer out
- Select ‘Request PAC’ option and you will shown a PAC
Alternatively, request a PAC via our in-app chat. An agent can provide it there, or if you wish to obtain the PAC over a call, simply inform the in-app agent and you will receive a deep link for an in-app call, which will connect you directly to a live phone agent who can provide the PAC.

## Will the transfer cause service interruption?
Number transfer occurs between 08:00 and 00:00, most finish before 16:00. You may experience intermittent service on your new SIM for a few hours after the number transfers over. This can include a period of time where you can receive calls and texts but cannot make calls, send texts or use data.
This is normal and is usually resolved within a few hours, but can take up to 24 hours.

## What if my new operator blocks the number transfer?
We will notify you in-app if your number transfer request is denied for any reason. If you have an active plan, it will remain active until the end of the billing period. If you do not have an active plan, you can purchase a monthly mobile plan to resume service with us.

## Is my plan automatically canceled when I transfer my number?
Your Revolut mobile plan is automatically cancelled when you transfer your number to a different provider using PAC. 
A refund for unused service is provided if you transferred your phone number away from Revolut within 14 days of starting your Revolut mobile plan. No refund will be issued if the transfer occurred after the initial 14-day period.
To stop a number transfer out, contact the provider you're transferring your number to.

title: Reporting vulnerable circumstances to Revolut
content_text:
We understand how certain circumstances can impact your finances and the way you interact with us. When we face difficult situations, we can feel vulnerable — emotionally, mentally, and physically.

## What are vulnerable circumstances?
Examples of vulnerable circumstances include, but aren't limited to:
- Addiction (e.g. alcoholism, gambling)
- Disability (e.g. injury, visual impairment)
- Financial difficulty
- Grief
- Mental health challenges

## How do I share vulnerable circumstances with Revolut? 
We want to give you the right kind of financial support, no matter what. By sharing your circumstances with us through the 'Tell us something' formhttps://revolut.onelink.me/E528/?af_channel=website_organic&af_dp=revolut%3A%2F%2Fapp%2Fform%2F13e7ebea-1153-42ce-9f4f-390c30eb1814&af_force_deeplink=true&af_sub1=%257B%2522conversion_page_url%2522%253A%2522https%253A%252F%252Fhelp.revolut.com%252Fhelp%252Fmore%252Fhow-can-i-disclose-that-there-is-something-affecting-my-finances-or-my-interaction-with%252F%2522%252C%2522cookie_consent%2522%253A%255B%2522ads%2522%252C%2522analytics%2522%255D%252C%2522landing_page_url%2522%253A%2522https%253A%252F%252Fhelp.revolut.com%252Fhelp%252Fprofile-and-plan%252Fsecurity-and-personal-data%252Fgambling-block%252Fwhat-is-gambling-block%252F%2522%252C%2522qr_code%2522%253Afalse%257D&af_web_dp=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.revolut.com%2Fform%2F13e7ebea-1153-42ce-9f4f-390c30eb1814&pid=website, we can understand you and your needs better. Filling out the form isn't mandatory, and anything you share with us is strictly confidential.
If you previously filled out the form, here are some points to consider:
- If you need to update your circumstances, we recommend completing the form again
- If you feel the information you’ve shared with us isn't relevant or no longer impacts your finances or interactions with Revolut, you can let us know via this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/01ade6a2-8ff6-4ca3-8bdc-4f2d64aa9b86?faqEntryPoint=d0734cb3-6889-450c-9a95-4576f3489feerevolut://app/form/01ade6a2-8ff6-4ca3-8bdc-4f2d64aa9b86?faqEntryPoint=d0734cb3-6889-450c-9a95-4576f3489fee. We'll take care of removing the information for you

title: How to redeem RevPoints for gift cards
content_text:
You can use your RevPoints to redeem gift cards for popular brands, directly within the Revolut app. You can use your RevPoints to get 2 types of gift cards — a code-redeemable gift card or an auto-applied gift card.

## Code-redeemable gift cards
When you redeem your RevPoints for a code-redeemable gift card, you'll receive the gift card code. Copy the code and apply it at the checkout of the relevant brand’s website.
How to redeem and use a code-redeemable gift card:
- Select 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap Gift Cards
- Tap your preferred brand
- Select the amount of RevPoints you'd like to use, and tap 'Redeem'
- Your gift card is ready. Simply copy the code and apply it at checkout on the brand's website

## Auto-applied gift cards
Redeem the gift card using RevPoints and pay an eligible merchant with your personal Revolut card. Your account is automatically reimbursed for the gift card amount — no code required. Gift Cards T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/revolut-gift-card/?geo-redirect=true apply.
How to redeem an auto-applied gift card:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap Gift Cards
- Tap your preferred brand
- Read the 'How it works' section to find out if an auto-applied gift card is available for that brand
- Select the amount of RevPoints you'd like to use, and tap 'Redeem'

## Accessing your gift cards
- Open the Revolut app
- Select 'RevPoints' from the bottom menu
- Tap Gift Cards
- Tap Your cards at the top
- You’ll see all the gift cards you’ve redeemed using RevPoints, which can be used immediately
Gift cards are non-refundable, can't be exchanged for cash, and can't be cancelled once purchased. Auto-applied Revolut gift cards are valid for 1 year from the day you redeem them and are for personal use only. For further help, contact our in-app chat. RevPoints T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/en-PT/legal/RevPoints/?geo-redirect=true apply.

title: Finding your gift cards
content_text:
You can find your purchased gift cards by following these in-app steps:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap Gift cards
- Tap My cards in the top-right corner
All previously redeemed gift cards will be listed, and you can copy the code to use them. Some gift cards are auto-applied and don't have a code. For these, the amount is automatically reimbursed to your account after you pay the merchant with your Revolut card.

title: Shops
content_text:
## How to use Shops
Shops allows you to make daily purchases with your favourite brands and earn RevPoints in return. Shops T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/shops/?geo-redirect=true and RevPoints T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RevPoints/?geo-redirect=true apply.
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Shops'
- Scroll down and choose your favourite brands and start shopping

## Shop online
- Search for a brand using the search bar, or scroll through Shops and select your chosen brand
- Follow the merchant instructions
- Pay with your Revolut card directly or via Revolut card on Google Pay, Apple Pay, PayPal, or RevPay (when a campaign is available)
- RevPoints will be credited to your account within an hour
If you used the points multiplier to earn additional RevPoints (up to 20x your debit card rate), they'll be added to your account once the transaction is completed.

## Shop in-store
If available in your country, this section features brand spots that you can walk into and shop in-store and earn extra RevPoints. From the Revolut app:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Shops'
- Check for nearby stores that support RevPoints. You'll need to enable location sharing
- Tap a store to activate the RevPoints offer
- Follow the merchant instructions
- Pay in-store with your Revolut card or via Revolut card on any digital wallet

## Purchase isn't eligible for RevPoints if the customer:
- paid online for an in-store offer
- paid in-store for an online offer
- hasn't activated the offer in the Revolut app
- paid for an online purchase after 30 minutes have passed

title: Can I get a VAT invoice for my Stays booking?
content_text:
Unfortunately, Revolut can't provide VAT invoices for Stays bookings. 

## Pay Now bookings
We can provide a document with the breakdown of the total payment made, including the base rate and the applicable fees and taxes. This is available on request. You can reach live support by typing 'Live agent' in the in-app chat. This document isn't a VAT invoice.

## Pay at Property bookings
Similar to Pay Now bookings, we can provide a breakdown of the total payment. Alternatively, you can request ‌a VAT invoice at the property during your checkout.

## Vrbo bookings
There are two parts to the payment (base rate and service charge). You can request the base-rate VAT invoice from the host. Neither Vrbo nor Revolut can share the base-rate VAT invoice.
You can download a VAT invoice for the service charge via Vrbo:
- Log in to the Vrbo portalhttps://www.vrbo.com/login?enable_login=true
- Select your booking
- Tap 'Payments'
- Download the VAT invoice

title: How are properties sorted?
content_text:
By default, properties are sorted by our recommended search order, based on factors like price competitiveness, guest review, complaints, booking success, and commission paid to us. If two properties score the same, the property that pays a higher commission will be shown first.
- Price competitiveness
- Guest review
- Complaints
- Booking success
- Commission paid to us. If two properties score the same, the property that pays a higher commission will be shown first

## Customising the sort order
You can also sort properties by price or guest rating, which will override our recommended order:
- By rating: results will be ordered from highest to lowest average rating
- By price: results will be ordered from least to most expensive

title: Manage a Stays booking
content_text:
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Stays'
- Tap 'My bookings' or 'Bookings' at the top
- Choose a booking
- Tap 'Manage booking' to cancel or change the dates
- The cancellation policy for your booking will be displayed after tapping 'Manage booking'
Before you change or cancel your booking, we'll show you the expected refund, if any. The 'Manage booking' option may not be available for all bookings. Availability depends on the type of booking and its specific terms.

## Change room selection
The easiest way to change your room selection is to cancel the booking and book a new room. If you're unable to do this, we recommend that you contact the hotel first. Alternatively, request live support by typing 'Live agent' in the in-app chat.

## Vrbo bookings
For Vrbo bookings, you can request changes to your booking by clicking the link provided in your confirmation email. If you can't find the email, or need additional support, reach out to Vrbo customer service as we're unable to make any changes to your booking.
Stays T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/stays/?geo-redirect=true apply.

title: Make a Stays booking
content_text:
## Make a new booking
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu of the Revolut app
- Tap 'Stays'
- Enter the city, dates, and number of guests
- Tap 'Search'
- Filter and choose a property
- Choose your room and complete your booking
Don’t navigate away until you see a pop-up confirming that your booking is successful.

## Pay for the booking
Choose either 'Pay now' or 'Pay at the property', depending on the options available for that property. Stays T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/stays/?geo-redirect=true apply.
- Pay now: rooms require you to pay for the booking immediately, using your main currency account. You’ll instantly get RevPoints
- Pay at property: choose the 'Pay at property' option to complete your reservation and pay upon arrival. Choose which Revolut card to reserve the room with. Use your Revolut card to pay at the property to earn RevPoints

title: What does the Experiences feature offer?
content_text:
Experiences is a unique feature that enables you to book tours and tickets for various attractions worldwide.

## Make a new booking
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Earn' below your balance
- Go to 'Experiences'
- Select a city
- Tap 'Search'
- Find an experience and complete your booking
Don't leave the booking screen until you see a pop-up confirming your booking is successful.

## Manage a booking
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Earn' below your balance
- Go to 'Experiences'
- Choose 'Bookings' at the top
- Select a booking
On the booking page, you can see additional details and mobile/entry tickets. The cancellation policy for your booking will be displayed after tapping 'Manage booking'. Experiences T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/experiences/?geo-redirect=true apply.

title: Purchase RevPoints in app
content_text:
When you purchase RevPoints in-app, you pay for them with your account balance. Points purchased are non-refundable. Here’s how to do it: 
- Go to ‘RevPoints’ on the bottom menu
- Below your points balance, tap ‘Earn’
- Select ‘Buy points’ for a one-off purchase or ‘Recurring buy’ to set a monthly recurring purchase
- Select your desired points package
- Tap ‘Pay’
Recurring purchases allow you to schedule a points purchase every month. You will be charged monthly on the same date. For purchases activated on a date that does not repeat every month (29th, 30th, or 31st), the purchase will be billed on the last day of the month. 

## Disable RevPoints recurring purchase
- Go to ‘RevPoints’ on the bottom menu
- Below your points balance, tap ‘Earn’
- Select ‘Recurring buy’  
- Tap ‘Deactivate’ on the bottom left

## Manage RevPoints recurring purchase
- Go to ‘RevPoints’ on the bottom menu
- Below your points balance, tap ‘Earn’
- Select ‘Recurring buy’
- Select your desired points package
- If you’ve selected a points package of less points than you currently pay, tap ‘Save’
- If you’ve selected a points package of more points than you currently pay, tap ‘Pay'
Your billing cycle will automatically reset, which means that the next recurring purchase will happen on the date it has reset every month — unless today’s date does not repeat every month (29th, 30th, or 31st),  in which case the purchase will be billed on the last day of the month.

title: Redeem RevPoints
content_text:
You can redeem your points by transferring them to air miles through airline loyalty programmes, by making Stays and Experiences bookings, by redeeming them for a discount at any checkout with Revolut Pay, or by transferring them to your friends.

## Transferring RevPoints to air miles
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu of the Revolut app
- Tap 'Miles'
- Choose one of the available providers and link your account
- Choose an amount of points and tap 'Transfer'
The name on your airline provider account must match the name on your Revolut account, and the order of the names should be the same.

## Booking Stays and Experiences using RevPoints
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu of the Revolut app
- Choose 'Stays' or 'Experiences'
- The options for redeeming points are shown on the checkout screen
- Choose a discount amount among the options and complete the booking
Available on selected booking types only, and minimum threshold to redemption applies. Stays T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/stays/?geo-redirect=true apply.

## Transferring RevPoints to friends
- At the bottom of this FAQ, look for ‘Need more help?’ and tap ‘Chat with us’
- In chat, type: 'I wish to transfer RevPoints'
- Share the number of points to transfer and RevTag of the recipient
- A transfer fee of 10% points will be applied (e.g. if you send 10k points, we will also charge 1k points)
Recipient must be a UK-based Revolut customer with an active RevPoints account. Transfers are final and non-reversible. Both sender and recipient will be notified when the transfer is completed. Most transfers are instant, though some may take up to 24 hours. Also transfers will follow our T&Cs.

## Order of redemption
We will generally redeem the RevPoints you have bought via spare change first. This is to reduce the chances of points expiring. As a part of this change, if your earned RevPoints are to expire in the next 3 months, we'll redeem those first. Here are some examples to explain how this works:
- You have 200 RevPoints — 100 from spare change and 100 from actions in the Revolut app. The earned points expire in 1 month, and the spare change points expire in 2 years. You redeem 80 points, leaving 20 points expiring in 1 month and 100 points expiring in 2 years
- You have 200 RevPoints — 100 from spare change and 100 from app actions. All your points expire tomorrow. You redeem 150 points, leaving 50 points expiring tomorrow. This way, you don't lose any value from the points you purchased

title: How to earn RevPoints
content_text:
## Earning RevPoints
To begin earning RevPoints, make sure you've joined the programme by following the steps in the RevPoint loyalty FAQ/help/revpoints/what-is-revpoints/question-what-is-the-revpoint-loyalty-program. You can earn points by:
- Using your Revolut debit card for eligible purchases (details below)
- Booking Stays and Experiences in-app. Experiences T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/es-ES/legal/experiences/?geo-redirect=true and Stays T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/stays/?geo-redirect=true apply
- Buying with Revolut Shops in eligible countries. Shops T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/shops/?geo-redirect=true apply
- Converting spare change to RevPoints, or buying them
- Using Revolut Pay for one-click checkout. Revolut Pay T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/pay-with-revolut-checkout/?geo-redirect=true apply

## Transactions that don't earn RevPoints
- Transactions made using your Revolut Pro account
- Financial, payment, insurance services; money transfers, wallet top-ups, crypto purchases, brokerage activity, any other liquid or cash-like transactions
- Tax, fines, penalties, support or other payments to or required by governments or judicial entities
- Transactions with certain retailers excluded based on their merchant category code, including those selling cigars, tobacco, or similar products
- Purchases from lotteries, gambling or betting services, utility providers, charities, political or religious organisations, and educational institutions
- Payments related to niche business services, such as real estate, postal services, rental, housing, parking lots, garages, cleaning, janitorial, maintenance, advertising, marketing, and educational services, as well as unclassified business-to-business goods and services

## How many RevPoints do I earn on my spend?
Depending on your plan, you'll receive a different earn rate (on eligible transactions). When you upgrade your plan, you may start earning more RevPoints (see the rates per plan below).
- Standard: £10 for 1 RevPoint
- Plus: £10 for 1 RevPoint
- Premium: £4 for 1 RevPoint
- Metal: £2 for 1 RevPoint
- Ultra: £1 for 1 RevPoint
Paid Plans T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/paid-plans/?geo-redirect=true apply.

## RevPoints accumulation and expiry
- Check your points by going to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu, then tap your points balance
- Points are valid for 3 years from the date they're issued. Learn more in this FAQ/help/revpoints/what-is-revpoints/question-how-can-i-redeem-my-revpoints
For more information, visit our RevPoints T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RevPoints/?geo-redirect=true and Paid Plans T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/paid-plans/?geo-redirect=true.

## RevPoint refunds
RevPoints are loyalty points and are therefore not refundable and cannot be exchanged for money or currency as stated in RevPoints T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RevPoints/?geo-redirect=true. They can only be used by redeeming them according to RevPoints T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RevPoints/?geo-redirect=true and are not accepted by any third party as a means of payment.

title: RevPoints spare change
content_text:
Our spare change feature allows you to acquire RevPoints with the spare change from your payments. For example, if you buy a coffee for GBP 2.46, you can acquire RevPoints with the spare change of GBP 0.54. RevPoints acquired with spare change payments are non-refundable.

## Enable or disable spare change RevPoints
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Below your points balance, tap 'Earn'
- Select 'Spare change'
- Tap 'Done' to enable this feature, or 'Deactivate' to disable it

## Manage the spare change round ups multiplier in-app
Our spare change multiplier increases the amount rounded up from each card payment, so you set aside more money to buy more RevPoints.
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Below your points balance, tap 'Earn'
- Tap 'Spare Change' and choose the multiplier amount
- Tap 'Save'
For more information, visit our RevPoints T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RevPoints/?geo-redirect=true.

title: What is the RevPoints loyalty program?
content_text:
RevPoints is Revolut's loyalty program where you can earn points and redeem them for rewards. RevPoints can't be exchanged for cash.

## How do I join RevPoints?
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'RevPoints' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Join for free'
- Read the feature information and tap 'Get started' 
- On the Your RevPoints screen, read your earning options, and tap 'Continue'
- Follow the prompts to turn on spare change, or tap 'Not now' to skip this step

## I haven't received my RevPoints
RevPoints aren't awarded for certain transactions. You can find all excluded transactions in this FAQ/help/revpoints/what-is-revpoints/question-how-to-earn-revpoints. 
For eligible card payments, points are added to your account immediately after the transaction is made. For Shops rewards, points may take a little longer to be issued.

## Where can I see my RevPoints?
In the Revolut app:
- Go to RevPoints on the bottom menu
- Tap your points balance

## Closing my RevPoints account
Having a RevPoints account is completely free. However, if you wish to close it, simply contact us via our in-app chat, and we'll close it for you.
Once you confirm you want to disable RevPoints, all your existing points will be voided, and you'll no longer be able to earn RevPoints. Voided points cannot be recovered.
Read the RevPoints T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/en-ES/legal/RevPoints/?geo-redirect=true for more information.

title: Personalise RevPoints
content_text:
## What is RevPoints personalisation?
RevPoints personalisation helps your rewards feed show offers you’re more likely to use, instead of lots of random or irrelevant rewards.
We use machine learning to sort and highlight offers based on how you use Revolut and RevPoints. This helps us predict what you'll like and put the most relevant rewards at the top of your feed.
You can turn off individual personalisation at any time. If you do, you’ll still see rewards, just based on broader groups rather than you specifically.

## What data do you use to tailor my offers?
We use the following information from your Revolut account and app activity to decide which rewards to show and in what order:
- Basics like your plan, country, and age range
- Your in-app activity, like offers you’ve seen or clicked
- Your spending patterns (for example, types of shops or the brands you like)
- How you use RevPoints features and your points activity

## Can I choose how my data is used for offers?
Yes, you can manage this anytime using the in-app RevPoints privacy toggles found here:
- Click your profile icon in the top left of the screen
- Open 'Privacy' and scroll down to 'RevPoints personalisation'
If you turn off a toggle you will opt out of individual personalisation for either transaction data, product usage data, or RevPoints data. If individual personalisation is off, we use similar data but in aggregated groups, so offers stay relevant without being tailored to you personally.

## How private and secure is my data?
Your data is stored in dedicated secure databases. Reporting data is pseudonymised. Retention is limited to what’s needed under our Global Data Retention Framework.
- Sensitive categories are excluded (medical, religious, political, and gambling)
- No minors’ data is used
This data doesn’t affect your rights or account status. We don’t share your personal data with merchants for their own marketing. In rare cases, limited protected data may be shared to confirm a reward should be given.

title: APP fraud reimbursement eligibility
content_text:
## Who is eligible?
The Authorised Push Payment (APP) protections apply to all UK customers, including individuals, charities with annual income <£1,000,000, and microenterprises (a business with no more than 10 employees or less than €2,000,000 in revenue).

## What types of payment are eligible?
- Payments made to a UK account number and sort code
- Instant fiat currency (e.g. GBP, EUR) transfers from your UK Revolut account to another Revolut account

## What's the maximum I can claim?
You're eligible to be reimbursed for all claims up to £120,000.

## Is there an excess on my reimbursement?
We'll apply a £100 excess per claim for all potential losses.

## Can I apply for reimbursement on old transactions?
Only transactions made on or after 7 October 2024 will be eligible for reimbursement. You must raise a claim no later than 13 months after the last suspected fraudulent transaction.
You won’t receive a refund if you are found to have been complicit in the fraud or grossly negligent in accordance with the Customer Standard of Caution. 
This specific exception, along with any applicable excess, does not apply if we determine that you were a vulnerable customer at the time of the APP scam payment and that this vulnerability impacted your ability to protect yourself from the scam.

title: Authorised Push Payment fraud reimbursement exclusions
content_text:
The following cases won't be accepted for Authorised Push Payment (APP) reimbursements:
- First-party fraud: If you intentionally engage in fraud or work directly with the fraudster — including if you've lied or misrepresented your circumstances for financial gain — you won't be reimbursed
- International payments: Any payment made overseas to an account held outside of the UK won't be eligible for reimbursement
- Receiving accounts in control of the customer: Payments you've made to another account that you control won't be eligible for reimbursement. This includes any accounts you hold with crypto exchanges
- Card, cash, or cheques: Any payments made by cards or with cash or cheques won't be eligible for reimbursement
- Civil disputes: Any payment made to a genuine retailer or business where you aren't happy with the product or service you've received
- Investments: Any payment made as an investment that isn't deemed to be fraudulent
- Credit unions: Any payments made to a credit union, municipal bank, or national savings bank

title: Authorised Push Payment fraud reimbursement requirements
content_text:
To be eligible for reimbursement, you must meet all of the following requirements: 

## You must be truthful
If your payment request triggers Revolut's scam warning flows, you must have regard to all warnings provided by Revolut and be truthful in all your answers to the questions presented to you.
 If you don't answer truthfully, and as a result, this prevents us from providing appropriate levels of intervention, we may not reimburse you. This is because you may not have acted with the appropriate standard of caution when making the payment.

## You must conduct basic checks before payment
If the payment triggers Revolut's scam warning flows, you must complete all the checks requested by Revolut in order to make the payment.
If you don't conduct these checks or you aren't truthful when asked if you've conducted these checks, then it may prevent us from providing appropriate levels of intervention, and we may not reimburse you. This is because you may not have acted with caution when making the payment.

## You must share all relevant information
If you believe you have‌ been defrauded, then you must provide all relevant information requested in order for us to assess the claim. This includes screenshots, information about the scammers, and where the fraud originated. If you don't provide the information we request, we may not reimburse you.

## You must act promptly
If you've been defrauded, then you must report the fraud within 13 months of the last transaction. If you don't report the fraud to us within this timeframe, we may not reimburse you.

## You must authorise the claim to be raised with the police
If you've been defrauded, we may wish to raise the issue with authorities. We can make this claim on your behalf, unless you want to make it yourself or already have done so. If we ask for your permission to raise your claim with the police and you decline, we may not reimburse you.
If we assess that you were a vulnerable customer at the time of the APP scam payment, and this has had a material impact on your ability to protect yourself from the scam, then the Consumer Standard of Caution won't apply to you.
If we don't believe you have met all of these standards, you may be found to be grossly negligent and denied reimbursement.

title: Authorised Push Payment fraud reimbursement
content_text:
## What is an Authorised Push Payment scam?
An Authorised Push Payment (APP) scam is when a scammer misleads you into sending your own money via bank transfer to an account they control. They've either pretended to be someone you intended to pay, or tricked you into making a payment for something you didn't intend to. 

## Rights under the new APP scam reimbursement requirement
From 7 October 2024, any payments that fall under the APP scam category will be considered for reimbursement.

title: How long will the Authorised Push Payment fraud reimbursement process take?
content_text:
If your claim is valid and both you and other payment firms provide us with relevant information to assess your claim, we'll typically reimburse you within 5 business days of you reporting it. 
This time limit is subject to you providing us with relevant information to assess your claim. However, some more complex claims may take up to 35 business days to be reimbursed. This will only happen when we need extra time to gather information from you or the bank that received the payment. We'll let you know if we need extra time to assess your claim.

title: Locked bootloader requirement for Android devices
content_text:
## What is a bootloader?
A bootloader is the software that starts a device's operating system. A locked bootloader prevents a device from loading unauthorised software, and ensures only the official, unmodified OS can run. Unlocking the bootloader could put a device at risk.

## How to check the bootloader status
- Open 'Settings' and tap 'Developer options'
- Look for the OEM unlocking toggle
- The position of the toggle will indicate whether the bootloader is locked
These steps may vary depending on the device manufacturer. Contact your manufacturer's help centre if you need additional support.

## How to enable developer options
- Open 'Settings'
- Go to 'About phone' and select 'Software information'
- Tap 'Build number' seven times until you see confirmation that developer options have been enabled
- Return to 'Settings'. 'Developer options' should now be listed in the menu
These steps may vary depending on the device manufacturer. Contact your manufacturer's help centre if you need additional support.

## How to relock the bootloader
Relocking an unlocked bootloader can be complicated. The steps vary depending on the device and manufacturer, and attempting it yourself can cause problems if it's done incorrectly.
For more technical details, check the official Android documentationhttps://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/bootloader/locking_unlocking.
If you're not sure how to safely relock the bootloader, contact your device manufacturer’s official help centre or the shop where you purchased the device for professional support.

title: Contacting your device manufacturer
content_text:
## Getting expert help with your device
A device's manufacturer is the expert on its specific hardware and firmware. For technical processes like restoring an operating system, the manufacturer is the most reliable source of support.

## When to contact a manufacturer
Contact your device manufacturer's support centre for device related issues, or if you need help:
- Installing the latest official security updates
- Locking the bootloader
- Restoring official firmware
- Backing up data and performing a factory reset

title: Ensuring security by using the official Revolut app
content_text:
To ensure an account is secure and has the best experience, it's essential to only install Revolut applications from official, trusted sources. 
Apps downloaded from other websites or third-party stores may be modified, contain malware, or lack the latest security features.

## Updates and continuous security improvements
Revolut app updates may include security improvements, so keeping the app up to date helps protect your account. To check if your app is up to date, go to the official store you downloaded it from and check whether an update is available. 

## Always use official app stores
To make sure the correct and most secure version of the Revolut app is installed, install or update the application directly from one of the following official stores:
- App Store 
- Google Play
- Huawei App Gallery

title: Operating system and security updates
content_text:
## Keeping your operating system up to date
- Over time, new security weaknesses may be found on mobile devices
- Manufacturers release operating system updates and security patches to address these issues
- Keeping your device updated keeps your device protected, and performing well 

## How to check for updates
These steps may vary depending on your device
- Go to 'Settings' on your device
- Look for or search 'Software update' or similar

## Step-by-step instructions
For detailed instructions, check your manufacturer’s official guides:
- Apple guidehttps://support.apple.com/en-us/118575
- Google guidehttps://support.google.com/android/answer/7680439

## I can't update or find the update option
If you're not sure how to update your device, get in touch with your device manufacturer’s support centre, or visit an official retail store for help.

title: Security risks of root access
content_text:
## What is root access?
Root access means gaining administrative privileges on a device. While this allows deep customisation of the device, it bypasses the standard Android security model and puts the entire system at risk.

## How does enabling root access put my device at risk?
Enabling root access puts the whole device and operating system at risk. It allows applications to operate outside their normal permissions, which can compromise the data of all applications on the device. For security reasons, Revolut applications cannot trust devices with granted root access.

## How can I disable root access?
The procedure to remove root access and restore a device's original security requires advanced knowledge and support from the device manufacturer.
If you're not sure how to safely perform this procedure, contact the device manufacturer’s official support team for professional assistance.

title: Unofficial (custom) firmware
content_text:
## What is unofficial firmware?
Unofficial firmware is third-party software that isn't supported by the manufacturer. It may have lower security standards, cause compatibility issues with hardware or apps, and be less reliable than official firmware.

## Platform security certification
Official firmware is provided by manufacturers who are certified partners of the platform, such as Google for Android devices and Apple for iOS devices.
Certification ensures that devices meet strict requirements for security, performance, and compatibility.
For further information, see the Google Play Protect certified deviceshttps://android.com/certified/partners/ and Apple Platform Security certificationshttps://support.apple.com/guide/security/certifications-and-compliance-sec59b0b31ff/web.

## How to restore official firmware
Replacing custom firmware with original firmware is a technical task, that can damage your device if done incorrectly.
If you're not sure how to safely restore the official firmware, contact your device manufacturer’s help centre for professional support.

title: Why is the Revolut app no longer supported on my device?
content_text:
App security is always improving to help protect against new and evolving threats.
A recent update might have added stricter checks to make the app experience safer and more reliable.
As a result of these stricter security standards, your device may no longer meet the requirements needed to support the Revolut app, and you might need to make some changes to continue using it.

## How to meet device integrity requirements
For the app to run securely, your device needs to meet all of the following requirements:
- Be using the official Revolut app, downloaded from a trusted storehttps://help.revolut.com/help/security-logging-in/device-integrity-requirements/official-application-requirement/?geo-redirect=true
- Have the latest OS and security updates installedhttps://help.revolut.com/help/security-logging-in/device-integrity-requirements/operating-system-and-security-updates/?geo-redirect=true
- Have a locked bootloaderhttps://help.revolut.com/help/security-logging-in/device-integrity-requirements/bootloader-lock-requirement-android/?geo-redirect=true
- Be using official, certified firmware (no custom ROMs)https://help.revolut.com/help/security-logging-in/device-integrity-requirements/unofficial-custom-firmware/?geo-redirect=true
- Have no unlocked root accesshttps://help.revolut.com/help/security-logging-in/device-integrity-requirements/root-access-restrictions/?geo-redirect=true

title: Review my information with CIFAS Marker
content_text:
CIFAS is the UK's fraud prevention service, a non-profit organisation with a database of fraudulent activities. Over 260 members across banking, credit cards, finance, retail, insurance, and telecommunications use this information to reduce fraud and financial loss.

## For your information with CIFAS to be reviewed:
Contact us at marker-disputes@revolut.com with all the relevant details about your account and explain why you believe the CIFAS warning should be removed. Our experts will review your case and provide a Final Response Letter via email.

## If you disagree with Revolut's final decision:
If you don't agree with our decision, the next step is to submit a formal complaint to CIFAS. You can find more information on how to do this on the CIFAS official websitehttps://www.cifas.org.uk/contact-us/i-want-to-make-a-complaint.

title: Understanding the account review process
content_text:
## Why is my account under review?
As a regulated financial institution, we’re required to carry out periodic account reviews. We understand this may be inconvenient, so we aim to do this as quickly as possible.

## What happens during the review?
We verify your account information to ensure compliance with regulations, and we carry out routine security checks. During this time, some account activities may be temporarily restricted.

## Do I need to do anything?
At this stage, no action is required from you. Rest assured, if we need additional information, we’ll let you know.

title: Fraud and cybercrime reporting to national authorities
content_text:
If you have been a victim of fraud, your first step should always be to submit a fraud report. Afterwards, use the guidance below to report the incident to the appropriate national authorities.

## United Kingdom
- Report to Action Fraud, the UK’s national centre for fraud and cybercrime, via actionfraud.police.uk
- Call their helpline on 0300 123 2040

title: Hidden accounts and transactions
content_text:
Revolut allows you to hide specific accounts and transactions behind an extra layer of security.

## How do I hide an account?
- Go to 'Accounts' and select the one you want to hide
- Swipe left on it
- Tap the 'Eye icon' next to it to hide it
You can access your hidden accounts by going to 'Accounts' and scrolling to the 'Hidden accounts folder'. You will need to perform a biometric check to access your hidden account folder.
Hidden account balances are excluded from your total wealth. To add them back, simply unhide the account.

## Which accounts can be hidden?
- Personal accounts (except main accounts), Pockets, Savings accounts, Joint accounts (including Joint Pockets and Savings), Pro accounts, Kids & Teens accounts (including Pockets and Savings), and linked accounts

## How do I hide a transaction?
- Go to your 'Transaction list'
- Swipe left on the transaction you want to hide
- Tap the 'Eye icon' next to it to hide it
You can access the list of your hidden transactions from 'Security hub', tapping 'Hidden transactions'. You will be asked for a biometric check to access them.
Hidden transactions are excluded from analytics, but not from your balance.

title: Someone wants to give you a lot of money?
content_text:
"I got an email from a lawyer in another country saying a distant cousin had passed away and left me GBP 50,000. It sounded so real. He sent legal documents and said I just needed to pay GBP 450 for a Certificate of Ownership."
"I paid it, but then he said the account needed another GBP 800 for clearance. That’s when I realized the GBP 50,000 was never coming and I’d just been throwing my own money away."

## Summary
This situation involves a form of fraud where you are led to believe that a significant, life-changing windfall is waiting for you. You may receive an unexpected email, letter, or a phone call from someone posing as a lawyer, a government official, or a representative from a lottery board. 
You are advised you have won a sweepstake, you are the beneficiary of a distant relative’s vast estate, or you have been approved for a loan or high-paying job. The goal is to trigger a state of intense optimism, making you focus on the potential payout rather than the logic of the situation.
The scammer claims a reward is ready but requires small, up-front fees. After the first payment, they invent new problems requiring larger payments. This trap drives victims to keep paying, hoping the next fee will unlock the prize. The cycle continues until you stop or run out of money.

## Common red flags
- No legitimate windfall requires upfront payment: If you are asked to pay a fee to receive a prize, loan, inheritance, or sweepstakes winnings that you did not explicitly apply for, it is a scam. Legitimate organizations deduct fees from the winnings, if necessary
- Be skeptical of unexpected miracles: If a financial opportunity arrives entirely unsolicited and promises immense wealth for no effort, treat it as a definitive trap
- Be wary of secrecy: Scammers often insist the offer is confidential and demand you do not tell anyone. This is a tactic to isolate you from trusted advisors who would spot the fraud

## Safety best practices
- Research the offer and sender: Use a search engine to look up the name of the lottery, law firm, or organization making the offer, along with the word scam. If it is a known scheme, results will appear immediately
- Examine communication quality: Check the email address for typos, incorrect grammar, or a generic sender. Professional institutions use official domain names
- Consult a trusted third party: Before making any payments speak to a friend, a family member, or a professional advisor. Scammers rely on the 'tunnel vision' caused by excitement. An outside perspective is the fastest way to break the psychological hold of the scam and see the red flags

title: Strange small charges on your card?
content_text:
"I noticed a transaction for GBP 0.15 from a company abroad. I almost ignored it because it was so small. But then I saw another one for GBP 1.00. I froze my card through the Revolut app and called the fraud team. It turned out my card was part of a 'BIN attack'."
"The scammers were testing thousands of numbers. Because I caught the 'penny test', I stopped them before they could make a GBP 2,000 purchase at an electronics store."

## Summary
This situation involves a highly technical and automated form of fraud known as a BIN ('issuer identification number') attack. The BIN refers to the first 6–8 digits of a credit or debit card, which are public and identify the specific issuer and card type.
Criminals use sophisticated software to generate thousands of potential combinations to guess the remaining digits of a card number, along with its expiry date and the 3-digit security code (CVV).
These attacks typically begin with what is known as a 'micro-transaction' or 'penny test'. You might notice a tiny, unrecognised charge from an unrecognised company. Once they confirm a card is live, they authorise much larger purchases before you even realise your details have been compromised.

## Common red flags
- Monitor the 'penny tests': Regularly review your transaction history for small, unrecognised charges. A tiny charge is a definitive warning sign that a criminal has successfully guessed your card details and is testing your account
- Scrutinise recurring fees: Scammers often hide unauthorised spending as small monthly 'subscriptions' (for example, GBP 4.99). Periodically check your active card subscriptions and cancel anything you did not explicitly authorise
- Verify website security: Be cautious of where you enter your card details. If a website looks unprofessional or lacks a secure https:// connection, do not enter your card information

## Safety best practices
- Use the 'Freeze' feature immediately: If you see any transaction you do not recognise, no matter how small, do not wait to contact us. Use the 'Freeze card' feature in-app instantly. This stops all further activity while you investigate
- Use virtual cards for online shopping: For purchases on new or less-familiar websites, use a virtual or disposable card. These have a different number from your physical card. If a virtual card is caught in an attack, you can simply delete it and create a new one without affecting your main card
- Pay with secure digital wallets: Avoid saving your physical card details on multiple small websites. Use services like Apple Pay or Google Pay, which use tokenisation to process payments without sharing your actual card number with the merchant

title: Feeling pressured or threatened?
content_text:
"A man called saying he was from the tax department and that I'd made a mistake on a filing from 4 years ago. He said the police were on their way to my house right now, but I could stop the arrest if I paid the GBP 2,000 fine immediately."
"I was so terrified of going to jail that I immediately sent the money. It was only after I realised the police would never ask for tax money over the phone."

## Summary
This situation involves a form of fraud that weaponizes threats to bypass your logic. Scammers posing as police, tax, or court officials, call to claim there is an arrest warrant for you, relying on creating immediate, intense panic or fear.
Another common and deeply distressing variant is 'sextortion'. In this scenario, a criminal sends an email claiming they have hacked your computer’s webcam and recorded you. They threaten to send the footage to your contact list, including family, friends, and employers, unless you pay to stop.
Criminals know that when you are in a panic, you are more likely to seek the fastest possible resolution rather than questioning the validity of the threat. They will demand that you remain on the line, isolate you from others, and insist on immediate payment.

## Common red flags
- Identify the pressure tactic and remember that real authorities follow formal, documented legal procedures, and a legitimate official will always allow you time to seek legal advice or verify a claim through official channels
- Check the method of payment and keep in mind that no legitimate government agency, police force, or court will ever demand a fine or settlement via gift cards, as these offer no formal dispute process for recovery

## Safety best practices
- Terminate the contact immediately: If you receive a call or email that makes you feel threatened or ashamed, stop the interaction. Hang up the phone or close the email. Disconnecting is the fastest way to break the scammer's psychological hold over you
- Verify the claim independently: If you are worried about a legal threat, do not use any contact information provided by the person threatening you. Instead, look up the official phone number for your local police station or the relevant government department on a trusted website
- Seek support: Scammers rely on your shame to keep you quiet. Talk to a trusted friend or family member about the interaction. An outside perspective will help you see the holes in the scammer's story. If you are being blackmailed, contact the police or victim support organisations

title: Urgent text from a kid or friend?
content_text:
"I got a message from 'my daughter' saying she'd lost her phone and was using a friend's phone to message me. She sounded so stressed. She asked me to send GBP 200 to an account so she had access to some money and could resolve the situation."
"I felt so bad for her that I did it immediately. 10 minutes later, my actual daughter called me from her real phone. I realised I'd just sent GBP 200 to a stranger."

## Summary
This situation typically begins with a message from an unrecognised phone number or via social media. The criminal poses as a loved one, or close friend and introduces an urgent financial crisis that supposedly cannot be resolved because they have no access to their device or money. 
They might claim they have an overdue bill that must be paid within the hour, or that they are stranded and need money for emergency travel. They rely on you acting out of panic rather than logic. They will ask you to transfer funds to a friend's account that actually belongs to a fraudster.
The fraud is usually only discovered hours later when you finally speak to your actual relative on their original, working phone, by which time the stolen money has been cleared out of the recipient account.

## Common red flags
- Requests for secrecy and speed: The scammer will insist the situation is urgent and that you must transfer the money immediately to a new or unknown account. You should always take a moment to pause and verify the story before making any financial transfer
- Rejecting voice calling: If the person claims they can only text because their 'microphone is broken', treat this as a major red flag. In a real emergency, a loved one will almost always find a way to let you hear their voice
- Challenge with a personal question: If you cannot reach the person by phone, reply to the message or call with a question only the real loved one would know the answer to. Scammers who have only hacked an account will typically fail this check

## Safety best practices
- Verify the emergency using a different channel: If you receive an urgent request for money from a family member via text or social media message, immediately try to contact them through a known, independent method. Call their official mobile phone number or speak to another close family member
- Never transfer to an unknown account: Do not transfer money, RevPoints, or cryptocurrency to any new account or account details provided in an unsolicited message. Legitimate family members will not typically ask you to use non-refundable methods for an emergency payment
- Secure the compromised account: If you confirm the message is fraudulent, contact the social media platform or mobile provider immediately to report the profile and secure the compromised account to prevent the scammer from targeting others using your loved one's identity

title: Looking for a high return?
content_text:
"I saw an ad on social media for a crypto trading group. The coach seemed so knowledgeable and shared screenshots of people making millions. I started with GBP 500 and my dashboard showed it grew to GBP 5,000 in a week."
"I kept investing more and more. When I tried to withdraw, they told me I had to pay a GBP 10,000 'tax' first. I paid it, and then they stopped replying."

## Summary
This situation involves a highly sophisticated attack with promise of financial returns with little to no risk. Scammers typically target victims through professional-looking social media ads, often featuring AI-generated videos of celebrities or trusted financial experts endorsing a new platform. 
Once you engage, you're directed to a convincing trading portal that appears to show your balance increasing exponentially in sectors like cryptocurrency, gold, or foreign exchange. The profits you see are fabricated, designed to trigger your excitement and build a false sense of security.
The goal of the scammer is to convince you to deposit as much of your capital as possible, often pressuring you to maximize the opportunity. The fraud is usually revealed only when you attempt to withdraw your funds, which can be months or years later.

## Common red flags
- It sounds too good to be true: Be highly skeptical of any investment opportunity promising guaranteed high returns with little to no risk. All legitimate investments carry risk. Scammers use urgency to prevent you from doing proper research. Do not feel pressured to invest immediately or secretly
- Be extremely skeptical of social media ads: Scammers pay for sponsored posts on Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok because they know they can reach a wide audience. Just because an ad appears on a major platform or features a recognizable face does not mean it is legitimate
- Beware of 'cloned' websites: Scammers often create websites that look nearly identical to real, famous investment firms but have a slightly different URL. Always type the website address in yourself rather than clicking a link provided in an ad or private message

## Safety best practices
- Verify platform registration: Before investing, verify the company or platform's registration status with official financial regulators in your region. If they are not registered, do not invest
- Reject 'expert' mentorship on messaging apps: Be highly wary of anyone who contacts you on Telegram, WhatsApp, or LinkedIn offering 'exclusive trading signals' or a 'personal wealth coach'. Legitimate investment firms do not recruit via private messaging apps
- Consult an independent financial advisor: Before making a major financial commitment to a new platform, speak to a qualified, independent advisor who has no connection to the opportunity. Taking a moment is the best defense against high-pressure sales tactics and emotional manipulation

title: Paying a bill with new bank details?
content_text:
"We were paying our builder for our new kitchen. He sent an invoice for £8,000. 5 minutes later, we got another email saying, 'Use this account instead, the other one is for our old business.'"
"We didn't think twice and sent the money. A week later the builder called asking for payment. The second email was from a hacker who had been watching his account. Our money was gone."

## Summary
This situation typically begins when a criminal successfully gains unauthorised access to the email account of a supplier you are already dealing with — such as a builder, a solicitor, or a small business owner.
