The deception occurs when you receive an email that appears to be part of your ongoing thread with the supplier. The criminal sends an updated invoice or a follow-up message claiming that their account details have recently changed due to an error with their usual account.
Because the email comes from the correct address and references specific details about your project or purchase, it is incredibly convincing. You authorise the payment and only discover the fraud weeks later when the real supplier contacts you to ask why their bill hasn't been paid.

## Common red flags
- Scrutinise email sender: Check the 'From' address carefully. Scammers often use email addresses that are one letter off from the real address
- Look for pressure and secrecy: Be wary of emails demanding immediate payment or claiming the bank details must be kept confidential due to a recent merger or audit
- Inspect the invoice format: Look closely at the PDF or invoice document for signs of tampering. Blurry logos, different font styles in the bank details section, or spelling mistakes are common indicators that a legitimate invoice has been intercepted and hastily edited by a criminal

## Safety best practices
- Verify bank details by phone: If you receive a payment request that includes a change to the supplier's bank account number, always verify the change by calling a known, pre-existing phone number for that company. Do not use the phone number listed on the email or invoice
- Confirm the total amount: If the invoiced amount is slightly different from the expected or usual amount, use this as a trigger to verify the details
- Use company portals when possible: Pay via a dedicated, secure payment portal or platform run by the company, rather than relying on bank transfer details provided in an email

title: Have you been offered money to move funds?
content_text:
"I saw a post on social media for a 'Payment Processing Assistant'. It sounded perfect for a student. I just had to receive money into my account, keep 10%, and send the rest to a crypto wallet. I did it 3 times and made GBP 300."
"Then my account was blocked. I found out the money was stolen from someone else’s account. Now I have a fraud marker on my file, I've been kicked out of university, and I can't even get an account or a phone contract."

## Summary
This situation occurs when a person is recruited to use their personal account to receive and transfer stolen money. This rarely looks criminal at first. Instead, it is often disguised as a legitimate home-job offer, an investment agent role, or even a request from someone you have met.
The pitch is almost always the same: you receive a payment into your account, keep a small percentage as a 'commission' or 'fee', and then transfer the remaining balance to a different account or buy cryptocurrency with it.
Even if you are unaware that the money is the proceeds of crime, by allowing your account to be used this way, you are legally participating in money laundering. Criminals use mules to create a 'break' in the digital trail, making it harder for the police to track stolen funds back to the source.

## Common red flags
- Ignore 'Easy Cash' job ads: Be extremely wary of job descriptions that involve 'processing payments', 'transferring funds', or 'acting as a local agent' for an overseas company. No legitimate business will ever ask an employee to use a personal account to handle company money
- Beware of social media requests: Scammers often target students and young people on social media with ads promising 'Quick Cash' or 'Same Day Payouts'. They may also pose as a friend-of-a-friend who 'can't access their own account' and needs you to receive a refund for them

## Safety best practices
- Your account is for your money only: This is the golden rule of personal finance. Never allow anyone else to use your account, regardless of how much you trust them or how desperate their story sounds. If you move stolen money, you are the one held legally responsible for the transaction
- Research every opportunity: Before accepting any role that involves handling money, search for the company name online alongside the word 'scam'. If the business has no physical office, no registered phone number, or a very recent website, it is likely a front for criminal activity

title: Received an unexpected link?
content_text:
"I was expecting a birthday gift from a friend abroad, so when I got a text about a GBP 2 shipping fee, it seemed totally normal. I clicked the link, it looked just like a courier site, and I entered my card details."
"2 hours later, someone impersonating Revolut called me. They had my login details from the link and just needed my one-time passcode to 'secure' my account. I gave it to them, and they cleared my savings in minutes. The link was the first step, the call was the second."

## Summary
This situation typically begins with an unsolicited communication, either an email or a text message, that appears to be from a trusted organisation such as a courier service, a government department, a utility provider, or even a popular subscription service like Netflix or Amazon. 
The message is designed to trigger an immediate emotional response, usually by claiming there is an urgent problem that requires your attention. Common hooks include an 'Unpaid shipping fee', a 'Tax rebate', or a 'Security breach' that requires you to verify your identity.
These messages contain a link leading to a 'Clone' website and request you enter personal details, card information, or account login details to resolve the problem. They can use this information to perform a complete account takeover, drain your savings, or sell your identity.

## Common red flags
- Never log in via a link: This is the most important rule. We will never send you a direct link to a login page in a text or email. If you receive a message about your account, always ignore the link and log in through our official app or by manually typing our web address into your browser
- Analyse the sender’s address: Scammers often use URLs one letter off from the real thing (for example, support@revoIut.com using a capital 'I' instead of an 'L'). Check every character carefully. If the URL looks odd, it is likely a scam
- Identify the small fee trap: Be wary of tiny payments like delivery fees. The scammer’s goal isn’t the small amount, it’s to trick you into entering your card details to attempt larger, unauthorised transactions

## Safety best practices
- Treat urgency as a threat: If a message threatens that your account will be suspended, frozen, or that you will face legal action if you don't click immediately, it is a scam. Real organisations follow formal, slower procedures and will never use high-pressure, threatening tactics
- Protect your personal data: Be cautious about verification forms that ask for your personal data or common security question answers. Scammers can use the details gathered to impersonate you
- Use a password manager: Utilising a password manager helps you identify phishing sites because the manager will not auto-fill your login details on a fake URL, even if the site looks identical to the real one

title: Buying something online?
content_text:
"I found a console that was sold out everywhere else for retail price on a social media marketplace. The seller seemed nice and sent a photo of the box."
"They asked me to pay via bank transfer to 'save on fees'. I sent the money, and they blocked me immediately. I never got the console and couldn't get my money back."

## Summary
Fraudulent purchases often start online, when you find items on social media marketplaces or a classifieds website. The listings are typically highly professional, featuring high-resolution images and a seller profile that appears trustworthy, often with numerous positive reviews.
Scammers lure victims with an unbeatable price or immediate availability for an item. To rush the transaction and bypass security checks, they create high-velocity urgency, claiming they are moving, have an emergency, or that many others are interested in the item.
The seller will often pressure you to pay via a non-reversible method such as cryptocurrency or gift cards, claiming these methods allow them to keep the price low by avoiding business fees. Once the funds are sent they may immediately block you, or cease all contact, and the item never arrives.

## Common red flags
- Be sceptical of extreme discounts: If a price for a high-demand item is significantly lower than retail value across all other platforms, treat it as a major red flag
- Urgency: If a seller is pressuring you to pay right now, or claiming they will move to the next person, they are likely trying to stop you from performing due diligence. Take 5 minutes to step away from the screen. Scammers rely on your fear of missing out to make you ignore your gut instincts

## Safety best practices
- Use a protected payment method: Never use non-reversible methods like direct bank transfers, gift cards, or cryptocurrency for purchases, as these prevent disputes and result in immediate loss of your money
- Verify the seller and platform: Before buying, check the seller's rating, transaction history, and contact information. If buying from a dedicated website, look for legitimate contact details (phone, physical address) and a secure checkout (indicated by https:// in the URL)
- Insist on meeting in public (for local ads): If you are buying through a local marketplace, never pay for the item before you have physically seen and inspected it. If the seller makes excuses about why they can only ship the item, walk away from the deal

title: Met someone new online?
content_text:
"He told me he was an engineer working on a project overseas. We talked every day for 3 months. He felt like my best friend. Then he said his equipment was seized by customs and he’d lose his job if he didn't pay the fine.
I sent him £2,000. Then he needed more for the 'shipping'. When I finally said no, he disappeared and blocked me."

## Summary
In a romance scam, scammers often use a tactic called 'love bombing', where they overwhelm you with intense affection, constant messaging, and premature promises of a shared future or marriage. They can keep up this fictitious persona for multiple months to effectively draw you in.
This process is designed to lower your natural defences and create a sense of absolute trust. Once you are emotionally invested, the criminal introduces a 'manufactured crisis'. They weaponise the empathy and love you feel for them to create a sense of urgency.
They will ask for a loan, promising to pay you back the moment they return. However, the first request is rarely the last, if you pay once, new 'emergencies' arise, continuing until you are financially drained or begin to ask too many questions, at which point the scammer vanishes instantly.

## Common red flags
- Identify the signs of 'love bombing': Be wary of someone who professes deep commitment, love, or the desire to marry you within days of meeting online. While it feels flattering, this is a calculated psychological tactic used to bypass your logic
- Moving off trusted platforms: Move slowly if they immediately ask you to switch from a dating app to a private messaging platform. This is often done to avoid detection by the app's security measures and to prevent other customers from reporting their activity
- Refusing video chat: If they refuse to video chat, offer constant excuses, or their camera quality is consistently poor, this is a major red flag. Legitimate partners will usually want to see you

## Safety best practices
- Never send money to someone you haven't met: No matter how much you care for them, how long you have been talking, or how tragic their situation sounds, never send money to an online contact. A genuine partner will never treat a new relationship as a source of capital
- Verify their identity: Perform a reverse image search on any photos they send. Scammers frequently use or manipulate images stolen from models, military personnel, or public figures
- Seek an outside perspective: Scammers thrive on isolation. They will often tell you to keep the relationship a 'secret' because 'others won't understand our love'. Break this silence by talking to a trusted friend or family member about the person

title: My phone suddenly lost signal
content_text:
"I was at home and suddenly my phone went to 'No Service.' I thought it was just a network glitch. I didn't realise until the next morning that someone had called my mobile provider, pretended to be me, and moved my number to their SIM."
"They used my phone number to reset my account password and stole £3,000 while I was asleep. Because I had no signal, I didn't get any of the account's alert texts until it was too late."

## Summary
SIM Swap fraud is a silent but devastating attack where a criminal steals your mobile identity. It begins with the criminal gathering your personal data, like your full name, date of birth, and address, through social media or phishing. 
They then contact your mobile provider, impersonate you, and claim they have lost their phone. They trick the provider into porting your phone number to a new SIM card that is physically in the criminal’s possession.
The moment the switch happens, your phone will suddenly lose all signal, displaying 'SOS only' or 'SIM not provisioned'. While you are disconnected and troubleshooting your 'Network glitch', the criminal is using your phone to reset your account passwords and authorise transfers.

## Common red flags
- Act on signal loss immediately: If your phone loses signal unexpectedly in a place you usually have full service, do not assume it’s a network error. Contact your mobile provider immediately from another phone to check if a SIM swap has been requested
- Monitor your email: Scammers often reset your email password first to monitor your account alerts. If you see 'New login' notifications for your email, change your passwords and check your mobile signal instantly

## Safety best practices
- Set a SIM PIN: Add a 4-digit PIN to your SIM card in your phone settings. This ensures that even if a criminal tries to move your number or use your physical SIM in another device, it cannot be used without the code
- Move to authenticator apps: Whenever possible, switch your 2-Factor Authentication (2FA) from SMS texts to a reliable authenticator app. These are tied to your physical hardware, not your phone number, making them immune to SIM swaps
- Protect your device: Ensure your phone is protected by a strong passcode and biometric security. Familiarise yourself with 'Find My Device'. If your phone is stolen, these tools stop a thief from accessing your apps to prevent fraudulent access

title: An urgent call out of the blue?
content_text:
"A man called saying he was from my internet provider and that my router was being used by hackers. He was so patient and spent 2 hours 'helping' me. He asked me to download an app so he could fix the security.
I saw my mouse moving on its own, but he said that was just him 'running scans'. While I was on the phone, he logged into my account, saw my balance, and used my phone to get the codes. By the time I hung up, my account was empty."

## Summary
Voice phishing, or 'vishing', is a form of impersonation scam that occurs when a criminal calls you pretending to be an authority figure. They frequently pose as Revolut employees, police officers, or IT technicians.
The call is designed to induce extreme panic. They may tell you that your account is currently being drained by a hacker or that your computer has a virus stealing your login details.
The goal is to trick you into performing a high-risk action while you are in a state of alarm. They might tell you to move your funds to a 'safe account', ask you to read back a one-time passcode, or persuade you to download software that gives them remote control over your device.

## Common red flags
- The 'safe account' is a lie: we will never ask you to move your money to a new or 'safe' account to protect it. If anyone on a call tells you to transfer funds for security reasons, it is 100% a scam. Hang up immediately
- Hang up and wait: scammers can 'hold the line' open on landlines even after you hang up. If you receive a call you're unsure about, wait at least 10 minutes before making a new call, or use a different phone entirely to ensure you are not still connected to the scammer
- Be wary of secrecy: if a caller tells you not to talk to Revolut staff or to lie to them about the reason for a transfer, they are trying to hide their crime. Real investigators encourage transparency and formal verification

## Safety best practices
- Codes are for you only: never read a one-time passcode (OTP) back to someone over the phone, even if they claim to be from our fraud team. Real Revolut employees will never ask for this code, it is the final key used to authorise a payment
- Reject remote access: never download software at the request of a caller. These tools allow a criminal to see your screen, watch you type passwords, and move your mouse to perform transactions while you watch
- Use the call checker in the Revolut app: your app includes a live detection banner that appears the moment you open it while on a phone call. This system cross-references the call with our internal systems to verify if the person you are speaking with is a legitimate Revolut employee

title: How can I keep my account safe from fraud?
content_text:
If you get a suspicious call, email, link, or text from someone pretending to be from Revolut, stay vigilant and don't click any links. This keeps your information safe and helps us combat cybercrime.

## Best practices when shopping online
- Be wary of 'too good to be true' offers
- Never share PINs, passwords, or passcodes via phone or chat
- Verify websites and sellers through reviews
- Inspect high-value items in person or via video call with documentation
- Type URLs directly, avoid unsolicited links
- Pause payment if Revolut flags payee issues
If you've fallen victim to a scam, follow the below steps.

## Secure your Revolut account
- Stop communication with the scammer
- Freeze your card by tapping the card icon in the top-right corner, selecting the card you want to freeze, and tapping 'Freeze'. Alternatively, you can terminate it by going to 'Settings' and tapping 'Terminate card'
- We recommend reporting the matter to the police

## Report the fraudulent transaction in-app
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Select the transaction from your transaction listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Select 'Get help'
- Choose the most relevant option

title: How to report a suspicious call, email, link, or text
content_text:
Revolut will never ask you to make any payments or ask you to share any verification or security codes over the phone, or call you without booking the call and confirming it with you in advance.

## How to keep your account safe
- Reach out to Revolut Support via the in-app chat to confirm whether the contact is legitimate or not
- For emails, check the email address of the sender for typos or suspicious formats. Legitimate emails use official domains (e.g. @revolut.com instead of revolut@gmail.com)
- For calls, check if you're able to see the call details in the of the Revolut app. Support calls from us are carried out directly within the Revolut app, which criminals can’t replicate 
- If you're not sure of the contact's authenticity, don't share your personal or financial information

## What to do if you've fallen victim to a scam
Secure your account:
- Stop all communication with the scammer
- Freeze your card in your Revolut app by tapping the card icon in the top right corner, selecting the card you want to freeze, and tapping 'Freeze'. Alternatively, you can terminate it by going to 'Settings' and tapping 'Terminate card'
- Reach out to Revolut Support via the in-app chat for assistance
- Report the fraudulent transaction in-app. See below for steps on how to do this
- If possible, report the matter to the police

## Report a fraudulent transaction in-app
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Select the transaction from your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Select 'Get help'
- Choose the most relevant option

title: How does Revolut keep my money safe?
content_text:
## What safety measures does Revolut take for UK customers?
Revolut takes extensive measures to safeguard your money, no matter which Revolut entity you’re registered with.

## How does Revolut safeguard money for UK customers?
As an e-money institution in the UK, Revolut LTD is required to safeguard your money.
Your money is held in a safeguarded account with a regulated bank or as high quality liquid assets. Your savings are deposited with partner banks and eligible deposits are protected up to £120k per bank.
Revolut is authorised as a bank in the UK.https://help.revolut.com/help/accounts/what-does-the-uk-bank-licence-mean-for-my-account/ While we roll the bank out to all customers, some customers may have an e-money account, while others have a bank account which is covered by FSCS protectionhttps://help.revolut.com/help/accounts/what-does-the-uk-bank-licence-mean-for-my-account/question-now-that-revolut-is-a-bank-in-the-uk-how-is-my-money-protected/ of up to £120k per person, per bank. To find out which type of account you have, use this linkhttps://help.revolut.com/help/accounts/what-does-the-uk-bank-licence-mean-for-my-account/question-how-will-i-know-when-my-account-has-been-transferred/.

## What are the safety features of Revolut?
Revolut uses a range of robust features to protect your money and personal data, including:
- Strict identity checks
- Biometric security
- Gambling blocks
- Disposable virtual cards 
For more information about how we protect your funds, visit this pagehttps://www.revolut.com/how-we-keep-your-money-safe/?geo-redirect=true.

title: How does Revolut protect me against fraud?
content_text:
We're dedicated to securing your finances from fraudsters. Here's how we do it:

## Secure access protocols
When you attempt to access the Revolut app, our systems check for unusual activity. We use multiple layers of security to keep your account secure.
Sometimes, we'll send you a one-time password (OTP) via SMS.

## In-app fraud warnings
Our vigilant automated security system monitors transactions for signs of potential fraud and scams. If suspicious activity is detected, we'll warn you and ask you for additional information, if needed.
Always take the time to consider your transactions and any warning messages. If in doubt, you can get in touch with us via the in-app chat. Revolut Support is available 24/7.

## Online purchase verification
If the merchant needs you to verify a transaction, we'll notify you. Simply follow the in-app prompts to confirm the request before proceeding with the payment. We may also ask for additional layers of verification, such as two-factor authentication.

## Confirmation for new recipients
Before sending money to a new recipient, we'll ask you to confirm the transfer in-app. This step helps make sure you trust the intended recipient, adding an extra layer of security to your transactions.

## Virtual disposable cards
Our single-use virtual cards offer an extra layer of protection against online threats. As soon as the virtual card is used for a transaction, its details are instantly deleted to prevent unauthorised access. To set up a virtual card in-app, go to the cards section.
For tips on how to proactively prevent fraud, you can visit this FAQ/help/security-logging-in/how-do-i-protect-myself-from-fraudsters/question-how-can-i-prevent-fraud in our Help Centre.

## Wealth Protection
Revolut allows you to set up biometric authentication for withdrawals from your investments, Pockets, Savings, and crypto as an extra layer of protection.

title: Accessing your transaction history from your Revolut account
content_text:
## On account closure
When you close your account, we'll email you with a bank statement showing your full payment and transaction history. This is password protected for your security. We recommend keeping the document and password secure. 
 If you lose this document, follow the process below.

## After your account is closed
- Download the Revolut app
- Tap 'Log In'
- Enter the phone number associated with your account
- Enter your passcode
- Take a selfie to verify your identity
- Enter the one-time 6 digit code which will be texted to your phone number
- You'll have the option to re-open your account and access all of your previous transactions, or you can choose to access your documents and statements
- If you're downloading documents and statements, you'll be able to look through the different documents and statements available, and download whichever files you need
- We hold documents in line with regulatory requirements and cannot guarantee access to documents beyond certain time frames

title: What are linked wallets?
content_text:
Linked wallets are digital wallet apps, like Apple Wallet and Google Wallet, where your Revolut card is linked for easy payments.
Revolut lets you view and manage your cards linked to these wallets, giving you better control over subscriptions, payments, and where your card is used.
As an extra layer of protection, we’ll notify you whenever your Revolut card is linked to a wallet.

## Supported wallets 
- Apple Wallet
- Google Wallet
- Garmin Wallet
- Fitbit Wallet

## Manage linked wallets
To manage your linked wallets in the Revolut mobile app:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select ‘Security’
- Choose ‘Linked wallets’
You’ll be able to view and manage the linked wallets for all your Revolut cards.

## You can also manage linked wallets from your card settings:
- Tap the card icon in the top-right corner
- Select a card
- Tap ‘Settings’
- Choose ‘Linked wallets’
You’ll be able to view and manage the linked wallets for the selected Revolut card.

title: What are Wealth Protection limits?
content_text:
Revolut allows you to set limits for funds leaving your accounts, for example, transfers or account withdrawals. This doesn't include card payments or physical ATM withdrawals. Here's how it works:
- Limits are set by you and can be applied to certain eligible transactions
- The limits then apply to these outgoing transactions
- Limits are flexible, not fixed. If your goals or lifestyle change, you can adjust them anytime
- Your limit will refresh every 24 hours
If you do reach your limit, you can still authorise funds to leave your account by carrying out a selfie check first.

## How to set, manage, or disable limits
- Tap your profile icon in the top left corner 
- Select ‘Security’
- Select Wealth protection
- Select the limit you wish to set — for example, 'Transfers' — and enter a new value to set or change your desired limit, or turn the toggle off to disable your limit

## The transfer limit applies to:
- Transfers between Revolut friends
- Bank transfer
- Transfer links
- Scheduled transfer
- Push-to-card
- Money requests
- Split and Group Bills
- Crypto wallet
- Lifestyle purchases (Stays, Experiences, Donations, Lounges and eSIM)
A withdrawal limit can also be set up for: 
- Savings, Pockets, Commodities, and crypto transactions

## Wealth Protection limits don't apply to:
- ATM withdrawals
- Point of Sale (POS) transactions
- Online ecommerce transactions
- Direct Debits
- Revolut Pay

title: Getting started with Street Mode
content_text:
Street Mode is a transfer limits feature that provides an extra layer of protection against phone theft or coercion. 
Street Mode is disabled by default. Once enabled, you can set up 'Trusted locations'. Outside of these locations, you'll need to wait one hour and perform a biometric check before the transfer can be completed.
This means if your phone is stolen and taken outside a trusted location, your account is protected so that someone else can't access your account or transfer money out of it. 

## How to set up Street Mode
To activate Street Mode in the Revolut app:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Security', 'Wealth Protection', and 'Transfers'
- Enable the 'Street Mode' toggle
- Follow the steps to enable location-based protection and set up your 'Trusted locations'
If you enable Street Mode, you'll need to set a minimum transfer limit.

## To add, amend, or remove a trusted location
- Head to 'Transfers' in the Wealth Protection section of your Security Hub
- Tap 'Trusted locations'
- Select the location you want to amend or remove
- Update location details or tap 'Remove' in the top-right corner
There's no limit to the number of trusted locations you can have at once. You can set up trusted locations by searching for an address within your region, or selecting an address outside of your region directly on the map. To add another trusted location:
- Head to 'Transfers' in the Wealth Protection section of your Security Hub
- Tap 'Trusted locations'
- Tap 'Add new' and follow the steps to add a location

## How to turn off Street Mode
- Head to 'Transfers' in the Wealth Protection section of your Security Hub
- Turn off 'Street Mode' using the toggle on the screen
With Street Mode on, instant services like WERO, Pix, Bizum, BLIK, and iDEAL will be declined if they exceed your limit and are initiated outside of your trusted location. You'll be redirected to your Wealth Protection settings to adjust the limit.
Street Mode is an optional security feature and does not guarantee complete protection.

title: Limits
content_text:
## ATM limits
ATM daily and monthly withdrawal limits apply to your account.

## Daily withdrawal limit
Daily ATM limit applies to the maximum total amount you can withdraw from ATMs globally within a 24-hour timeframe. You cannot remove this limit.

## Monthly withdrawal limit
Monthly ATM limit applies to the maximum total amount you can withdraw from ATMs globally, in a 31-day rolling period. If you are eligible, you can ask for a temporary increase of your limit by tapping the 'Increase limit' button.

title: Trouble with my selfie verification for Wealth Protection
content_text:
The selfie must be a 'live' picture — meaning one that's taken in the moment, rather than a scan or a picture of an existing photo. The selfie must be taken by you and you should be the only person that appears in it. 

## Steps to follow when taking a selfie
- Find good lighting, remove any face coverings (like glasses or hats)
- In your device's settings, approve access to your camera for the Revolut app
- Move your face into the oval in the middle of the screen, then wait until the oval turns green
- Press the image capture button to take a photo (or the photo may be taken automatically)
- Double-check all of the selfie requirements are met
- Hit 'Confirm' and continue with the transaction

## If the selfie verification fails
If we're unable to verify your identity, a chat will be automatically opened and an agent will help you further.
If we suspect it's not you, we'll temporarily restrict your account to protect your security.

## What to do if your account is temporarily restricted
- Follow the in-app prompts to provide any missing information
- Make sure any uploaded documents are clear, valid, and not expired
- Have recent transaction documents ready for potential verification

title: Getting started with Wealth Protection
content_text:
As an extra layer of protection, Revolut allows you to set up biometric authentication for outgoing transfers or withdrawals from your investments, savings, crypto, Pockets, or main balance.
Wealth Protection is disabled by default, but once enabled, we’ll ask for biometric authentication when you make a transfer or withdrawal over your chosen limit from your selected balances or assets.

## Manage Wealth Protection
To activate Wealth Protection in the Revolut mobile app:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Security' and 'Wealth protection'
- Tap 'Agree' and follow the steps. You'll be able to choose which investments or balances are protected
To disable this feature, tap the active option and then tap 'Remove' on the next screen.

## Street Mode
You can also add Street Mode to the Wealth Protection feature. Street Mode protects your money when you’re on the move. It delays transfers over your limits when you’re away from trusted locations, and asks for extra confirmation after that delay, to make sure you’re in control.
Street Mode is disabled by default. To enable it:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Security', 'Wealth protection', and Transfers
- Enable the 'Street Mode' toggle
- Follow the steps to enable location-based protection and set up your 'Trusted locations'

## How Street Mode works
Once active, if you make a transfer over your Wealth Protection limit away from a trusted location:
- We’ll ask for biometric authentication, then delay that transfer by 1 hour
- After the hour, we’ll ask for a second authentication to confirm the transfer. You’ll have 1 hour to complete the second confirmation after the security delay
If you want to know more, visit this FAQ/help/security-logging-in/question-what-is-street-mode.

title: What are Revolut's safety features?
content_text:
At Revolut, the security of your finances is our top priority. We take advanced measures to protect your identity, ensure data privacy, and safeguard your financial assets. 

## Is my Revolut account safe against fraud and scams?
- Strict identity checks: our machine learning technology verifies identities quickly and thoroughly, ensuring the highest level of security and allowing only authorised customers to access their Revolut accounts
- Security hub: go to 'Security' in-app and personalise your settings with 10+ customisable features
- Proactive fraud detection: our machine learning models detect suspicious activity instantly. We'll send you warnings directly in-app 
- Account locking: we may sometimes need to temporarily restrict accounts, as a last resort, to protect you from threats and make sure your money remains secure

## Is my Revolut card safe?
- Physical card activation and PIN security: your physical card must be activated in your Revolut app to be used, and that’s the only place where your PIN is available
- Geolocation: our location-based security checks make sure your mobile device's last known location matches the card terminal's location during a payment. If they don't, the transaction may be declined to protect your money
- Disposable cards: regenerating card details mean your details can’t be reused, providing an additional layer of security

## Is my information safe with Revolut?
- Personal information: your mobile device can be linked to your unique biometrics. Whether your phone is stolen or your account is accessed from a new device, the same strict biometric checks apply
- Data privacyhttps://www.revolut.com/privacy-policy/?geo-redirect=true: we take the security and confidentiality of your personal information seriously. We use secure data centres with restricted access to store and process your data. Data transmissions between our mobile apps, servers, and third parties are encrypted

## Is my money safe with Revolut?
Revolut is compliant with Electronic Money Regulations, meaning your main account balances are safeguarded. To find out more, read this FAQ about how Revolut keeps your money safe /help/security-logging-in/how-does-revolut-keep-my-money-safeor this blog posthttps://www.revolut.com/blog/post/is-revolut-safe/?geo-redirect=true.

## Are my investments secure with Revolut?
Enable Wealth Protection to activate ‌biometrics to keep your personal finances safe and private.
Revolut Support is available 24/7 via the in-app chat with support in over 100 languages. All chats are encrypted to keep your personal information safe.

title: What will happen to my funds if Revolut goes bankrupt?
content_text:
## How will Revolut’s bankruptcy affect my funds?
As the account you hold with us is an electronic money account, your money is held in a safeguarded account with a regulated institution or high-quality liquid assets. Your savings are deposited with partner entities and eligible deposits are protected up to £120,000 per institution.
If we ever became insolvent (unable to pay our debts), your money would be prioritised. Our customers’ claims would be paid out first from our dedicated client money bank and asset accounts, ensuring your funds are secured before any other claims are addressed.
Safeguarding your funds is our priority. Learn more by visiting Revolut T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/terms/?geo-redirect=true.

## Which Revolut entity am I registered to?
The different ways that Revolut safeguards your money depends on ‌the Revolut entity you’re registered to. To find out ‌which Revolut entity you're registered to:
- Tap your profile icon in the top left corner
- Scroll to the bottom of the page
- Find the name of the Revolut branch you’re registered to at the very bottom of the page

## How does Revolut protect my funds?
To find out how Revolut keeps your money safe, read this FAQ/help/security-logging-in/how-does-revolut-keep-my-money-safe in our Help Centre.
Read more about how we protect your funds in this blog posthttps://www.revolut.com/blog/post/how-we-keep-your-money-safe/.

title: How do I upload a document?
content_text:
Follow these steps when taking a live picture of your physical identity document:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Account'
- Choose 'Personal details' and tap on 'Verify my identity'
- Select your nationality and choose the document(s) from the list (e.g. passport, identity card)
- Ensure your document is valid and not expired. If it is, try using a different one
- Take a live picture of the document. Make sure to fit the document within the frame, ensuring all four corners of the document are visible
- Check that the picture of your document is clear and fully readable
- Press 'Confirm' and continue the sign up process

title: Difficulty with selfie authentication
content_text:
To keep your account secure, we may occasionally ask you to provide a selfie in-app to verify your identity when performing certain actions or editing your personal details.
If you don't have an operational front camera, you won't be able to pass the selfie verification step.

## Why a selfie authentication might fail
- Camera or microphone access wasn't enabled for Revolut
- There was poor lighting in the photo or video
- You wore glasses, a hat, a wig, or excessive jewellery
- There was another person in the frame
- You had your eyes closed or didn’t look into the camera
- You were moving or you were lying down
- Your appearance has changed significantly since your last verification. Make sure your face is close to the camera and your facial features are clearly visible
Have your ID at hand in case we ask you to provide a photo of your ID together with the selfie. Make sure the picture is clear, and any requested documents are fully visible in the frame. If there’s a problem with your document verification, you can visit this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/how-do-i-verify-my-identity in our Help Centre for guidance.

title: What is my tax identification number?
content_text:
Your Tax Identification Number (TIN) is a unique number issued by the government for tax purposes. The number used depends on the country, and may be called something other than a Tax Identification Number (TIN) in your region.
When you add a country to your tax residency list in-app, you'll be shown exactly what number is needed.
You can read about each country's TIN, or equivalent, on the OECD websitehttps://www.oecd.org/tax/automatic-exchange/crs-implementation-and-assistance/tax-identification-numbers/.

title: Confirm your personal details again
content_text:
Sometimes, for compliance reasons, we may need you to re-confirm your personal details. When this happens, we’ll let you know via email or an in-app banner.
If you don’t provide the requested details within a certain timeframe, your account will be temporarily restricted.

## Why am I being asked for this?
Confirming the details you provided during onboarding is required for the Know Your Customer (KYC) and Customer Due Diligence (CDD) processes. These processes are mandated by our regulators globally and ensure that your money is received and used legitimately.
We collect this information to verify your identity, secure your account, and reduce fraud. Knowing what you do for a living, your tax residency, and your account usage helps us identify any unusual activity. All personal data is encrypted and securely stored in line with data safety laws.

## To keep your KYC and CDD details up to date, we may ask for:
- Personal details (name, address, date of birth)
- Your account’s purpose (e.g. using it to receive your salary, make payments while you travel, money transfers, or for savings)
- Tax residencies
We may also ask you to update your tax information. To learn more, read this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/update-personal-details/update-my-tax-information in our Help Centre.

title: Why is my incoming transfer pending?
content_text:
A 'pending' status means the transfer is with us, but we need to complete some final checks before releasing the funds to your account. Common reasons for a pending transfer:
- We're waiting for required information from the sending bank
- The transfer needs to be reviewed to meet regulatory requirements
Pending transfers are usually processed within 3 business days. You should wait up to 5 business days before reporting the transfer as delayed or seeking support. If the transfer is still pending after this time, please contact our support team for help.

## My transfer was reverted
A 'reverted' status means we were unable to process the transfer, and the funds have been sent back to the sender's account. Common reasons for a reverted transfer:
- The transfer details were incorrect or incomplete (e.g. a missing reference number)
- We requested missing personal information for your Revolut account and didn't receive it in time

## What happens next?
The funds will automatically be returned to the sender, but the timeframe depends on the transfer type. Revolut cannot speed up this process as it depends on the sending bank and any intermediary banks.
- Domestic transfers: should arrive back in the sender's account within 5 business days
- International (SWIFT) transfers: can take up to 15 to 20 business days due to the multiple intermediary banks involved
The sender should contact their bank if the funds don't arrive within these timeframes. Once they receive the money, they can attempt to send you the transfer again using the correct details.

title: Can I cover intermediary bank fees upfront?
content_text:
When you make an international payment, intermediary banks may deduct fees from the amount you send. Our 'pay all fees' feature allows you to pay a flat upfront fee, which guarantees that the recipient receives the full amount. This will be charged instead of the standard international payment fee.
The amount of this fee depends on your base currency. It can change from time to time, but you'll always see the current fee in-app before you confirm the transfer.

## Eligible transfers
- USD to outside of the US (where USD isn't the national currency)
- GBP to outside of the UK (where GBP isn't the national currency)
- CHF to outside of Switzerland (except Liechtenstein)
- EUR to outside of SEPA countries (except Montenegro and Kosovo)
A discount on this fee will be applied according to your price plan in the same way as for other international payment fees. For our exact fees, take a look at your current Revolut planhttps://www.revolut.com/legal/fees/?geo-redirect=true.

title: Add, edit, or delete a beneficiary
content_text:
## How to add a beneficiary
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap the plus symbol in the top-right corner
- Select 'Bank recipient'
- Follow the prompts to enter the required details
- Tap 'Add recipient' and the beneficiary will be created
You’ll then be given the option to send this person a transfer or, if you don’t need to send a transfer right away, tap the arrow in the top-left corner to go back. Your beneficiary details will still be saved for future use.

## How to edit or delete a beneficiary
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- In the search bar, tap 'Accounts' and select the recipient 
- Tap their name or profile icon at the top 
To edit the beneficiary, tap the pen icon next to the 'IBAN' or 'Account number' field. Update the details and save securely.
To delete the beneficiary, scroll down and tap the 'Delete' button and confirm. Your beneficiary will be deleted.

title: Trusted recipients
content_text:
## What is a trusted recipient?
When you pay an individual for the first time, we add them to a list of payees that you know and consider them trusted recipients. This means that in subsequent transactions, payments to them won't need two-factor authentication, except when transfers exceed certain limits.

## Can I mark a trusted payee as not trusted?
To mark a previously trusted payee as not trusted, find the contact in-app and tap. Tap the contact to see the recipient details screen, and use the toggle in the 'Authentication' section to mark them as not trusted.

## What happens if I mark a payee as untrusted?
Payments to that payee will always require two-factor authentication.

title: Transfer I sent arrived in a different currency
content_text:
When creating a beneficiary, you need to choose a currency you know is supported by their account. This will ensure you send the correct currency and money is exchanged at the available exchange rate.
If your transfer arrives in different currency, it means that you sent a currency that isn’t by the beneficiary account. This means your money had to be exchanged at the rate of the beneficiary or intermediary bank. These exchange rates can vary and aren't controlled by Revolut.

## Here’s an example
If you choose USD as the transfer currency for a transfer being sent to a GBP account, then the beneficiary bank will exchange your USD to GBP at their own exchange rate.

title: Why was my transfer sent via an intermediary bank?
content_text:
## What is an intermediary bank?
An intermediary (or correspondent) bank acts as a middle payment processing party for international payments when Revolut and the recipient's bank don't have a direct commercial relationship. The intermediary bank connects the two entities to successfully complete the payment.

## What does this mean in terms of transfer time?
We provide an Estimated Delivery Time (EDT) which we always aim to meet. The EDT we provide already factors in any intermediary bank steps. This is designed to help ensure your payment arrives on time, and tracking is available via the outbound transfer status toolhttps://app.revolut.com/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=963be14a-6ac5-44cc-b8f5-397d1c167d5erevolut://app/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=963be14a-6ac5-44cc-b8f5-397d1c167d5e.

## What does this mean in terms of potential fees?
The impact of fees depends on the transfer type:
- SWIFT Transfers: the recipient's bank might deduct a processing fee. This fee is charged by the other banks involved, not by us
- Other international transfers: for these, all fees are upfront and visible to you, so there are no surprise deductions
At the time of initiation of transfer, we provide you with an option to cover all intermediary fees upfront. More details about covering intermediary bank fee upfront are available in this FAQ.

title: I received a transfer from an unknown sender
content_text:
If you receive a transfer you didn’t expect or recognise, contact our Customer Support team by requesting a live agent in the in-app chat before sending the money back. Our team will guide you through the steps to help keep your account safe.
Rest assured, returning the money won’t be associated with any financial losses.

title: Timeframes for transfers
content_text:
## Receiving or sending GBP transfers
The time to receive a GBP transfer may vary according to the payment type:
- Faster payments (FPS): a few hours, but can take up to 1 business day
- Salary payments (usually BACS): 2 to 3 business days
- CHAPS: up to 1 business day
Sending local transfers from a Revolut account can take up to 1 business day.

## EUR transfers
SEPA Instant transfers should arrive within a few minutes. Regular SEPA transfers can take up to 2 business days.

## International transfers
SWIFT transfers can take up to 2 business days to arrive.

## Tracking a transfer
Use our in-app incoming transfer tracker to track your transfer and see its timelines. The tracker will provide specific dates based on your transfer.
You can also track the status of a transfer via the outbound transfer statushttps://app.revolut.com/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.timeframes-for-transfersrevolut://app/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.timeframes-for-transfers tool.

title: Bank transfer fees
content_text:
## Sending a bank transfer
- Local bank transfers: we never charge for a bank transfer if it's sent to an account in the United Kingdom and sent in pound sterling (GBP)
- International transfers: we don't charge for transfers inside the SEPA regionhttps://app.revolut.com/form/dbee2bf9-4d23-4d62-8162-8ec3ac09878a?faqEntryPoint=question.which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transferrevolut://app/form/dbee2bf9-4d23-4d62-8162-8ec3ac09878a?faqEntryPoint=question.which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer. Outside this region and the United Kingdom, a small fee is charged. We'll always show you any fees in-app before you send a bank transfer
Check our international Fees Pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/fees/?geo-redirect=true for more information. When your money is in transit, intermediary banks are used to complete the transaction and may also charge a fee. Revolut doesn't have visibility or control over these fees.

## Paid plan discounts on international transfer fees¹
- Premium: 20% discount
- Metal: 40% discount 
- Ultra: 100% discount (no transfer fees)
¹ For customers aged 18 or above only

## Receiving a bank transfer
Revolut doesn't charge any fees for adding money via bank transfer to your account. However, there are non-Revolut fees that can be charged.
- Your bank might charge a fee for sending a transfer. Contact them to clarify their fees. For international (SWIFT) transfers, the external bank may apply fees. Intermediary banks may also charge handling fees of USD15–50
If your transfer is in a currency not supported by Revolut, your bank may convert the transfer at their exchange rate, which may lead to a currency conversion fee. That's why — to avoid fees — we recommend using local details, supported countries, and currencies, which you can check in this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/which-countries-and-currencies-are-supported-to-receive-money/which-countries-are-not-supported-for-inbound-transfers.

title: Can I receive large bank transfers into my Revolut account?
content_text:
There is no maximum amount of money you can receive into your Revolut account via bank transfer. This applies to international and local transfers. Certain transfers may have limits, which we'll always let you know about in-app.
Your account also has an annual deposit limit on all top-up methods, including incoming bank transfers and internal transfers from other Revolut customers, card top-ups, and top-ups using Apple Pay or Google Pay. This limit is set automatically and can't be checked or manually altered.

## Do I need to do anything before receiving a large transfer?
You don't need to do anything to be able to receive a large transfer. In rare cases, we might ask you for a source of funds document. To read more, check out this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/source-of-funds-check/i-need-to-verify-my-source-of-funds.

title: Can I receive my salary into my Revolut account?
content_text:
You can use your account details to receive transfers from your employer directly into your Revolut account in AED, AUD, CAD, CHF, CZK, DKK, EUR, GBP, HKD, HUF, ILS, JPY, MXN, NOK, NZD, PLN, QAR, RON, RSD, SAR, SEK, SGD, THB, TRY, USD, ZAR.

## Locate your account details
To find the account details you need to have your salary paid into your Revolut account, follow these steps:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup'
- Under the amount, choose your payment methodhttps://app.revolut.com/auto-topuprevolut://app/topup-method
- Select the currency in which you want to receive money by pressing the arrow pointing down
- Scroll down and select 'Regular bank transferhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup-bank-transfer'
This will take you to the selected currency details, which you can share with your employer so that they can transfer your paycheck to your account.

## Timeframes and fees
A salary transfer is very similar to a regular transfer. For this reason, the timeframes and fees will usually be the same. You can opt in to start receiving your salary transfers earlier, with no extra cost. To learn more about Early Salary, visit this FAQ/help/adding-money/add-money-with-a-bank-transfer/get-paid-with-early-salary.
For more information about timeframes, check this FAQ. For information about fees, check this article/help/transfers/bank-transfers/which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer.
If you didn't receive your salary, check this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/having-an-issue-with-receiving-money/what-should-i-do-if-a-transfer-doesn-t-arrive-to-my-revolut-account in our Help Centre.

title: Locate a bank transfer sent to my Revolut account
content_text:
Use our in-app incoming transfer tracker to track your transfer and get transfer timelines. The tracker will provide specific dates based on your transfer, but you can review the general next steps below.
For general transfer timeframes, read this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/timeframes-for-transfers. Remember to only count business days when calculating the expected arrival time (weekends and public holidays don't count).

## Timeframe hasn't passed
If the estimated arrival date hasn't passed, there's no need to worry. The transfer is on schedule and will be processed in due course.
- Once the transfer arrives on our end, we'll notify you and it will be visible in-app right away
- We don’t have visibility on transfers that haven’t reached Revolut yet, even if they’ve left the sender bank
In the meantime, wait until the provided arrival date. If the provided date passes and the transfer still has not arrived, check your other currency accounts to see if the transfer was received in a different currency. If not, see the guidance below.

## The timeframe has passed
If the expected timeframe passed and your transfer didn’t arrive, the next steps must be taken by the sending bank and its partners, as they are responsible for ensuring the payment reaches Revolut. We advise taking the following actions for the best chance of receiving your transfer:
- Contact sender bank to locate the payment through their systems and their partners
- For SWIFT transfers, get a GPI tracker or Trace from the sending bank to track the funds
- Ask the sending bank to start a recall
- If you have a confirmation document, use our missing incoming transfer to open a case
In the case of international (SWIFT) transfers, this document must be a GPI Tracker as it will show exactly where the funds are. Other documents will only show that the transfer was sent, but won’t allow confirmation of where the funds are exactly.

title: Why did I receive less money?
content_text:
Revolut doesn’t charge any fees for receiving a bank transfer. However, the sending or intermediary banks may apply fees. It's more common for fees to be charged for international transfers. If the transfer involved a currency exchange, then the sending bank may have applied an exchange fee.
Learn more about what fees may apply to your transfer in this article/help/transfers/bank-transfers/which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer.

title: Why was the transfer to my account reverted?
content_text:
## How to check why your transfer was reverted
In the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- In your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list, tap the reverted transfer
- Scroll down and tap 'Get help' for specific troubleshooting steps

## When will I receive the reverted funds?
Local transfers will be returned in 3 to 5 business days. International transfers will be returned in 15 to 20 business days. Once a transfer has been reverted, we're unable to re-assign it to the recipient account, nor can we expedite the return process.
If your reverted transfers exceeded the timeframe, contact our support team through the in-app chat in order to get a return confirmation, which will help the sending bank locate the funds.
The timing of the transfer reversal, when incorrect account details are used, is determined by the originating bank. We can't track or influence its status before it's completed.

## Common reasons for transfers being reverted:
- Check your registered email address for any communication from us asking for documentation or additional information
- Follow the instructions provided in the email to resolve the issue
- If you've been requested to update your tax records, you can do this by going to your 'Personal details'
- Confirm that you have used the accurate account details. For international transfers, make sure to use your SWIFT details
- Check which account details to use for receiving a bank transfer in this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/how-to-receive-money-from-another-bank/what-account-details-should-i-use-to-transfer-money-to-my-revolut-account/what-account-details-are-available-for-me
- Your transfer may be reverted if its currency or originating country is unsupported. For a complete list of affected countries and currencies, check out this article/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/which-countries-and-currencies-are-supported-to-receive-money/which-countries-are-not-supported-for-inbound-transfers
- To ensure your easy bank transfer is successful, the name on your external bank account must match the name on your Revolut account

title: Supported currencies for holding
content_text:
Currently, you can create an account in the following currencies, as well as hold and exchange them: AED, AUD, CAD, CHF, CLP, CNY, COP, CZK, DKK, EGP, EUR, GBP, HKD, HUF, IDR, ILS, INR, ISK, JPY, KRW, KZT, MAD, MXN, NOK, NZD, PHP, PLN, QAR, RON, RSD, SAR, SEK, SGD, THB, TRY, USD, VND, ZAR
If a currency isn't supported for holding, you can’t create an account in this currency, but you can still make card payments and withdrawals in that currency. In these cases, the currency will be exchanged at the time of the transaction.
Find out more about currency spending order by reading this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/spending-abroad-or-in-different-currencies/what-currency-will-i-be-charged-in in our Help Centre. To learn about supported cryptocurrencies, visit this article/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/understanding-revolut-cryptocurrency/are-there-any-limits-on-crypto-exchanges.

title: Switch your account to Revolut
content_text:
Our Switch Service helps you easily switch your existing bank account to Revolut while ensuring your payments continue smoothly.
You can opt to move all payments and remaining balances to Revolut, and close your old account, using the current account switching service. Your account is guaranteed to be switched to Revolut within seven days.
Additionally, you can also decide to only move payments from your old account to Revolut, keeping your old account open and existing debit card active.

## Which payments are eligible?
- Incoming payments like your salary, pension, or benefits
- Direct Debit payments, such as your utility bills
- Scheduled payments
We won't transfer any bills or subscriptions set up with your old debit card, or any overdraft you may have with your previous provider, over to Revolut.
For full account switches — under the CASS guarantee — we'll transfer your remaining GBP balance to Revolut and close your old account when the switch is complete. Any debit card linked to the old account will be terminated.

## How to switch your account to Revolut
- Tap this link to request to move your payments to Revolut
- Provide details of the account you want to switch to Revolut, including the debit card number, and switch starting date
It’ll take us seven working days to validate and execute the switch, and we'll let you know when it's done.

## How the switching process works
- Days 1 to 2: we contact your old bank to start the switch
- Days 3 to 6: we transfer your payment details. Make sure your new account is ready and funded
- Day 7 and beyond: your switch is complete. Your balance has been moved, old account has been closed, and payments have been redirected
Direct Debits and scheduled transfers will be set up and come from your Revolut account only from the switching date onwards, but will continue to come out of your old account before the switch date.
Any incoming transfers — like your salary — which arrive in your old account will be redirected to Revolut by the Switch Service or forwarded by the new bank to Revolut. Redirection of funds will happen for three years. Make sure to inform your employer about your new Revolut account details.

title: What currency account details are available for me?
content_text:
## Available details
All Revolut customers registered in the UK have access to:
- GBP local account sort code and account number
- SWIFT account for international transfers in all supported currencies

## View your currency account details
In your Revolut app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Accounts'
- Select the desired currency 
- Below your balance, tap 'Details'
If local details aren't available for the desired currency, check your SWIFT details — they'll be marked as 'For cross-border transfers only' in-app. To learn which details you should use for incoming transfers, check this article/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/how-to-receive-money-from-another-bank/what-currency-and-details-to-choose-for-a-top-up-by-transfer.

title: What's Revolut's official name and address?
content_text:
Make sure to use the correct address for your situation to avoid delays or failed transfers.

## Local GBP
- Name: Revolut Ltd
- Address: 30 South Colonnade, London, United Kingdom, E14 5HX

## EUR SEPA
- Name: Revolut Ltd
- Address: 30 South Colonnade, London, United Kingdom, E14 5HX

## SWIFT transfers
- Name: Revolut Ltd
- Address: 30 South Colonnade, London, United Kingdom, E14 5HX

title: What account details should I use for incoming transfers?
content_text:
## Find your account details
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Accounts'
- Select the currency you wish to receive money in
- Below your account balance, tap 'Details'
- Choose 'Local' or 'International'
- Share these details with the sender or sender bank
Revolut's official name, address, and correspondent BIC details are provided on the same details screen. If you're asked for intermediary bank details, you'll need to provide the correspondent bank's BIC.
If your bank auto-completes the BIC/SWIFT, and it's different from the one you see in your account details, you'll need to contact them and ask them to update their Standard Settlement Instructions (SSIs) before making the transfer.

## Which details should I use?
You must use details for an account in the same currency as the transfer being sent. Sending a transfer to incorrect account details might result in it being rejected and returned to the sender. If local details aren't available for the currency you wish to receive money in, use the SWIFT details.
 You can find fee information in this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer.

title: How to transfer funds to Revolut
content_text:
## Timeframes
If you're expecting a bank transfer to your Revolut account, we usually can't track its progress until it appears in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. How long a transfer will take to reach your account depends on the sending bank, so you'll need to reach out to the sending bank for their timeframes.
Because this bank handles initiating the transfer, we can't influence its status or processing before it reaches us. However, you can get an estimated arrival date by using our in-app transfer tracker, which you can access via this link.
For any delays, check with the sending bank for updates. Once the transfer arrives, it will be instantly accessible, and you'll receive an automatic notification. For general transfer timeframes, check this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/timeframes-for-transfers. 

## How to receive a bank transfer
To receive a bank transfer, you must share your bank details with the sender so that they can initiate the transfer. To learn how to find these details in-app, visit this article/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/how-to-receive-money-from-another-bank/what-account-details-should-i-use-to-transfer-money-to-my-revolut-account/what-account-details-are-available-for-me.
We don't support transfers from some countries and currencies. You can find out more about unsupported transfers on this page/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/which-countries-and-currencies-are-supported-to-receive-money/which-countries-are-not-supported-for-inbound-transfers.

## Limits and fees
There are no limits for receiving a bank transfer. Usually, no additional steps are needed to receive large amounts of money. If we do need additional information, we'll reach out to you via email.
Revolut doesn't charge any fees for receiving a bank transfer. Intermediary banks may charge fees. You can learn more about intermediary fees in this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer.

## Difficulty receiving a bank transfer
If there are delays, contact the sending bank for more updates, ask for a transfer confirmation document or a GPI tracker in case of international SWIFT transfers, and submit it to us.
If our tracker considers your transfer to be taking longer than usual, our team will review it. If it's still within the expected timeframe, wait until the timeframe passes.
If your transfer didn't arrive within the expected timeframe, you can find the next steps in this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/having-an-issue-with-receiving-money/what-should-i-do-if-a-transfer-doesn-t-arrive-to-my-revolut-account.

title: Unsupported inbound bank transfers
content_text:
## Unsupported countries and regions
We don't support transfers from the following countries and regions: 
- Belarus
- Cuba
- Donetsk People's Republic
- Iran
- Kherson (Ukraine)
- Democratic People's Republic of Korea
- Libya
- Luhansk People's Republic
- Myanmar
- Russian Federation
- Syrian Arab Republic
- Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela
- Zaporizhia
- Zimbabwe

## Unsupported currencies and sources
USD transfers from:
- Barbados (BRB)
- Curaçao (CW)
- Gibraltar (GIB)
- Malta (MLT)
- St Maarten (SXM)
- Mauritius (MU)
EUR transfers from St Maarten (SXM), CHF and NOK transfers from Malta (MLT), INR from any country or region.

title: Difficulty adding money with an easy bank transfer
content_text:
If you are having trouble adding money via easy bank transfer, it may be due to one of the reasons below:

## Availability or incomplete authorisation
We only offer this feature from a certain list of local banks that support this type of payment. The list of banks offered depends on your Revolut account country, and not your nationality.
After selecting a bank, you'll be redirected to the bank's app or site to authorise the payment (in some cases via an intermediary partner's screen). If you fail or cancel the payment authorisation, the money will not arrive to your Revolut account.

## External bank errors or rejections
- Most errors encountered during payment authorisation are caused by the external banks' system 
- External bank rejections can also cause your payment to fail. This solely lies at the discretion of your external bank
In these cases, reach out to your external bank or try again later, as we don't have any control over the external bank availability and rejections.

## Other non-technical issues
- Account name mismatch: You can only add money with linked accounts if the name of your external account is the exact same as the one in your Revolut account.
- You can either change the name attached to your Revolut account or use a different bank account which has the correct name attached. To find out how to change the name on your Revolut account, read these FAQs/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/update-personal-details
- Insufficient funds: Double-check your current balance in the external bank account, and add more money if necessary
- If you are requested to input an IBAN before being redirected to your external bank, ensure it's in the correct format and belongs to your external bank account

title: Limits for transfers to other Revolut customers
content_text:
Limits depend on your destination country and base currency. Most currencies have no transfer limits. We’ll show you any exceptions in the Revolut app before you send money.
When you send a high amount (e.g. for a down payment on an apartment), we might need some additional time to perform extra checks. However, there’s nothing to worry about. You can send large amounts of money in the same way you would with small amounts.
For more information on this, refer to our Terms and Conditionshttps://www.revolut.com/en-SG/legal/terms/.

title: Block or report another Revolut customer
content_text:
You can't completely remove a Revolut customer from your Revolut contacts. However, you can block another Revolut customer at any time. That customer will then be unable to send you messages, send money to you, receive money from you, or add you to Group Pockets.

## Block or report someone in-app
- Go to Payments on the bottom menu
- Search and select the Revolut customer
- Tap their profile icon in the top-right corner
- Select 'Block' or 'Block and report'
If you choose to block the person, you can follow the same steps again to unblock them.

title: Group Bills
content_text:
## What is Group Bills?
Group Bills helps you easily manage shared costs with friends. Just create a group, add your expenses, and we'll figure out who owes what.
You can now invite anyone — even those without a Revolut account — to Group Bills, and they can join a group without signing up for Revolut. Find out more in this FAQ.

## How to create a group
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap the plus symbol '+'
- Tap 'Group'
- Name your group
- Select the group currency
- Tap 'Create group'
- Add members directly, or share a group QR code or invite link with your friends

## How to split bills with a group
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Select your group
- Tap 'Add a bill'
- Select the transaction(s) you want to split or tap 'Create a custom bill'
- Choose how to split the amount between members

## How to leave a group
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Select your group 
- Tap the group icon in the top right corner 
- Select 'Manage'
- Tap the door icon next to your avatar to leave the group
To be able to leave a group, you must not owe or be owed any money. You can use 'Mark as paid' to record any outstanding payments.

title: I sent a transfer to the wrong Revolut customer
content_text:
Transfers between Revolut customers are usually instant. Once the transfer is processed, we're not able to cancel it. If you sent money to the wrong Revolut customer, you'll need to ask the recipient to return the money to you as soon as possible.
To do this, reach out to them via the chat between you both in the Revolut app. This chat is created once you send them money. To use this chat: 
- On the bottom menu, go to Payments
- Select the person you've sent money to
- A chat window will open, and you'll be able to type your message to the recipient of the payment there

## The recipient isn't answering my messages
If the recipient isn't responding, you can contact our customer support team and we'll try to reach out to them on your behalf. However, we can't guarantee they will respond or agree to return the funds. Without the recipient's consent, we're unable to retrieve the money.

title: My transfer to another Revolut customer is pending
content_text:
If your transfer to another Revolut customer is pending, you can check if it's eligible for cancellation. To do this: 
- Go to Payments on the bottom menu 
- Select the person who you've sent the pending transfer to
- On the payments screen between you and this person, select the pending transfer
- Tap 'Cancel'
After cancelling your transfer, be sure to double-check that all beneficiary details that were used were correct, including the recipient's Revtag or phone number. Once you're sure you have the right details, send the transfer again if needed.

## I can't cancel my transfer
If you can't cancel your transfer in-app, it's probably being reviewed by our automated security system. You may be prompted to answer a few questions in-app about the transfer or beneficiary. Once you've answered any questions, allow a few hours for the transfer to be processed.
If the pending transfer is being reviewed, we can't cancel it until the review is complete. If you have any further questions, contact Revolut Support.

title: Send or request money from other Revolut customers
content_text:
## How to send or request money
- Go to Payments on the bottom menu 
- Tap the plus sign in the top-right corner
- Search for a past recipient or tap 'Revolut friend'
- Search by Revtag, phone number, or email
- Tap 'Send' or 'Request'
- Enter the amount
- Tap 'Send' or 'Request' again
For privacy reasons, some people won't show up as a Revolut customer until you've sent or received money from them.

## Difficulty with transfers
Simply tap the transfer you need help with in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to get specialised assistance.
For more help with transfers between Revolut customers, see this FAQ.

## Limits and fees
There are no transfer fees when making transfers between Revolut customers. Most currencies and countries have no limits. However, if any exchange fees or transfer limits apply to your transfer, we'll show you before you send money in the app.

title: Revtags
content_text:
## What is a Revtag?
Revtags are unique handles Revolut customers can use to send and receive transfers without having to share personal details.

## Where can I find my Revtag?
To view, manage, and share your Revtag in-app, tap your profile icon in the top-left corner. You'll see your Revtag under your name. Simply tap it to explore your options. To learn how to change your Revtag, you can read this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/how-do-i-update-my-revtag in our Help Centre.

## Manage Revtag discoverability
You can disable discoverability by Revtag by following the steps below:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Privacy'
- Tap 'Make me discoverable'
- Disable the 'Revtag' toggle
If you disable Revtag discoverability, Revolut customers who have not saved you as a contact won't be able to search your Revtag in-app or access your Revolut.me link.

title: My transfer to or from someone on Revolut wasn't received
content_text:
## I can't find a transfer that was sent to me
If you're having trouble finding a transfer from someone on Revolut, here's what you can do:
- Double-check with the sender that they used the correct details, such as your Revtag or phone number
- Check your other currency accounts by going to 'Homehttps://app.revolut.com/accountsrevolut://app/wallet' on the bottom menu and tapping 'Accounts' under your balance
- Make sure you have the latest version of the Revolut app installed on your device
If you've tried all of the above and still can't find the transfer, ask the sender to reach out to Revolut Support via the in-app chat.

## My recipient can't access their Revolut account
If a Revolut-to-Revolut transfer was completed, that means the recipient’s account was active at the time. If your recipient has forgotten their passcode or phone number, they can access their account by following the steps below:
- If they've forgotten their passcode, they need to enter their phone number on the log-in screen, then tap 'Forgot your passcode?' and follow the prompts
- If they lost access to their phone number, they'll need to dismiss any pre-filled login details, then tap 'Log in'. They can then try logging in using another method, or tap 'Lost access to my phone number' and follow the prompts.

title: I'm unable to send money to another Revolut customer
content_text:
If you're unable to send or receive transfers from other Revolut customers, check your profile to make sure your account is verified and is not restricted. Ask the recipient to do the same, as the problem may be coming from their side.
For more information about restricted accounts, read this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/security-and-personal-data/my-account-is-locked/why-is-my-account-locked in our Help Centre. To learn more about verifying your identity, visit this article/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/how-do-i-verify-my-identity. Additionally,‌ the recipient may have blocked you, which could be the reason the transfer isn't going through.

## Internal transfers disabled
If internal transfers are disabled, your transfers to or from other Revolut customers won't work. Check if you and the recipient have the 'Make me discoverable' option turned on. To do this:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select Privacyhttps://app.revolut.com/settings/security/privacyrevolut://app/privacy
- Check if the toggle under 'Make me discoverable' option is turned on

## Compliance reasons
Revolut must complete necessary checks, by law, to validate the security of all transfers. If your transfer doesn't meet the requirements of this check, it will be declined.

## Insufficient funds
If you don't have enough funds to complete a transfer, you won't be able to fulfil a request or send a new transfer. If that's the case, you'll need to add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup to your account.

title: Transferring funds to an external card
content_text:
To send a transfer to a card:
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'New' (plus symbol in the top-right corner)
- Choose 'Card recipient'
- Enter the receiver's 16-digit card number
- Enter the amount and choose the currency
- Submit the transfer after reviewing the amount sent and fees charged
We don't support transfers to some cards, countries, and currencies. You can find out more about supported transfers in this article/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/card-transfers/which-cards-can-i-send-money-to.

## Minimum amount
