We require a minimum of $2 (or other currency equivalent) for transfers to a card within Europe and a minimum of $10 (or other currency equivalent) for transfers to a card outside Europe. 

## Fees and limits
We charge a small fee for sending card transfers. We show you the fee in-app before you send a card transfer.
The fee amount will depend on the transaction itself, such as how much you are sending and where the money is going. If you need to exchange money before or while sending a transfer, currency exchange fees may also apply. Learn more about exchange fees in this article/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies.
Limits on card transfers depend on the destination country and currency. We show you any limits before you send a card transfer. The receiving bank may have different daily, weekly, or monthly limits for card transfers which may also apply.

## Timeframes
Sending money to a card is usually instant, but can take up to 30 minutes. In very rare cases, banks may delay transferring funds to cards, which could take 48 hours. If this happens, read this article/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/card-transfers/my-beneficiary-has-not-received-my-card-transfer to find out more.
If you're experiencing difficulties with your transfer to a card, tap on the transfer in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to get specialised help.

title: I want to cancel my transfer to a card
content_text:
## Transfers made by mistake
If you've made a mistake, such as sending funds to the wrong card, a duplicate transaction, or an incorrect amount, the fastest way to correct the transfer is to ask the recipient to return it to you. Alternatively, you can try to download the transfer confirmation and share it with the recipient's bank and ask them to return the transfer. Learn how to download the confirmation in this article/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/how-to-send-money-to-another-bank/how-do-i-download-my-transfer-confirmation.
Revolut can also initiate this process, but it's lengthy and requires approval from the recipient's bank. If the recipient or bank refuses to assist, we won't be able to get the transfer back.

## Suspected transfer fraud
If you suspect the card transfer you sent was directed to a fraudulent source:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Search for the transfer in your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap on 'Get help'
- Select the option 'Report this transfer as fraud' for further guidance

title: My beneficiary has not received my card transfer
content_text:
In rare cases, banks may delay transferring funds to cards, which could take 48 hours. To get more information on when the funds will arrive, download the transfer confirmation and share it with the recipient's bank. Learn how to download it in this article/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/how-to-send-money-to-another-bank/how-do-i-download-my-transfer-confirmation.
If the funds have not arrived after 48 hours and the bank has not provided guidance:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Select the transaction from your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap 'Get help'
- Select the option 'When will my transfer arrive?' for further guidance

title: Which countries can I send a card transfer to?
content_text:
## Supported countries and currencies
We support transfers to cards in over 90 countries and 70 currencies. To check your available options:
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'New' ('+' symbol in the top-right corner)
- Select 'Send international' feature and choose 'Card' as transfer method

## Supported card types
You can send to most debit, credit, and in a few instances, prepaid debit cards issued by Visa or Mastercard.
If a bank doesn't accept transfers to a card, we'll let you know in-app, and you won't be able to save the card recipient. Alternatively, you can try sending a bank transfer instead.

title: Why has my card transfer failed or been declined?
content_text:
There are several reasons why a card transfer may have been rejected or failed, including incorrect recipient details, a closed recipient account, suspected fraud, or if the recipient bank doesn't support card transfers.
Tap the transfer in question in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to check the failure reason and find additional details.
It's important to make sure the card details you have for your recipient are up-to-date. If you need help or more information regarding the recipient, contact the recipient bank. Alternatively, you can try sending a bank transfer instead. Learn more about sending bank transfers in this article/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/how-to-send-money-to-another-bank/how-do-i-send-money-to-a-bank-account.

title: Why is my card transfer still pending?
content_text:
When your instant card transfer is pending, it simply means that it's being processed and reviewed, which can take some time. To check the status of your instant card transfer, go to your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list and tap the transfer for more details. To verify the payment, we may ask you additional details about it. If the transfer is still being verified after 1 business day, request live support by typing 'Live agent' in the in-app chat.
If the transfer remains pending for 48 hours, it'll be automatically cancelled and funds will be returned to your account.

title: I want to cancel a bank transfer
content_text:
The ability to cancel a transfer depends on multiple factors like payment type, destination country, and timing. Not all transfers can be cancelled once initiated.
To confirm eligibility for cancellation, tap on the transaction from the transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to access the transfer tracker. If eligible, a 'Cancel' button will appear on the transfer tracker.
If the cancel button does not appear, it means that the transfer is no longer cancellable. When cancellation isn't possible, it's best to request a refund directly from the recipient's bank.

## I need to change the transfer details
Changing transfer details is only possible for international SWIFT transfers. However, for local transfersif you've missed or entered the wrong name, the transfer will likely still reach the recipient.
Errors in recipient details may result in the transfer bouncing back to your account within 5 working days for local transfers, or up to 20 working days for SWIFT transfers.
We have no visibility on the transfer's progress until it reaches our system, at which point you will see it in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. The timing of transfer reversals is determined by the sending bank, and we can't track or influence the status of such reversals.

## Recipient's bank can't refund my transfer
We recommend requesting a refund from the recipient directly. If that's not possible, you may be able to request a recall in the Revolut app (for eligible transfers). If your transfer is eligible for a recall try the following: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Find and tap the relevant transfer in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap 'Get help'
- Select 'I want to cancel my transfer'
- Tap 'Attempt a recall'
As part of the recall process, we will contact the recipient bank to request the reversal of the funds. We can't guarantee a successful recall or how long it will take. This is out of our control as the money is with the recipient bank. If you think you sent a fraudulent transfer, read this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/security-and-personal-data/online-fraud/what-do-i-do-if-i-ve-been-a-victim-of-bank-transfer-fraud.

## Transfer from you external bank to another Revolut customer
Since the transfer has been sent from your external bank account to another person's Revolut account, we can't check their account and help you with the reversal. 
Reach out to the sending bank and request the transfer to be recalled from their end to recover the funds.
We don't have the option to take action from our end, but if the sending bank reviews this case and reaches out to us regarding the case, our team will be able to assist them.

title: Locate a bank transfer sent from Revolut
content_text:
To check the latest status and get help with a bank transfer you've sent, tap your profile icon in the top-left corner, select 'Help' and Outbound transfer statushttps://app.revolut.com/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.my-recipient-has-not-received-the-money-i-sentrevolut://app/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.my-recipient-has-not-received-the-money-i-sent, then choose the transaction.
You'll find the estimated date of arrival for the transfer, along with guidelines in case you need additional support.

## Transfer is pending
When you make a transfer, it can take longer to be sent to your recipient in some situations. This is known as a pending transfer. Unfortunately, there's nothing we can do to speed up the process. Pending transfers can happen for several reasons:
- Being sent on a weekend or bank holiday when there's no processing
- Additional processing checks
- Additional information required
- Incorrect recipient details
- Currency or payment scheme used for transfer not supported by recipient or recipient bank
A transfer can be pending for up to 72 hours, but is usually completed in a few hours. If the transfer fails to reach the recipient, the transaction will be reverted. The funds should be available to you within 5 business days for domestic transfers and 15 business days for international transfers.

## Transfer is completed, but recipient hasn't received it
Sometimes, once a transfer is completed, recipient banks can take a bit longer (1-2 business days) to credit the funds to the recipient's account. 
If this time is exceeded, make sure you've entered the recipient's correct account details. If the details are correct, as the transfer has already left Revolut, you'll need to contact the recipient’s bank with the transfer confirmation.
You can download it after selecting your transfer in your transactions list. Next to 'Confirmation', tap 'Download'. 

## Transfer is reverted, but still hasn't reached my account
In the event of your outbound transfer being reverted but not reaching your account, you should request a return confirmation from the bank that reverted it. Most of the time, the recipient can request the document in PDF format from their bank.
It can take up to 5 business days for a domestic transfer and up to 15 business days for an international transfer to be returned to your Revolut account.

title: Why is my transfer to a bank account still pending?
content_text:
When you make a transfer, it can take extra time to be sent to your recipient in some situations. This is known as a pending transfer, and it may occur for several reasons: 
- Being sent on a weekend or public holiday when there’s no processing
- Additional processing checks
- Additional information required
- Incorrect recipient details
- Currency or payment scheme used for transfer not being supported by recipient or recipient bank
You can track the status of your transfer by using the outbound transfer statushttps://app.revolut.com/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.why-is-my-transfer-still-pendingrevolut://app/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.why-is-my-transfer-still-pending tool in the Revolut app.

## When will the transfer go through?
A transfer may remain pending for up to 72 hours. In most cases, a transfer will eventually be marked as completed after a few hours.
If the transfer fails and doesn’t reach the recipient, the transaction will be reverted, and the funds should be available in your Revolut account promptly. To learn more about the potential reasons for a failed transfer, read this article/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/having-an-issue-with-sending-money/whys-my-transfer-been-declined.

title: Why was my outbound bank transfer reverted?
content_text:
When a transfer is reverted, it means that while successfully sent, it was 'stopped' along the way by an intermediary bank, or ultimately rejected by a beneficiary bank. There are multiple reasons why this might happen, including: 
- Incorrect details: The recipient bank may have rejected the transfer if the recipient’s account details didn’t match
- Closed account: The account you’re transferring to may have been closed by the beneficiary bank
- Wrong currency: Some recipients may only receive a transfer in a specific currency
- Wrong transfer type: The account selected might be unable to accept certain types of transfer (i.e. SWIFT or SEPA)
- Compliance reasons: Your transfer might not meet compliance requirements imposed by one of the banks involved

## When will I receive the reverted funds?
In case of domestic transfers, the funds should be received in up to 5 business days. In the case of international transfers, transfers can take up to 15–20 days.

title: I can't send a transfer or create a recipient
content_text:
## Possible reasons for failed transfers
- Compliance reasons: Revolut must complete necessary checks in accordance with the law to validate the security of the transfer process
- Locked or unverified account: if your account has restrictions or is not verified, transfers may be declined. Complete necessary processes and wait for verification
- Insufficient funds: standing orders (recurring transfers) commonly face this problem. If you don't have enough funds, you should receive a notification 24 hours before the scheduled payment which will remind you to add money in time for the payment
If the transfer ‌failed, the funds haven't left your account, and you won't need to wait for their reversal. They should be available instantly. 

## Problems with the recipient account or details provided
- Unsupported recipient: we're unable to make transfers to certain recipients. This includes some cryptocurrency exchanges, transfers to certain countries, banks, and merchants
- Incorrect recipient details: if the recipient account details don't match, the recipient's bank may reject the transfer
- Recipient account closed: the account you are transferring to may be closed. Ensure that your recipient account details are updated

## I can't create a recipient
If you're having difficulty creating a recipient, check out some of the most common reasons (and solutions) below:
- Account number is invalid: reach out to the recipient to double-check their details 
- Postal code or address isn't valid: change the postal code to a correct one  
- IBAN is invalid: double-check the recipient details to see if there aren't any potential errors

title: Can I make a local transfer in a different currency?
content_text:
You can transfer funds locally or internationally in different currencies. For local transfers in a different currency, it will be processed as SWIFT, intermediary banks may charge fees, and processing time usually takes up to 2 business days.
For international transfers to a bank in a different currency, it will be also processed as SWIFT. The fees and timeframes may vary based on the currency and location of the receiving bank.
To learn more about processing times, read this article/help/transfers/bank-transfers/timeframes-for-transfers. Find out more about potential fees for international transfers on this page/help/transfers/bank-transfers/which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer.

title: Download a transfer confirmation
content_text:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Find the transfer in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Select the transfer
- Next to 'Confirmation', tap 'Download'
- Save or share the PDF
Transfer confirmations will be downloaded in the same language as your app and will include the MT-103 document for international SWIFT transfers. We include all available reference numbers in the transfer confirmation.
It's not possible to download a transfer confirmation for failed transfers or transfers from Joint accounts.

title: Sending money from Revolut to a bank account
content_text:
## How to send a bank transfer
- Go to Payments on the bottom menu
- Tap the plus symbol in the top-right corner 
- Select 'Bank', 'International', or scroll down to search for your previous recipients
- Follow the prompts to enter the required details
To add a reference to your transfer, tap the 'Reference' field located below the amount you want to send. If you're sending a transfer in a different currency than the recipient's base currency (the currency of their country), tap the downward arrow and select the appropriate currency from the list.
Revolut supports transfers to over 160+ countries and in 70+ currencies. However, we don't support transfers to some countries and currencies. You can find out more about unsupported transfers in this FAQ/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/what-countries-and-currencies-are-supported-for-outbound-transfer.

## Timeframes
You'll see the estimated arrival of your transfer in-app before you send money. You can check this, and track your transfer, by tapping it in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. To check our transfer timeframes before sending money, go to this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/timeframes-for-transfers.
Additionally, you can track the status of a transfer you have sent via the outbound transfer statushttps://app.revolut.com/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.how-do-i-send-money-to-a-bank-accountrevolut://app/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.how-do-i-send-money-to-a-bank-account tool.

## Fees
You'll be shown a detailed breakdown of Revolut fees in-app before you send your transfer. You can also use our toolhttps://www.revolut.com/international-transfers/?geo-redirect=true to estimate fees for international transfers. We don't have control or visibility over fees from other banks. To learn more about non-Revolut fees, visit this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer.
Revolut fees for sending a bank transfer:
- No fee for GBP transfers within the UK
- No fee for EUR transfers within the SEPA region
- Small fee for transfers outside the UK & SEPA region. Paid plan customers get discounts on international transfer fees. Find out more on this pagehttps://www.revolut.com/our-pricing-plans/?geo-redirect=true
- Exchange fee for transfers may apply depending on your plan, see FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies

## Limits
We'll always show you if the amount you enter is above the maximum or below the minimum transfer limit. Unfortunately, these limits can't be changed.
You can send large amounts as usual. If we need any extra information from you to process a large transfer, we'll let you know. 
Additionally, your transfers may also be limited by Wealth Protection limits. These limits are set and controlled by you in-app. To learn more about Wealth Protection limits, visit this FAQ.

## Problems with a sent bank transfer
Tap your transfer in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to download your transfer confirmation, track your transfer, and get specialised help on your transfer if needed.
If you need more help with a bank transfer, read these FAQs/help/transfers/bank-transfers-issues.

title: Problem with a Direct Debit
content_text:
## Insufficient funds for a Direct Debit
If you don’t have enough funds in the Direct Debit currency, we’ll check other currency balances in the order below, and take the full amount from the first one with enough funds. We can’t combine multiple currencies to complete the payment. We'll check your accounts for funds in this order:
- Transaction currency
- Your base currency
- Next active currency with enough balance
If no currency has enough funds in the order above, the Direct Debit will fail. You can customise your preferred currency for a specific Direct Debit in the 'Scheduled payments' section of the Revolut app.

## Direct Debit didn't trigger
There are a few reasons why a Direct Debit might not have been processed when you were expecting. It could have been previously deactivated, or the merchant may have scheduled it for a different date. To check if the Direct Debit is still active, and to verify its scheduled date:
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap the calendar icon in the top-right corner
- Choose the transaction to view the scheduled charge date
If you can't see the transfer, it was either disabled, or it wasn't successfully created in the first place. In these cases, contact the merchant directly to clarify the situation, or set it up again.

## Direct Debit failed or reverted
Check to see why your Direct Debit failed or was reverted in your transactions list. Just tap on the unsuccessful transaction for more info.

## Dispute a Direct Debit
Tap ‌the Direct Debit in your transactions list and choose the problem you're experiencing. You can then follow the prompts to dispute the Direct Debit.

title: Manage Direct Debits
content_text:
## How to view my Direct Debits
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap on the calendar icon in the top-right
- View active Direct Debits

## Cancel a Direct Debit
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap on the calendar icon in the top-right
- Tap on an active Direct Debit
- Tap 'Cancel future payments'

## Change the date or amount of a Direct Debit
Revolut can't change the amount or ‌date of a Direct Debit. You must cancel it and contact the merchant to set up a new Direct Debit.

## Problems with a Direct Debit
If you have a problem with your Direct Debit, open your scheduled payments or tap on a completed scheduled payment in your transactions list to get specialised support.
If you need more help with a Direct Debit, read this FAQ.

title: Setting up a Direct Debit
content_text:
A Direct Debit is when you authorise someone else to withdraw from your account at regular intervals. Usually, Direct Debits are set up to pay rent, bills, or mortgages. Direct Debits are only available for local EUR and GBP transfers.

## How to set up a Direct Debit
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Tap 'Account'
- Tap Account details
- Tap 'EUR' or 'GBP'
- Tap 'Share' to provide your Revolut account details to the merchant
If your Direct Debit was previously deactivated, you'll need to set it up again by following the same process as before. We can't set up Direct Debits on your behalf. This must be done by you directly with the merchant.

## Fees and limits
We don't charge any fees for setting up or processing Direct Debits. You can set up as many different Direct Debits as you want, for any amount.

## Difficulty with a Direct Debit
To manage your Direct Debit, go to 'Scheduled payments'. Read more about managing Direct Debits in this FAQ.
If you have a problem with your Direct Debit, go to 'Scheduled payments' or tap a completed transfer in your transactions list to get specialised support.
If you need more help with a Direct Debit, read this article.

title: Sending and requesting money with a payment link
content_text:
Payment links are a secure way to send or receive money without sharing personal information or account details.
They can also be used to send money to a card, bank account, or Revolut account, and to request money from a card, external bank account, or a Revolut account.

## How to create a payment link
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'New' (the + sign) in the top-right corner
- Tap 'Create a payment link' 
- Select 'Send money' or 'Request money'
- Set the amount to send or request
When you send money, the recipient will have 7 days to accept your payment. If unclaimed, the money will automatically be returned to your account. When you request money, the sender will have 10 days to complete your request and then the link will expire.

## Limits for sending money with a payment link
There are no limits to sending money via payment links.

## Limits for requesting money with a payment link
You can receive a maximum of £250 per week (or the equivalent in other currencies) via card payments. To request money from non-Revolut customers, Revtag discoverability should be enabled in-app while sharing the payment link. To enable it, in the Revolut app:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Accounts' and 'Privacyhttps://app.revolut.com/settings/security/privacyrevolut://app/privacy'
- Tap 'Make me discoverable' and choose 'Revtag'

title: Why did my recipient receive less money?
content_text:
Revolut fees for transfers are always visible in-app before sending the transfer, as well as in the transfer details after completion. To check them, tap on the transfer in your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. Common reasons your recipient may have received less than the sent amount:
- Transfers in different currencies: The recipient’s bank may charge an exchange fee if the currency is not the same as the recipient's account
- SWIFT transfers: Intermediary banks may charge handling fees
- Recipient bank fees: The recipient bank may charge a fee for incoming transfers
Learn more about what fees may apply to your transfer, in this article/help/transfers/bank-transfers/which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer.

title: I was asked for additional information about my transaction
content_text:
To ensure the safety of your account and maintain compliance, we may need to request additional information about a transaction — for example, evidence regarding a transaction or verification of your identity. These measures are part of our commitment to secure financial practices.

## How do I submit the information?
- Follow the instructions you see in-app to verify your identity and submit what's required
- Make sure that the documents you upload are clear, readable, and contain all the necessary details as outlined in the verification instructions

title: More help with sending money via a payment link
content_text:
## Change the amount
To change the amount on a payment link, you need to cancel it and create a new one.

## Cancelling a payment link
You can cancel a payment link at any time before it expires, as long as it's not already claimed. To do so, tap the payment in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list, and select 'Cancel'. 

## Why did my transfer to a Revolut customer appear as a link?
If the other person isn't your friend on Revolut, has their discoverability limited, or isn't on Revolut yet, then your transfer to their email or phone number will be sent as a pending payment link.

## Why is my payment link transfer pending?
If you're trying to send money using a phone number, it's possible that this transfer was executed as a payment link. Go to your transactions list, select the relevant transfer (it will be marked as 'sent via payment link'), and tap 'Cancel'.
Once the transaction is cancelled, check if the phone number matches your intended recipient, including the country code, and try again. Alternatively, you can ask this person for their Revtag, which can be found in their profile.

## Transfer was a fraud or scam
If your transfer link has been redeemed by the beneficiary, the money was sent, and it ended up being fraudulent, immediately end all communication with the fraudster or scammer and follow these steps in the Revolut app:
- Select the transfer in your transactions list
- Tap 'Report this transfer as fraud' and follow the instructions

title: Revolut.me link
content_text:
## What is a Revolut.me link?
Revolut.me links are a secure way to receive money without sharing personal or account details. They can be used to request money from a card or a Revolut account.

## How to create a payment link
- Tap on 'Payments'
- Tap 'New' (plus sign in the top-right corner)
- Tap 'Create a payment link'
- Choose if you wish to request money
- Share a generic link and allow the sender to input the amount

## Revolut.me limits
- You can receive a maximum of £250 per week (or the equivalent in other currencies) via card payments
- A Revolut.me link can't receive more than 20 completed top-ups within a week
- A Revolut.me link can't receive more than 40 completed top-ups within a month

title: Insufficient funds for scheduled transfer
content_text: If you don't have enough money in the relevant currency account or assigned Pocket, the transfer will fail. You'll need to complete a one-time transfer to send the money.

title: Setting up scheduled payments to another Revolut customer
content_text:
- Go to 'Payments' on the  bottom menu 
- Tap the search bar at the top of the page
- Search for and select a friend or contact to schedule payments to
- To add a new contact, search for them by phone number, email, Revtag, or scan their QR code
- Once selected, tap 'Send'
- Enter the amount you’d like to transfer and tap the calendar icon on the left
- From the calendar, you can schedule when you’d like the payment to be carried out
- Tap 'Send' again to finalise the scheduled payment
Once scheduled, you can manage your scheduled transfers in the ‘Upcoming’ section of your 'Transactionshttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list'.

title: Can I transfer money to or from Russia or Belarus?
content_text: Revolut does not currently support transfers to or from Russia and Belarus.

title: Can I transfer money to Ukraine?
content_text:
## Bank transfer
You can send bank transfers to Ukraine, except unsupported regions, such as Crimea, Donetsk, Kherson, Luhansk, Sevastopol, and Zaporizhzhia. Fees may apply. Learn more about bank transfer fees in this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer.

## Transfer to a card
You are entitled to one fee-free card transfer to Ukraine per month. After the first transfer, there will be a 1% fee per transfer (1% of the transfer amount).

title: Supported currencies for outgoing transfers
content_text:
## Transfer money in the following currencies
AED, AUD, BOB, CHF, CLP, CAD, COP, CRC, CZK, DKK, EGP, EUR, GBP, GTQ, HKD, HUF, IDR, ILS, INR, JPY, KRW, LKR, MXN, MYR, NOK, NPR, NZD, PEN, PHP, PLN, RON, RSD, SAR, SEK, SGD, THB, TRY, USD, VND, ZAR.
Currently, we don't support transfers in: BOB, CRC, EGP, GTQ, IDR, INR, MYR, PEN, PHP, and VND outside of Bolivia, Costa Rica, Egypt, Guatemala, Indonesia, India, Malaysia, Peru, Philippines, and Vietnam, respectively.

## Other unsupported currencies
- USD transfers from Barbados, Curaçao, Gibraltar, Jersey, Malta, Mauritius, and St Maarten
- EUR transfers from Mauritius and Sint Maarten
- CHF and NOK transfers from Malta
These lists are subject to change as we expand our support for new currencies.

title: Benefits and risks of trading crypto ETNs
content_text:
## Risk warning
You should never invest unless you’re prepared to lose all the money you invest. This is a high-risk investment and you shouldn't expect to be protected if something goes wrong. We recommend taking a minute or two to learn more on this pagehttps://cdn.revolut.com/legal/terms/Revolut_Trading_Ltd/ETN-Risk-Warning.pdf.

## Trading crypto ETNs
While crypto exchange-traded notes (crypto ETNs) offer a way to gain exposure to crypto assets through your investment account, they're still subject to the high volatility of cryptoassets in general, among other risks.

## Benefits of trading crypto ETNs
- Crypto ETNs allow you to gain exposure to volatile crypto markets without dealing with cryptoasset wallets or private keys. They can be bought and sold through your standard investment account
- Crypto ETNs are traded on exchanges throughout the trading day. Trading volumes and execution prices will vary depending on market conditions
- Crypto ETNs typically publish daily NAV (Net Asset Value) and disclose their holdings
- The underlying cryptocurrency is held by regulated custodians. At least 90% of the cryptoassets are maintained in cold storage or equivalent arrangements

## Risks of trading crypto ETNs
- Cryptoassets are complex and highly volatile, with prices rising or falling quickly. Investing in crypto ETNs means you could lose all the money you invest
- The crypto assets market is largely unregulated, with risks from cyberattacks, financial crime, and firm failures that could also cause losses
- You should do your own research before investing. If something sounds too good to be true, it probably is
- Putting all your money into a single type of investment is risky. A good rule of thumb is not to invest more than 10% of your money in high-risk investments
- The Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) doesn’t protect crypto ETNs, because they're not a type of investment that the FSCS can protect. Learn more on this websitehttps://www.fscs.org.uk/check/investment-protection-checker/.

title: Get started with crypto ETNs
content_text:
You should never invest unless you’re prepared to lose all the money you invest. This is a high-risk investment and you shouldn't expect to be protected if something goes wrong.

## How can I access crypto ETNs?
Crypto exchange-traded notes (crypto ETNs) are high-risk investments and can be highly volatile. They're traded on the London Stock Exchange, and can be accessed through general investment account. Crypto ETNs are not available for trading in Stocks & Shares ISA.
However, access may be subject to restrictions based on your jurisdiction, account type, or investor classification, and not all Revolut customers may be eligible to invest. Before you can access crypto ETNs, we are required to assess whether these investments are appropriate for you.
If crypto ETNs are deemed appropriate for you, you will be subject to a 24-hour cool-down period before you can start investing in them.

## How is appropriateness assessed?
To determine whether crypto ETNs are appropriate for you, you'll need to complete an appropriateness test. This test assesses your investment experience, your knowledge and understanding of the product’s characteristics, and your understanding of the risks of investing in crypto ETNs.
We'll review your categorisation to see if you're a restricted or high net worth investor. Read about restricted investors in this FAQ, or high net worth investors on this page/help/wealth/available-securities-and-instruments/etps/question-invest-high-net-worth-investor.
If our assessment indicates that the product may not be appropriate for you, we may not be able to offer access or we may provide additional guidance and information to help you make an informed decision.

## Important investor protection notice
The Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) doesn’t protect this type of investment because it’s not a type of investment that the FSCS can protect. Learn more by using the FSCS investment protection checker on this pagehttps://www.fscs.org.uk/check/investment-protection-checker/.
Protection from the Financial Ombudsman Service (FOS) does not cover poor investment performance. If you have a complaint against an FCA-regulated firm, FOS may be able to consider it. To learn more about FOS protection, visit this websitehttps://www.financial-ombudsman.org.uk/consumers.

title: Get started with non-crypto ETPs
content_text:
## How can I access exchange-traded products (ETPs)?
Standard exchange-traded products (ETPs), such as plain-vanilla Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs), are traded on regulated stock exchanges and can be accessed through your investment account.
Complex exchange-traded products (complex ETPs) — including ETNs, ETCs, crypto ETNs, and leveraged or inverse ETFs — are high-risk investments. While they are also traded on regulated stock exchanges, they can only be accessed through eligible investment accounts.
Your access to complex ETPs may be subject to restrictions based on your investment profile, and not all retail clients may be eligible to invest. Before you can access complex ETPs, we are required to assess whether these investments are appropriate for you.

## How is appropriateness assessed?
To determine whether complex ETPs are appropriate for you, you will need to complete an appropriateness test. This test assesses your investment experience, as well as your knowledge and understanding of the product’s characteristics and the risks of investing in complex ETPs.
To access crypto ETNs, you will need to complete a separate appropriateness test, which is distinct from the test for other complex ETPs. 
If the assessment indicates that the product may not be appropriate for you, we may not be able to offer access, or we may provide additional guidance and information to help you make an informed decision.

## What happens if I don’t pass the test?
If you fail the appropriateness assessment, you will be able to retake the test after a cool-down period. You should only retake the test if you have the appropriate knowledge and experience to invest in complex ETPs and fully understand the risks involved.

title: What does it mean to be a high-net-worth investor?
content_text:
To invest in crypto ETNs, you are required to certify whether you qualify as a high-net-worth investor or a restricted investor. 
By selecting 'high-net-worth investor' as your client classification, you confirm that you qualify as a high-net-worth investor on the basis that — in the last financial year — at least one of the following applies:
- You had an annual income of £100,000 or more (this doesn't include any one-off pension withdrawals)
- You had net assets of £250,000 or more. Net assets don't include your home (primary residence), your pension (or any pension withdrawals), or any rights under qualifying contracts of insurance

## High-net-worth investor risk acknowledgement
By selecting this client classification, you accept that being a high-net-worth investor will expose you to promotions for investments where there is a significant risk of losing all the money you invest.
With this client classification, you also confirm you’re aware that it is open to you to seek professional advice before making any investment in a high-risk investment.

title: What does it mean to be a restricted investor?
content_text:
To invest in crypto exchange-traded notes (ETNs), you're required to certify whether you qualify as a high-net-worth investor or a restricted investor. 
Putting all your money into a single business or type of investment is risky. Spreading your money across different investments makes you less dependent on any one to do well.
You should not invest more than 10% of your net assets in high-risk investments. Doing so could expose you to significant losses.

## Definitions of net assets and high-risk investments
For the purposes of this classification,
- Net assets do not include: your home (primary residence), your pension (or any pension withdrawals) or any rights under qualifying contracts of insurance
- High-risk investments are: cryptoassets (such as bitcoin), cryptoasset exchange traded notes, unlisted debt and equity (such as companies not listed on an exchange like the London Stock Exchange), peer-to-peer (P2P) loans, investment based crowdfunding, and units in a long-term asset fund

## Restricted investor eligibility
By selecting this client classification, you confirm that you qualify as a Restricted investor on the basis that:
- In the past 12 months, you have invested less than 10% of your net assets in high-risk investments (as defined above), and
- You're not planning on investing more than 10% of your net assets in high-risk investments (as defined above) in the next 12 months

## Acknowledgement of risks and advice
By selecting this client classification, you also accept that being a restricted investor will expose you to promotions for investments where there is a risk of losing all the money you invest.
You also confirm you’re aware that it is open to you to seek professional advice before making any investment in a high-risk investment.

title: Leveraged and inverse ETPs
content_text:
## What are leveraged and inverse ETPs?
Both leveraged and inverse exchange-traded products (ETPs) are intended primarily for short-term trading or hedging strategies and are generally not suitable for retail investors and investors with long-term investment goals due to daily compounding and high volatility.

## What is a leveraged ETP?
Leveraged ETPs are designed to deliver a multiple (e.g., 2× or 3×) of the daily performance of an underlying asset. For example, if the underlying index rises by 1% in a day, a 2x leveraged ETP could aim to increase by 2%.
While leverage can magnify potential gains, it also amplifies losses, making these products highly volatile and riskier than non-leveraged ETPs. Additionally, daily compounding can affect returns over longer periods, regardless of the performance of the underlying assets.
Leverage is not available for crypto ETNs.

## What is an inverse ETP?
Inverse ETPs are designed to deliver the opposite daily performance of an underlying asset. For example, if the underlying index falls by 1% in a day, a 1× inverse ETP could aim to increase by 1%.
Inverse ETPs, while offering potential profit from asset declines, are highly risky. Losses are magnified if the asset rises, and daily compounding can negatively impact long-term returns regardless of the asset's performance.
Inverse strategy is not available for crypto ETNs.

title: What is a crypto ETN?
content_text:
Don’t invest unless you’re prepared to lose all the money you invest. This is a high-risk investment and you should not expect to be protected if something goes wrong. 

## Crypto exchange-traded notes
A crypto exchange-traded note (ETN) is a financial product that allows you to invest in cryptoassets, like Bitcoin or Ethereum, directly through your investment account without needing to buy or store the cryptoassets yourself.
Crypto ETNs are debt instruments issued by a financial institution, which means you are exposed to the issuer’s credit risk. However, this risk (while not eliminated) is reduced because the issuer physically holds the underlying cryptoassets. 
In practice, this means that each note is backed by real cryptoassets held in custody. In addition to being physically backed, the crypto ETNs we offer are listed on the London Stock Exchange (LSE), and the underlying cryptoasset is Bitcoin or Ethereum.

## Crypto ETNs vs cryptoassets
The main difference between cryptoassets and crypto ETNs is that buying cryptoassets means you own the actual crypto, while crypto ETNs allow you invest in the price of the crypto through a product on the stock exchange, without needing to store or manage the cryptoassets yourself.
Crypto ETNs do not eliminate the inherent volatility of cryptoassets. The value of a crypto ETN still rises and falls with the price of the underlying cryptoasset, so investors remain exposed to the same high volatility risks.

## Important investor protection notice
The Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) doesn’t protect this type of investment because it’s not a type of investment that the FSCS can protect. Learn more by using the FSCS investment protection checker on this pagehttps://www.fscs.org.uk/check/investment-protection-checker/.
Protection from the Financial Ombudsman Service (FOS) does not cover poor investment performance. If you have a complaint against an FCA-regulated firm, FOS may be able to consider it. Read more about FOS protection on this pagehttps://www.financial-ombudsman.org.uk/consumers.

title: Get started with ETCs
content_text:
## What are ETCs?
Exchange-Traded Commodities (ETCs) allow you to gain exposure to the performance of a commodity, such as gold, oil or other raw materials, directly through your investment account without needing to buy or store the commodities yourself.
ETCs are debt instruments issued by a financial institution (the issuer), which means you are exposed to the issuer’s credit risk. Some may be physically backed, while others are synthetically replicated (find out what this means below).
ETCs can help diversify your portfolio by introducing exposure to commodities. However, the value of an ETC rises and falls with the price of the underlying commodity, which may potentially be highly volatile.

## What are the different types of ETCs?
A physically backed ETC means that the issuer physically holds the underlying commodity, such as gold bars, and its value tracks the commodity’s price. While this reduces counterparty risk because the asset is held in custody, it is not entirely eliminated.
A synthetic ETC means that the issuer does not hold the underlying commodity, but instead uses derivatives or other financial instruments to replicate its performance. This introduces additional counterparty risk, as returns depend on the issuer’s financial obligations.
Some synthetic ETCs may be leveraged and/or inverse and carry higher risk. Read more about these in this FAQ.

## Do ETCs pay out dividends?
ETCs typically don’t pay out dividends.

title: Get started with ETNs
content_text:
## What are ETNs? 
Exchange-Traded Notes (ETNs) allow you to gain exposure to the performance of an underlying index or asset class. They are debt securities issued by a financial institution (the issuer), which you can trade through your investment account.
ETNs can provide access to markets or asset classes that may be difficult to invest in directly, helping to diversify a portfolio by offering exposure to assets that may behave differently from other investments, without holding the underlying assets directly.
However, in addition to being exposed to the credit risk of the issuer, ETNs can sometimes experience high volatility and limited liquidity. You should be aware of these risks before investing.

## What is the difference between an ETF and an ETN?
While some ETNs and ETFs may aim to track the same index or group of assets, they are fundamentally different instruments. ETNs are debt securities issued by a financial institution, not funds that hold assets. 
When you invest in an ETN, you are lending money to the issuer which means you are exposed to the issuer’s credit risk. If the issuer cannot meet its obligations, you may lose some or all of your investment, even if the underlying asset performs well.
Some ETNs may be leveraged and/or inverse and carry higher risk. Read more about these in this FAQ.

## Do ETNs pay out dividends?
ETNs typically don’t pay out dividends.

title: What is an Exchange-Traded Product (ETP)?
content_text:
An Exchange-Traded Product (ETP) is a type of financial instrument that is traded on a stock exchange. ETPs are designed to track the performance of an underlying asset, such as indices, commodities, or other financial instruments, without requiring you to hold the asset directly.
ETPs allow you to gain exposure to markets that might otherwise be difficult, through your investment account. 
Depending on their structure, ETPs may track the underlying asset’s price directly or through other financial mechanisms, and they carry risks related to both the underlying asset and the structure of the product itself.

## What are the different types of ETPs?
ETPs come in different types and levels of complexity, depending on how they track the underlying asset. The risks and benefits vary depending on the structure of the product and the underlying assets.

## Different Types of ETPs
- Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) are funds that hold a portfolio of assets and typically aim to track the performance of an asset class or index. Learn more in this FAQ
- Exchange-Traded Commodities (ETCs) are debt instruments that track the performance of commodities, such as gold, oil, or other raw materials. Learn more on this page
- Standard Exchange-Traded Notes (ETNs) are debt instruments that track the performance of an underlying asset, such as an index or asset class. Learn more in this article
- Crypto Exchange-Traded Notes (Crypto ETNs) are debt instruments that track the performance of a cryptoasset. Learn more in this FAQ

## Which ETPs are complex ETPs?
Crypto ETNs, ETNs, and ETCs are considered complex because their value depends not only on potentially highly volatile underlying assets, but in some cases also on the financial health of the issuer. 
Some of these complex ETPs also use derivatives or other financial instruments, which can affect how their price moves.
Because of this, these products can be harder to understand than standard investments such as ETFs. However, some ETFs will also be considered complex ETPs if they use leverage or have inverse characteristics.

title: Exceeding my ISA allowance
content_text:
## What happens if I exceed my overall ISA allowance?
If you overcontribute to your ISAs, any excess payments won't qualify for tax relief. The ISA allowance is a total annual limit that applies across all the ISAs you might have, including Stocks and Shares, Cash, Innovative Finance, and Lifetime ISAs. 

## What should I do if I exceed my ISA allowance?
If you exceed your £20,000 annual ISA allowance, you’ll be contacted by the HMRC in due course. While we have measures in place to help ensure you don't exceed your allowance within your Revolut Stocks and Shares ISA, we don't have visibility into any ISAs you hold with external providers.

title: Investing with the Stocks and Shares ISA
content_text:
## How can I fund my Stocks and Shares ISA?
Similar to our investment accounts, you can top up your Stocks and Shares ISA from your Revolut current account.

## What can I invest in?
With a Revolut Stocks and Shares ISA, you can invest in shares and exchange-traded funds (ETFs) available under the ‘Invest’ tab in-app.

## Year-to-date contributions 
You can see all your Stocks and Shares ISA contributions to date under the ‘Invest’ tab in-app.

title: Stocks and Shares Individual Savings Account (ISA)
content_text:
## Stocks and Shares ISA
Stocks and Shares ISAs offer a tax-efficient method for investing. With Revolut's Stocks and Shares ISA, you can invest in shares and exchange-traded funds (ETFs) available in-app.
A key benefit of a Stocks and Shares ISA is that any interest or income you earn are not subject to income tax or capital gains tax in the UK. The value of tax benefits and reliefs always depends on your personal circumstances and tax rules may change in the future.
You can contribute up to the annual ISA allowance limit set by HMRC, which is £20,000 for the 2025/2026 tax year. This allowance can be split across different ISA types, allowing you to contribute the annual allowance across the Stocks and Shares ISA and other ISAs at the same time.

## What is a flexible Stocks and Shares ISA?
ISA flexibility determines whether withdrawals from your ISA impact your annual allowance. A flexible ISA allows you to withdraw funds and deposit them into the Stocks and Shares ISA within the same tax year, without reducing your annual ISA annual allowance.
For example, if you deposit £15,000, withdraw the same amount, and redeposit it, your remaining allowance is still £5,000. The allowance is based on net deposits per tax year (total deposits minus withdrawals). Withdrawals must be carried out within the same tax year to maintain the allowance.

## Am I eligible for a Stocks and Shares ISA?
To open a Stocks and Shares ISA, you must meet the following criteria:
- You’re at least 18 years old
- You’re a UK resident for tax purposes
- You haven’t exceeded your £20,000 ISA limit for the current tax year
You’re also eligible to open a Stocks and Shares ISA if you’re an existing customer of Revolut Trading Ltd with an open investment account and you meet the above criteria.

## How do I open a Stocks and Shares ISA?
If you're new to investing with Revolut, you can open a Stocks and Shares ISA in your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Invest' on the bottom menu
- Select 'Start investing'
- Choose ‘Stocks and Shares ISA’
If you’re an existing customer of Revolut Trading Ltd with a general investment account, select the Stocks and Shares ISA in the 'Invest' section. You’ll need to complete the Stocks and Shares ISA onboarding process to create your account.

title: Stocks and Shares ISA rules
content_text:
## ISA rules and contribution limits
A Stocks and Shares ISA is a tax-efficient way to invest in the stock market and other eligible assets, allowing you to earn returns exempt from the UK tax. For the 2025/2026 financial year, you can contribute up to £20,000 across all your ISAs combined.
This means your total ISA allowance applies to all types of ISAs you might have across different providers, including Stocks and Shares, Cash, Innovative Finance, and Lifetime ISAs. 
You can choose to invest the full £20,000 in a Stocks and Shares ISA, or divide it between different ISA types based on your financial needs.

## How many ISAs can I have?
There's no limit on the number of ISAs you can hold across different providers. However, you can only have one Stocks and Shares ISA with Revolut at a time.

title: Stocks and Shares ISA transfers
content_text:
## Stocks and Shares ISA transfer types
We don't currently accept transfers into your Revolut Stocks and Shares ISA from other ISA providers.
However, you may transfer all of the investments out of your Revolut Stocks and Shares ISA to a new ISA provider at any time. 
We'll issue a transfer to the new ISA provider in cash. This means that before the transfer, you'll need to liquidate the relevant investments in your Revolut Stocks and Shares ISA. We'll then transfer the proceeds to your new ISA provider, subject to the deduction of any fees (where appropriate).

## ISA transfer timelines
We'll process any transfer from your Revolut Stocks and Shares ISA within 30 days of receiving the request from your new ISA provider.
For specific timeframes, we recommend checking with both us and the new provider.

## What are the transfer charges?
We don’t charge any fees for Stocks and Shares ISA transfers. 

## How to initiate an outbound Stocks and Shares ISA transfer
- Contact your new ISA provider and request a transfer
- Complete their transfer authority form
- Your new broker should send the request to us via email at stock-transfers@revolut.com 
- We'll ask you to approve the transfer once we receive it
- We'll keep you updated in-app until the transfer is fully completed

title: What are the Stocks and Shares ISA fees?
content_text:
## Variable fees for transactions
Any orders submitted beyond your monthly allowance will incur a commission fee. If you have both a Revolut investment account and a Stocks and Shares ISA, your commission-free order allowance applies across both accounts - there's no separate allowance for the Stocks and Shares ISA. 
- Standard, Plus, Premium, or Metal customers: 0.25% of the order amount after your monthly plan allowance
- Ultra and/or Trading Pro customers: 0.12% of the order amount after your monthly plan allowance
Learn more about your plan limits in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/trading-fees/what-fees-will-i-be-charged-for-my-trading/.

##  Other important information
- There are no custody fees 
- There are no subscription fees for maintaining a Stocks and Shares ISA
- We don’t charge any fees for transfers out from your Stocks and Shares ISA with Revolut
You can read more about general trading fees in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/trading-fees/what-fees-will-i-be-charged-for-my-trading/.
You can find the Ex-Ante Cost and Charges Disclosure documents applicable for all stock types on this pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-ex-ante-costs-and-charges-disclosure/.

title: What happens to my Stocks and Shares ISA if I move abroad?
content_text:
## Proof of address 
As a Stocks and Shares ISA provider, we need to ensure that our customers are residing in the UK. We may request you to provide proof of your residential address during the sign-up process. Documents need to be recent (e.g. issued within the past 3 months) to be considered valid.

## Accepted proof of address documents
We can accept any of the following documents for address verification:
- Sky, cable TV, or any other utility bill
- Bank, building society or credit card statement
- Council tax bill or statement
- HMRC documentation
- Tenancy agreement or mortgage statement
- Driving licence (UK full or provisional)
- Official correspondence from a UK government department
- Electoral register confirmation
 Your document must meet the criteria outlined in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/i-need-to-verify-my-address/ in our Help Centre.

## What happens to my Stocks and Shares ISA if I move abroad?
If you open a Stocks and Shares ISA and later move abroad and become non-UK resident, you can still keep it open, but you won't be able to add new funds. You must notify us when this move happens.
Any money or investments already held in your ISA will continue to qualify for UK tax benefits as they did when you were a UK resident. 
Remember that the value of tax benefits always depends on your personal circumstances and tax rules may change in the future. For more details, refer to the UK government’s ISA guidance on this page.

title: Trading Terminal
content_text:
## What is the Web Terminal and who can use it?
The Web Terminal is our browser-based trading platform designed for a desktop-optimised experience. It allows you to manage your portfolio and execute trades on a larger screen with enhanced viewing tools.
The Web Terminal is available to eligible customers in the UK and operates under the same regulatory framework, terms, and investor protections as our mobile app.

## How do I access the Web Terminal?
To access the Web Terminal, go to the Revolut Invest websitehttps://invest.revolut.com/. You will be able to log in with the same credentials and password you use to access your accounts in the mobile app. For security purposes, you’ll be prompted to authenticate the login via your mobile app.

## How does the Web Terminal differ from the mobile app?
The Web Terminal is built specifically for larger screens, offering an expanded layout and navigation style that differs from the mobile experience. While we're working toward full feature parity, there are currently a few key differences:
- You can't open new investment accounts or complete 'Appropriateness tests' directly on the Web Terminal. All initial onboarding and product activations must be completed within the mobile app
- We're actively working towards feature parity between the mobile app and the Web Terminal, but certain products and features may not currently be supported on the Web Terminal. Portfolio transfers and commodities (which are provided by Revolut Ltd) are not currently available on the Web Terminal

title: Crypto collections
content_text:
A collection is a group of cryptocurrency tokens that we've grouped based on the underlying use-case of the token. You'll find themes like gaming, metaverse, or DeFi related collections.

## Getting started with collections 
You have the option to buy a complete collection. If you want to sell the tokens you bought from a collection, you’ll need to sell each token individually. You can only invest in collections using your fiat balance.
To find collections in your Revolut app:
- Go to the 'Crypto' section
- Tap 'Discover'
- Scroll down to 'Collections'
- Tap 'See all' or 'Buy' if you want to invest
Crypto fees don’t apply, however fair usage fee and limits will apply to collections. To learn more about fees, read this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies in our Help section.

## Minimum investment amount
For Standard and Plus customers, the required minimum investment amount is £1 (or currency equivalent). The amount invested will be evenly distributed among the selected tokens after deducting the fee.

## Reflection of collections in your portfolio
After investing in a crypto collection, you'll see the balances for each token individually among your holdings, in the same way you’d see these tokens if you had bought them separately.

title: Accessing Revolut X
content_text: Revolut provides 2-factor authentication (2FA) for added security when logging into the Revolut X. Enter your phone number and Revolut passcode, then verify with 2FA by confirming via a one-time password from your email or phone, or by a push notification in your phone if accessing via web.

title: Revolut X top-ups and withdrawals
content_text:
## Send and receive money
Currently, your Revolut X account supports sending and receiving funds from your personal Revolut account and crypto withdrawals to external addresses in the web terminal. We're actively working on adding deposits from external wallets.

## Top up or withdraw on the web platform
- Tap 'Deposit' or 'Withdrawal' in the top-right corner
- For withdrawals, pick the Revolut X balance matching the currency you want to withdraw. For top-ups, choose the balance from your retail app in crypto, or add USD, EUR or GBP using any available currency
- Enter the amount and confirm
- The transaction's confirmation details will appear if the transaction requires an exchange (if you selected a balance different from the currency you're adding)

## Top up or withdraw on Revolut X
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Select 'Deposit' or 'Withdrawal' in the centre
- Once the option is selected, select 'Crypto' or 'Cash' on the pop-up
- Enter the amount and confirm
- The transaction's confirmation details will appear if the transaction requires an exchange (if you selected a balance different from the currency you're adding)

title: Get started with Revolut X
content_text:
## What is Revolut X?
Revolut X is our cryptocurrency trading platform. 

## Start using Revolut X
There are two ways to join Revolut X. If you already have a Revolut account, you can start using Revolut X by following these steps:
- Go to the Revolut X platformhttps://exchange.revolut.com or the Revolut X app
- Log in with your Revolut account details. A pop-up will ask you to open your Revolut account to enable access to Revolut X
- Accept the Revolut X T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/en-PL/legal/crypto-exchange-terms/?geo-redirect=true
- Answer a few questions about yourself and the purpose of your account to start using Revolut X
If you don’t have a Revolut account, you can still sign up to Revolut X directly using the app or platform.

## Who can use Revolut X?
- You must be at least 18 years old
- You must complete the sign-up process and have your documents verified by our team
To open and use a Revolut X account, you must provide:
- A valid mobile phone number
- A valid email address
- Personal information, including but not limited to your address, date of birth, full name, and citizenship(s)
- A valid government-issued ID for identity verification
- A selfie for account security

## Revolut X fees
Revolut X fees vary based on whether your order is a maker (placement in order book) or taker (immediate execution of order). Details on fees can be found on our Crypto Exchange Fees Pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/crypto-exchange-fees, or within the 'Trades' section in your 'Activity' tab after an order is filled.

title: What types of orders can I create on Revolut X?
content_text:
## Order form
To use the order form on Revolut X, you’ll need to:
- Go to Tradehttps://exchange.revolut.com/trade
- Select the trading pair at the top of the 'Order form' widget
- Choose to buy or sell and select the type of order
- Enter the amount of the asset you wish to trade
- Confirm to buy or sell

## Cancelling the order
Market orders can’t be cancelled once they’re created.
To cancel a limit or stop-loss order on Revolut X:
- Go to Tradehttps://exchange.revolut.com/trade
- Navigate to the 'Activity' widget
- Select the order under 'Open orders'
- Tap 'X' on the right side of the pending order you aim to cancel

title: About crypto unified balance
content_text:
Your existing crypto balance will now be unified across both the main Revolut app and Revolut X. This means that you’ll see a single crypto balance across both platforms.
All your open orders will be cancelled on the day of the migration before your balance is consolidated, and your portfolio analytics may take up to 1 day to update after the migration. We'll email you with the migration date.
Unified balances remove the need for topping up or withdrawing crypto assets from your main account to your X account, and vice versa. For tokens not supported on Revolut X, the balance will be visible, but will not be available for trading on Revolut X.

## What happens to my fiat balance on Revolut X?
There will be a dedicated fiat currency balance in your Revolut X account, which you can use exclusively for Revolut X orders. You can top up, withdraw, and transfer this balance, similar to your main Revolut app fiat currency balance.
If you wish, you can also transfer your fiat currency balance to or from your main Revolut account.

## Do I need to take any action for the unified balance setup?
The unification of the crypto balance across Revolut X and your main Revolut account will be automatic. You’ll see the updated balance in both of the platforms, once the unified balance setup has officially launched.

## Can I generate a statement for only Revolut X transactions?
With the launch of a unified crypto balance, any transaction statements generated in Revolut X or the main Revolut app will have transaction histories from both platforms by default.
If you need a statement exclusively for Revolut X or your main Revolut account transactions, it can be generated on demand. Reach out to our support team and they will help you get this statement.

title: Revolut X fees
content_text:
Revolut X has different fees applicable to different functionalities of the platform.

## Revolut X trading fees
We offer exposure to cryptocurrencies trading. The exchange rate for your transaction is determined by the type of your order, which you can see on our Trading Rules pagehttps://www.revolut.com/en-PL/legal/crypto-exchange-trading-rules/?geo-redirect=true. To see how much fees you will be paying, see our Fees pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/crypto-exchange-fees/?geo-redirect=true. 

## Revolut X deposits and withdrawals fees
For adding funds to Revolut X, you can use your balance from your Revolut account, with zero fees. See how to add or withdraw funds using Revolut account in this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/crypto-exchange/how-can-i-make-a-top-up-in-the-crypto-exchange. When you withdraw cryptocurrency externally in your Revolut X, you'll be charged a fee comprised of 2 parts.
- Revolut service fee: 1 GBP (or equivalent in local currency) for XRP, XLM, DOT, SOL, AVAX, XTZ, ALGO, and ADA; 3 GBP (or equivalent in local currency) for all other cryptocurrencies
- Network fee: The network fee is paid to the blockchain miners or validators who process the transaction
The network fee is variable and depends on the transaction speed and the traffic on the blockchain network. Revolut calculates the network cost, adds the service fee, and displays the total estimate cost. You'll never be charged more than what is quoted in the app.

title: Failed orders in Revolut X
content_text:
## Common reasons for order failures:
- Your account has insufficient funds
- You've exceeded the exchange limit
- The order amount is below the minimum or above the maximum limit

## For limit orders:
- The order has expired
- The target price wasn’t met
- There wasn’t enough market liquidity

title: Why can't I trade in Revolut X?
content_text:
## Insufficient Funds
In order to trade in Revolut X, you must have funds available in your Revolut account:
- For buying crypto: You must have any fiat currency balance available 
- For selling crypto: You must have balance available on the asset you're attempting to sell
Make sure you have enough balance to cover the minimum order size. You can check this in the Trading Ruleshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/crypto-exchange-trading-rules/?geo-redirect=true.

## There's an unplanned maintenance
Revolut X may execute unplanned maintenance sometimes to ensure that our markets are properly executing all customer orders.
What happens during the maintenance period:
- Orders: Your orders may get pending and will not be executed in the meantime
- Transfers: Your transfers may get pending and can't be processed in the meantime
- Portfolio: Your portfolio value may be affected in the meantime

## When will the maintenance be solved?
After maintenance occurs, transfers will be processed and the portfolio will get back to normal.
For orders, if it is a limit order and the market has not reached the conditions you set after maintenance, it will remain pending. For market orders, it will be cancelled (if not executed immediately when instructed and got stuck pending).
If you cannot use Revolut X even though you accepted the terms, your account is verified, and there's no maintenance, request live support by typing 'Live agent' in the chat.

title: Why is part of my Revolut X portfolio balance unavailable?
content_text:
## Locked balances
If you added limit orders to Revolut X, and this order is pending, the amount that you used to instruct the order will be locked to ensure that you have sufficient balance when the execution is triggered.
In case you need to use the balance for other purposes, you may cancel the pending orders before execution is triggered.

## To cancel a limit order on the Revolut X web platform:
- Go to 'Tradehttps://exchange.revolut.com/trade' on the left menu
- Navigate to the 'Open orders' widget in the inferior side
- Select the order under 'Open orders'
- Tap 'x' on the right side of the pending order you aim to cancel

## To cancel a limit order in the Revolut X app:
- Tap 'Activity' on the bottom menu
- Select the order under 'Open orders' tab
- Tap 'Cancel' at the top of the pending order you aim to cancel

title: Issues with Take Profit or Stop Loss orders
content_text:
## My order was cancelled
Your order may be cancelled if it couldn't be executed within the allowed price range or after multiple attempts. For Stop Loss orders, the system will try to sell your crypto within a ±2% range of your target price during the execution window.
If the price moves too far outside this range, the system will pause and retry up to 10 times, checking the rate every 5 minutes. If after 10 attempts the system cannot execute the order, it will cancel it automatically.

## My Stop Loss didn't trigger
If the market price moved too quickly and fell outside the ±2% range before the system could trigger your Stop Loss, the order may not have been executed.
The system retries the order up to 10 times, pausing for 5 minutes between attempts. If the market continues to move outside this range, the order will eventually be cancelled.

## My order executed, but not at the price I set
In fast-moving markets, the price may fluctuate during the execution window (the time between the trigger and the execution of your order). While the system tries to execute the order at your target price, the final price could be different due to these changes.
The system will always execute the order at the best available price at the time, which could be higher or lower than your set price.
For more details, refer to our Crypto T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/cryptocurrency-terms/?geo-redirect=true.

title: Take Profit and Stop Loss orders
content_text:
## What are Take Profit and Stop Loss orders?
Take Profit allows you to automatically close a trade when your asset's price reaches your target, helping you lock in profits.
Stop Loss does the opposite: it automatically closes a trade when the price drops to your set level, helping to limit your losses. The system tracks the current market rates and triggers the order when your chosen price is reached.
However, due to market volatility and potential system delays, the price can change quickly during the execution window (the time between the trigger and the execution of your order). This means the order may be filled at a different price or, in some cases, not executed at all.

## Can I modify or cancel my Take Profit or Stop Loss order?
You cannot modify an existing order. However, you can cancel the current order and set up a new one if needed, as long as the original order hasn't been triggered yet. Once the order is triggered, it cannot be changed or cancelled.

## Are there any fees for using Take Profit or Stop Loss?
There are no extra fees for setting Take Profit or Stop Loss orders, but standard trading fees apply when the order is executed. Make sure to review the platform's fee structure for details.
For more details, refer to our Crypto T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/cryptocurrency-terms/?geo-redirect=true.

title: Setting up a virtual crypto card
content_text:
You can set up a virtual crypto card that allows you to pay with cryptocurrency. You can use it to pay anywhere, whether in person or online.
We'll automatically convert your crypto to the currency of your purchase without any fees. Fair usage fees may apply depending on your plan. Check out this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies about fair usage fees and this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/crypto-payments/when-i-choose-to-pay-from-all-crypto-accounts-in-which-order-will-revolut-deduct-from-my about crypto card payments.

## Set up a virtual crypto card
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the card icon in the top-right corner
- Tap 'Add new'
- Select 'Crypto'
- Tap 'Get Crypto Virtual Card for FREE'
- Label your card, pick the design, and choose which Pocket to use or if you want to use all Pockets
Once your card is ready, you can add it to Apple Pay or Google Pay. You can also change your spending Pocket selection at any time in the card's settings. 
If you'd prefer not to create a new virtual crypto card, you can link any of your existing Revolut cards to a crypto Pocket, except Revolut Pro and Revolut Business cards. If you'd like to order a regular card, visit this FAQ/help/cards/card-order/physical-card/ordering-a-physical-card.

title: Crypto payment fees, rates, and limits
content_text:
## Crypto card payments fees
You won’t be charged any exchange fees for crypto transactions. However, fair usage fees may apply depending on your plan. Check out this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies for more information.

## Crypto card payments exchange rates
Revolut uses the real-time exchange rate at the time your card payment is completed to calculate how much crypto will be deducted from your balance.
To see the exchange rate that was applied to your crypto card payment, tap the transaction to see the details.

## Crypto card payments limits
- Crypto exchange limits: there's a limit of maximum 100 exchanges in 24 hours. These 100 exchanges will be subjected to a minimum and a maximum limit depending on the cryptocurrency
- ATM withdrawal limits: for cash withdrawals from ATMs, there's a limit of £3,000 (or currency equivalent) per 24 hours
- Spending limits: there's a limit of £100,000 (or currency equivalent) per transaction. Transactions exceeding this amount will be declined
Paying with crypto is just like selling crypto. In many countries, this is considered a taxable activity. For guidance on taxable and non-taxable activities, you should seek your own independent legal advice.

title: Crypto payments and how they work
content_text:
You can link any Revolut card to a crypto Pocket, except Revolut Pro and Revolut Business cards. You can also create a new virtual crypto card. To find out how, visit this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/crypto-payments/setting-up-a-virtual-crypto-card in our Help Centre.
When you make a crypto card payment, we'll exchange your crypto into the currency of your purchase to pay the merchant. 
You can choose to spend from all crypto accounts or one specific crypto account.

## Spending from all crypto accounts
Revolut will deduct from the cryptocurrency with the highest account balance first, followed by the one with the next highest balance, and so on, until the total amount is paid.

## Spending from a specific crypto account
If you don't have enough crypto for the chosen payment, the transaction will be declined. If this happens, try one of the below options.
- Buying more crypto
- Choosing to spend from a different crypto
- Opting to pay from all crypto accounts
- Opting to pay from all cash accounts
Once that's done, you can try the transaction again. We don't offer cashback for crypto card payments.

## Refunded payment
For crypto card refunds, you'll receive them in the fiat currency of your purchase (not a cryptocurrency). Fair usage fees may apply depending on your plan. See this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies for more information.

title: View your crypto card payments
content_text:
There are several ways to view your crypto card payments in-app.

## In your crypto account statement
In the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Choose a cryptocurrency
- Tap 'Statement'
- Choose a timeframe and tap 'Generate'

## In your transactions list
In the Revolut mobile app:
Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu → search through your crypto card payment(s) in your transactions list.

## In the cards section
In the Revolut mobile app:
Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu → tap the card icon in the top-right corner → select the card you used for the crypto card payment.
To see the applied exchange rate, select each transaction. To learn more about exchange rates, go to this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/crypto-payments/what-are-the-fees-for-crypto-transactions-using-my-revolut-card.

title: How does crypto staking work?
content_text:
Staking is the process of actively participating in block validation on proof-of-stake (PoS) blockchains, similar to mining on proof-of-work blockchains. By staking your cryptocurrency, you support network security and earn token rewards.

## Tokens supported for staking
- Cardano (ADA)
- Algorand (ALGO)
- Cosmos (ATOM)
- Polkadot (DOT)
- Ethereum (ETH)
- Injective (INJ)
- Kusama (KSM)
- NEAR Protocol (NEAR)
- Osmosis (OSMO)
- Polygon (POL)
- Solana (SOL)
- Sui (SUI)
- Celestia (TIA)
- Toncoin (TON)
- TRON (TRX)
- Tezos (XTZ)
For most protocols, you cannot sell or send your assets while they're staked. Once you request to unstake your assets, you’ll need to wait for a period of time before you can access them again. This is known as a lockup period.
We'll make this time clear to you, but you'll be exposed to price changes while your assets remain staked. During the lockup period, most protocols don't give rewards.
