## What is APY and how are staking rewards calculated?
The Annual Percentage Yield (APY) rate you see in-app is a variable projection based on the rewards we've received historically for each token and that we expect to continue to receive in the future. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. 
Each network sets the underlying return rate depending on the number of staking participants. We take a commission on all rewards received, and the return rate for our customers reflects this commission. The APY you see in-app is the net of Revolut's commission.
Crypto staking rewards are not guaranteed. Tax may be payable on the rewards received.

## Is staking crypto safe?
We work with trusted third-party providers to protect your funds from exposure to risk. Staking crypto carries risks, such as slashing and downtime penalties, which can result in loss of stake.
Revolut will continue holding staked cryptoassets on your behalf and you'll remain the beneficial owner of your cryptoassets.
It's important for you to understand the unique risks that staking cryptoassets carry. Read our Cryptoasset Specific Riskshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/cryptoasset-specific-risks/ page to learn more.

title: Is there a fee associated with crypto staking?
content_text:
Revolut doesn’t charge any fees on your staking rewards. We give you 100% of the staking rewards we receive on-chain, and no extra fees are taken by Revolut.
For some blockchains, a small validator fee of up to 3% of gross staking rewards is deducted on-chain by third-party validators to cover their costs. This is taken before staking rewards reach Revolut.
The annual percentage yield (APY) shown in-app is the net estimated rate you're expected to earn.

title: Stake and unstake crypto
content_text:
## Get started with staking
To start staking in your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Select one of the supported tokens
- Tap 'Stake'
Some tokens might have a waiting period before you can earn rewards. Rewards can be delayed due to factors beyond our control. The minimum staking amount varies by crypto token and will be displayed in-app when you stake.

## Cancelling staking
You can request to unstake at any time in your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Select the token you want to stop staking
- Tap 'Unstake'
- Specify the amount you want to unstake and tap 'Confirm'
Some tokens may have lock-up periods where funds aren't immediately accessible post-unstaking, and no rewards are issued during this time. You’ll see the expected waiting period in-app. For details on staking fees, visit this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/crypto-staking/crypto-staking-costs-and-benefits/is-there-a-fee-associated-with-staking in our Help Centre.

title: Calculation of crypto tiered pricing
content_text:
We calculate your pricing tier based on your total trade value over the last 30 days, updating this calculation every minute. For example, a trade made on 6 February 2024 at 15:15 Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) counts towards your 30-day trading volume until 7 March 2024 at 15:14 GMT.

## Check your current tier and trading volume
In your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'More'
- Select 'Limits and fees'
- Tap 'Exchange fee'
You'll be able to see your current tier, 30-day trading volume, and tiers on other plans if you wish to upgrade.
If you are on the Standard or Plus plan, you'll be charged either a percentage-based fee or a flat minimum fee, whichever is greater. More information on these fee structures is available on our Fees pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/exchangingcryptocurrenciespersonalfees/?geo-redirect=true.

## Currency conversion
We'll convert the amount you intend to sell to your domestic currency at the exchange rate at that time.
Below are some examples if you're based in Denmark:
- Sell DKK and buy BTC: We'll simply take the DKK value of the trade
- Sell GBP and buy ETH: We'll take the value of GBP and notionally convert it to DKK at the GBP/DKK rate at that time
- Sell USDT and buy XAU: We'll take the value of USDT and notionally convert it to DKK at the USDT/DKK rate at that time

title: Tier progression and account changes
content_text:
## Tier adjustments and progression
Real-time tier adjustments
When trading with Revolut, your crypto tier can shift just as a trade is placed due to real-time calculations of your 30-day trading volume. If your tier lowers during a trade, refunds for the higher fees can't be issued. This system ensures tiers reflect your most current trading activity, calculated to the minute. For details on your specific usage, check the Exchange fees page in your app or visit this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/crypto-tiered-pricing/how-is-my-previous-30-day-crypto-trading-volume-calculated.
Tier progression
You can advance through multiple tiers based on your trading volume. However, transactions made just as you move to a higher tier will incur fees from your current tier. This policy applies until the trade that transitions you to the next tier is completed.

## How account changes affect your tiered pricing and volume
Plan change
Plan upgrades or downgrades don't affect your trading volume, ensuring your progress towards the next tier remains uninterrupted. However, your pricing tier will be immediately reflected.
Address change
Moving to a different country resets your 30-day traded balance even between two countries that are eligible for the promotion.

title: Trading with crypto tiered pricing
content_text:
Crypto tiered pricing is a pricing structure that gives you access to lower fees, once you trade certain volumes. Depending on your plan, location, and previous 30 days trading volume, you can benefit from lower fees if your trading volume falls within a specific tier.
For details on your specific usage, check the Exchange fees page in your app or this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/crypto-tiered-pricing/how-is-my-previous-30-day-crypto-trading-volume-calculated.

## Included transactions
The trading volume is applicable to buy and sell crypto transactions made in the Revolut main app only. We don't include the following transactions:
- Trades from stablecoin to fiat and vice versa
- Trades between stablecoins
- Trades between commodities and crypto assets
- Crypto withdrawals or deposits
- Transactions involving staking or rewards associated with staking
- Cashback and spare change round ups
Trades made prior to the introduction of tiered pricing don't count towards your trading volume. Tiered pricing begins as per the dates mentioned on our Fees pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/exchangingcryptocurrenciespersonalfees/?geo-redirect=true.

## Reviewing fees before a trade
We'll let you know about applicable fees before you submit the trade in-app. This will be shown on the 'Order confirmation' screen. Here you can view the specific fee details by taking the following steps:
- Select 'Fee'
- Tap 'Exchange fee' 
- Check the fee amount being applied to your trade
On the same exchange fee window, if you select 'Learn more', you'll be able to see the pricing tiers applicable to your plan.

## Fees and tier changes
If a transaction moves you into a higher pricing tier, the fee assessed will be based on the tier you're currently in, not the new tier. See our Fees pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/exchangingcryptocurrenciespersonalfees/?geo-redirect=true for pricing per plan.

title: How to make sure crypto withdrawals are processed smoothly
content_text:
Revolut is designed to complete genuine transfers promptly. Read more in this blog posthttps://www.revolut.com/blog/post/crypto-trust-uk/.

## Input information correctly
When attempting to withdraw crypto, it's important to carefully double-check all the information you enter to avoid potential losses. This includes:
- Making sure the address is entered correctly
- Make sure you're transferring on the chain of the recipient wallet

## Respond to us truthfully
If we request additional information from you, it’s because we're trying to protect you. If you don't respond, the withdrawal will be cancelled. There's no impact on your account if this happens. However, if you respond untruthfully, there's a risk you might lose your funds. 

## Don't transact with unknown addresses
If we see a transfer towards an address we think is suspicious, we have the right to cancel it. In very rare circumstances, such as illegal activity, we may restrict or close your account. This is only done as a last resort.

title: How to ensure my crypto is transferred successfully?
content_text:
Revolut is designed to complete genuine transfers immediately, with 95% of transactions completed within 1 minute. Only 4.1% of transactions require additional checks, which are typically completed within 3 hours. For less than 1% of transactions, we may require more information to process. 

## How to complete crypto deposits
It's important to carefully check all the information you enter to avoid potential losses. Make sure:
- Your Revolut deposit address has been pasted correctly in the external sending wallet
- You transfer a token Revolut supports
- You transfer on a chain Revolut supports

## How to complete crypto withdrawals
It's important to carefully check all the information you enter to avoid potential losses. Make sure:
- Receiving address has been pasted correctly in send flow
- The address is compatible with the selected cryptocurrency or is on a correct network
If we detect a scam or potential fraud during a withdrawal, we trigger additional questions to raise your awareness and protect you from potential loss. Always answer carefully and remain vigilant.

## If further information is requested
We'll send you a notification, as well as display in-app banners, to get your attention if we need additional information. Just tap the notifications to see exactly what's required from you.
The additional information should be straightforward. For example, a bank statement that shows proof of funds, so we can ensure the source of the payment is genuine. We explain crypto transfers and withdrawals in more detail in this blog posthttps://www.revolut.com/blog/post/crypto-trust-uk/?geo-redirect=true.

title: Crypto notifications
content_text:
## Rates widget
Rates is an in-app widget that monitors current exchange rates for selected cryptocurrencies.
To add it to your 'Home' screen:
- Go to 'Home' screen on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Add widgets'
- Select 'Rates'

## Crypto price volatility notification
You can receive automated price volatility alerts and be notified whenever the price of cryptocurrencies moves more than 2%. 
To activate this alert in your Revolut app:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner 
- Select 'Notification settings' 
- Enable the 'Crypto volatility alerts' toggle
To make sure you stay updated with real-time notifications about your transactions, enable notifications for the Revolut app in your mobile device’s settings.

title: Crypto recurring buy
content_text:
Automating crypto purchases at regular intervals, also known as dollar cost averaging, helps minimise volatility by averaging the price over time.
For example, buying BTC monthly for a year results in buying at the yearly average price.

## Set up crypto recurring buy
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Trade' and select the token
- Tap 'Buy'
- Tap the order type switcher on the order screen (next to the 'Review order' button) and select 'Recurring buy'
- Enter the purchase amount
- Set the start date and frequency, then tap 'Schedule'
- Review your order, read the risk disclosure, and tap 'Confirm order'

## Delete or deactivate a crypto recurring buy order
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Scroll down to 'Orders'
- Select the cryptocurrency recurring buy order you wish to stop
- Select 'Deactivate' or 'Delete'
Deactivating makes the recurring buy visible but inactive — you can reactivate it anytime. Deleting removes it completely, requiring a new setup if you change your mind.

## Why would a crypto recurring buy fail?
- Insufficient funds in your account
- Exceeded exchange limits (see this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-to-make-currency-exchanges/how-do-i-exchange-money for details)
In such cases, the transaction will be cancelled, and the recurring buy will resume from the next period. For example, if a weekly purchase fails, it will be cancelled and resume the following week.

title: Crypto rates and spread
content_text:
We calculate cryptocurrency prices by using a volume weighted average price (VWAP), which averages prices from exchanges we've partnered with, considering factors like market depth and volatility.
When buying, you might notice the ask rate (the highest price buyers are ready to pay) is usually higher than the bid rate (the lowest price sellers accept). This difference is called the spread. To learn about fees for buying and selling crypto, read this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/getting-cryptocurrency-exposure/how-much-does-it-cost-to-buy-or-sell-cryptocurrency in our Help section.

title: Fees for trading crypto
content_text:
## Fee pricing structure 
Crypto trades will be charged either a minimum applicable fee or a tiered pricing fee, whichever is greater.
Minimum fees are charged on lower value trades, with specific amounts subject to your local currency and plan. Minimum fees per local currency are set out on our Fees pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/exchangingcryptocurrenciespersonalfees/.
Crypto tiered pricing is a pricing structure that gives you access to lower fees, subject to plan and trading volumes. See this pagehttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/crypto-tiered-pricing/what-is-crypto-tiered-pricing-and-how-does-it-work/ for more information on tiered pricing.

## Fair usage fees
Fair usage fees are only applicable to certain plans. If the fair usage limit is exceeded per plan, you'll pay an additional fair usage fee. Read this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies/ to learn more.

## ﻿Fees for exchanging stablecoins
Exchanging between fiat (regular money) and stablecoins, or vice versa, doesn't have a crypto fee. However, if you exchange stablecoins for other cryptocurrencies, or vice versa, there’ll be a crypto fee applied. See herehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/exchangingcryptocurrenciespersonalfees/ for more information on these fees.

title: Crypto limit order
content_text:
A limit order is an order to buy or sell at a specified price or better. Buy limit orders will be executed at the specified price or lower. Sell limit orders will be executed at the specified price or higher.
Limit orders aren't guaranteed to execute. Limit orders will only be executed if the price of the crypto increases or decreases to the set limit price.

## Setting a limit order
For a buy limit order, the crypto is purchased at your limit price or lower. For a sell limit order, the crypto is sold at your limit price or higher. To set a limit order in-app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Trade' and select a cryptocurrency
- Tap 'Buy' or 'Sell'
- Tap the order type switcher on the order screen (next 'Review order') and select 'Limit order'
- Enter the limit price value, and tap 'Continue'
- Enter your limit order purchase amount, and tap 'Review order'
- Review your order, read the risk disclosure, and tap 'Submit order'

## Cancel an order
In the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Select the cryptocurrency related to the order you wish to deactivate or cancel
- Tap 'Orders'
- Select 'Cancel' for limit orders

## Examples of how crypto limit orders work:
- Buy limit order: Crypto 'X' is trading at USD 5, but you’re only interested in buying if it goes below USD 4. Set your limit price to USD 4. If crypto 'X' drops to USD 4 or lower, your order will be executed at USD 4 or lower. If it doesn’t drop to USD 4, your order won’t execute
- Sell limit order: Crypto 'X' is trading at USD 5, but you're only interested in selling if its price goes above USD 6. Set your limit price to USD 6. If crypto 'X' rises to USD 6 or higher, your order will be executed at USD 6 or higher. If it doesn't rise to USD 6, your order won't execute

title: What is a crypto market order?
content_text:
A market order is an order to buy or sell a crypto at the market’s current best available price. Market orders are often prioritised over other order types, which generally means they're matched and executed immediately.
It usually ensures execution but does not guarantee a specified price. Therefore, it is often used by investors who want to fill their orders quickly.
To place a market order, select the crypto you want to buy or sell and enter your order details (you'll be able to preview any fee)

title: Crypto stop order
content_text:
A stop order is an order to buy or sell crypto once it reaches a specified price, called the stop price. When this price is reached, the stop order becomes a market order and is executed at the best available price (which might be higher or lower than the stop price).
Stop orders aren't guaranteed to execute until the crypto reaches the stop price.

## Setting a stop price
For a sell stop order, set a stop price below the current crypto price. If the crypto falls to your stop price, the sell stop order becomes a sell market order. The execution price may vary from the stop price. To set a stop order in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Trade' and select a cryptocurrency
- Tap 'Buy' or 'Sell'
- Tap the order type switcher on the order screen (next to the 'Review order' button) and select 'Stop order'
- Enter the stop price value, and tap 'Continue'
- Enter your stop order purchase amount, and tap 'Review order'
- Review your order, read the risk disclosure, and tap 'Submit order'

## Cancel an order
In the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Select the cryptocurrency related to the order you wish to deactivate or cancel
- Tap 'Orders'
- Select 'Cancel' for stop orders

## An example of how a crypto stop order works
If crypto 'X' is currently trading at USD 5, and you want to sell the crypto as soon as its price goes below USD 4, you'd set your stop price to USD 4. This may be because you suspect that price might fall further, and you want to stop holding the crypto when it reaches USD 4. 
Here's what happens next:
- Price falls to stop price: If the price of crypto 'X' falls from USD 5 to USD 4, your sell stop order will become a sell market order and will be executed at the best price available
- Price doesn't reach stop price: If the price of crypto 'X' doesn’t reach USD 4, your order won't be triggered

title: Crypto Learn
content_text:
Crypto Learn is an in-app education tool where you learn about cryptocurrencies.

## Access Crypto Learn:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Select 'Discover'
- Scroll down and tap 'Learn'
- Tap 'See all' to check all the available courses

## Available countries
Currently, Crypto Learn is supported in the EEA, the UK, Switzerland, Australia, New Zealand, Singapore and Brazil. Some courses might not be available in all regions.

## Access issues
If you're unable to access a course, please make sure your app is updated to the latest version and try again.

title: How does converting cryptocurrency to fiat currency work?
content_text:
With Revolut you can convert your cryptocurrency from and to fiat in just a few seconds, if you select a market order. The transaction will first go into a pending state within the app, and then we'll attempt to fulfil the exchange.
Once confirmed within our platform, the price will be locked in and your transaction will be updated and completed. If you select the limit order, stop order, or recurring boy options, these orders will be executed at a future date when they are triggered.

title: Crypto delisting
content_text:
Upon delisting, the affected token is removed from our app, with any remaining balances converted to your main currency at the current market rate. Delistings can lead to increased volatility and‌ significant price drops, influenced by reduced liquidity and negative investor sentiment.

## Possible reasons for delisting

## Regulatory and compliance issues 
If a token is found to be in violation of regulations (e.g. sanctions, deemed to be a security, etc.), it may be delisted.

## Insufficient liquidity
Low liquidity can pose risks to customers and exchanges, including high volatility and slippage. We, along with our partners, enforce specific liquidity requirements. Failure to meet these can lead to delisting.

## Tokens with very low value 
These tokens are susceptible to price manipulation, which is a risk we can’t tolerate.

## Other factors
Long-term token viability can be impacted by security issues or negative publicity, such as fraud or unethical actions by the supporting team.
Tokens may still be delisted despite partner support if they meet our liquidity requirements, exceed our risk tolerance, or due to regulatory requirements in specific jurisdictions.
Revolut isn’t liable for losses if a token's value falls to zero or if it must be delisted due to unforeseen external events. Read more about delisting in section 2 of our Crypto T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/en-PL/legal/cryptocurrency-terms/?geo-redirect=true.

title: Crypto account onboarding requirements
content_text:
## Regulatory background
Crypto assets are categorised as 'restricted mass market investments' by the British regulator. 
We've made two changes to our crypto offering in response to current regulation:
- We changed the way we display risk warnings to make them more visible in your app, to follow regulatory requirements for this asset class
- When you open your account, you'll need to complete onboarding requirements and provide some investor information on an annual basis

## Account onboarding steps
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu and tap 'Start trading'
- Tap 'Continue' and answer investor categorisation questions
- Tap 'Start quiz' and complete a short assessment to test your knowledge of crypto
- Tap 'Accept' to proceed with accepting our investor statement and our Terms & Conditionshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/cryptocurrency-terms/
We'll let you know how many attempts you get to pass the assessment in-app. If you fail these attempts, you'll be given a cool-down period before you can attempt the assessment again. 
Once you pass it, you'll need to wait 24 hours before you can start trading crypto. You'll be able to see in-app how long you have left in the cool-down period and we'll notify you once this period is completed.

## If you've previously onboarded to crypto
If you had a Crypto account open with us before 8 October 2023, you'll need to go through the process above, as the same requirements will apply to you (except for the cool-down period).

## Available crypto features before completing onboarding
New and existing crypto customers can:
- View available tokens
- Complete 'Crypto Learn' lessons
- Set watchlists
- Set price alerts
- Deposit crypto
If you're an existing crypto customer, you can also withdraw, sell, and unstake your crypto.

## Annual attestation of investor details
As part of these new regulations, you'll need to review and confirm or update your investor categorisation details you provided the previous year to continue trading. This is to take into account any potential changes in circumstances. This process requires you to: 
- Update your Revolut app to the latest version (minimum 10.59, later if available)
- Confirm your investor categorisation
- Confirm some information based on the information you provided last year
Available crypto features before completing the annual attestation include selling, unstaking, withdrawing and depositing crypto. You may also view available tokens, set watchlists or price alerts, and complete 'Crypto Learn' lessons. Read this FAQ/help/crypto/glossary-of-onboarding-terms to see the glossary of onboarding terms.

title: Other crypto token information
content_text:
## How to view newly added tokens in-app
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Scroll down to 'Newly added'
- If you can't find this section, tap '+Add widgets' at the very bottom, then select 'Newly added' and 'Add widget'

title: Revolut crypto features and products
content_text:
Revolut combines traditional financial services with cryptocurrency management, offering an all-in-one crypto platform with a range of features. 

## Trading and sending crypto in the Revolut app
Revolut allows you to buy and sell 200+ cryptocurrencies through its app, suitable for both beginners and experienced crypto traders. To help you get started, you can: 
- view most-traded tokens and top movers 
- deep dive into analytics for each token
- stay up-to-date with crypto news 
- set up push and alerts to stay on top of your trades
- sending crypto between Revolut customers
You can send crypto between Revolut accounts instantly and for no added fees, making it easy to transfer cryptocurrency to friends and family.

## Sending crypto to and from external addresses
- Crypto deposits: you can deposit 30+ cryptocurrencies into your Revolut account from external wallets (e.g. self-hosted wallets, such as Metamask, or wallets with other providers, such as Coinbase or Binance) 
- Crypto withdrawals: you can withdraw 30+ cryptocurrencies from Revolut to external wallets 
Read these FAQs/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/crypto-withdraw-deposit about Revolut crypto remittances to learn more.

## More crypto features
- Crypto spare change: round up card payments and invest the difference in crypto
- Koinly tax reports: get discounted crypto tax reporting with Koinly
- Crypto Learn: learn about crypto and blockchain, for all levels
- Crypto card payments: spend crypto anywhere‌ — ‌Revolut converts it to fiat at checkout
- Crypto collections: buy and manage sets of crypto assets in one click
- Safety features: we store the crypto assets with fully licensed custodians using multi-signature wallets and cold storage. Your assets are held 1:1 and never lent out. For extra security, you may enable Wealth Protection
Read this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/understanding-revolut-cryptocurrency/crypto-safety/are-my-cryptocurrencies-secure about crypto safety in Revolut to learn more. Visit the Crypto website pagehttps://www.revolut.com/crypto/?geo-redirect=true to learn more about all supported features.

title: Can I set target prices for exchanging cryptocurrency?
content_text:
To exchange crypto automatically, you can use limit or stop orders.
A stop order is an order to buy or sell once the price of the asset reaches a specified price, known as the stop price. When the stop price is reached, a stop order becomes a market order and is executed at the best available price (which can be lower or higher than the stop price).
A limit order is an order to buy or sell at a specified price or better. Buy limit orders will be executed at the specified price or lower, sell limit orders will be executed at the specified price or higher.

title: Receive (deposit) crypto from an external address
content_text:
A crypto deposit is a transaction that allows you to send cryptocurrency from external wallets or platforms to your Revolut account via a blockchain. There is no limit to how much you can deposit.

## Find your crypto address
Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu and tap 'Receive', then select the desired cryptocurrency.
Review the minimum required deposit amount is specific to each token. Read this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/transferring-cryptocurrencies/depositing-cryptocurrencies/issues-with-crypto-deposits/why-is-my-crypto-deposit-pending to find out more about what happens if you make a deposit below the minimum amount.
Revolut does not charge fees for depositing crypto, but network fees will be deducted.

## Check deposit address carefully
Always use the address displayed in the 'Receive crypto' section for the specific cryptocurrency and network.
Do not deposit crypto to the same address used for withdrawals, and never use random wallet addresses provided by others. If you deposit to a different address than the one shown in the 'Receive crypto' section, you risk losing your funds.

## Supported tokens and networks
It's essential to use crypto and networks that Revolut supports for your transactions to make sure funds aren't lost. For example, for USDT we support Ethereum and Tron networks. Sending USDT on another network that may not be supported by Revolut will result in funds being lost.

title: Return or manage crypto deposits
content_text:
## Returning crypto deposits 
There are a few reasons why Revolut might not accept a crypto deposit:
- Depositing from an unsupported exchange (read this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/transferring-cryptocurrencies/depositing-cryptocurrencies/what-network-should-i-use-for-my-crypto-deposit to find out which exchanges are unsupported)
- Depositing from an unsupported wallet address (e.g. an address associated with illegal on-chain activities)
- Not providing additional information about a deposit, in cases where this is requested

## How to return a deposit
If you need to revert a deposit, you'll be notified in-app. Navigate to the transaction and begin the revert process through the banner on the transaction details.  Follow the steps and ensure the wallet address you want to revert to corresponds to the correct asset and is on the correct network.  To initiate a return:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Scroll to your transactions list or select the crypto you deposited
- Find your deposit transaction and tap on it
- Follow the steps of the flow
- Pick one of your existing wallets or enter a new one to revert
Your funds may be lost if:
- The address is not compatible with the selected cryptocurrency or is on a different network from the original deposit
- You reuse an address previously used for a crypto withdrawal without verifying it or use a random address provided by others 
- You can review the status of the reverted transaction in the transactions list 

## Returning the deposit failed
Sending your funds back might have failed due to issues with the address you provided. For instance, we do not allow returns to addresses associated with illegal on-chain activities. In such cases, you will need to attempt the return again using a different address.

## Cancelling crypto deposits
Deposits into your account can't be cancelled. However, you can sell, transfer, or potentially withdraw the cryptocurrency received. Any applicable fees will be visible in-app before any transaction is completed.

title: Failed crypto deposits
content_text:
Your deposit might be in a failed state if the blockchain rejects the transaction. This can happen if the network fee you paid was too low. 
In this event, the blockchain treats this deposit as if it never happened. The funds will be credited back to the wallet you sent them from. In case of any difficulties, contact the application where you initiated the transaction.

title: Addresses supported for withdrawing and reverting crypto deposits
content_text:
To send crypto (withdraw or revert a rejected deposit), you'll need to select an address compatible with the network you want to send cryptocurrency on. For example, if you'd like to send USDT on TRON, you need to select a TRON address and not an Ethereum address.
Additionally, as part of our internal checks, certain addresses may be disabled for withdrawing or reverting crypto, including previously used ones. In such cases, you'll need to withdraw or revert your funds to an alternative address instead.

## Addresses unsupported for reverting deposits
- Addresses that aren't compatible with the selected cryptocurrency or not on the same network as the original deposit
- Addresses that are pending internal checks or rejected
- Your Revolut address or another Revolut customer's address
- The address you used to make the deposit from

## Address formats and standards
Different networks have different address formats and standards. Here are some supported wallets and addresses for sending or reverting BTC, ETH, and ERC-20 tokens.
Bitcoin (BTC) address types:
- Pay-to-Public-Key-Hash (P2PKH) addresses: Starts with '1'
- Pay-to-Script-Hash (P2SH) addresses: Starts with '3'
- Bech32 addresses: Starts with 'bc1'
Ethereum (ETH) and ERC-20 wallet types:
- Ethereum addresses: Compatible with ETH and ERC-20 tokens
- Address format: Hexadecimal format starting with '0x'
Other wallet types:
- TRON (TRX): Addresses are alphanumeric strings starting with 'T'
- Ripple (XRP): Addresses are alphanumeric strings starting with 'r'
- Stellar (XLM): Addresses are alphanumeric strings starting with 'G'
- Solana (SOL): Addresses start with 'S'

title: Can't see my deposit in-app
content_text:
Your deposit may not be appearing in your Revolut app due to one of the reasons detailed below.

## Deposits on a network or crypto that Revolut doesn't support
Deposits of unsupported tokens or via unsupported networks won't be processed, and funds won't be added to your balance.
Even though such transactions might appear as completed on the blockchain, Revolut doesn't receive these transactions because it doesn't interact with blockchains or token contracts it doesn't support. To learn which tokens and networks are supported, read this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/transferring-cryptocurrencies/depositing-cryptocurrencies/what-network-should-i-use-for-my-crypto-deposit.

## Incorrect address details
Only use the address that you see in the 'Receive crypto' section for the cryptocurrency and the network shown on the screen.
Don't deposit crypto to the same address you used for withdrawing crypto and don't use random wallet addresses provided by others. You might lose your funds if you deposit to a different address from what you see in the 'Receive crypto' section.

## Deposit not submitted to the blockchain
Your deposit might not reach Revolut if it has not yet been confirmed on chain. To check this:
- Get the transaction hash from the sending platform (e.g. your Metamask wallet or your Coinbase account)
- Use the hash on a blockchain scanner to verify the transaction has been submitted on chain successfully
If you can't find the transaction hash then contact the support on the sending platform. In addition, there may be a time lag between when the transaction is initiated, and when the platform executes it on the blockchain. This varies depending on the sending platform.

title: Pending crypto deposits
content_text:
You can see the reason why your deposit is pending on the transaction details screen. To see the status of your deposit:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Scroll to your transactions list or select the crypto you deposited
- Find your deposit transaction
- Tap on it to see the details of pending reason
Your crypto deposit could be pending for a number of reasons.

## Internal checks
Revolut performs automatic internal checks on every deposit, which are typically completed within minutes. However, in rare cases where additional information is needed, the process can take longer.
If we need more information about your deposit, a banner will appear on your home screen. You can tap and follow the steps on screen to provide what's requested. Once you submit the information, it may take up to 3 working days for us to verify it.

## Deposit is below minimum threshold
If your deposit is below the required minimum, it won't be added to your balance right away. To fix this:
- Make another deposit in the same cryptocurrency and on the same network to meet the minimum amount
- Deposits on different networks won't count toward the same minimum. For example, if you made a deposit on the Tron network, adding funds on the Ethereum network won't help meet the minimum requirement
- The total balance will be credited in one consolidated deposit once the minimum is reached
- There is no time limit to meet the minimum deposit requirement
- For the latest information on minimum deposits for specific tokens, visit the respective crypto section for the token or refer to our T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/cryptocurrency-terms/?geo-redirect=true.

## Deposit not yet confirmed on the blockchain
If your deposit isn't confirmed on the blockchain, you can track its status using the transaction hash on a blockchain explorer. If a transaction remains in 'Confirming' or 'Pending' status for too long, it may be due to low network fees.
Blockchain settlement can take anywhere from a few minutes to 2 hours, depending on the network's activity. The amount of confirmations we require to consider a transfer final varies per blockchain. To see details on the blockchain explorer:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Scroll down to select your transaction or select the crypto you are depositing and find your pending transaction there
- Tap 'Blockchain hash'
- Choose 'View my transaction' to see details on the blockchain explorer

## We are experiencing technical issues
In very rare cases, deposits might take longer than expected due to technical issues. If this happens, we will notify you in-app.

title: Supported crypto deposit currencies, networks, and exchanges
content_text:
## How to check supported tokens and networks
To see the supported tokens and chain:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'More'
- Tap 'Limits and fees'
- Tap 'Multi-network transfer'

## Examples of supported currencies and networks
- BTC transfers on the Bitcoin network
- ETH, USDC, and MATIC on the Ethereum network 
- USDT on both the Ethereum and TRON networks
If you use an unsupported network (e.g.: attempt to transfer USDT on BSC network or ETH on BASE network), you might lose your crypto.

## View tokens available for deposits
To see the full list of available tokens in-app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Receive'
- You'll see the full list of tokens you can receive from an external wallet. Select the one you want to receive, and you'll see your deposit address for this token
- Select the network you want to receive it on — make sure you're sending it on the right network from your external wallet

## Supported crypto exchanges
We support deposits from major crypto exchanges like Coinbase, Binance, Kraken, Bitstamp, eToro, and more. 
In rare cases, we may also reject deposits from supported exchanges if we do not receive the necessary data that we need for compliance purposes.
We currently don't support deposits from several exchanges, such as Bitfinex, Bittrex, and Roqqu.

title: Do I have an individual cryptocurrency wallet?
content_text:
We aren't issuing individual wallets at the moment. Once purchased, your cryptocurrencies are securely stored in a 'pooled' virtual currency account, which combines both 'hot' and 'cold' wallets. This is a repository of virtual currency private keys.
We maintain our own internal ledger, which records your exposure that is reflected in the cryptocurrency account in your Revolut app.

title: Add a new wallet to withdraw crypto
content_text:
## Add a new crypto wallet
In your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Send'
- Choose the token
- Tap ‘Add wallet address'

## Wallet ownership
If the wallet address belongs to you, indicate so on the top of the page, where you can see 'My wallet'. If the wallet belongs to someone else, you'll be asked to provide the name and surname of the owner. We're obliged to collect this information in order to comply with Travel Rule regulations.
Before the wallet is added, you'll need to complete a security check by providing a 6-digit code that will be sent to your phone.

## Multiple tokens per wallet depending on wallet type
Depending on the type of wallet you're adding, you might be able to send more than one token. For example, if you add an Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) compatible address, you can send to it all supported ERC-20 tokens.

title: Crypto withdrawal fees
content_text:
When you withdraw a cryptocurrency in your Revolut app, you'll be charged a fee comprised of two parts:

## Revolut service fee
- 1 GBP (or equivalent in local currency) for XRP, XLM, DOT, SOL, AVAX, XTZ, ALGO, and ADA
- 3 GBP (or equivalent in local currency) for all other cryptocurrencies

## Network fee
This is paid to the blockchain miners or validators who process the transaction. 
The network fee is variable and depends on the speed of the transaction and the amount of traffic on the blockchain network. To provide an estimate, Revolut calculates the anticipated network cost, adds the service fee, and displays the total cost in-app. 
In some cases, the network fee may be higher than usual due to network demand. However, you'll never be charged more than what is quoted in the app just before you submit your withdrawal.

title: Crypto withdrawal limits
content_text:
## Daily and monthly limits
Daily and monthly limits apply to crypto withdrawals to prevent monetary loss and protect your account. Limits are periodically reviewed and may be adjusted based on account activity and any potential risk factors. You can see the limits by using this link or by following the steps below:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'More'
- Select 'Limits and fees'
- Tap Sending limits
Daily limits reset 24 hours after the last withdrawal and monthly limits reset 30 days after the last withdrawal. For example, if you withdraw £1,000 worth of BTC on 2 March, this will be deducted from your monthly limit which will reset 30 days later on 1 April.

## Crypto address limits
As an additional safeguard, separate limits may be applied to certain external crypto addresses, based on an assessment ‌of the address and your account's past activity.
If one of your addresses has specific limits, you will see that on the 'Select address' screen in your app. This limit will be reassessed every time you transact with this address, so it may disappear in ‌the future.
The limits will be shown to you on the 'Select address' screen when you enter an amount for crypto withdrawal. 

## Wealth protection limits
Additionally, your crypto withdrawals might be limited by Wealth protection limits. These limits are set and controlled by you in-app. To learn more about these limits, visit this FAQ.

title: Cryptocurrencies and networks available for withdrawals
content_text:
We only support sending certain cryptocurrencies on certain blockchain networks.

## Examples of supported currencies and networks
- BTC transfers on the Bitcoin network
- ETH, USDC, and MATIC on the Ethereum network 
- USDT on both the Ethereum and TRON networks
- XRP on the Ripple network
- SOL on the Solana network
If you try to send on a network not supported by the wallet you're trying to send to, you might lose your crypto.

## View tokens available for withdrawals
To see the full list of available tokens in-app:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Send'
- You'll see the full list of tokens you can send to an external wallet. Select the one you want to withdraw, and select an address you want to send to
- Select the amount you want to send 
You'll be able to see the network you're about to send on, as well as any other available networks you can use to send your crypto on.

title: Send (withdraw) crypto to an external address
content_text:
A crypto withdrawal is a transaction that allows you to transfer your crypto to an external wallet or exchange via a blockchain. It's an 'on-chain' transaction, which is irreversible.
We only support sending to external wallets for certain cryptocurrencies on certain blockchain networks. To learn more about supported tokens and networks, go to this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/transferring-cryptocurrencies/withdrawing-cryptocurrencies/crypto-withdrawal-basics/what-cryptocurrencies-are-available-for-sending-to-external-wallet.

## Send crypto to an external address
Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu and tap 'Send'. Choose the cryptocurrency you'd like to send and follow these steps:
- Select one of the already added compatible wallets or tap 'New' to add a new address. You can also paste a new wallet address in the search bar to proceed
- Enter the amount you want to send or withdraw and tap 'Send'
- Review the transfer, check the details and network
- Tap 'Send' to confirm 
Check that the recipient address is accurate by starting with a small transaction amount. We can't retrieve funds sent to an incorrect address or on a network not compatible with the address you selected.

## Sending crypto can't be reversed
After confirming the transaction, you can't cancel or reverse it (even if the status of the transaction is pending).

## Limits and fees of crypto withdrawals
Crypto withdrawals are subject to limits and fees. To check your crypto withdrawal limits:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'More'
- Tap 'Limits and fees'
- Go to 'Sending limits'
You can also read more about withdrawal limits and fees in this FAQ section. If you have a problem with your withdrawal, check this FAQ section.

title: Pending crypto withdrawals
content_text:
You can see the reason why your withdrawal is pending on the transaction details screen. To see the status of your withdrawal:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Scroll to the transactions list or select the crypto you withdrew
- Find your withdrawal transaction
- Tap on it to see the reason why it's pending
Your crypto withdrawal could be pending because it's going through internal assessment, broadcasting to the network, or confirmation on-chain.

## Crypto withdrawal internal assessment
We apply a number of different automated standard checks on every crypto withdrawal. Part of these checks is making sure you're not being scammed by bad actors.
In most cases, these checks will be completed within seconds. In some exceptional cases, we may ask you to provide more information in-app.
If we think there's a risk you're falling victim to a scam, we will ask you to go through a questionnaire to understand more about why you're sending crypto. Go through the steps with care and stay vigilant. Consider taking 5 minutes to think about it.

## Broadcasting crypto withdrawals to the network
Crypto transfers are cryptographically signed and broadcasted on a blockchain network. This step is automatic and is usually completed within seconds.
If there is a delay in this step it can mean that we're experiencing some technical difficulties not specific to your transaction. If this happens, we will notify you in-app with a banner on your home screen.
Once the issue is resolved, we'll either automatically reprocess your withdrawal, or you'll see your withdrawal fail, in which case you'll have to re-submit it.

## Confirming the crypto withdrawal on chain
When you send crypto, this is an on-chain transaction. This means that your transaction will have to be verified by the nodes of the blockchain. Different blockchains have different transaction confirmation methods and times, so how much time your transaction needs to finalise is variable.
You can expect your transaction to finalise within minutes. If there is high traffic on the selected blockchain network, this can take more time. You can monitor your transaction on the blockchain scanner by tapping 'Blockchain hash' on the transaction, then 'View my transaction'.
The blockchain explorer is the source of truth to check your transaction status. If your withdrawal is shown as completed on the explorer but the funds have not yet arrived on your receiving wallet, we recommend reaching out to the provider of the receiving wallet for further support.

title: Failed crypto withdrawals
content_text:
If your crypto withdrawal has failed, it might be due to:

## Address review
You tried sending crypto to an address ‌which is under review or has failed our checks. Addresses added by users are reviewed at multiple stages. Previously-used addresses can also be flagged and disabled due to automated checks.
For example, if Revolut determines that an address is linked to an unsupported exchange, future withdrawals to that address will be blocked (even if you have successfully withdrawn to it in the past). In case your wallet address was disabled or rejected:
- you can try to add another address 
- there's no option to unblock a wallet once rejected

## Recent account changes or activity restrictions
You may have tried to send crypto while your account is temporarily restricted. Check your notifications or emails for details.

## Crypto withdrawals not supported in your location
Your device location was flagged as being in a high-risk or sanctioned jurisdiction. We block certain withdrawals to comply with laws and regulations.
You can attempt your withdrawal again once you are in the country you onboarded in. We still reserve the right to block any withdrawals that don't pass our internal checks, as per our Crypto T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/cryptocurrency-terms/?geo-redirect=true.

## Potentially fraudulent withdrawals
We run checks on every transaction to make sure you don't fall victim to scams. If we identify the withdrawal as highly likely to be a scam, we'll either:
- notify you in-app that we think this transaction is fraudulent and ask you to answer a series of questions. Take care in answering them and consider allowing 5 minutes to question whether you might be falling victim to fraud and cancelling your transaction
- cancel it ourselves to make sure you don't lose your money

title: Cryptocurrency networks
content_text:
Cryptocurrency transfers are broadcasted and validated via networks of computers called blockchain networks or cryptocurrency networks.
A crypto token can be supported on multiple networks. And one network can support multiple cryptocurrencies.
For example, USDT can be transferred on not only the Ethereum network, but also on Tron. Similarly, the Ethereum network supports different tokens such as ETH, USDC and USDC, among many others.

## Network fees
Different networks have different network fees and different times of execution. It's very important to make sure that the selected network is the same as the network of the platform you're depositing or withdrawing funds to.
If you select the wrong network, it may not be possible to recover the funds.

title: Buy and sell crypto
content_text:
## Available cryptocurrencies
You can buy and sell 250+ tokens with Revolut, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, Polygon, Solana, Dogecoin, and more. You can view the full list in the Revolut app at any time by visiting the Crypto section.
You can also purchase groups of tokens. Read this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/collections/what-is-a-collection-and-where-can-i-see-it to learn more about collections.

## Buy or sell crypto (crypto exchanges):
When you buy or sell crypto in Revolut, you’re making what’s known as a crypto exchange — swapping one currency (like GBP or EUR) for a cryptocurrency, or vice versa. To do so in the Revolut mobile app:
- Go to Crypto on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Trade'
- Choose a cryptocurrency
- Select 'Buy' or 'Sell'
- Add the amount
- Review and confirm your order
Once a crypto exchange has been completed, it isn't possible to cancel a crypto transaction. The only way to revert it is to execute a new transaction in the opposite direction.

## Limits
You can make up to 200 exchanges in 24 hours. These 200 exchanges will be subjected to a minimum and a maximum limit depending on the cryptocurrency you wish to exchange.

title: Crypto basics
content_text:
## Cryptocurrency
Cryptocurrencies are digital assets secured through a peer-to-peer network of computers, not a central entity like a bank. They are volatile and unregulated, meaning their value can change rapidly. Tax may apply to any gains made through trading cryptocurrencies.

## Blockchain
A blockchain is a decentralized digital ledger of transactions. It grows in 'blocks' that contain recent transactions, ensuring transparency and security without a centralised ledger.

## Fiat currency
Fiat money is a government-issued currency not backed by a physical commodity, such as gold or silver. Examples include the US dollar and the euro. Cryptocurrencies are not tied to fiat currencies, nor are they issued or backed by any government.

## Private keys
A private key is a unique code tied to a cryptocurrency wallet address used to access cryptocurrencies. Revolut appoints third-party custodians to protect the private keys of all users.

title: Is my crypto safe with Revolut?
content_text:
## How does Revolut store my crypto?
- Your crypto assets stored in Revolut are held with trusted custodians 
- We use some of the most secure methods, such as multi-signature wallets, and the majority of assets are held in cold storage
- Your assets are held 1:1 and never lent out
For an extra layer of security for your crypto transactions, enable our in-app Wealth Protection feature. Check this FAQ/help/security-logging-in/wealth-protection/what-is-wealth-protection to learn more. 
If you have any questions or concerns, reach out to Revolut Support via the in-app chat at any time. 

## Do I own my crypto in Revolut?
While Revolut customers have beneficial ownership of their cryptocurrency assets, they don't have direct control over the private keys. Nevertheless, Revolut does operate based on your instructions, meaning we execute transactions as requested by the account holder.
We appoint third-party custodians on your behalf who protect the private keys of all Revolut customers. Check this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/understanding-revolut-cryptocurrency/what-are-private-keys-and-where-are-mine about private keys and crypto exposure for more information.

## What are some of the risks of investing in cryptocurrencies?
- Regulatory risks: Cryptocurrencies aren't regulated in many regions and don't have protections like other investments might
- Volatility: Cryptocurrencies are highly volatile and can significantly fluctuate in price, posing substantial risks of loss
- Scams: The unregulated nature and anonymity of transactions may make it easier for scammers to get away with fraud. Learn more in this blog posthttps://www.revolut.com/blog/post/p-2-p-crypto-scams/?geo-redirect=true on our website 
Consider investing only if you're comfortable with the potential risks, including the possibility of losing the full amount. If you're unsure about investing in crypto, it's wise to consult with an authorised and independent financial adviser. We don't endorse or advise on cryptocurrency transactions. For more information, read our Crypto T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/cryptocurrency-terms/?geo-redirect=true and this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/understanding-revolut-cryptocurrency/crypto-safety/who-provides-revolut-s-crypto-services about who provides crypto services.

title: Who provides Revolut's crypto services?
content_text:
In the UK, crypto services are provided by Revolut Ltd. 
Revolut operates as a registered cryptoasset firm ('Revolut Ltd', FRN 900562) under the Money Laundering, Terrorist Financing and Transfer of Funds (Information on the Payer) Regulations 2017. 
Funds received by us in relation to cryptocurrency transactions won't be safeguarded (unlike fiat currency transactions, pursuant to our obligations under the UK Electronic Money Regulations 2011). Any cryptocurrency we hold for you isn't covered by the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS).

## Learn more
Consider investing only if you're comfortable with the potential risks, including the possibility of losing the full amount. Check the risks associated with crypto on our Crypto Risk Summary pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/crypto-risk-summary/.
To learn more, check this pagehttps://www.revolut.com/how-we-keep-your-money-safe/?geo-redirect=true about how Revolut keeps your money safe and this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/understanding-revolut-cryptocurrency/crypto-safety/are-my-cryptocurrencies-secure about the safety of your crypto in Revolut.

title: Taxes on crypto
content_text:
## Calculating crypto gains
You can calculate your Profit and Loss (P&L) by downloading your transaction statements in-app. To access the statements:
- Go to 'Crypto' on the bottom menu
- Select your desired token
- Tap 'Statement'
You’ll be able to select the timeframe and the format for your statement.

## Tax obligations
Paying taxes on crypto gains will depend on the country you live in. We recommend you seek professional tax advice. In most countries, you may have to pay tax on the profits you make from buying and selling cryptocurrencies.
You can automatically sync your crypto transactions with many third-party tax report providers via third-party application programming interfaces (APIs). However, we currently have a partnership with Koinly, which gives you a special discount on crypto tax reports.
To be eligible for the discount, check that you have the latest version of the Revolut app installed and visit Koinly's website to set up an account in just a few taps. To learn more about Koinly, read this FAQ/help/wealth/cryptocurrencies/understanding-revolut-cryptocurrency/taxes-on-cryptocurrencies/what-discount-can-i-get-for-koinly-tax-reports in our Help section.

title: Koinly crypto tax reports
content_text:
Koinly services are available in more than 100 countries. Please check if your country is supported before completing the purchase. See this pagehttps://support.koinly.io/hc/en-us/articles/9908251419804-Which-countries-does-Koinly-calculate-taxes-for to view the full list of supported countries.
Register through Revolut to get a 20% discount on Koinly’s reports. Revolut crypto users with fewer than 250 transactions qualify for a 60% discount, applied at checkout.

## Eligibility
To be eligible for a Koinly’s discount, you must be:
- A first-time user of Koinly services
- A Revolut customer
Make sure your Revolut app is updated to the latest version before visiting Koinly's website to set up an account.
To find Koinly offer in-app:
- Go to 'Crypto' section on the bottom menu
- Tap 'More'
- Select 'Tax report'
T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/en-PL/legal/cryptocurrency-terms/?geo-redirect=true apply, and you’re solely responsible for filing your own taxes.

## Creating tax reports
Register via Revolut to auto-sync your transactions. Other accounts can be linked with read-only APIs or upload transactions manually via CSV or Excel files.
Your tax report becomes available immediately after payment. For registration, you’ll need to provide your name, email, and sync your Revolut crypto data with Koinly. For more information, you can refer to this pagehttps://support.koinly.io/en.

## Koinly support
You’re responsible for your tax filings and should seek independent advice. Koinly generates a report of your crypto tax gains for the year. If you encounter issues or have questions post-purchase, please reach out to Koinly’s support via their website or at support@koinly.io.
Remember, while Revolut offers a discount, it doesn’t provide Koinly’s services directly.

title: Why do I need to name the wallet owner for withdrawals?
content_text:
To comply with the FATF Travel Rule, which is a set of global standards aimed at enhancing the safety of virtual asset transfers, Revolut, as a virtual asset service provider (VASP), needs you to provide recipient information when making crypto withdrawals.

## Information required for compliance
- Sender's name (and address for transactions exceeding €1,000)
- Recipient's name
- Transaction hash (ID)
This information helps create a more transparent and traceable system for monitoring virtual asset transactions, contributing to global efforts to enhance transaction safety.

title: Crypto private keys and exposure on the blockchain
content_text:
## Can I access private keys of my crypto in Revolut?
A private key is a unique code tied to a cryptocurrency wallet address and is used to access cryptocurrencies. At Revolut, we appoint third-party custodians on your behalf who protect the private keys for all our crypto customers. While you control how your assets are used (such as buying, selling, or withdrawing), you don't have direct access to your private keys, which are typically required for managing crypto outside the Revolut ecosystem. 

## Can I view the blockchain exposure of the crypto in my Revolut account?
Your cryptocurrency exposure is stored on our internal ledger, which is a record of all debits and credits that occur within the Revolut platform, and is used to attribute funds to your account. That's why it's not possible to view your crypto exposure on the blockchain. However, you can manage your balances in fiat and cryptocurrency from your Revolut app at any time.

title: Crypto spare change
content_text:
The crypto spare change feature automatically rounds up your Revolut card transactions to the nearest whole number, using the spare change to buy a cryptocurrency of your choice.

## Set up spare change:
- Go to 'Crypto' in the bottom menu
- Tap 'More'
- Choose 'Settings'
- Select 'Spare change'

## Activity supported with spare change:
- Buy your desired cryptocurrency with your spare change
- Choose a multiplier to increase your investment up to 10 times the spare change
- Disable the feature at any time by following the same steps and selecting 'Remove'
You won’t be charged any exchange fees unless you exceed your plan's fair usage limit. Read more about it in this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies.

## Spare change round ups 
Rounding rules vary depending on the currency:
- Most currencies: transactions are rounded to the nearest whole number
- PLN and RON: rounded to the nearest multiple of 5
- SEK, NOK, DKK, CZK, ZAR: rounded to the nearest multiple of 10
- HUF and JPY: rounded to the nearest multiple of 100
Changes in the crypto asset space are constant, and prices may change dramatically. Be aware you could lose some or all of your investment.

title: What exchange rates does Revolut offer?
content_text:
Revolut offers competitive rates for currency exchange. These variable rates are set based on data from multiple independent sources.

## How to check the exchange rate
You can view the current currency exchange rates on our websitehttps://www.revolut.com/currency-converter/?geo-redirect=true or within the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Scroll down until you see '+ Add widgets'
- Tap '+ Add widgets'
- Scroll down and select 'Watchlist'
- Tap 'Add widget' and you'll see the new widget in your home screen
- You'll be able to add more currencies by tapping 'See all' and 'Add to watchlist'

## Why are the buy and sell prices different?
It's normal that the sell and buy prices are different. The difference between the two rates is called a spread.
Where possible, the rate, any fee, and the total cost will be shown to you in-app before you make an exchange (e.g. for in-app exchanges or sending a transfer).
For card purchases or ATM withdrawals requiring real-time currency exchange, you’ll be able to view the breakdown of the total cost within the app after the transaction.

## My exchange rate is wrong
Exchange rates are determined based on currency exchange market data feeds that we get from a range of different independent sources.
Depending on your plan, you'll have a rolling monthly allowance of exchanges without Revolut fees. Read this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies to learn about usage limits and fees for exchanges made on weekends.
The rate and potential fees can be reviewed before completing an exchange from the app or when making a transfer. Check our Fees Pagehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/fees/?geo-redirect=true for a full breakdown of your fees.

title: Currency exchange fees and limits
content_text:
## Fair usage fees
Depending on your plan, there's a threshold of exchanges that can be made with no additional fees during the weekdays. This limit is called a fair usage limit. To check your usage and reset date, tap your profile icon in the top-left corner and select Your plan or Your limits.
- Standard: 1% fee charged for exchanges over £1,000 or equivalent
- Plus: 0.5% fee charged for exchanges over £3,000 or equivalent
- Premium, Metal, and Ultra: no additional exchange fees
The fair usage limit applies to all fiat currency, cryptocurrency, and commodity exchanges, including exchanges made automatically at the time of a transaction. Your fair usage limit resets each rolling month.

## Fees for exchanges made on weekends
Depending on your plan, you may be charged a fee on all exchanges made on the weekend from 17:00 Friday to 18:00 Sunday, Eastern Time (ET). For Standard customers, this fee is 1%. For Plus, it is 0.5%. Premium, Metal, and Ultra customers don't have to pay extra fees on weekends.

## Currency exchange limits
You can perform up to 200 exchanges within a 24-hour period. The limit includes currency exchange, cryptocurrency exchange, and commodity exchange.

title: How do I exchange money?
content_text:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'More'
- Choose Exchange and select the currencies
- Tap the arrow in the middle to switch between 'Buy' and 'Sell'
- Choose the amount to buy or sell
- Tap 'Review order'
- Review the order, including any fees, before you complete the exchange
It's not possible to cancel currency exchanges, once the exchange has been completed. The only way to revert it is to make a new exchange.
Read this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/how-to-receive-money-from-another-bank/what-account-details-should-i-use-to-transfer-money-to-my-revolut-account/supported-currencies-for-holding to see which currencies you can hold and learn about supported currencies. For details on fees and exchange limits, check out this article/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies.

title: Currency exchange orders
content_text:
To exchange currencies and cryptocurrencies automatically, you can set up limit and stop orders. To do so in your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'More'
- Tap Exchange
- Tap 'Market order' in the top-right corner
- Choose 'Stop order' or 'Limit order'
For fiat currencies, orders won't be executed — regardless of the exchange rate — between 5pm on Friday and 6pm on Sunday (Eastern Time) to avoid mark-up fees. Crypto orders are executed 24/7, 365 days a year.

## Stop order
A stop order helps you buy or sell currency at a lower rate than the current market rate, but only when the exchange rate hits a specific level you set. This order type is often used to limit potential losses if the rate moves unfavourably.
For example, if the current EUR to USD rate is 1.10, and you're worried it might fall, you could set a stop order to sell EUR at 1.09. This means if the rate drops to 1.09, your currency will be sold automatically to prevent further loss.

## Limit order
A limit order is used to buy or sell currency at a better rate than the current market rate. You set a specific rate at which you want the order to execute. This type of order is useful if you believe the exchange rate will move in a favourable direction.
For example, if EUR to USD rate is currently 1.10, and you think it'll improve, you might set a limit order to buy EUR at 1.11. Your order will only be executed if the rate reaches or exceeds 1.11, ensuring you benefit from the better rate.

## Manage your currency exchange limit and stop orders
In your Revolut app:
- Go to Payments on the bottom menu
- Tap the calendar icon in top-right corner
- Scroll down to find 'Orders'
You can't modify orders once they're set up. If you need to change an order, you'll have to cancel it and set up a new one. To cancel an order, select it and tap 'Cancel'. For more information on why your order might fail, refer to this FAQ.

title: I just made an exchange, but I can't find my money
content_text:
If you've recently made an exchange, but can't find your funds, it's likely that your currency account is inactive.
To re-activate the currency account, follow these steps:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap 'Accounts'
- Tap 'Add new'
- Select your destination currency from the list

title: Why might my currency order fail or not execute?
content_text:
## Common order failure reasons
- Low balances: to execute an order, a sufficient account balance is necessary. If your balance is too low, the order will be cancelled. You must add funds and place a new order
- Daily exchange limit: we impose a limit of 200 transactions per 24 hours for security. Exceeding this will result in order failure. You can try again the following day
- Crypto transaction amount limit: a single crypto transaction cannot exceed €15,000. Orders beyond this amount will fail

## Account issues
- Locked account: a locked account prevents order execution. Once unlocked, orders will proceed without needing resubmission
- Deactivated currency account: orders for a currency with an inactive account will fail. Ensure your currency account is active or learn how to open one

## Price slippage
Slippage can affect orders in volatile markets. If the rate deviates significantly during execution, the order will be retried when the target rate is achievable.

title: Open and manage additional currency accounts
content_text:
## Open an additional currency account
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Below your balance, tap Accounts
- Tap 'Add new'
- Choose 'Currency account'
- From the list of available currencies, select the one you want
- Follow the steps to set up your currency account
- Alternatively, tap Account details and add the missing currency from the bottom list
Although you can hold and spend available currencies, it doesn't necessarily mean you can send or receive transfers with them. To see a list of unsupported countries for receiving transfers, go to this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/which-countries-and-currencies-are-supported-to-receive-money/which-countries-are-not-supported-for-inbound-transfers. A list of unsupported countries for sending transfers can be found on this page/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/what-countries-and-currencies-are-supported-for-outbound-transfer.
To find your Revolut account details, check this article/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/how-to-receive-money-from-another-bank/what-account-details-should-i-use-to-transfer-money-to-my-revolut-account/what-account-details-are-available-for-me in our Help Centre. You can only have one currency account for each currency. As a workaround, you can use Pocketshttps://help.revolut.com/help/app-features/vaults/what-are-revolut-vaults/ to manage your finances.

## Remove a currency account
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Below your balance, tap Accounts
- Press the relevant account and swipe left to reveal options
- Tap 'Remove'
- This won't block incoming money, but will block spending from that account
In order to remove your account, you'll be required to clear your balance in that account. Your main currency account cannot be removed.

title: Investor related tax information
content_text:
## Why does Revolut need information about my tax residency?
As a financial institution, we’re required to comply with domestic legislation that mandates the need to collect a self-certification from our customers regarding your residence for tax purposes. This must be kept up to date and may need to be submitted on an annual basis with account-specific information, to the tax authority of the Revolut entity under the Common Reporting Standard (CRS) and Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (FATCA).
If you update your tax residency within the Revolut app, we'll require an updated form W-8BEN and any new tax identification numbers (TINs) within 30 days of the change. Your tax residency forms are important part of our CRS and FATCA reporting obligations and may impact the rate of withholding tax applicable to your dividend and interest income.

## What is a TIN and why does Revolut ask me for this?
The tax identification number (TIN) is the unique identifier assigned to you by the tax administration in your jurisdiction of tax residence. It's a unique combination of letters and/or numbers used to identify you for the purposes of administering the tax laws of that jurisdiction. As a financial institution, we require your TIN to accurately comply with rules under the CRS and FATCA. In the event that you are deemed a tax resident in more than one jurisdiction, we require all your TINs.

## What is form W-8BEN and why has Revolut asked for this?
When opening an investment account with Revolut, we request completion of the US form W-8BEN. This form, issued by the US Internal Revenue Service, certifies that you’re not a US tax resident and enables Revolut to determine the appropriate rate of US withholding tax to apply to payments of US source income in accordance with where you are deemed tax resident. No information on this form is shared outside of Revolut.

## What is FATCA?
The Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (FATCA) requires Revolut to identify any customers that may be US persons and report account information to the local authorities for onward transmission to the US Internal Revenue Service. The purpose of FATCA is to ensure that US persons disclose all of the income that they are receiving in any foreign accounts.

## What is CRS?
Under the Common Reporting Standard (CRS), Revolut is required to identify your tax residency. The objective of the CRS is to identify customers who are tax resident outside of the country where they hold their Revolut account. Under CRS, financial institutions such as Revolut are required to report account information to the country tax authority. Countries have committed to exchange information in order to identify where accounts are being maintained by individuals outside their country of tax residence. This was introduced as a measure to target tax evasion.

title: How does the commission-free allowance work?
content_text:
## Your plan allowance
Depending on your Revolut subscription plan, you’ll be able to submit 1 to 10 commission-free trades per month. The allowance applies to stocks and ETPs trades within your brokerage account. To check how many commission-free trades you have left:
- Tap your profile icon in the top left corner
- Select ‘Your plan’
- Scroll down to ‘Plan your wealth’
Trades refer to orders submitted during the billing cycle, whether or not they're executed. Trades outside of the commission-free allowance will be subject to a commission fee. For more information on the quotas for each plan and more about Revolut’s trading fees, visit this FAQ.https://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/trading-fees/what-fees-will-i-be-charged-for-my-trading/?geo-redirect=true

## Placing an order
The commission-free trade will be deducted from your allowance when you hit 'Submit' at the order confirmation page. Your allowance will also be deducted for future recurring buys when they're next scheduled.
Commission-free trades that are placed in the current billing cycle but get executed in subsequent billing cycles won't consume additional commission-free trades.
For example, a Metal customer has a billing cycle that starts on the first of the calendar month. They placed a Limit Order using a commission-free trade from May 2025's allowance. The Limit Order gets executed in June 2025 and the customer will still have 10 commission-free trades remaining.

## Cancelling an order
If the commission-free trade is cancelled within the same billing cycle, the commission-free trade will be added back to your allowance. 
Commission-free trades cancelled in a subsequent billing cycle will not be added back to your allowance.

title: Trading orders routing
content_text:
## Order routing process
The order routing process works as follows:
- You submit an order
- We receive and review your order
- We transmit it to the third-party broker
- The third-party broker executes the order or transmits it further for final execution
We’re obligated to act in your best interests when transmitting your order to another entity for execution or onward transmission. You can find more information about how we route orders and how we do best in our Best Execution Policy Disclosurehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-best-execution-policy-disclosure/.
We and the third-party broker have the final say on whether or not an order submitted by you is accepted. For further information, consult our Terms of Businesshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-terms-of-business/.

title: What are trading regulatory fees and taxes?
content_text:
## US stocks
Regulators, such as the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA), impose fees on trading US stocks. When applicable, the fees are charged on sell orders and withheld from your sales proceeds.
From 4 April 2026, the SEC levied a fee of $20.60 per $1,000,000 of principal sold, rounded up to the nearest cent. The fee is applied proportionally for lower transactions under $1,000,000, with the fee minimum of $0.01. For example, a $1,000 sell order will have an SEC fee of $0.02.
From 1 January 2026, FINRA adds a Trading Activity Fee (TAF) for sales of stocks at a rate of $0.000195 per share. A minimum charge of $0.01 and a maximum charge of $9.79 apply. The 'SEC' and 'Trading activity' fees can be found in the 'Fee breakdown' before you submit your order in-app.

## European stocks
Certain countries impose a transaction tax (the Financial Transaction Tax), related to buying or selling financial instruments of companies incorporated in that country. This may vary per country and is levied by ‌local authorities.
This 'Financial Transaction Tax' can be found in the 'Fee breakdown' before you submit your order in-app and is applicable for buy orders for French, Spanish, and Italian-based companies only:
- FR: 0.4% of the order amount
- ES: 0.2% of the order amount
- IT: 0.2% of the order amount

## UK stocks
When purchasing UK-listed shares, a 0.5% Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (SDRT) is applied to the transaction value and collected automatically. For transactions over GBP 10,000, a GBP 1.50 Panel on Takeovers and Mergers (PTM) levy is charged to support the UK’s Takeover Panel.

## Other fees
For more information, refer to our Trading Fees FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/trading-fees/what-fees-will-i-be-charged-for-my-trading/?geo-redirect=true/ and our Ex-ante Costs & Charges page.https://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-ex-ante-costs-and-charges-disclosure/

title: Buying or selling commodities
content_text:
You can exchange commodities (precious metals) with fiat or crypto currencies.

## How to buy or sell commodities
- Go to Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading on the bottom menu
- Scroll down and tap 'Commodities' 
- Tap the commodity you wish to buy or sell
- Tap 'Buy' or 'Sell'
- Select the currency and enter the amount 
- Review your order and select 'Confirm order'

## Supported order types
- Market orders to immediately exchange at the rates provided at the time of the transaction
