- Stop orders or limit orders to automatically exchange commodities at a target price in the future. See this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-to-make-currency-exchanges/how-do-i-exchange-money-automatically to learn more about the order types
Once completed, transactions can't be returned or cancelled.
To see information about rates and fees, check this FAQ/help/wealth/precious-metals/getting-started-with-precious-metals/how-much-does-precious-metals-exposure-cost-and-how-is-it-calculated.

title: Commodities prices, rates, and fees
content_text:
## Variable exchange rates
The commodity price depends on whether you're buying or selling. You'll see either the buy rate (ask) or the sell rate (bid) in-app before making the transaction. The difference is called the 'spread' and has two components: the market spread and Revolut's mark-up applied per order.

## Commodities fees
We apply a fee when you buy or sell commodities. This fee may vary depending on your Revolut plan. You can find more information on our Commodities Services Terms of Businesshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/precious-metals/?geo-redirect=true.
- Standard or Plus plan: The fee is the higher of a fixed minimum fee of £1, or a variable fee which is 0.99% of the order value
- Premium, Metal, or Ultra plan: The fee is the higher of a fixed minimum fee of £1, or a variable fee which is 0.49% of the order value
On some plans, your commodities conversions may count towards your exchange fair usage limit. You can learn more in this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies in our Help Centre. We'll show you the variable exchange rate, the fees, and the total cost in the Revolut app before you make any exchange.

## Prices and measurement
The price shown in-app is expressed per troy ounce. This is the market value of one troy ounce, which is the standard unit of measure for commodities and roughly equates to 31 grams of gold.

title: Commodities supported features
content_text:
Commodities (precious metals) can be used with certain Revolut features.

## Supported features
- Cashback
- Spare change round ups
- Stop orders and limit orders
- Send commodities to other Revolut users who have this feature enabled

## Unsupported activities
- Delivery of physical commodities bars. For further information about the characteristics of the commodities offering of Revolut, consult the Terms and Conditionshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/precious-metals/
- Transferring commodities to or from outside Revolut
The services for commodities are provided by Revolut Ltd and are not regulated, nor covered by a compensation scheme. Check this FAQ to learn more about it.

title: Which precious metals are supported?
content_text: Currently, you can invest in gold (XAU), silver (XAG), platinum (XPT), and palladium (XPD).

title: Start with Revolut Ramp
content_text:
## What is Revolut Ramp?
Revolut Ramp allows you to purchase cryptocurrency with your external bank card or Revolut account balance and send it to an external crypto wallet of your choice.

## Supported countries
Revolut Ramp is available to customers in the UK and most of Europe. If you aren't in an eligible location, we'll let you know during the onboarding process on the Revolut Ramp website.
We’re bringing Revolut Ramp to more countries soon, so stay tuned for updates!

## Risks of trading crypto
Before you start trading crypto, you should understand the associated risks and decide whether it's right for you. Crypto assets are volatile and aren’t regulated or protected by investor compensation schemes. Depending on your country, they might also be subject to taxes.

## Open a Revolut Ramp account
You don’t have to join Revolut to use Ramp. You can open a Revolut Ramp account without a Revolut bank account or e-money account.
If you don't have a personal Revolut account, you'll need to complete our identity verification process.
If you’re an existing Revolut customer, you can skip our identity verification process, as we can use the information you’ve already provided to open your Revolut account in-app.

## To close your Ramp account, please contact us:
- Log in to your Revolut Ramp account on the Revolut Ramp websitehttps://ramp.revolut.com
- Open the menu in the top-right corner
- Select 'Contact us'

title: How can I get a full statement of my Ramp transaction?
content_text:
To see your completed trade transactions, you’ll need to review the statement or transaction listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list for the card you used.
If you paid with your Revolut card, you should see Ramp transactions within your transaction list in our app. This can be seen from the home screen for the account you paid for. Click 'See all' under 'Recent Transactions' to see all previous transactions.
Once crypto is successfully delivered, it should be visible within the wallet it was sent to.

title: Pay with Revolut Ramp
content_text:
## How do I use Revolut Ramp?
Revolut Ramp is web-based and not in the Revolut app. You can start using Revolut Ramp by visiting our Revolut Ramp pagehttps://ramp.revolut.com, or by choosing to pay with Revolut from one of our crypto partners' websites, such as Metamaskhttps://portfolio.metamask.io/buy.
To make a Revolut Ramp transaction:
- Take a picture of the identity document requested on the Ramp portal, e.g. your passport or driving licence
- Take a video of yourself to verify the identity document you uploaded
We might ask for more verification information if the document you provided isn't supported, or if there's a problem with its quality. 
Once we complete the verification of your identity, you're all set. All you need to do is go to Revolut Ramp pagehttps://ramp.revolut.com/, select the desired amount, currency, and wallet, and confirm the transaction. You can also select Revolut Ramp as an on-ramp provider within one of our supported wallet partners (e.g. Metamask, Ledger).

## Transaction timeframes
Transactions are usually processed within 30 minutes. However, if the network is busy, they can be pending for up to 24 hours. If your payment takes longer than this, reach out to our Revolut Ramp Supporthttps://ramp.revolut.com/support so we can look into it for you.
Once the payment has been made, it isn't possible to cancel or revert the transaction, as crypto payments are irreversible.

## View Ramp transactions
To see your completed trade transactions, you'll need to review the statement or transaction list for the card you used.
If you paid with your Revolut card, you should see Ramp transactions within your in-app transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list.

title: What are the fees for Revolut Ramp?
content_text:
When you use Revolut Ramp, any applicable fees are displayed at the starting screen.
When paying with Revolut Ramp on one of our partners’ websites, any breakdown of fees should be shown before you choose your payment method. How fees are displayed may differ depending on the vendor, so always be sure to review all details and fees carefully before making a payment.
Rest assured that you’ll always see a summary of fees before the final payment screen.

title: I sent crypto to an incorrect wallet address. What can I do?
content_text:
Unfortunately, crypto payments are irreversible. If you use the wrong details, like the wrong wallet address, before making the purchase, you’ll lose the money from that payment.
You'll always review your details, including the wallet address, before you confirm your order. Please take care when entering details and review them carefully before finalising a transaction.

title: I was charged extra by my bank for a Ramp payment. Why?
content_text:
Banks may charge transaction fees if you’ve paid for crypto in a currency different to the one your card uses by default.
For example, if you buy crypto through Ramp, and pay using a UK card, then we always recommend paying in GBP.
This avoids unexpected conversions and currency fees from your bank.

title: My Ramp payment failed. What should I do?
My Ramp transaction is completed but I didn't receive the funds
content_text:
## Completed transaction
If you have received a confirmation email from us with the blockchain transaction hash, then we've sent your crypto to you.
You can always check the blockchain explorer and make sure enough block confirmations have been completed for your payment to be confirmed.
If this has happened, but you still can't see your crypto in your wallet, try one of these solutions:
- Check that the wallet address you're viewing is the same address you specified for the purchase
- Make sure you check the correct network within your wallet. If you can't see it, make sure to add it
- Make sure the token is imported to your wallet within the correct network
- If none of these steps work, reach out to our Revolut Ramp Supporthttps://ramp.revolut.com/support and we'll look into this for you

## Failed transaction
If the transaction fails after making the payment, you won't be charged. Any pending funds will be returned to your bank account within 5 business days.
In the case of a failed transaction — if you don't receive your money back in 5 business days, reach out to our Revolut Ramp Supporthttps://ramp.revolut.com/support and we'll look into this for you.

title: My Ramp payment was completed but I didn’t receive the crypto. What should I do?
content_text:
Make sure to check the transaction hash sent to you via email or accessible in your transaction history on the Revolut Ramp websitehttp://ramp.revolut.com. With the transaction hash, you can track the transaction on a block explorer (such as Etherscanhttps://etherscan.io/ for transactions on Ethereum).
Sometimes, the interface of your wallet app may not show the transaction history even though the transaction has completed - check whether your balance has increased by the amount of the transaction. 
If you sent the crypto to the incorrect address, unfortunately there is nothing we can do as crypto transfers are irreversible.

title: Revolut Ramp supported currencies and limits
content_text:
## Supported tokens
Token availability on a particular wallet depends on the compatibility of the tokens with said wallet. For buying, the following tokens are available:
- 1INCH, AAVE, APE, BAL, BTC, CHZ, CRV, ETH, GRT, LINK, MATIC, MKR, PERP, QNT, SHIB, SNX, UMA, UNI, USDC, USDT, USDT (TRON), USDT (Polygon), USDC (Polygon), POL (Polygon).
For selling crypto, it's crucial to use cryptocurrencies and networks supported by Revolut for your transactions to avoid losing funds. For instance, Revolut supports the Polygon and Tron networks for USDT. Using an unsupported network may result in lost funds.

## Supported fiat currencies
- AED, AUD, CAD, CHF, CZK, DKK, EUR, GBP, HKD, HUF, ILS, ISK, JPY, MXN, NOK, NZD, PLN, QAR, RON, RSD, SAR, SEK, SGD, THB, TRY, USD, and ZAR

## Ramp order limits for buying
Transaction limits for buying crypto for:
- Revolut customers: GBP20,000 (or currency equivalent) daily or GBP100,000 (or currency equivalent) monthly
- Non-Revolut customers: GBP3,000 (or currency equivalent) daily or GBP15,000 (or currency equivalent) monthly

## Ramp order limits for selling
Transaction limits for selling crypto:
- GBP10,000 (or currency equivalent) daily
- GBP100,000 (or currency equivalent) monthly
Limits apply separately for buying and selling crypto with Revolut Ramp. Lower limits may apply in specific cases.You'll be alerted when you're about to exceed these limits. Similarly, any order minimums that apply will be shown on the Ramp websitehttps://ramp.revolut.com/.

title: Why did I receive less crypto than I paid for?
content_text:
Revolut always sends the exact amount of crypto stated in the order confirmation.
The confirmation screen is updated with the live exchange rate, so you know exactly how much crypto you’re getting at the point of purchase.
You can also see this information after the transaction by going to the 'Menu' and clicking on 'Transactions' on the Revolut Ramp websitehttp://ramp.revolut.com. We’ll also send you a transaction summary with these detail to your email.

title: Why was I charged more than I was shown?
content_text:
Revolut always communicates clearly how much you are being charged, including a breakdown of any applicable fees. This is how much we charge your card.
The payment confirmation screen is updated with the live exchange rate, so you know exactly how much crypto you’re getting and how much it costs. All applicable fees are displayed for your review before you make your purchase.
If you end up being charged more after you make your payment, it’s possible a transaction fee has been applied by your bank or card provider. You’ll need to contact them for more information. We share some more details about these types of fees in this article/help/wealth/revolut-ramp/i-was-charged-extra-by-my-bank-for-a-ramp-payment-why.

title: Advantages of investing in bonds
content_text:
## Capital preservation
Non-complex bullet bonds typically repay principal at the maturity date (if the issuer does not default), unlike equities which depend on the share price fluctuation.
Non-complex callable bonds include a make-whole clause, which requires the issuer to pay the investor a lump sum equal to the net present value of all remaining future interest payments.
Non-complex bonds have higher seniority than equities, as bondholders must be fully repaid before shareholders start receiving any money during a liquidation or bankruptcy process.

## Steady income streams
Many bonds pay a fixed amount of interest (called a coupon) at regular intervals until they mature, which can provide a predictable income stream. However, these payments are not guaranteed.
If the issuer is unable to meet its obligations or defaults, you may not receive some or all of the expected payments. Refer to our Risk Disclosurehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-risk-disclosure/ for more details.

## Diversification and hedging benefits
Bonds have a low correlation with equities. This helps diversify your portfolio. During economic slowdowns, when equities underperform, bonds often outperform due to lower interest rates typically triggered by slow GDP growth.
For more information on the risks associated with financial instrument transactions, consult our Risk Disclosurehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-risk-disclosure/.

title: Get started with Bonds
content_text:
A bond is a fixed-income instrument that represents a loan made by an investor to a borrower. Bonds can be used by governments and corporations for financing instead of a bank loan. The bond issuer receives the needed funding during the bond offering phase.
Generally, investors receive periodic interest (coupon) payments until the bond reaches maturity. At that point, the bond’s principal amount is repaid to the investor.

## Coupon payments
Typically, coupon payments are made annually, but the frequency can vary depending on the bond. The amount you receive depends on how much you've purchased. Coupons can take time to settle but are typically paid within 2 business days of the official coupon date.
To view past payments in the Revolut app:
- Go to Investhttps://get.revolut.com/E528/?af_channel=website_direct&af_dp=revolut%3A%2F%2Fapp%2Ftrading&af_sub1=%7B%22conversion_page_url%22%3A%22https%3A%2F%2Fhelp.revolut.com%2Fen-PL%2Fhelp%2Fwealth%2Fstocks%2Fbonds%2Fbonds-basics%2F%22%2C%22cookie_consent%22%3A%5B%22ads%22%2C%22analytics%22%5D%2C%22landing_page_url%22%3A%22https%3A%2F%2Fhelp.revolut.com%2Fen-PL%2Fhelp%2Fwealth%2Fstocks%2Fbonds%2F%22%2C%22qr_code%22%3Afalse%2C%22website_client_id%22%3A%22bfa80342-251f-4303-bb6f-ed71a0033b8f%22%7D&af_web_dp=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.revolut.com%2Fhub%2Fstocks&deep_link_sub1=DEEPLINK&deep_link_value=revolut%3A%2F%2Fapp%2Ftrading&pid=website on the bottom menu
- Scroll down to ‘Transactions’
- Tap ‘See all’
- Select the coupon transaction to see details

## How to check next coupon date in the Revolut app
- Go to Investhttps://get.revolut.com/E528/?af_channel=website_direct&af_dp=revolut%3A%2F%2Fapp%2Ftrading&af_sub1=%7B%22conversion_page_url%22%3A%22https%3A%2F%2Fhelp.revolut.com%2Fen-PL%2Fhelp%2Fwealth%2Fstocks%2Fbonds%2Fbonds-basics%2F%22%2C%22cookie_consent%22%3A%5B%22ads%22%2C%22analytics%22%5D%2C%22landing_page_url%22%3A%22https%3A%2F%2Fhelp.revolut.com%2Fen-PL%2Fhelp%2Fwealth%2Fstocks%2Fbonds%2F%22%2C%22qr_code%22%3Afalse%2C%22website_client_id%22%3A%22bfa80342-251f-4303-bb6f-ed71a0033b8f%22%7D&af_web_dp=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.revolut.com%2Fhub%2Fstocks&deep_link_sub1=DEEPLINK&deep_link_value=revolut%3A%2F%2Fapp%2Ftrading&pid=website on the bottom menu
- Select the bond
- Scroll to ‘Attributes’ and check ‘Next coupon date’

## Revolut bond offerings
We offer two types of bonds:
- Corporate bonds
- Government bonds
Corporate bonds are issued by private-sector companies, while government bonds include sovereign debt. A sub-sector of government bonds includes agency and quasi-government bonds, which focus on infrastructure, research, and social welfare projects.

title: Bonds fees
content_text:
Commission is charged from the first trade at a rate of 0.25% of the trade value or £0.01 per trade (whichever is higher) for Standard, Plus, Premium, and Metal. Commission is charged at a rate of 0.12% and no minimum fee for Ultra and Trading Pro customers.
No other fee is applicable for bond trades:
- Commission-free trades as per your plan apply to bonds, see our Ex-Ante Disclosurehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-ex-ante-costs-and-charges-disclosure/ for more information
- There are no custody fees for bonds
- There are no repayment fees

## For more information
Currency exchange fees may apply if you purchase a bond in a different currency to the currency of the account you're using to fund the transaction. Refer to our Ex-Ante Costs & Charges Disclosure https://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-ex-ante-costs-and-charges-disclosure/ for more information.
You can sell a bond without waiting for its maturity. The price of the bond can fluctuate, exposing you to potential gains or losses. For more information, refer to this Risk Disclosurehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-risk-disclosure/.

title: Bonds terms and definitions
content_text:
## Bond pricing, yields, and returns
- Clean price: the quoted bond price, excluding accrued interest
- Dirty price: the actual purchase price, including accrued interest
- Accrued interest: unpaid interest earned since the last coupon payment, added to the bond price when traded
- Coupon rate: the fixed annual interest paid, based on the bond’s face value
- Current yield: annual coupon payments as a percentage of the bond’s current market price
- Yield to maturity (YTM): the total return if held until maturity, factoring in price changes and reinvested coupons

## Bond structure and maturity
- Bond face value: the amount repaid at maturity
- Bond maturity: the date when the bond’s life ends, and the face value is repaid

## What are callable bonds and make-whole clauses?
- Callable bonds: Bonds the issuer can repay before maturity. All callable bonds available to you on the Revolut app include a make-whole clause.
- Make-whole clause: Ensures the issuer pays a premium if redeeming early, compensating you for lost interest.
Always check the bond’s terms for details.

## What's the relation between bond prices and bond yields?
Bond prices and bond yields are inversely proportional. This means that when the price of a bond increases, its yield decreases, and vice versa.
For more information, see our Risk Disclosurehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-risk-disclosure/.

title: Bond credit ratings
content_text:
## What are credit ratings?
Bond credit ratings show how likely it is that the issuer will repay its obligations. Independent agencies like Moody's, S&P Global Ratings, and Fitch Ratings assign these ratings to help investors understand risk.
Ratings typically range from very high quality to very high risk.

## Types of ratings
- Highest ratings: ‘AAA’ (S&P/Fitch) or ‘Aaa’ (Moody’s) – considered the safest and with lowest risk of default
- Investment grade: ‘AAA’ to ‘BBB-’ (S&P/Fitch) or ‘Aaa’ to ‘Baa3’ (Moody’s) – generally lower risk
- Below investment grade (high-yield or ‘junk’): anything below ‘BBB-’ / ‘Baa3’ – higher risk of default
- Lowest ratings: ‘D’ (S&P/Fitch) or equivalent – the issuer is already in default

## What ratings does Revolut provide?
At Revolut, we provide Fitch ratings. The definitions for each rating can be found in-app for all rated bonds.

## To access these definitions:
- Select a bond
- Scroll down to 'Fitch ratings'
- Tap 'See all'

title: Buy bonds in the Revolut app
content_text:
## Buying bonds in the Revolut app
Trading hours for bonds are between 11:00 and 20:00, Gulf Standard Time (GST, UTC+4), Monday to Friday, excluding the UAE public holidays. Due to Daylight Saving Time (DST), trading for bonds in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is as follows:
- 06:00 to 15:00 UTC from the last Sunday of October to the last Sunday of March, and 
- 07:00 to 16:00 UTC from the last Sunday of March to the last Sunday of October
DST switches occur overnight from Saturday to Sunday at 01:00 UTC.

## How to buy bonds
In your Revolut app:
- Tap Investhttps://get.revolut.com/E528/?af_channel=website_direct&af_dp=revolut%3A%2F%2Fapp%2Ftrading&af_sub1=%7B%22conversion_page_url%22%3A%22https%3A%2F%2Fhelp.revolut.com%2Fen-PL%2Fhelp%2Fwealth%2Fstocks%2Fbonds%2Fbonds-basics%2F%22%2C%22cookie_consent%22%3A%5B%22ads%22%2C%22analytics%22%5D%2C%22landing_page_url%22%3A%22https%3A%2F%2Fhelp.revolut.com%2Fen-PL%2Fhelp%2Fwealth%2Fstocks%2Fbonds%2F%22%2C%22qr_code%22%3Afalse%2C%22website_client_id%22%3A%22bfa80342-251f-4303-bb6f-ed71a0033b8f%22%7D&af_web_dp=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.revolut.com%2Fhub%2Fstocks&deep_link_sub1=DEEPLINK&deep_link_value=revolut%3A%2F%2Fapp%2Ftrading&pid=website in the bottom menu
- Tap ‘Trade’ or ‘Discover’
- Select a bond
- Tap ‘Buy’ to continue to the order screen
Alternatively, use the search box at the top to discover bonds. You can buy the minimum amount, known as a ‘lot’, containing a fixed number of bond units. The lot price (in USD, EUR, or GBP) is the smallest currency value you can buy. Currently, we only support market orders for bonds.

title: Buying a corporate bond vs buying stock in the same company
content_text:
## What is the difference?
When you're buying a stock of a company, you're buying part of the ownership of that company. This means you rely on potential dividends or the stock price to move upwards to generate a return, while being exposed to the risk of the stock price dropping.
When you buy a corporate bond, you lend money to the company in exchange for periodic interest (coupon) payments until maturity, when the principal is repaid. 
Bonds can offer more stable income but carry risks such as interest rate and credit risk, while equities offer higher growth potential with greater price volatility. For more information on the risks associated with financial instrument transactions, consult our Risk Disclosurehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-risk-disclosure/.

title: Bond name interpretation in the Revolut app
content_text:
## Bond name interpretation 
We always structure our bonds as follows:
Issuer — coupon rate — maturity date

## Example 1: BNP Paribas SA 2.375% 05/24
- Issuer: BNP Paribas SA. Coupon rate: 2.375%. Maturity date: May 2024

## Example 2: United States of America 4.5% 11/25
- Issuer: United States of America. Coupon rate: 4.5%. Date of maturity: November 2025

title: How does Revolut process corporate actions?
content_text:
Corporate actions are events within a public company that affect its issued shares or debt. Here's how Revolut handles them and what you need to know.

## Types of corporate actions
The most common corporate actions are:
- Dividends: Payments made to shareholders from a company's profits
- Stock splits: Increase the number of shares outstanding by splitting existing shares
- Reverse stock splits: Decrease the number of shares outstanding by reducing the number of existing shares
- Mergers and acquisitions (M&A): The combination of two companies into one entity that can result in cash and shares gain for the shareholder
- Spin-offs: Creation of a new independent company through the sale or distribution of new shares

## Important dates for corporate actions
- Declaration date: Announcement by the company's board of directors regarding a significant event, such as dividends
- Record date: Determines which shareholders are eligible to receive dividends or participate in other corporate actions
- Effective date: When the corporate action officially takes effect
- Ex-dividend date: The first day of trading when the buyer of a stock is no longer entitled to the dividend payment
During corporate events or restructurings, such as mergers or spin-offs, you may receive secondary items, like rights or warrants. If these are distributed on a security you hold, we'll liquidate them into cash whenever possible, as Revolut does not currently support these security types.

title: Dividends
content_text:
A dividend is a distribution of a company's earnings to shareholders, determined by its board of directors. To receive dividends, you must hold securities (whole or fractional) before the ex-dividend date. Any stocks bought on or after this date are ineligible.

## Taxes on dividends
Revolut automatically processes dividends into your investment account. Revolut doesn't advise on tax matters. If you have any questions or concerns, you should seek independent professional advice.
- For US stocks: non-US persons may be subject to up to a 30% withholding tax on US dividends, which can be reduced based on their country's tax treaty 
- For EEA stocks and ETFs: withholding tax percentage depends on the origin country of security, and can be up to 35%
- For UK stocks and ETFs: there is generally no withholding tax on dividends

## Types of dividends
- Cash dividends: you can view cash dividend details, including any withholding tax, in your investment transactions under 'Dividend'. We'll notify you when dividends are credited to your account
- Stock dividends: if you receive a dividend, your portfolio holdings will be adjusted accordingly

## How to view dividends
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Under the 'Portfolio' tab, scroll down to 'Transactions' and tap 'See all'
- Tap the dividend transaction to see the details
At Revolut, eligible shareholders typically receive dividends into their investment account 1-2 trading days after the payment date. Currently, our app doesn't offer automatic reinvestment of dividends into the shares of the paying company.

title: Merger or acquisition of a company whose shares I own
content_text:
If any stock in your portfolio is affected by mergers and acquisitions (M&A), we'll handle all necessary updates automatically, with no actions required from you.

## Mergers
In a merger, where two companies combine, your stock position will typically be replaced with shares of the newly formed entity and its corresponding ticker symbol.

## Acquisitions
In an acquisition, where one company purchases another, changes to your portfolio will depend on whether you hold shares of the acquiring company or the acquired company. Possible scenarios are:
- If you own shares of the acquiring company, your holdings will remain unchanged
- If you own shares of the acquired company, those shares may be replaced with cash, shares of the acquiring company, or a combination of both, based on the terms of the acquisition deal
Stock prices can fluctuate during this period, and could affect the market value of your holdings as the market responds to the M&A news.

title: Stock delisting
content_text:
When a company is delisted, its stock no longer trades on one of the major stock exchanges. You still own the same percentage of ‌companies as before.

## OTC trading
Upon delisting, some stocks may begin trading over-the-counter (OTC), although this isn't guaranteed. In some cases, your investment could be affected or lost.
If a stock is delisted and transitions to OTC trading, we aim to notify you about the available OTC instrument to sell either a portion of your shares or your entire position. 
Our third-party brokers aim to execute the trade within 2 trading days, though delays may happen. See this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/introduction-to-stock-trading/otc-stocks to learn more about OTC trading. 

## Unsold positions
If no sell instruction is received, the delisted stock will remain in your portfolio at indicative estimated prices in-app.

## Automatic liquidation
There may be circumstances in which the stock is delisted, and our third-party broker might liquidate your open position on your behalf. In these cases, we'll aim to communicate this to you in advance. Any proceeds from the liquidation of your position will be credited to your investment account.

title: Stock split
content_text:
Stock and reverse stock splits are corporate events that affect the number of shares of your open position. If any security you hold is impacted by a split, it will be automatically adjusted without requiring any action from you.

## Stock splits
A stock split increases the number of shares held for a security, while maintaining the overall market value of your holdings.
For instance, if you own 1 share of company A, trading at $100 per share and the company declares a 2:1 stock split, you'll now hold 2 shares priced at $50 each. Your total market value remains $100.

## Reverse stock splits
A reverse stock split decreases the number of shares held and increases the price per share. For example, if you own 10 shares of company B, trading at $5 per share and the company declares a 1:2 reverse stock split, you'll hold 5 shares priced at $10 each. Your total market value remains at $50.
Companies often use splits to adjust share prices either upward or downward.

title: Trading customer support
content_text:
## Contact us
If you have any questions related to our trading services, you can reach out via the in-app chat.
To start a new chat session:
Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner → select 'Help' → scroll down and tap 'Support' to chat with us

## Types of support we don't provide
- Personal financial advice, investment advice, or recommendations
- Performance guarantees or any guarantee, such as the timing of an investment or withdrawal
- Financial planning advice
- Transaction requests
- Legal and/or tax advice

## Investment and financial advice
The trading service we offer is an execution-only service. It’s restricted to only the execution of trades, without the client receiving any advice about the merits or risks of the investments or their appropriateness and suitability.
Revolut doesn’t provide personal financial advice on what to buy or sell, or offer recommendations of any kind.

title: Which Revolut Trading entity is providing me services?
content_text:
To see which Revolut trading entity is providing you with services:
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Scroll to the bottom of the page 
- Review the disclaimer content

title: Financial ratios for company analysis
content_text:
## What is Earnings Per Share (EPS)?
Earnings Per Share (EPS) indicates how much profit could be distributed to investors. A high or rising EPS is seen as positive, while a low or falling EPS can deter investors as it may signal a downturn.
EPS = (net profit - preferred dividends) ÷ outstanding shares

## What is a price-to-earnings ratio?
The price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio is found by dividing a stock's price per share by its earnings per share. It helps investors determine if a stock is over or undervalued compared to competitors or the sector.
P/E = price per share ÷ earnings per share

## What is a price-to-cashflow ratio?
The price-to-cashflow (P/CF) ratio is calculated by dividing a company's market capitalisation by its operating cash flow. It's an alternative to the price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio, as cash flows are less affected by accounting practices. 
A lower P/CF ratio may suggest an undervalued stock, while a higher ratio could mean it's overvalued.
P/CF = market capitalisation (value of all shares) ÷ operating cash flow

## What is return on equity?
Return on equity (ROE) measures a company's net income relative to its shareholders' equity. It shows how efficiently a company uses equity to generate profit. A higher ROE can indicate efficient management, but may also imply high debt levels. 
ROE = net income ÷ shareholders' equity
When making investment decisions, financial analysis and ratios should not be looked at in isolation.

title: Market analysis
content_text:
## What is technical analysis?
Technical analysis aims to predict future stock price movements by studying historical price and volume data. Analysts look for patterns to identify potential bullish or bearish trends.

## What is fundamental analysis?
Fundamental analysis looks at whether a stock is overvalued or undervalued by examining company data, financial statements, and management decisions. It focuses on the company's ability to generate profit. 

## What are analyst recommendations?
Analysts from investment banks or brokerage firms provide buy or sell recommendations based on extensive research. They may also offer a price target, estimating where the stock price might be in 12 months. 

## How do news and social media impact stocks?
News and social media can significantly influence stock prices. Negative news, like poor earnings reports or political uncertainty, can lead to selling. Social media can reflect market sentiment, but often includes misinformation, which can rapidly influence trends.
When making investment decisions, financial analysis and ratios should not be looked at in isolation.

title: OTC stocks
content_text:
Over-the-counter (OTC) stocks are not listed on the major exchanges, they are instead traded directly between two parties in a decentralised market. For example, if a company stock gets delisted, it may begin trading OTC. To learn more about stock delisting, visit this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/corporate-events/what-happens-to-my-stocks-if-a-company-is-delisted.
Stocks that trade via OTC are commonly smaller companies that don't meet the exchange listing requirementshttps://www.investopedia.com/terms/l/listingrequirements.asp of formal exchanges. OTC stocks can be riskier, more volatile, and illiquid (harder to convert into cash).
The price displayed in-app is indicative only, and market orders may execute at a different price. Trading OTC may be one of the reasons a stock can be restricted to selling only. To learn more about 'sell only' stocks, visit this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/trading-limits/why-is-my-stock-on-sell-only.

title: Stock basics
content_text:
## What is a stock?
A stock is a financial instrument that entitles the holder to a fraction of ownership in a publicly listed company. Owning stock may provide the holder with a portion of the company's earnings, distributed as dividends. Units of stock are called shares.

## What are stock charts?
A stock chart displays a stock's price over time, with typical timeframes being daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annually. Investors may use stock charts to forecast price movements. 
Short-term traders often focus on daily and intraday data, while long-term investors typically analyse weekly and monthly charts to identify trends.

## What is stock ownership?
Stock ownership is determined by the number of shares a person holds relative to the total shares issued by the company. For instance, if a company has 1,000 shares and a person owns 100, they hold 10% of the company's assets and earnings.

## What is a stock broker?
To buy stocks, you need a stockbroker licensed to purchase financial assets directly from stock markets on your behalf.
Revolut Trading Ltd provides customers with the ability to buy and sell stocks on a non-advisory (i.e. execution-only) basis via the Revolut app, and transmits those orders to a third-party broker.

title: Stock markets
content_text:
## What are stock markets?
Stock markets are exchanges where existing owners of shares can trade with potential buyers. They’re known as secondary markets because you buy shares from existing shareholders, not directly from the company.

## Bullish vs bearish markets
A bear market is one in which prices are heading down and a bull market is used to describe a market where prices are rising.
When a market is said to be bullish, people are looking to invest as confidence is high and markets are rising. In bearish markets, stock prices are falling because investors are selling their shares, especially those of higher-risk investments.

title: What is a fractional share?
content_text:
## An overview of fractional shares
A fractional share is a fraction of a whole share. Companies issue whole units of stocks, called shares, which are then traded on the public market for investors to buy. If you buy a fractional share, that means you're buying a piece of a company's stock.
Whereas a single share in a company might cost USD1,000, fractional shares mean you can still invest in a company with USD10 or USD100.

## Limits to buy fractional shares
You'll need to invest at least USD1 for US securities and EUR1 for EU securities. The minimum amount of shares you can buy is 0.00000001.
Currently, you can't submit fractional shares for limit orders.

## Fractional share risks
Fractional shares do not carry voting rights, meaning you cannot participate in shareholder votes for those specific holdings. They also have transferability limitations and may be subject to additional market risks, such as high volatility and liquidity constraints.

title: What is a trading halt?
content_text:
A trading halt is a temporary suspension of trading to affected stocks, or the market as a whole. During a trading halt, investors cannot buy or sell the affected stocks. These usually last less than an hour, but may take longer depending on the nature of the halt.
The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) can also suspend trading for up to 10 days if the situation deems it in the public interest and for the protection of investors.
Trading halts are implemented by the stock exchange where the stock is listed (e.g. NYSE or NASDAQ), or by the SEC. Revolut does not determine or control trading halts.

## Reasons for trading halts
Reasons for trading halts can include:
- impactful news being released on a specific stock
- extreme volatility
- regulatory concern 
- major corporate action

## What happens to my orders during a trading halt?
When a stock has been halted for trading by the exchange, you'll see a banner in your Revolut app for the stock affected. You can't make new orders during this period, and any orders in a pending state will be queued until the halt is over, and the stock starts trading once again.
When the halt is over and the stock re-starts trading, your orders will be executed. Prices during this time may change, and your expected price could be different from the time you placed your order.

title: How do I update my trading account details?
content_text:
You can update a few details (such as your address) in-app, following these steps:
- Open your Revolut app and go to the 'Home' tab
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Choose 'Account' and select 'Personal details'
These details will be saved for your trading account. If your tax identification number, nationality, occupation or name have changed, let our support team know, and they will help update the information.

title: Why are you asking me for documents to open or continue using Revolut trading?
content_text:
Occasionally, we may need you to provide additional documentation or re-verify a few documents and information. We try to keep these requests to a minimum, but we need all the necessary information, for regulatory reasons. If we do need more documents from you, we'll make the experience as straightforward for you as possible, in-app.
If you don't give us the necessary information we need, we may have to restrict your trading account until we receive this information, or we'll have to close it permanently, if we don't get these documents on time.

title: Understanding our onboarding question about affiliations
content_text:
## Why do I need to declare my affiliations?
When you sign up for an investment account with Revolut, we ask if you or someone close to you is employed by or affiliated with an investment firm or publicly traded company. We need this information for regulatory compliance reasons. 
If you or someone close to you is employed by or affiliated with an investment firm, there may be specific rules you’ll need to follow, for example, personal account dealing rules. 
By identifying this early, we can guide you through any additional steps required for you to start or continue using our services. Similarly, if you or someone close to you is employed by or affiliated with a publicly traded company, you may be subject to certain requirements when investing. 

## What is an investment firm? 
An investment firm is a company that's received official approval or authorisation from a regulatory body to provide investment services, for example, investment management, financial advisory, and brokerage services, such as securities trading, etc.

## What is a publicly traded company? 
A publicly traded company is a company whose shares are available to be bought and sold by the public on a stock exchange or other trading platforms.

## What does 'someone close to you' mean?
'Someone close to you' typically refers to a person with whom you have a close personal relationship, such as an immediate family member, like a spouse, child, or partner, or a close friend.
If you or someone close to you is employed by or affiliated with an investment firm or publicly traded company, it doesn't mean you can't use our investment services. 
However, we may need to request further information from you or your employer to make sure we’re complying with the relevant regulations.

title: Close my investment account
content_text:
## To close your Investment account
- Go to 'Invest' on the bottom menu
- Select 'More' on the right side of the screen
- Tap 'Settings'
- Select ‘Close your brokerage account’
- Carefully review and agree to the 'Trading account closure' term

## Investment account closure process
- To close your account, follow the instructions above
- Your assets will be sold once you agree to the 'Trading Account Closure' terms
- Sale proceeds will be transferred to your designated account based on the instrument's trading currency 
- Your account will be closed once the balance reaches zero
You'll get statements for your closed account in an offboarding email, and can also request them via chat. If you've closed your investment account, and if you're still eligible, you can open a new account.
As selling assets in your investment account may trigger capital gains or losses, you should carefully consider the potential tax consequences of closing your investment account. Revolut doesn't advise on tax-related matters. If you need advice, we recommend contacting a financial advisor.

title: Reasons an investment account may be closed
content_text:
We may need to suspend or close your investment account for different reasons

## Possible reasons for investment account closure/suspension
Breach of our Terms of Business: for example, if we become aware that you've breached our Terms of Businesshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-terms-of-business-omnibus, we may suspend or terminate your investment account on a temporary or permanent basis
Insufficient information: if you don't provide the information we need to comply with our regulatory reporting requirements (normally requested via the Revolut app and email), we may temporarily restrict, or even close, your investment account
Unsupported country: if the country where you're living is in a jurisdiction that our trading entity is unable to provide trading services to, we'll have to close your investment account. To find out which of our entities is providing you with services, have a look at this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/how-do-i-know-which-revolut-trading-entity-is-providing-me-services

## Account closure process
If we have to close your investment account, you'll have a 60-day notice period to allow you to close any open positions prior to the closure of your account. During this 60-day notice period, you won’t be able to submit any new buy orders
If you haven't closed all your open positions within this period, we'll liquidate your investment account on your behalf at the next available opportunity
Once the liquidation orders have settled, any proceeds from the sale or liquidation of your instruments will be credited to your Revolut account. Check this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/setting-up-your-trading-account/adding-money-to-your-trading-account/why-cant-i-withdraw-my-funds-from-my-trading-account to learn more about the settlement process and its regular timeframes. If you’d like to close your investment account, read this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/managing-your-trading-account/closing-your-trading-account/how-do-i-close-my-trading-account

title: How is the portfolio value calculated?
content_text: The total portfolio value shown in the app is indicative only and is based on the 'last' price we have for each of the securities you own plus any cash you have in your account. You will also be provided with monthly statements showing the valuation of your investments for the period.

title: Overview of Revolut Trading Ltd's omnibus account
content_text:
The omnibus account model is a market standard for trading services, where each of our customer's accounts are consolidated and managed collectively under a single account structure within Revolut Trading Ltd. This allows us to enhance our services and product offering.

## Contractual relationships and account structure
- Contractual relationships: in the omnibus account model, you only have a contractual relationship with Revolut Trading Ltd, who manages the execution and assets safekeeping
- Account structure: Revolut Trading Ltd holds a collective (omnibus) brokerage account with each of their third-party brokers, on behalf of all our customers

## Investment protection
In the omnibus account model, Revolut Trading Ltd is responsible for the safekeeping of your instruments. Therefore, your investments may qualify for the UK Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) protection. To find out more, read this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/stocks/more-help-with-stocks/investment-protection/how-are-my-investments-with-revolut-protected/.

## Cash protection
In the omnibus account model, Revolut Trading Ltd is responsible for safeguarding your money (uninvested funds) and ensuring it is kept separate from our own money. To ensure this, your funds are held in a client money account with a regulated bank.

title: Trading watchlist and price alerts
content_text:
## Watchlist
Watchlist is an in-app widget you can use to monitor the price of your favourite assets. 
To add a watchlist for stocks in your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Scroll down to 'Watchlist'
- Tap 'Add new'
You can also add a security to your watchlist by visiting the main details page of any asset and tapping the star icon in the top-right corner.

## Price alerts
You can set up a price alert for any of the available stocks in 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu.
To set a price alert:
- Tap a stock to view its details
- Tap the bell icon in the top-right corner
- Add the price and tap 'Create an alert'
To delete an alert:
- Tap a stock to view its details
- Scroll down and tap the alert
- Tap 'Delete'
To manage all alerts:
- Tap any stock to view its details
- Scroll down to 'Alerts'
- Tap 'See all'

## Enabling in-app notifications
In your Revolut app:
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Scroll down to 'Notifications settings'
- Enable the 'Trading volatility alerts' toggle

title: Trading account information, statements and reports
content_text:
The statements for your investment account are provided by Revolut Trading Ltd.

## Download statements and reports
To access documents and statements for your investment account, follow the steps below:
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'More'
- Tap 'Documents'
- Tap 'Stocks'
- Select what type of the statement you're looking for
Below are the statements types available for you.

## Account statement
Our account statement provides you with a detailed summary of all financial transactions in your account over any specified time period of your choosing. 
The statement includes:
- List of transactions: cash top-ups, withdrawals, buy and sell orders, dividends, and more
- Balance information: starting and ending balances, so you can see how your account value has changed
- Portfolio snapshot: a breakdown of your portfolio at the end of the reporting period, giving insight into your asset allocation and holdings

## Profit and loss statement
A profit and loss (P&L) statement provides a high-level summary of the income generated and any gains or losses within a specified period. 
It includes:
- Sales summary: profits or losses from assets sold during the period
- Other income: additional income sources, such as dividends received from investments
This statement offers a clear view of your overall earnings, helping you understand both gains and losses from sales and other income over your selected timeframe.

## Costs and charges report
This document provides details of all the costs and charges associated with your investment account. It's generated on an annual basis only.
Particularly, cost and charges reports are produced at the beginning of each year for the preceding year.
We don't issue hard-copy documents or provide stamps on requested documents. All statements and confirmation documents are electronically generated.

title: Trade confirmation statements
content_text:
Confirmations are available at the end of the day and will contain all trades for the day. You can get them from the app as a PDF.

## Download trade confirmation statements
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu 
- Scroll down to see your transactions and tap 'See all'
- Find your relevant transaction and tap on it
- Download trade confirmation

title: Fund my investment account
content_text:
## How to add funds
In your Revolut mobile app: 
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu 
- Choose 'Add money'
- Select the currency and the amount of the deposit

## Currency exchange fees
If you fund your investment account in currencies other than the ones in which the instrument is traded, we’ll apply the Revolut currency exchange rate and fees. For more information on currency exchanges, read this FAQ/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies.
If any fees were charged on your exchange, you can see them by tapping the transaction in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. All fees are visible in-app before you confirm a transaction.
For more details on costs and charges associated with our services, check out this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/trading-fees/what-fees-will-i-be-charged-for-my-trading.

title: Using your credit card or a personal loan to trade stocks
content_text:
Using credit cards or personal loans to invest in the stock market can be tempting, but it comes with real risks. There is no guarantee that you will make a profit or get your initial investment back. It's important to only invest what you can afford to lose.
Investing your disposable income over time may be a better option to achieve long-term goals. Making more money to pay back a loan is not always easy, and investing in stocks doesn't guarantee a return equal to or greater than the interest rate on the loan.

title: How to withdraw funds from my investment account
content_text:
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Withdraw'
- Enter the amount
When trading, the proceeds from the sale will be settled in the currency of the asset traded (e.g. EUR, GBP, or USD). If you withdraw the sale proceeds, these will be deposited into your EUR, GBP, or USD current account. To view your various account balances, go to 'Accounts'. 

## When can I withdraw funds from my investment account? 
When you sell a security, the funds from the sale will be added to your USD, EUR, or GBP balance in your investment account. You can either use them to buy another security or withdraw to your USD, EUR, or GBP balance in your main Revolut account. 
You'll be able to immediately reinvest the cash proceeds into another security. However, you won't be able to withdraw funds until they've been settled. This normally takes 1 trading day for US securities, and 2 trading days for EU or GB securities. 
If 1 trading day (for US securities) or 2 trading days (for EU or GB securities) have passed, but the funds are still not available for withdrawal, make sure to check if there were any market holidays.

title: Open an investment account
content_text:
To open an investment account, you'll need to first open a Revolut account, verify your identity, and meet eligibility requirements. 
Investment accounts are available on all Revolut plans. You can open a Revolut investment account, even if you have an account with another provider. You can only have 1 investment account with Revolut.

## Eligibility requirements:
- You are at least 18 years old
- You have a valid government-issued document for identity verification
- You have the latest version of the Revolut app
You can submit an application in the 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' section by clicking on 'Get started'. We'll contact you within 2 business days to let you know if your investment account is ready to use or if we need some more information from you.

## Required information
When providing your tax identification number during the sign-up process, make sure it corresponds to your country of residence as listed in-app. For more information on tax identification numbers, read this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/update-personal-details/update-my-tax-information.
You can find more details about the required information in this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/what-information-do-i-need-to-provide-to-onboard-to-trading.

title: Can I transfer my account from another broker?
content_text:
You can transfer your investments held in other brokers into your Revolut investment account. 
This can be done in two ways.

## DRS transfers
A Direct Registration System (DRS) transfer involves moving your shares between a Brokerage account and a transfer agent. The transfer will result in shares held electronically registered in the customer's own name. DRS transfers are usually completed within 10 working days, but can take up to 30 days. During this time, the position will be frozen, and you'll be unable to buy or sell this security, regardless of market conditions and price swings.
Currently, we can only support a DRS transfer to Computershare. Upon completion of the transfer, your security/securities will be directly registered in your name on the issuer's books. Further buys or sells on your securities may be executed at the prevailing market price or time. These orders are usually bulked and executed at established times during the week and carry some additional conditions.
Costs and charges per position apply. We’ll show you these in-app before you submit the transfer request.

## ACAT transfers
Automated customer account transfer service (ACAT) is a system used to transfer the entire portfolio of assets (stocks, bonds, mutual funds, etc.) from one Brokerage account to another. It's managed by the National Securities Clearing Corporation (NSCC) and automates the transfer process to reduce errors and speed up transactions.
You’ll need to contact your current broker to arrange the transfer. The request will be sent to our custodian (DriveWealth) and processed once received. We receive notification after the transfer is complete, so we recommend checking back for updates on your portfolio once the event is processed.

title: Why does Revolut need my national identifier?
content_text:
As a UK regulated entity, we are obliged to comply with all relevant regulatory reporting relating to the trading of listed securities. Each user who places a trade with a financial institution such as Revolut Trading must provide their identifier for reporting purposes. The identifier required is based on the country of your nationality (which may not be the same as the country you currently reside in).

## What national identifier do I need to provide?
The national ID that you need to provide corresponds to your nationality. Where several forms of national ID are possible, they are assigned a priority ranking. You should always aim to provide the highest priority national ID possible. 
The full list of applicable national IDs based on each country can be found below:
- Austria (AT): no national ID is required
- Belgium (BE): Belgian national number (Numéro de registre national – Rijksregisternummer)
- Bulgaria (BG): Bulgarian personal number
- Croatia (HR): personal identification number (OIB – Osobni identifikacijski broj)
- Cyprus (CY): national passport number
- Czech Republic (CZ): national identification number (Rodné číslo) 
- Denmark (DK): personal identity code 10 digits alphanumeric: DDMMYYXXXX
- Estonia (EE): Estonian personal identification code (Isikukood)
- Finland (FI): personal identity code
- France (FR): no national ID required
- Germany (DE): no national ID required
- Greece (GR): 10 DSS digit investor share code. If you do not possess a DSS Code, skip, and regulation allows Revolut to provide a unique identifier on your behalf
- Hungary (HU): no national ID required
- Iceland (IS): personal identification code
- Ireland (IE): no national ID required
- Italy (IT): fiscal code (Codice fiscale)
- Latvia (LV): personal code (Personas kods)
- Liechtenstein (LI): national passport number. If you do not have a national passport number, use the following: national identity card number
- Lithuania (LT): personal code (Asmens kodas)
- Luxembourg (LU): no national ID required
- Malta (MT): national identification number 
- Netherlands (NL): national passport number. If you do not have a national passport number, use the following: national identity card number
- Norway (NO): 11 digit personal ID (Foedselsnummer)
- Poland (PL): national identification number (PESEL)
- Portugal (PT): tax number (Número de Identificação Fiscal) 
- Romania (RO): national identification number (Cod Numeric Personal)
- Slovakia (SK): personal number (Rodné číslo) 
- Slovenia (SI): personal identification number (EMŠO: Enotna Matična Številka Občana)
- Spain (ES): tax identification number (Código de identificación fiscal)
- Sweden (SE): personal identity number
- United Kingdom (GB): UK National Insurance number
- All other countries: national passport number

## What National ID should I provide as a national of multiple countries?
The regulation defines which country’s National ID takes priority, and the following logic applies:
- As a person who has multiple EEA nationalities, you must provide the National ID of the country which comes first alphabetically (according to the countries two-letter code)
- As a person who has an EEA nationality, as well as a non-EEA nationality, you must provide the National ID of the EEA country
- As a person who has multiple non-EEA nationalities, you must provide the National ID of the first country in the list when sorted alphabetically (according to the countries two-letter code)
We will use the nationalities that you declared during Revolut trading onboarding to determine which national ID you should provide. If you believe that we are requesting the incorrect national ID, or your nationalities have changed, contact us via customer support and our agents will be able to assist you. 

## How do I provide this information?
- Update your app to the latest version
- Head to the 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' screen on your app
- Follow the instructions within the banner notification to submit your information
If you don’t see a banner notification in the app, then we don’t need you to provide any additional information at this time. 

## What if I do not provide the national identifier?
If you do not provide this information by the time set on the email we have sent you, your account will be restricted. This will involve your account being placed into 'sell only' mode and, you will not be able to open or increase positions. To remove the restriction, you will need to provide the information.

## What if I don’t have the national identifier you are requesting?
Contact us via in-app chat and our agents will be able to assist you.

title: What information do I need to provide to onboard to trading?
content_text:
During the sign-up process for trading, you'll need to answer multiple questions and provide some information for regulatory purposes.
They include (but are not limited to):
- Confirming you aren't a US citizen: We can't open investment accounts for US citizens outside of the USA as per regulatory requirements
- National identifier details: The national identifier, or national identity number (NIN), is related to your first nationality, as a national of a given country. For more information about the national identifier or national identity number (NIN), go to this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/setting-up-your-trading-account/why-does-revolut-need-my-national-identifier
- Tax identification number: Your TIN has to match the country of your residency (where your Revolut account is registered) 
If you don't have a TIN, for example, if you're a student, you won't be able to open an investment account with us. 
If your country of residence is different from your country of nationality, we'll request a transaction reporting number, which is usually a tax ID number.
To learn more about updating your Tax information, check out this FAQ in our Help Centre.

## Common reasons investment account applications may be declined
Your application may be rejected or delayed in the following circumstances:
- You provided incorrect TIN(s)
- The TIN(s) provided in your main Revolut account should match the one you enter during the sign-up process. To learn more, read this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/update-personal-details/update-my-tax-information in our Help Centre
- If you've previously had an investment account with Revolut, we need to undertake some additional checks before we can approve the sign-up
If your investment account verification is pending for longer than 2 business days, you can contact us via the in-app chat.

title: What is an execution-only service?
content_text: Execution-only service is a trading service that is restricted to only the execution of trades, without the client receiving any advice about the merits or risks of the investments or their appropriateness and suitability.

title: What is a Special Purpose Acquisition Company (SPAC)?
content_text:
Special Purpose Acquisition Companies (also referred to as “blank cheque” or “shell” companies) are companies with no commercial operations, that are formed strictly to raise capital for the purpose of acquiring another company or listing on stock exchanges to attract potential investors for their ventures.
A company that’s already in operation can then merge with, or be acquired by, the SPAC and become a listed company itself, instead of executing its own traditional Initial Public Offering (IPO).
When a company plans to go public through a traditional IPO, the process can take a year or more to complete. By using a SPAC, it may be a lot quicker.
SPACs are created for the purpose of effecting a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, re-organisation or similar business combination with one or more businesses.

## How does the SPAC’s acquisition process work?
From the establishment of a SPAC, the company usually has 24 months to complete the entire acquisition process.
In this process, many steps are performed, but the main ones are:
- Searching for a target company to acquire
- Negotiating a takeover agreement
- Holding investor meetings and seeking approval for the acquisition from its shareholders
- Execution of the merger
- If the SPAC does not complete a merger within this limited time frame, the SPAC liquidates and investments are returned to its public shareholders.

## What happens to the SPAC after the merger announcement?
This last step of creating the listed successor company is referred to as a “de-SPAC” transaction.
After the SPAC completes a merger with a target company, the previously privately held company will become publicly listed. The stock ticker for the SPAC is changed to reflect the name of the acquired company and with this it will start trading on the selected exchange.

## What are the main risks when investing in a SPAC?
A SPAC is a high-risk investment. SPACs have recently been very popular with investors around the world, however this investment carries risks due to several factors:

## No Merger
The SPAC may fail to identify a target company within the required time period (typically 24 months) and have to return funds to its investors, causing no return to be achieved over the period that the capital was invested.

## Targets
Increasing competition in this space can drive SPACs to targeting companies at ever earlier development stages and so influence and drive-up investment risks, especially if they try to invest in ‘hot’ or newer sectors such as emerging technologies (e.g.: space exploration). Early-stage companies may have a short financial history and still be developing their internal controls. They may also be in early negotiations with their sponsors (founders) and be heavily based on forward-looking projections. Targets will need to be able to withstand the rigour and requirements that will apply once they become a public company.

## Conflicts of Interests
Sponsors and directors of SPACs may have personal interests that compete with the best interests of public shareholders.
Investors must place a great deal of trust in the SPAC’s management team to find the best target company for a merger since some parties may be financially incentivised to ensure that the SPAC completes a merger within the limited time available.

## IPO versus SPAC merger
In contrast to an IPO, in the SPAC’s merger process there is no underwriter so the comprehensive due diligence which is usually associated with a company going public may not be carried out.
SPAC sponsors have a strictly limited time to close a deal, which could influence the effectiveness of their acquisition research, especially when it is not guaranteed that the sponsor or management team has continued involvement in the target company post-merger.
Because of this time constraint the SPAC founders may focus on their ability to close a transaction rather than the suitability of the target company going public.

title: American depositary receipts (ADRs) and pass-through fees
content_text:
American depositary receipts (ADRs) are a negotiable certificate issued by a US depositary bank representing a specified number of shares‌ — ‌usually one share‌ — ‌of a foreign company's stock. ADRs trade on US stock markets as any domestic shares would.
ADRs offer investors a way to purchase stock in overseas companies that wouldn't otherwise be available, as well as giving non-US companies easier access to the US stock markets.
ADRs are normally subject to periodic service pass-through fees to cover custodial services costs. The Depository Trust Company (DTC) collects these fees from its participant brokers, including our third-party broker, who charges these fees to you.
ADR pass-through fees generally range from $0.01 to $0.5 per share. ADR fees are normally charged once per year, but this depends on the security. The ADR fee may be deducted from any dividend paid or as a separate fee showing on your account statement and annual costs and charges disclosure.

title: Products available to trade
content_text:
## Stocks and American Depositary Receipts (ADRs)
We currently have 4,000+ stocks available to trade and we're adding more on a continuous basis. At the moment, we offer stocks listed on the US, EEA, and UK markets.
American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) are a certificate issued by a US depositary bank representing a specified number of shares‌ — ‌usually one share‌ — ‌of a foreign company's stock. ADRs trade on US stock markets as any domestic shares would.
ADRs offer investors a way to purchase stock in overseas companies that wouldn't otherwise be available, as well as giving non-US companies easier access to the US stock markets. Read this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/what-s-available-to-trade/what-is-an-adr to learn more. 

## Exchange Trading Products (ETPs)
An exchange-traded product (ETP) is a type of financial instrument that is traded on a stock exchange. ETPs are designed to track the performance of an underlying asset, such as indices, commodities, or other financial instruments, without requiring you to hold the asset directly.
ETPs come in different types and levels of complexity, depending on how they track the underlying asset. Read this FAQ/help/wealth/available-securities-and-instruments/etps/question-what-is-an-etp to learn more about ETPs.

## Special Purpose Acquisition Company (SPAC)
Special Purpose Acquisition Companies (SPAC) are companies with no commercial operations, that are formed strictly to raise capital for the purpose of acquiring another company or listing on stock exchanges to attract potential investors for their ventures.
SPACs are created for the purpose of effecting a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, re-organisation or similar business combination with one or more businesses. Read this FAQ /help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/what-s-available-to-trade/what-is-a-special-purpose-acquisition-company-spacto learn more. 

## Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT)
A REIT is a company that owns, operates, or finances income-generating real estate across various sectors like residential, commercial, and industrial properties. REITs allow investors to earn passive income through dividends without directly owning or managing properties.
You can check out our available investment products in the 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' section of the app, even without creating an investment account.

title: Can I have a trading account if I’m a US Person?
content_text:
Only Revolut Securities Inc., our Revolut Trading Entity in the US, can offer trading services to US persons residing in the US.
For regulatory reasons, Revolut Trading Entities outside of the US cannot provide their services to US persons (i.e. a citizen or resident of the United States). For more information, please visit this pagehttps://www.irs.gov/individuals/international-taxpayers/classification-of-taxpayers-for-us-tax-purposes.

title: Who is Revolut Trading Ltd authorised by?
content_text: Revolut Trading Ltd is an investment firm (No. 11567840), which is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FRN: 933846).

title: What are non-tradable securities?
content_text:
We use the term 'non-tradable securities' when our third-party brokers aren't able to execute sell orders for certain illiquid securities in your investment account.
This happens when these securities don't have an active secondary market, or it's difficult or impossible to trade them over the counter for a prolonged period of time. Non-tradable securities are typically expected to be, and remain, near or entirely worthless.

## What if I have open positions in non-tradable securities?
If you have open positions in non-tradable securities at the time your investment account is closed, you agree — as part of our Terms of Businesshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-terms-of-business-omnibus/ — to have these securities removed from your account. 
This allows for the closure of your investment account. However, this also means that any proceeds arising from a potential future sale or liquidation won't be credited to you, but will be donated to charity.

title: Do I own my stocks?
content_text:
You own your stocks. If you open a stock position (in full shares or as a fraction of a share), our third-party broker will hold the stock on your behalf. Nonetheless, you are the beneficial owner of the stock position.
If you want to find out more about the protections applicable to your investment account, please refer to this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/more-help-with-stocks/investment-protection/how-are-my-investments-with-revolut-protected.

title: How are my investments with Revolut protected?
content_text:
We have put in place arrangements required under the FCA's Client Assets (CASS) rules to ensure that, in the unlikely event we go out of business, your money and instruments are protected and can be returned to you.
If, despite these arrangements, we go out of business owing you money, you may be able to claim compensation from the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS).
Refer to our Client Assets Protection Summaryhttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-client-assets-protection-summary for a comprehensive summary of your investment account's protection and applicable compensation arrangements.

## How is my money protected by the FSCS?
The FSCS can pay up to £85,000 per eligible person. The FSCS may be able to protect you if we go out of business and there is a shortfall in the money or assets that we or our sub-custodians are holding for you, but the FSCS cannot accept any claims that are for poor investment performance. 
Unfortunately, the nature of investments means their value can go down as well as up.
There is more information on the FSCS's websitehttps://www.fscs.org.uk about the kind of claims they can help with. The final decision on whether or not they will consider any claim is for the FSCS.

title: How are the funds in my investment account protected?
content_text:
The uninvested money you hold in your stock trading account, including any profits or other revenue generated from your trading activity, is safeguarded by Revolut Ltd.
Revolut Ltd is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FRN: 900562) as an Electronic Money Institution under the Electronic Money Regulations 2011.
Therefore, while safeguarding helps to protect you if Revolut Ltd becomes insolvent, your money is not protected by the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS). You can find more information about the practical difference between safeguarding and FSCS protection on this pagehttps://www.fscs.org.uk/.

title: What does capital at risk mean?
content_text:
As you might have noticed, there are multiple references to 'Capital at risk' within the 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' tab of the Revolut app, as well as within the communications sent by Revolut Trading Ltd (both via email and the app).
This is for a very specific reason: we want to ensure we notify you when a product or service places a risk on your capital, so that you can make an informed decision. Trading financial instruments involves capital risk.
The value of your investment may fall as well as go up, and you may get back less than your initial investment. In some cases, you may lose your entire initial investment. Therefore, when trading instruments we make available to you in-app, you must have the ability to bear losses.

title: Buy order block
content_text:
The buy order block allows you to self-exclude yourself from buying investment products. The buy order block is disabled by default, but once enabled, you won't be able to place new buy orders in your Revolut investment account until you deactivate the buy order block.

## Activate the buy order block
In your Revolut app:
- Go to Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading on the bottom menu
- Tap 'More'
- Go to 'Settings'
- Enable the 'Buy block' toggle
Once enabled, you won't be able to place new buy orders in your Revolut investment account until you disable the buy order block (by using the same toggle). Disabling the buy order block will take 48 hours.

title: Pending trading orders
content_text:
After you submit a trade, it goes into a pending status. It will remain pending until it's executed or cancelled. How long it remains pending will depend on the type of order, time placed, and specific conditions you may have provided at the time of entry.
The broker may ultimately reject a pending order. Execution is never guaranteed.

## When can an order be pending:
- A market order (or any other type of order) is submitted outside of market open hours. If placed outside market hours, this order type will remain pending until the market opens
- A stop or limit order has been submitted, but the target price hasn't been reached
Check this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/order-types/what-type-of-orders-can-i-submit to learn more about these types of orders.

## View pending orders on any stock
In your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Scroll down to 'Orders'
- Tap on an order to see its details

## Cancel a pending order
- Tap the order you'd like to cancel
- Select the 'Cancel' button
You can't cancel or reverse an order that's been executed.

title: Close a stock position
content_text:
If you want to close your entire stock position in a particular stock, you must submit a quantity-based order rather than a price-based order.
For example, if you have 0.482 units of XYZ (worth $79 in current market value) in your investment account, you can only choose to sell 0.482 units to sell all of it.

## Close the position
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Under 'Portfolio', tap the stock you want to close
- Tap 'Sell'
- Submit a quantity-based order on the XYZ cell — you can either manually enter 0.482 or select 'Sell all XYZ' to automatically fill in the maximum quantity you can sell
- Tap 'Sell XYZ' to submit your order
If you use the USD cell to enter the current market value of $79, this will submit a price-based order. Due to fluctuating prices, price-based orders won't guarantee that your entire stock holding will be sold.
Withdrawing money to your current Revolut account is only possible once the trade has settled.
