To learn more about the withdrawal process, check out this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/setting-up-your-trading-account/adding-money-to-your-trading-account/why-cant-i-withdraw-my-funds-from-my-trading-account in our Help Centre.

title: How to place a stock order
content_text:
There are several types of orders: market orders, stop orders, and limit orders. To learn more about each type, visit this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/order-types/what-type-of-orders-can-i-submit in our Help Centre. To place an order in the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'Trade'
- Select the stock you want to buy and tap 'Buy'
- Specify the number of shares or the exact cash amount you are willing to trade 
- Tap 'Review'
- Review the transaction details and submit the order
To change the order type, you can tap the button at the bottom left next to 'Review'.

## More about placing a trading order
You can buy or sell stocks you already own by tapping the stock in your stock list and selecting 'Buy' or 'Sell'. You'll also be able to select the type of order you're placing in the bottom-left corner. 
If you’re specifying a stop or limit order, you'll be asked to set up the specified price to submit the order. The order will only be executed once the price is reached. The prices in-app are only indicative. If the order wasn’t triggered, it means the price wasn't reached. 
After you submit an order, it may be in a pending status. You can refer to this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/order-execution/can-i-cancel-my-order to learn about pending orders. 

## Settlement
Once a trade has been executed, it still needs to settle. Learn more about settlement and withdrawing funds from your investment account in this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/setting-up-your-trading-account/adding-money-to-your-trading-account/why-cant-i-withdraw-my-funds-from-my-trading-account.

## How to view your trading orders
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Scroll down to ‘Orders’ to see pending orders and recurring buy orders 
- Scroll down to ‘Transactions’ to see already executed orders and other transactions, such as top-ups

title: Can I use leverage to trade?
content_text:
At the moment, we don’t offer the possibility to trade with leverage.
Leveraged trading, also known as margin trading or trading on margin, allows you to buy stocks with a broker with a small amount of capital in order to take a larger position in the market - i.e., through borrowed funds.
For example, if you leveraged 10 to 1, $100 can expose you to the market with $1,000. Leverage trading is considered high risk as it amplifies both gains as well as losses.

title: What is a GTC Order and how does it work?
content_text:
Good Till Cancelled (GTC) Order is a type of order that you can place to buy or sell shares that remain active until the order is executed or it's cancelled by you.
You can create a GTC Order while submitting Limit or Stop Orders. To submit a GTC order, you will need to follow the steps below:
- On the order screen, tap the “Expiry” field that is situated right below Stop/Limit price.
- Edit the “Expiry” field by setting the expiration date. You can set any value from the day you submit the order up until 6 months.

title: Trading limit orders
content_text:
A limit order is an instruction to buy or sell an instrument at a specified price or better. Buy limit orders execute at the specified price or lower, while sell limit orders execute at the specified price or higher. 
It isn't guaranteed and depends on the instrument price reaching your set limit. Trading fractional shares on limit orders isn't supported at the moment.

## How buy limit orders work
When you place a buy limit order, you specify the maximum price per share you're willing to pay. 
For instance, if stock X is trading at USD 5 per share, and you set a buy limit order at USD 4, your order will execute (partially or fully) if the price drops to USD 4 or below. If the price doesn't drop to USD 4, your order won’t execute.

## How sell limit orders work
With a sell limit order, you set the minimum price per share at which you're willing to sell. 
For example, if stock X is trading at USD 5 per share, and you set a sell limit order at USD 6, your order will execute (partially or fully) if the price rises to USD 6 or higher. If the price doesn't rise to USD 6, your order won't execute.

## Placing a limit order
To place a limit order in your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu
- Select your desired instrument and tap 'Buy' or 'Sell'
- Click on the 'Order types' button on the left of 'Review'
- Select 'Limit order' and enter your desired price

## Good 'til cancelled orders
Good 'til cancelled (GTC) orders are supported, allowing you to set expiry dates up to 6 months. You can choose to expire:
- On the same day
- In 90 days
- In 6 months
- On a custom date within the next 6 months
If the expiry date falls on a market holiday, your order will be cancelled at the end of the day on the previous trading day.

title: What is a stop order?
content_text:
A stop order is an order to buy or sell a stock once the price reaches a specified price, known as the stop price. When the stop price is reached, a stop order becomes a market order and is executed at the best available price (which can be lower or higher than the stop price).
Stop orders aren't guaranteed to execute until the price of the stock reaches the set stop price.

## How do sell stop orders work?
For a sell stop order, you can set a stop price which is below the current price of the stock. If the stock falls to your stop price, your sell stop order will become a sell market order. The execution price of the order may be lower or higher than the stop price. Example:
- Stock X is currently trading at $5 per share, but you want to sell the stock as soon as the stock price goes below $4 per share. This may be because you suspect that the price might fall further, and you want to stop holding the share when it reaches $4. You'd set your stop price to $4
- If the stock price of X falls from $5 to $4, your sell stop order will become a sell market order and your order will be executed at the best price available
- If stock price of X doesn’t reach $4, your order won't be triggered

## How can I set a stop order?
You can set a stop order on the Revolut app. On the order screen, tap on the market order dropdown, in the top right corner. You can select the type as stop order and enter the stop price value.
We support good-till-cancelled (GTC) orders. You can set up stop orders with expiry date of up to six months (you can choose from four options and set the order to expire on the same day, in 90 days, in six months. Alternatively, you can elect a custom date - any trading day in the next six months).

title: Trading order types
content_text:
We currently support market orders, market-on-open orders, limit orders, and stop orders.

## Market orders
A market order is an order to buy or sell a stock at the market’s current best available price. Market orders are often prioritised over other order types, which generally means they’re executed immediately during trading hours. 
To place a market order in-app, simply select the stock you want to buy or sell and enter your order details. You'll be able to preview any fee before putting the order through. 

## Market-on-open orders
Market orders placed outside of regular trading hours (9.30am to 4.00pm, Eastern Time) are submitted as market-on-open orders. These orders will be queued for execution at the start of the next regular trading session. 

## Limit orders
A limit order is an order to buy or sell at a specified price or better. Buy limit orders will be executed at the specified price or lower, sell limit orders will be executed at the specified price or higher. You can learn more about limit orders in this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/order-types/what-is-a-limit-order.

## Stop orders
A stop order is an order to buy or sell a stock once the price reaches a specified price, known as the stop price. When the stop price is reached, a stop order becomes a market order and is executed at the best available price (which can be lower or higher than the stop price).
You can learn more about stop trading orders in this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/order-types/what-is-a-stop-order. 

## Recurring buy order
A recurring buy order automates buying stocks or ETFs. You can choose which instrument you want to purchase, how much you want to buy, and how often you want to buy it. You can learn more about recurring buy orders in this FAQ.

title: Top Movers
content_text:
The Top Movers list shows the stocks with the largest percentage price changes over the selected time horizon. There are two filters that can be adjusted:
- Type of movement: filter by Top gainers to see a list of stocks that made the biggest percentage price increases, or by Top losers for the biggest percentage declines.
- Time horizon: the default time horizon is set to the past 1 day, but other options include the past 1 week, the past 1 month, the past 1 year or the past 5 years.
Percentage price changes are calculated as follows:
If the time horizon is set for the past day, the percentage price change is calculated by comparing the current price and the previous full market day’s close. Note: Market hours are from 14:30 - 21:00 GMT), e.g:
- During market hours: On Wednesday 15:30 (GMT), Feb 9, 2022 the percentage price change will be a comparison between the Wednesday 15:30 (GMT), Feb 9, 2022 price and the Tuesday Feb 8, 2022 Market close price,
- Out of market hours: On Wednesday 10:30 (GMT), Feb 9, 2022 the percentage price change will be a comparison between the Wednesday 10:30 (GMT), Feb 9, 2022 price and the Monday Feb 7, 2022 Market close price.
For all other time horizons, the percentage price change is calculated by comparing the current price and the previous open rate for the selected time horizon. Note: Market hours are from 14:30 - 21:00 GMT.
- During market hours: If the feature is set on past 1 week, on Wednesday 15:30 (GMT), Feb 9, 2022 the percentage price change will be a comparison between the Wednesday 15:30 (GMT), Feb 9, 2022 price and the Wednesday Feb 2, 2022 Market open price,
- Out of market hours: On Wednesday 10:30 (GMT), Feb 9, 2022 the percentage price change will be a comparison between the Wednesday 10:30 (GMT), Feb 9, 2022 price and the Wednesday Feb 2, 2022 Market open price.

title: Security prices
content_text:
We provide you with real-time prices at no cost. The price displayed is known as the 'last' price. This is the price of the last trade that had occurred, reported by our data source.
The price displayed in the app is only indicative, which means that your order may be executed at a slightly different rate than the one displayed. This is directly related to the fact that stock prices can sometimes change significantly in 1 minute or even second (as markets are dynamic).

title: What are Revolut's trading fees?
content_text:
## Commission fees
You’ll be able to submit a certain number of commission-free orders within your plan’s rolling monthly allowance. The start and reset dates of the rolling monthly allowance correspond to your plan’s billing cyclehttps://help.revolut.com/help/profile-and-plan/my-plan-benefits/plan-billing/?geo-redirect=true. Check the quota for each plan type below:
- Standard: 1 order
- Plus: 3 orders
- Premium: 5 orders
- Metal and Ultra: 10 orders
Any orders outside your rolling monthly allowance will be subject to a commission fee. This will be 0.25% of the order amount if you're a Standard, Plus, Premium, or Metal customer, or 0.12% of the order amount if you're an Ultra customer. There is a minimum fee of £0.01.

## ADR pass-through fees
These are fees charged by a depositary bank on your American Depositary Receipt (ADR) holdings. They generally range from USD 0.01 to USD 0.05 per share and are normally charged once per year, depending on ‌‌the ADR. For more information, visit this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/stocks/getting-started-with-trading/what-s-available-to-trade/what-is-an-adr/?geo-redirect=true.

## Currency exchange fees
Since the currency in which the order is executed is either USD, EUR, or GBP (depending on whether you're trading US, EEA, or UK stocks), you might need to convert money in your main account to USD, EUR, or GBP to carry out an order. To find out more about currency exchange, read this articlehttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/exchanging-money/how-much-does-it-cost-to-make-an-exchange/will-i-be-charged-for-exchanging-foreign-currencies/?geo-redirect=true.

## Regulatory fees
When applicable, the regulatory fees imposed by regulators will be withheld from your sales proceeds. To find out more about regulatory fees, visit this page/help/wealth/order-execution-fees-and-limits/trading-regulatory-fees.

title: Trading value limits
content_text:
## Minimum order value
The minimum order value is one unit of the relevant currency — e.g. EUR 1, USD 1 — depending on the currency in which the instrument is traded.

## Maximum order limit
You can place buy orders up to 100,000 units of the relevant currency, or up to 50,000 shares if the stock value is less than 100,000 units of the relevant currency at any given time.
You can place sell orders up to a maximum of 1,500,000 units of the relevant currency, or up to 50,000 shares if their value is less than 1,500,000 units of the relevant currency at any given time. For more details on closing a stock position, refer to this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/order-execution/closing-my-stock-position/. Trading limits are subject to change.

## Example of limit calculation
You buy USD 100,000 worth of a stock and then immediately buy another USD 100,000 worth of the same stock. However, if the USD 100,000 is for less than 50,000 shares each time, you can't buy USD 101,000 worth of stock (unless you’re subscribed to Trading Pro, which offers higher order limits).

## If you have Trading Pro
If you subscribe to the Trading Pro service, your order limit is increased up to 250,000 units of the asset currency, or up to 50,000 shares if their value is less than 250,000 units of the asset currency.
You can read more about our Trading Pro features in this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/trading-pro/what-is-trading-pro. Trading limits are subject to change.

title: Why is my stock on 'sell only'?
content_text:
In some cases, we might restrict only the possibility of buying new shares of a stock due to a variety of reasons, while still allowing you to keep or sell the ones you bought in the past. We refer to these stocks as 'sell only' stocks.
The reasons a stock will fall under this category is usually one of the following:
- Sanctions
- Corporate actions
- Operational decisions
- Stocks trading over-the-counter (OTC) or being priced extremely low
- Stocks facing impending delisting from exchanges

title: Orders submitted during extended hours trading
content_text:
## Available order types
Stop orders and market orders can be placed at any time, but will only be executed during regular trading hours, and when the conditions of the order are met.
Limit orders can be queued for extended trading hours, and regular trading hours. To learn more about limit orders, see this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/order-types/what-is-a-limit-order in our Help Centre.

## When will the order be executed?
Your limit order, if it includes extended hours trading, may execute within any pre-market, regular trading hours, and after-hour sessions until the order expiration date.
You have flexibility in setting your limit order expiration date to any trading day within the next 6 months.

## Order cancellation during extended hours
To cancel a pending order in your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu 
- Scroll down to the 'Orders' tab (it’s only visible if you have pending orders)
- Tap the order you'd like to cancel and select 'Cancel'
Executed trades can't be cancelled and in some circumstances, we may not be able to fulfil your request to cancel a trade.

title: Extended hours trading risks
content_text:
Extended hours trading for US stocks are exclusively executed on a limit order basis. Read this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/when-can-i-trade/extended-hours-trading/what-is-extended-hours-trading  to learn more on order execution for extended hours.
Extended hours trading offers more flexibility but comes with increased risks compared to regular trading hours, as outlined below.

## Lower liquidity and high volatility
Extended hours trading can result in greater price volatility, lower liquidity, and less competitive prices compared to regular trading hours, potentially leading to unexecuted or partially filled orders.

## Larger bid/ask spreads
The spread refers to the difference between the  price you can buy and sell a security for. Lower liquidity and higher volatility in extended hours trading may result in wider than normal spreads for a particular security.

## Unlinked markets
Prices on our extended hours trading system may not match those on other systems dealing in the same securities, potentially leading to an inferior price for you.

## Price discrepancies
The prices of securities traded in extended hours trading may not reflect the prices either at the end of regular trading hours, or upon next day’s open. As a result, you may receive an inferior price when engaging in extended hours trading than you would during regular trading hours.

## News and announcements 
Issuers often make news or financial information announcements outside of regular trading hours. These announcements, if combined with lower liquidity and higher volatility, may cause an exaggerated and unsustainable price movement.
Extended hours trading might not be suitable for everyone. For more details, review our Risk Disclosurehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-risk-disclosure/.

title: How extended hours trading works
content_text:
Extended hours trading is trading that occurs outside of regular trading hours. Only US whole shares on the platform are eligible for extended hours trading. See this FAQ/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/when-can-i-trade/extended-hours-trading/what-are-the-risks-of-extended-hours-trading to understand relevant risks. 
Regular trading hours for stock exchanges in the US are Monday to Friday from 9:30am to 4:00pm Eastern Time (ET). 
Extended hours trading occurs outside those hours — from 4:00am to 9:30am ET for pre-market and 4:00pm to 8:00pm ET for after-hours trading. Extended market hours will not be available during official US market holidays. 

## Order execution
Orders submitted for execution during extended market hours can only be limit orders in whole shares.
 Orders will begin to queue for pre-market hours starting after the close of post-market hours on the previous trading day and will be eligible for execution during pre-market hours. 
Any unexecuted or partially filled orders during pre-market hours will automatically roll over into regular market hours. If these are still not fully executed, the orders roll over into post-market hours. Orders may be cancelled if still unexecuted by the end of the post-market hours.

## Get started
You can submit an order for execution in extended hours by:
- Selecting 'Buy' or 'Sell' in-app and choosing 'Limit Order'
- Entering your preferred limit price
- Turning on the 'Include extended hours' option
- Setting an expiry date if desired, then reviewing and submitting the order
To learn more about extended hours trading orders, visit this FAQ./help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/when-can-i-trade/extended-hours-trading/what-are-the-order-types-i-can-submit-during-extended-hours

title: What is a trading day?
content_text:
For US-listed securities, a trading day runs from 9:30am to 4:00pm, Eastern Time (ET). Trading days are usually Monday to Friday, except for market holidays or shortened trading days.
For European-listed securities, a trading day starts at 7:00am and ends at 8:00pm, Monday to Friday, Universal Coordinated Time (UTC) — except for market holidays or shortened trading days.
For UK-listed securities, the trading day typically starts at 8:00 am and ends at 4:30 pm, Monday to Friday, Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) — excluding public holidays or any shortened trading days, as determined by the London Stock Exchange.

title: Trading hours
content_text:
## Regular trading (open market) hours
- For US securities: regular trading hours are Monday to Friday, 9.30am to 4pm (Eastern Time) — unless there's a market holiday or shortened trading days
- For European securities: orders will be executed from Monday to Friday, 7am to 8pm (Coordinated Universal Time, UTC) — except for market holidays or shortened trading days
- For UK securities: orders will be executed from Monday to Friday, 8:00 am to 4:30 pm (UTC) — except for public holidays or shortened trading days

## Hours for trading orders
You can still place market-on-open orders, which will be queued for execution at market open on the next trading day. 
Limit and stop orders can be placed and queued at any time, but will only be executed during market hours when the conditions of the order are met. Read this FAQhttps://help.revolut.com/help/wealth/stocks/trading-stocks/order-types/what-type-of-orders-can-i-submit/ to learn more about order types.

title: What are the fees for Trading Pro?
content_text:
The Trading Pro subscription fee is £15 per month. Currently, we don't offer an annual subscription payment for Trading Pro. If you haven’t previously activated Trading Pro, you can try it for 30 calendar days, free of charge. For more information, please visit our Trading Pro termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-trading-pro-terms.
We'll remind you when your free trial is about to end. Your subscription will automatically renew and you'll be charged the non-refundable monthly fee, unless you cancel it before your trial ends. We may also cancel the subscription for you before the trial ends if you haven't used it. 
If you cancel your subscription before your free trial ends, you can continue to use Trading Pro’s benefits until the end of your 30-day free trial. You can't reactivate or extend your free trial.

title: What is Trading Pro?
content_text:
Trading Pro gives you access to pricing benefits related to stock trading, as well as additional analytics features to enjoy in the Revolut app. Trading Pro is a subscription paid on a monthly basis, which you can cancel at any time. 

## Trading Pro benefits
- A discounted securities commission fee of 0.12% instead of 0.25%, applicable to all Revolut plans – except Ultra which already includes this benefit
- Higher securities order size limits, up to the lowest of 250,000 units of the asset currency or 50,000 shares
- Extra portfolio analytics in-app
You can try Trading Pro for 30 calendar days, free of charge. After the free trial period, the Trading Pro subscription is £15 per month.
Trading Pro is a service provided by Revolut Trading Ltd. For further information about the service and applicable conditions, please refer to our Trading Pro termshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/RTL-trading-pro-terms.

## To subscribe to Trading Pro
- Go to 'Investhttps://app.revolut.com/hub/stocksrevolut://app/trading' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'More'
- Tap 'Trading Pro'
